// DBML Schema for Business: Grocery
// Version: 1
// Generated on: 2026-05-04 18:34:41

Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Grocery - Version 1'
}

Table customer.shopper {
  shopper_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each grocery shopper record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key and golden record identifier for all B2C customer identity data integrated from Salesforce CRM and NCR POS loyalty enrollment.']
  format_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_format. Business justification: MARKETING: Preferred store format drives targeted campaigns and inventory allocation; shoppers often indicate format preference in loyalty app.']
  fulfillment_delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_route. Business justification: Preferred delivery route assignment for recurring grocery deliveries, used in route‑optimization and delivery‑performance reports.']
  personal_shopper_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Personal shopper program assigns a dedicated associate to a shopper; needed for service scheduling, performance tracking, and loyalty reporting.']
  preferred_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Category Preference Marketing Report needs each shopper\'s preferred category to target promotions; shoppers naturally have a preferred category, so a FK preferred_category_id → category.category_id is']
  preferred_pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: Supports loyalty program\'s preferred pharmacy assignment for auto‑refill and targeted marketing.']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.sla_policy. Business justification: Apply a shopper‑tier‑based SLA policy for order fulfillment guarantees; used in SLA compliance and compensation reporting.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shopper\'s most frequently visited or self-designated home store location. Used for store-level personalization, local ad circular targeting, pickup order routing, and store-level comp sales attribution.']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'Date on which the shopper account was permanently closed or deactivated. Populated only when account_status = \'closed\'. Used for GDPR right-to-erasure compliance tracking, retention policy enforcement, and churn analysis.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shopper account. \'active\' indicates a fully enrolled shopper; \'suspended\' indicates a temporarily restricted account; \'closed\' indicates a permanently deactivated account; \'pending_verification\' indicates registration awaiting email or identity confirmation; \'merged\' indicates the record was consolidated into another golden record.']
  acquisition_source varchar [note: 'Marketing or operational source that drove the shopper\'s initial registration (e.g., \'weekly_ad_circular\', \'referral\', \'loyalty_kiosk\', \'digital_coupon\', \'fuel_center_promo\'). Used for marketing attribution, campaign ROI analysis, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) modeling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weekly_ad_circular|referral|loyalty_kiosk|digital_coupon|fuel_center_promo|organic_web|paid_search|social_media — promote to reference product]']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the shopper\'s primary residential address (street number and street name). Used for direct mail delivery, last-mile delivery (LMD) routing, and geographic segmentation analytics.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the shopper\'s primary residential address (apartment, suite, unit number). Supplements address_line1 for complete delivery address resolution.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Date of birth of the shopper in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for age verification (alcohol, tobacco, pharmacy), birthday loyalty rewards, WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program eligibility, and demographic segmentation analytics.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the shopper\'s primary residential address. Used for geographic market segmentation, delivery zone assignment, and local promotional targeting.']
  cltv_tier varchar [note: 'Segmentation tier assigned to the shopper based on their Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) score. Drives differentiated marketing investment, personalized offer depth, and service prioritization. Tiers are recalculated periodically by the analytics platform.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the shopper\'s primary residential address (e.g., \'USA\'). Supports international shopper identification and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shopper record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer golden record store, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for the MASTER_PARTY role. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  crm_contact_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned to this shopper in Salesforce CRM (Customer Relationship Management). Used to reconcile the golden record with the CRM source system for B2C identity resolution and personalization workflows.']
  direct_mail_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has opted in to receive physical direct mail communications such as weekly Ad Circulars, personalized coupons, and promotional mailers. True = opted in. Drives mailing list inclusion for print marketing campaigns.']
  ebt_card_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has an EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) card registered to their account for online SNAP ordering. True = EBT card on file, enabling SNAP-eligible online checkout per USDA online purchasing pilot requirements.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the shopper account. Used as the principal digital contact channel for order confirmations, promotional communications, loyalty program updates, and account recovery. Sourced from Salesforce CRM.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has opted in to receive marketing communications via email. True = opted in. Must be honored in all outbound email campaigns. Consent state is tracked per CAN-SPAM Act and GDPR Article 7 requirements.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal given name of the shopper as captured at registration. Used for identity verification, personalized communications, and loyalty program correspondence.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-reported gender identity of the shopper. Used for demographic segmentation, personalized product recommendations, and diversity analytics. Collected with explicit consent per GDPR and CCPA requirements.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this shopper to a household grouping entity. Multiple shoppers sharing a household are linked via this identifier for household-level analytics, shared loyalty benefits, and targeted household promotions. Resolved through identity matching in Salesforce CRM.']
  identity_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper\'s identity has been verified through an identity confirmation process (e.g., email verification, phone OTP, or in-store ID check). True = identity confirmed. Used for fraud prevention, age-restricted purchase eligibility, and pharmacy patient linking.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal family name of the shopper as captured at registration. Combined with first_name to form the full legal identity used in account management and regulatory compliance.']
  loyalty_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper is currently enrolled in Grocery\'s loyalty rewards program. True = enrolled and eligible for points accrual, personalized offers, and loyalty pricing. False = not enrolled. Sourced from NCR POS loyalty integration.']
  loyalty_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the shopper enrolled in the loyalty rewards program. Used to calculate loyalty tenure, tier progression timelines, and anniversary benefit triggers.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the shopper, ranging from 0 to 10. Indicates likelihood to recommend Grocery to others. Used for customer satisfaction tracking, churn risk modeling, and service recovery prioritization.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) survey response was captured. Used to assess recency of satisfaction data and determine eligibility for re-survey.']
  pharmacy_patient boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper is also a registered pharmacy patient at a Grocery pharmacy location. True = pharmacy patient record exists (non-PHI flag only; clinical data is managed exclusively in McKesson Pharmacy Systems under HIPAA). Used for cross-domain identity linking without exposing protected health information.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the shopper, stored in E.164 international format. Used for SMS marketing opt-ins, order pickup notifications, delivery alerts, and two-factor authentication.']
  pos_loyalty_code varchar [note: 'Loyalty card or account identifier assigned by the NCR POS (Point of Sale) system at the time of in-store loyalty enrollment. Used to link POS transaction history to the shopper golden record.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the shopper\'s primary residential address. Used for delivery zone mapping, trade area analysis, demographic append, and targeted ad circular distribution.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the shopper prefers to be addressed by in communications and personalized experiences (e.g., nickname or chosen name). May differ from legal first name. Supports personalization in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language of the shopper expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'zh-TW\'). Drives localization of digital communications, app content, and in-store signage preferences for omnichannel personalization.']
  push_notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has enabled push notifications on the Grocery mobile app. True = push notifications enabled. Used for real-time order status updates, personalized offers, and pickup-ready alerts via Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  registration_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the shopper originally registered their account. Supports acquisition source analytics, channel attribution, and omnichannel engagement strategy. Values align with Grocery\'s omnichannel touchpoints including NCR POS in-store enrollment and Salesforce Commerce Cloud digital channels.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shopper account was first created. Used for tenure calculations, cohort analysis, anniversary loyalty rewards, and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) segmentation.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Marketing and analytics segment assigned to the shopper based on behavioral, demographic, and transactional data (e.g., \'health_conscious\', \'value_seeker\', \'organic_buyer\', \'family_household\', \'convenience_shopper\'). Drives personalized offer targeting and assortment planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health_conscious|value_seeker|organic_buyer|family_household|convenience_shopper|premium_shopper|deal_hunter — promote to reference product]']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has opted in to receive marketing and transactional communications via SMS text message. True = opted in. Consent is required under TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) prior to any commercial SMS transmission.']
  snap_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has been identified as eligible to use SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) / EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) for qualifying purchases. True = SNAP-eligible. Used for EBT tender routing at POS and SNAP-compliant product filtering in digital commerce.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code of the shopper\'s primary residential address (e.g., \'CA\', \'TX\'). Used for state-level regulatory compliance (e.g., state WIC agency reporting, state pharmacy board jurisdiction), tax jurisdiction assignment, and regional marketing segmentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shopper record in the Databricks Silver Layer, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  wic_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper is enrolled in or eligible for the WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) federal nutrition program. True = WIC-eligible. Enables WIC-approved product filtering, WIC tender acceptance at POS, and compliance reporting to state WIC agencies.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing every individual grocery shopper (B2C) known to Grocery. Serves as the single source of truth for shopper identity, capturing legal name, preferred name, date of birth, gender, primary language, registration channel (in-store, app, web), account status (active, suspended, closed), SNAP/WIC/EBT eligibility flags, loyalty enrollment status, CLTV tier, NPS score, acquisition source, and opt-in/opt-out flags for marketing communications. This is the golden record for all B2C customer identity data integrated from Salesforce CRM and NCR POS loyalty enrollment.'
}

Table customer.wholesale_account {
  wholesale_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wholesale_account']
  account_owner_shopper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.shopper. Business justification: Wholesale_account needs an in‑domain relationship to identify the owning shopper (account owner). Adding account_owner_shopper_id creates this link without affecting existing structure.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Grocery sales or account manager employee assigned to manage the relationship with this wholesale account. Used for territory management, performance reporting, and escalation routing.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Finance budgets are allocated to wholesale accounts for forecasting spend and tracking actuals against budgeted amounts.']
  dedicated_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier. Business justification: Wholesale accounts often have dedicated carrier contracts; needed for carrier billing, SLA compliance, and contract reporting.']
  edi_partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'The EDI interchange partner identifier (ISA qualifier + ID) assigned to the wholesale account for electronic order and invoice exchange. Required when edi_capable_flag is True. Used to route EDI transactions in the SAP EDI subsystem.']
  fulfillment_delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_route. Business justification: Specific delivery routes are assigned to bulk‑shipment wholesale accounts; required for route scheduling and capacity planning.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables profitability analysis per wholesale account; profit_center links sales to internal profit reporting structures.']
  supply_supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: Contract management: Supplier contracts are negotiated per wholesale account; linking enables contract compliance, SLA tracking, and account‑specific pricing.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wholesale account. Controls order eligibility, credit access, and system visibility. Active accounts may place orders; suspended accounts are blocked pending resolution; closed accounts are archived. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_PARTY role.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Segmentation tier assigned to the wholesale account based on revenue potential, purchase volume, and strategic importance. Determines service levels, dedicated account management, pricing tiers, and promotional eligibility. Values: strategic (top-tier, high-volume), standard (mid-market), small_business (emerging or low-volume).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the wholesale account by business type, indicating the nature of the organizational buyer. Drives pricing eligibility, product assortment access, and compliance requirements. Satisfies CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_PARTY role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restaurant|caterer|institutional|corporate|food_service|school_district|hospital|hotel — promote to reference product]']
  annual_revenue_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual gross revenue (in USD) of the wholesale account\'s business, sourced from credit applications, Dun & Bradstreet data, or account manager input. Used for account tier assignment, credit limit setting, and strategic segmentation.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the wholesale account\'s billing address. Used for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and accounts receivable correspondence.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary street address line for the wholesale account\'s billing address (suite, floor, unit number, etc.). Supplements billing_address_line1 for complete address representation.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the wholesale account\'s billing address. Used for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic segmentation of B2B accounts.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the wholesale account\'s billing address (e.g., USA, CAN). Supports international wholesale accounts and cross-border tax compliance.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the wholesale account\'s billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, delivery zone assignment, and geographic analytics.']
  billing_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code for the wholesale account\'s billing address (e.g., CA, TX, NY). Used for state tax compliance, sales tax nexus determination, and regional reporting.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the wholesale supply agreement or master service contract expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null indicates an open-ended or evergreen contract. Used for renewal alerts, pricing expiry, and account review workflows.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the wholesale supply agreement or master service contract between Grocery and the wholesale account becomes effective. Used for contract lifecycle management, pricing eligibility, and compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the wholesale account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails, onboarding analytics, and data lineage. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for MASTER_PARTY role.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wholesale account is currently on credit hold, blocking new order processing until outstanding balances are resolved or credit terms are renegotiated. Managed by the accounts receivable team in SAP FI.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance (in USD) approved for the wholesale account by the credit department. Orders that would cause the balance to exceed this limit are blocked pending credit review. Managed in SAP FI credit management.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal credit risk rating assigned to the wholesale account based on payment history, Dun & Bradstreet data, and financial review. Informs credit limit decisions and payment term negotiations.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'The trade name or \'Doing Business As\' (DBA) name under which the wholesale account operates, if different from the legal business name. Used for operational communications, order labels, and account management.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The preferred or contracted method by which wholesale orders are fulfilled and delivered to the account. DC delivery uses Grocery\'s Distribution Center (DC) fleet; DSD (Direct Store Delivery) uses vendor-direct routes; pickup indicates the account collects from a Grocery facility; third_party_carrier uses an external logistics provider.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number uniquely identifying the wholesale account\'s business entity globally. Used for credit risk assessment, supplier onboarding, and B2B identity verification.']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wholesale account is set up to exchange orders, invoices, and advance ship notices via Electronic Data Interchange (EDI). EDI-capable accounts use automated order processing workflows, reducing manual entry.']
  ein varchar [note: 'The federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) / Tax Identification Number (TIN) assigned by the IRS to the wholesale account. Required for tax reporting (1099 issuance), credit applications, and regulatory compliance. Format: XX-XXXXXXX.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent order placed by the wholesale account. Used for account activity monitoring, dormancy detection, and retention analytics. Updated by the order management system upon each new order.']
  legal_business_name varchar [note: 'The full legal registered name of the wholesale account as it appears on official business registration documents, contracts, and tax filings. Used for invoicing, credit agreements, and regulatory compliance. Satisfies IDENTITY_LABEL category for MASTER_PARTY role.']
  minimum_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum order value (in USD) required for the wholesale account to place a valid order. Orders below this threshold are flagged or blocked. Used to ensure order profitability and logistics efficiency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for account managers to record operational notes, special handling instructions, relationship context, or escalation history relevant to the wholesale account. Not used for structured analytics.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'The date on which the wholesale account was formally onboarded and approved to begin placing orders with Grocery. Distinct from created_timestamp (system record creation); this reflects business approval completion.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms governing when invoices are due for the wholesale account. Drives accounts receivable aging, cash flow forecasting, and order release decisions. Common values: net_30 (payment due 30 days from invoice), net_60, net_90, cod (Cash on Delivery), prepaid.']
  preferred_delivery_day varchar [note: 'The preferred day of the week for wholesale order deliveries to the account, as agreed during onboarding or account setup. Used by the Transportation Management System (TMS) for route optimization and scheduling.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for the main business contact at the wholesale account. Used for order confirmations, invoices, promotional communications, and account management. Satisfies PRIMARY_CONTACT category for MASTER_PARTY role.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person at the wholesale account. Used for account communications, order confirmations, and relationship management. This is a business contact, not a personal consumer.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the primary business contact at the wholesale account. Used for order inquiries, delivery coordination, and account management communications.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary business contact at the wholesale account (e.g., Purchasing Manager, Executive Chef, Director of Operations). Used for targeted communications and account segmentation.']
  sales_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic or organizational sales territory to which this wholesale account is assigned. Used for territory-based reporting, quota allocation, and account manager assignment.']
  snap_wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wholesale account is authorized to purchase SNAP/WIC-eligible products for resale or institutional use under applicable federal program guidelines. Relevant for school districts, food banks, and institutional buyers.']
  tax_exempt_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued tax exemption or resale certificate number for the wholesale account. Required when tax_exempt_flag is True. Used for sales tax audit compliance and exemption documentation.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wholesale account holds a valid tax exemption certificate (e.g., resale certificate, government entity, non-profit). When True, applicable sales taxes are suppressed on invoices. Requires a valid tax_exempt_certificate_number.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the wholesale account record was most recently modified in the system. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trails, and data freshness monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  wholesale_price_group varchar [note: 'Price group code assigned to the wholesale account that determines which wholesale pricing schedule or contract pricing tier applies when generating quotes and orders. Maps to pricing conditions in SAP SD.']

  Note: 'Master entity for B2B wholesale and institutional accounts served by Grocery (e.g., restaurants, food service operators, corporate cafeterias, school districts, hospitals). Captures legal business name, DBA name, business type (restaurant, caterer, institutional, corporate), tax ID (EIN), DUNS number, credit limit, payment terms (net-30, net-60, COD), assigned account manager, account tier (strategic, standard, small business), contract start/end dates, billing address, primary contact person, and annual revenue estimate. Distinct from the individual shopper entity — represents an organizational buyer rather than a person. Supports B2B order management, credit approval workflows, and wholesale pricing eligibility.'
}

Table customer.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the household record. Primary key for the household data product within the customer domain. Entity role: MASTER_PARTY — represents a household grouping that the business interacts with as a loyalty and marketing counterparty.']
  fulfillment_delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_route. Business justification: Household‑level default delivery route for scheduled deliveries, required for logistics planning and route scheduling.']
  merged_into_household_id varchar [note: 'When a household record is consolidated (status = \'merged\'), this field references the surviving household ID into which this record was merged. Enables data lineage tracking and prevents orphaned loyalty history. Null when the household has not been merged.']
  primary_shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer record designated as the primary loyalty cardholder and decision-maker for this household. Used for personalization, targeted promotions, and CLTV modeling. The primary shopper holds the loyalty account in their name.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.region. Business justification: REGIONAL LOYALTY: Household region is used for regional loyalty program eligibility and sales reporting per region.']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.sla_policy. Business justification: Household‑level SLA policy defines delivery expectations; required for customer‑service KPI tracking and breach notifications.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store location most frequently visited by this household, determined by transaction frequency analysis. Used for store-level personalization, localized ad circular targeting, and store trade area reporting.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the household was first acquired as a loyalty member. Used for marketing attribution, channel ROI analysis, and acquisition cost modeling. Supports optimization of loyalty enrollment investment across channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_store|digital|direct_mail|referral|pharmacy|fuel_center|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cltv_tier varchar [note: 'Household-level CLTV tier assignment based on estimated lifetime value scoring. Drives differentiated loyalty benefits, promotional investment levels, and personalization depth. Platinum represents highest-value households; untiered indicates insufficient data for classification.']
  communication_opt_in_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has opted in to receive marketing and promotional communications via email. Must be respected for all outbound email campaigns per CAN-SPAM Act and CCPA opt-out requirements.']
  communication_opt_in_mail boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has opted in to receive physical direct mail communications, including weekly ad circulars and promotional mailers. Used for print campaign targeting and postal budget allocation.']
  communication_opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has opted in to receive marketing and promotional communications via SMS/text message. Must be respected per TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) consent requirements for automated text messaging.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the household record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_PARTY entity. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.']
  crm_household_code varchar [note: 'External household identifier assigned by Salesforce CRM. Used to correlate the lakehouse household record with the corresponding Salesforce CRM household object for bi-directional integration and data lineage.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or channel through which this household record was first created. Used for data lineage, quality scoring, and source-system reconciliation. Values reflect the operational systems of record integrated into the lakehouse.']
  dietary_preference_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of self-reported or inferred dietary preferences for the household (e.g., \'organic,gluten_free,kosher,vegan\'). Used for personalized product recommendations, digital storefront filtering, and targeted promotions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic|gluten_free|kosher|halal|vegan|vegetarian|dairy_free|nut_free|low_sodium — promote to reference product]']
  digital_account_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household\'s loyalty account is linked to a digital account on the Salesforce Commerce Cloud eCommerce platform, enabling online ordering, digital coupons, and personalized web/app experiences.']
  ebt_card_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has an EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) card registered for use in online/pickup/delivery orders. Required for omnichannel SNAP/WIC benefit redemption compliance per USDA online EBT pilot program requirements.']
  estimated_annual_grocery_spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total annual grocery expenditure for the household across all channels (in-store, pickup, delivery), expressed in USD. Derived from loyalty transaction history and third-party spend data. Used as a key input for CLTV modeling, tier assignment, and promotional investment prioritization. Not a calculated aggregate — represents a modeled estimate stored as a business attribute.']
  fuel_rewards_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household is enrolled in the fuel rewards program, enabling per-gallon discount accrual at Grocery fuel centers. Used for fuel center promotional targeting and cross-category basket-building incentives.']
  home_ownership_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the household owns or rents their primary residence. Used as a demographic segmentation variable for targeted promotions (e.g., home improvement, cleaning supplies) and CLTV modeling. Sourced from third-party demographic append or loyalty enrollment data.']
  household_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the household, typically derived from the primary shopper\'s surname (e.g., \'Smith Household\'). Used in CRM displays, loyalty communications, and household-level reporting. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_PARTY entity.']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household record. \'active\' indicates an engaged household with recent loyalty activity. \'inactive\' indicates no transactions within the defined inactivity window. \'suspended\' indicates a temporary hold (e.g., fraud review). \'merged\' indicates the household was consolidated into another. \'deceased\' indicates all primary members are deceased.']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household by its primary relationship with the grocery business. Distinguishes B2C retail shoppers, B2B wholesale accounts, SNAP/WIC/EBT program participants, pharmacy patient households, and employee households. Drives eligibility rules, pricing tiers, and program enrollment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: b2c_shopper|b2b_wholesale|snap_wic_participant|pharmacy_patient|employee — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  income_band varchar [note: 'Estimated annual household income range, derived from third-party demographic data or self-reported during loyalty enrollment. Used for private-label brand targeting, SNAP/WIC eligibility modeling, and CLTV segmentation. Stored as a band rather than exact income to reduce sensitivity.']
  is_test_household boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this household record is a test or synthetic record created for QA, UAT, or system integration testing purposes. Test households must be excluded from all production analytics, reporting, and marketing campaign execution.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase transaction recorded for any member of this household across all channels (in-store, pickup, delivery). Used for recency scoring in RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) analysis, lapsed-shopper identification, and win-back campaign targeting.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the household\'s current life stage based on composition and demographics. Drives assortment recommendations, promotional targeting, and personalization strategies. Values: single (one adult), couple (two adults, no children), family_with_children, empty_nester (adults, children departed), senior (primary shopper 65+).']
  loyalty_account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known loyalty program account number associated with this household. Serves as the primary business identifier printed on loyalty cards and referenced at NCR POS during transaction processing. Links household to loyalty accrual and redemption activity.']
  loyalty_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the household was first enrolled in the grocery loyalty program. Marks the EFFECTIVE_FROM of the household\'s loyalty relationship. Used for tenure-based segmentation, anniversary promotions, and cohort analysis.']
  loyalty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the household\'s loyalty program membership expires or is scheduled for review. Nullable for open-ended memberships. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for the loyalty relationship. Used for renewal campaigns and lapsed-member win-back programs.']
  member_count_adult varchar [note: 'Number of adult members (age 18+) currently associated with this household. Used to differentiate adult-targeted promotions, alcohol/tobacco eligibility, and per-adult spend benchmarking from total household size.']
  member_count_child varchar [note: 'Number of minor members (age under 18) currently associated with this household. Drives targeting for baby/toddler, school-age, and family product categories, as well as WIC program eligibility assessment.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) recorded for this household, on a scale of 0–10. Derived from post-transaction or periodic satisfaction surveys. Used to classify households as Promoters (9–10), Passives (7–8), or Detractors (0–6) for loyalty and service recovery programs.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent NPS survey response was recorded for this household. Used to determine survey recency, control re-survey frequency, and track NPS trend over time.']
  pharmacy_household boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more members of this household are active pharmacy patients at a Grocery-operated pharmacy location. Non-PHI flag used for household-level pharmacy service targeting and cross-sell of health/wellness products. No prescription or clinical data is stored in this product.']
  preferred_fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The household\'s predominant order fulfillment preference based on historical transaction patterns. Values: in_store (traditional shopping), pickup (click-and-collect / curbside), delivery (last-mile delivery), mixed (no dominant channel). Used for omnichannel personalization and capacity planning.']
  private_label_affinity varchar [note: 'Household-level affinity classification for Grocery\'s private-label brand products, based on the share of private-label SKUs in the household\'s purchase history. Used for private-label brand development targeting, promotional investment, and category management insights.']
  residential_zip_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit (or ZIP+4) US postal code of the household\'s primary residence. Used for geographic segmentation, store trade area analysis, last-mile delivery (LMD) zone assignment, and targeted ad circular distribution. Not a full address — ZIP-level granularity balances utility with privacy.']
  size varchar [note: 'Total number of individuals currently associated with this household, including primary, secondary, and dependent members. Used for basket size benchmarking, per-capita spend analysis, and product assortment planning. Updated dynamically as members join or depart.']
  snap_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has been identified as eligible for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits. Used to enable EBT payment processing at POS, apply SNAP-eligible item filtering, and comply with USDA SNAP retailer authorization requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the household record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.']
  wic_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has been identified as eligible for WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits. Enables WIC-approved item filtering at POS and supports USDA WIC vendor compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Represents a household grouping of one or more shoppers sharing a residence and loyalty account. Serves as the single source of truth for household composition and member associations — no other product in this domain stores household membership data. Captures household ID, primary shopper reference, household size, estimated annual grocery spend, household income band, home ownership status, residential zip code, and household lifecycle stage (single, couple, family with children, empty nester, senior). Owns all member associations including member role (primary, secondary, dependent), join and departure dates, and primary loyalty cardholder designation. Enables household composition tracking over time, multi-member loyalty accrual rules, household-level personalization, targeted promotions, and CLTV modeling. Integrates with Salesforce CRM household object.'
}

Table customer.contact_info {
  contact_info_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each contact information record in the Grocery customer domain. Serves as the primary key for the contact_info data product.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.shopper. Business justification: Contact_info stores shopper addresses; linking to shopper normalizes address data and removes redundant address columns from shopper.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Grocery store location most closely associated with this address record, derived from geographic proximity analysis. Used for store assignment, click-and-collect (pickup) routing, and localized promotional targeting (ad circular distribution).']
  address_residential_indicator varchar [note: 'USPS-derived classification indicating whether the address is a residential or commercial location. Impacts last-mile delivery (LMD) carrier surcharge calculations, delivery time window eligibility, and cold chain logistics planning.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the purpose of the physical or mailing address captured in this record (e.g., home, billing, delivery, pickup, mailing). Null when the record represents a non-address contact type such as email or phone.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. \'active\' indicates the contact is valid and in use; \'bounced\' indicates email hard-bounce or phone disconnection; \'suppressed\' indicates the contact is on a suppression list for compliance reasons; \'pending_verification\' indicates awaiting confirmation.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the contact channel captured in this record (e.g., email, mobile phone, home phone, work phone, fax). Drives channel-specific communication routing and CAN-SPAM/TCPA compliance enforcement.']
  contact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual contact detail for non-address contact types: email address, mobile phone number, home phone number, work phone number, or fax number. Null when the record represents a physical address. Contains PII and must be handled per GDPR/CCPA requirements.']
  county varchar [note: 'County or parish name associated with the address. Used for local tax jurisdiction mapping, regulatory reporting, and geographic segmentation analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this contact record was first created in the Grocery customer data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR/CCPA data subject request processing.']
  delivery_zone_code varchar [note: 'Internal delivery zone identifier assigned to this address based on geographic proximity to fulfillment centers, micro-fulfillment centers (MFC), or store locations. Used by the Order Management System (OMS) and last-mile delivery (LMD) routing to determine delivery eligibility, time windows, and carrier assignment.']
  dnc_registry_checked_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which this phone contact record was checked against the FTC National Do Not Call (DNC) Registry. Required for TCPA compliance; phone numbers must be scrubbed against the DNC registry at least every 31 days before telemarketing calls.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all outbound communications to this contact record are suppressed regardless of opt-in status. Set when an entity is on a national Do Not Call (DNC) registry, has filed a legal complaint, or has requested complete communication cessation. Overrides all opt-in flags.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this contact record is no longer active or valid. Null for currently active records. Supports bi-temporal modeling, address history retention, and regulatory data retention requirements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact record is considered active and valid for use in communications and fulfillment operations. Supports bi-temporal modeling and historical contact data management.']
  entity_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent entity (shopper, wholesale account, or pharmacy patient) to whom this contact record belongs. Supports multiple contact records per entity.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the type of entity this contact record belongs to, enabling the contact_info product to serve B2C shoppers, B2B wholesale accounts, and pharmacy patients (non-PHI) within the customer domain.']
  is_default_address boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address record is the default address for the entity. Used by the Order Management System (OMS) and omnichannel fulfillment to pre-populate delivery and billing addresses at checkout.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact record is the primary contact of its type for the entity. Only one record per entity per contact_type should have is_primary = true. Used to determine the default channel for outbound communications.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact value (email or phone) or address has been verified through an active confirmation process (e.g., email click-through, SMS OTP, USPS validation). Drives communication eligibility and fraud risk scoring.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) derived from USPS address normalization and geocoding. Supports last-mile delivery (LMD) route optimization, store assignment, and geographic segmentation.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) derived from USPS address normalization and geocoding. Supports last-mile delivery (LMD) route optimization, store assignment, and geographic segmentation.']
  opt_in_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity has provided explicit opt-in consent to receive marketing email communications on this email contact record. Required for CAN-SPAM and GDPR compliance. Null or false for non-email contact types.']
  opt_in_push boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity has opted in to receive push notifications through the Grocery mobile application or digital commerce platform. Supports omnichannel personalization and promotional targeting via Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity has provided explicit opt-in consent to receive SMS/text message communications on this mobile phone contact record. Required for TCPA compliance. Null or false for non-mobile-phone contact types.']
  preferred_language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language tag indicating the entity\'s preferred language for communications delivered to this contact (e.g., en-US, es-MX, zh-CN). Supports multilingual marketing, ad circular distribution, and omnichannel personalization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this contact record originated (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud for digital registrations, NCR POS for in-store capture, SAP Retail for wholesale accounts, McKesson for pharmacy patients). Supports data lineage and master data management reconciliation.']
  source_system_contact_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this contact record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce Contact Point ID, SAP partner address number). Supports cross-system reconciliation and master data management (MDM) deduplication.']
  unsubscribe_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the entity unsubscribed or withdrew consent for communications on this contact record. Null if the entity has not unsubscribed. Required for CAN-SPAM 10-business-day honor period and GDPR right-to-withdraw-consent tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this contact record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  usps_delivery_point_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit USPS Delivery Point Code appended to the ZIP+4 to form the full 11-digit delivery point barcode. Confirms precise deliverability and supports carrier route optimization for last-mile delivery (LMD).']
  usps_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of USPS address normalization and validation process. \'verified\' indicates the address was confirmed deliverable by USPS; \'unverified\' indicates validation has not been performed or was inconclusive; \'invalid\' indicates the address failed USPS validation and is not deliverable.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the contact value or address was last successfully verified. Used to assess verification currency and trigger re-verification workflows when verification has lapsed.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'USPS ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the address record. Supports delivery zone routing, geographic segmentation, store assignment, and SNAP/WIC/EBT program eligibility determination.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all contact details and physical/mailing addresses for shoppers and wholesale accounts. Owns phone numbers, email addresses, and postal addresses — no other product in this domain stores contact or address data. Supports multiple records per entity with contact type (email, mobile phone, home phone) and address type (home, billing, delivery, pickup, mailing). Captures full USPS-validated address normalization including address lines, city, state, zip code, county, country, latitude/longitude coordinates, and validation status (USPS-verified, unverified, invalid). Tracks is_primary flag, is_verified flag, verification date, opt-in status per communication channel (SMS, email, push notification), unsubscribe timestamp, is_default flag, and effective date range. Supports omnichannel communication preferences, CAN-SPAM/TCPA compliance, last-mile delivery routing, store assignment, and geographic segmentation.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a customer segment record in the Grocery CRM and marketing analytics platform. Primary key for the customer_segment reference entity.']
  ad_circular_responsive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shoppers in this segment are known to respond to weekly promotional ad circulars (print or digital). Used to prioritize segment inclusion in weekly ad circular targeting and Temporary Price Reduction (TPR) campaigns.']
  applicable_channel varchar [note: 'The shopping channel(s) to which this segment applies for targeting and personalization. \'digital\' applies to Salesforce Commerce Cloud eCommerce and app experiences; \'in_store\' applies to NCR POS loyalty and in-store promotions; \'all\' applies across omnichannel touchpoints.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or username of the individual who formally approved this segment for activation. Supports governance audit trails and accountability for marketing targeting decisions.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this segment definition was formally approved by the owning team or governance committee for use in live marketing and personalization activities. Null if the segment has not yet been approved.']
  avg_basket_size_band varchar [note: 'Categorical band representing the typical transaction basket size (number of items or spend level) for shoppers in this segment. Used for assortment planning, endcap and gondola placement decisions, and targeted upsell promotions.']
  campaign_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when a customer qualifies for multiple segments simultaneously. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by the CRM and Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization engine to determine which segment\'s offer or experience takes precedence.']
  cltv_tier varchar [note: 'The Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) tier associated with this segment, used to prioritize marketing investment and personalization depth. Platinum represents the highest-value shoppers; unclassified indicates segments not defined by CLTV criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this segment record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and governance tracking.']
  digital_engagement_score_band varchar [note: 'Categorical band representing the digital engagement level of shoppers in this segment, derived from app usage, click-and-collect frequency, and eCommerce activity on Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Used to tailor digital vs. in-store communication strategies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this segment expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiration. Used to manage seasonal or campaign-specific segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this segment became or is scheduled to become active for use in campaigns, personalization, and targeting. Supports time-bounded segment activation aligned with promotional calendars.']
  external_segment_code varchar [note: 'The identifier for this segment as known in the originating external system (e.g., Salesforce CRM segment ID, NCR loyalty group code). Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration mapping during ETL and API synchronization.']
  fuel_rewards_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of this segment are eligible for Fuel Rewards program benefits, including per-gallon discounts at Grocery fuel centers. Used to target fuel-related promotions and cross-sell fuel center offers.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic scope at which this segment is defined and applied. National segments apply across all Grocery markets; regional segments apply to specific trade areas; local and store_cluster segments apply to specific store groups for hyper-local targeting.']
  is_pharmacy_segment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment targets pharmacy patients or customers with pharmacy-related behaviors (e.g., prescription refill frequency, OTC purchase patterns). Pharmacy segments must be handled in compliance with HIPAA privacy requirements for non-PHI customer data.']
  is_regulatory_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is associated with a government-regulated assistance program such as SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women, Infants, and Children), or EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer). Regulatory program segments require special handling for compliance and reporting.']
  is_suppression_segment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is used as a suppression list to exclude customers from specific campaigns or communications (e.g., opt-out customers, recently converted customers, do-not-contact lists). Suppression segments are applied as exclusion filters in campaign execution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this segment record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, cache invalidation in personalization engines, and audit compliance.']
  loyalty_program_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is linked to the Grocery loyalty program for targeted rewards, personalized offers, and NCR POS loyalty integration. Loyalty-linked segments receive differentiated promotional treatment at checkout.']
  max_member_count varchar [note: 'The configured upper limit on the number of customers that can be assigned to this segment at any given time. Used for capacity-constrained segments such as exclusive loyalty tiers or pilot program cohorts. Null indicates no cap.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, caveats, or operational notes about this segment provided by the owning team. May include information about known data quality issues, special handling instructions, or historical context not captured in structured fields.']
  nps_band varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) classification band associated with this segment, indicating the general advocacy level of shoppers in the segment. Promoters are highly satisfied advocates; detractors are at-risk shoppers. Used to prioritize retention and win-back campaigns.']
  organic_affinity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shoppers in this segment show a preference for USDA-certified organic products. Used to drive organic assortment decisions, planogram placement, and targeted promotions for organic categories.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business owner or data steward responsible for this segment. Used for governance notifications, approval workflows, and segment lifecycle management communications.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Name of the internal business team responsible for defining, maintaining, and governing this segment (e.g., \'CRM Analytics\', \'Marketing Strategy\', \'Loyalty Programs\', \'Pharmacy Marketing\'). Used for accountability and data stewardship.']
  preferred_fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'The order fulfillment method most commonly used by shoppers in this segment. Pickup = curbside or click-and-collect; delivery = last-mile delivery (LMD); in_store = traditional in-store shopping. Used to optimize omnichannel campaign targeting and OMS routing.']
  primary_store_format_affinity varchar [note: 'The Grocery store format that shoppers in this segment most frequently visit or are primarily associated with. Used to tailor assortment recommendations, planogram decisions, and format-specific promotions.']
  private_label_affinity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shoppers in this segment demonstrate a preference for Grocery\'s private-label (store-owned brand) products. Used to target private-label promotions, ad circular placements, and category management decisions.']
  purchase_frequency_band varchar [note: 'Categorical band representing the typical purchase frequency of shoppers in this segment. Used for lifecycle marketing, replenishment-driven promotions, and churn risk identification. Lapsed indicates shoppers who have not purchased within a defined recency window.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'How frequently the membership of this segment is recalculated and refreshed based on updated customer behavior and transaction data. Real-time segments are updated via streaming; manual segments require explicit re-run by the owning team.']
  regulatory_program_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific government assistance program associated with this segment when is_regulatory_program is true. SNAP = Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program; WIC = Women, Infants, and Children; EBT = Electronic Benefits Transfer. NONE if not a regulatory program segment.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short, externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment across systems (e.g., HCL-001, SNAP-EBT, ORG-ENT). Used as the business key in CRM integrations, Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization rules, and NCR POS loyalty targeting.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the segment\'s defining criteria, business purpose, and intended use cases. Authored by the owning marketing or CRM analytics team for documentation and governance purposes.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the market segment as displayed in CRM dashboards, marketing campaign tools, and personalization engines (e.g., \'High-CLTV Loyalists\', \'SNAP/EBT Shoppers\', \'Organic Enthusiasts\', \'Fuel Rewards Members\').']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment. Active segments are used in live campaigns and personalization. Draft segments are being defined. Under_review segments are pending approval. Archived segments are retained for historical analysis but no longer applied.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segmentation methodology used to define this segment. Behavioral segments are based on purchase patterns; demographic on age/income/household; value-based on Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) tiers; lifecycle on shopper journey stage; dietary_preference on food choices (organic, gluten-free, etc.).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this segment definition originated or is primarily managed. Supports data lineage tracking and system-of-record governance across Grocery\'s technology landscape.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for this segment definition. Incremented each time the segment criteria, name, or configuration is materially changed. Supports segment versioning for A/B testing, campaign attribution, and audit history.']

  Note: 'Defines named market segments used to classify shoppers for targeted marketing, personalization, and assortment decisions. Captures segment name, segment code, segment type (behavioral, demographic, value-based, lifecycle, dietary preference), description, creation date, owner team, active flag, and applicable channel (digital, in-store, all). Examples include: High-CLTV Loyalists, SNAP/EBT Shoppers, Organic Enthusiasts, Pharmacy Patients, Fuel Rewards Members. Reference entity managed by the CRM and marketing analytics teams.'
}

Table customer.segment_assignment {
  segment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shopper-to-segment assignment record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this transactional association entity.']
  ab_test_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the A/B or multivariate test experiment definition associated with this segment assignment. Populated only when ab_test_group is not null. Enables linking assignment outcomes to experiment results for segmentation strategy evaluation.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal workforce user (CRM analyst, marketing manager, or data scientist) who manually created or overrode this segment assignment. Populated only when assignment_method is \'manual_override\'. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the household grouping to which the shopper belongs at the time of segment assignment. Enables household-level segmentation analysis and supports promotions targeting at the household unit rather than the individual shopper level.']
  primary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer segment definition to which the shopper is being assigned. Links to the segment master in the Customer domain.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program under which this segment assignment was created. Segments may be scoped to specific loyalty programs (e.g., standard rewards, premium tier, fuel rewards). Null if the assignment is not tied to a specific loyalty program.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grocery shopper (customer) who is being assigned to the segment. Links to the customer master profile in the Customer domain.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary store associated with this segment assignment. Captures the shopper\'s home store or the store context in which the assignment was triggered. Supports store-level segmentation reporting and localized promotion targeting.']
  ab_test_group varchar [note: 'Designates the experimental group to which this shopper-segment assignment belongs when the assignment is part of an A/B or multivariate segmentation test. \'control\' is the baseline group; \'treatment_a\' and \'treatment_b\' are variant groups; \'holdout\' is excluded from all treatments for measurement purposes. Null if the assignment is not part of an active test.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the shopper was formally assigned to the segment. Used for time-series segment membership tracking and cohort analysis.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the shopper was assigned to this segment. \'rule_based\' indicates a deterministic business rule engine; \'ml_model\' indicates a machine learning model prediction; \'manual_override\' indicates a CRM analyst or marketer directly assigned the shopper; \'batch_import\' indicates a bulk load from an external system; \'api_push\' indicates a real-time API-driven assignment from an integrated platform such as Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  assignment_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this segment assignment. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across integrated platforms. Values correspond to known operational systems: Salesforce Commerce Cloud CRM, NCR POS loyalty integration, Blue Yonder demand planning, internal ML platform, manual CRM tooling, or batch ETL processes.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this shopper-to-segment assignment. \'active\' means the shopper currently belongs to the segment; \'expired\' means the assignment passed its expiry date; \'revoked\' means it was manually removed; \'pending\' means awaiting activation; \'superseded\' means replaced by a newer assignment for the same segment.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the assignment event was recorded in the system. Supports real-time segmentation pipelines and audit trails. Distinct from assignment_date which captures the business date.']
  batch_run_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the batch processing run (e.g., Blue Yonder or internal ML platform job execution) that generated this segment assignment. Enables traceability of bulk assignment events back to a specific pipeline execution for debugging, reprocessing, and data lineage auditing. Null for real-time API-driven or manual assignments.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the omnichannel shopping context in which this segment assignment is applicable. \'all_channels\' applies across all touchpoints; \'in_store\' applies only to physical store interactions; \'digital\' applies to eCommerce and app; \'pickup\' applies to Click-and-Collect (curbside or in-store order collection); \'delivery\' applies to last-mile delivery orders; \'pharmacy\' applies to pharmacy-specific interactions. Supports Grocery\'s omnichannel personalization strategy.']
  cltv_tier varchar [note: 'The Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) tier associated with this segment assignment at the time of assignment. Captures the shopper\'s estimated long-term revenue value tier as determined by the segmentation model or rule engine. Used to prioritize high-value shopper targeting in promotions and personalization. Values: platinum (highest), gold, silver, bronze, unclassified.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'A numeric probability score between 0.0000 and 1.0000 representing the model\'s or rule engine\'s confidence that the shopper belongs to this segment. Populated for ml_model and rule_based assignment methods. Used to filter high-confidence assignments for precision targeting and to support A/B testing of segmentation strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this segment assignment record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the audit trail creation marker. Distinct from assignment_timestamp which captures the business event time.']
  data_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has provided consent for their data to be used in segmentation and targeted marketing activities at the time of this assignment. True means consent is on record. Supports CCPA and GDPR-aligned data privacy compliance for customer data usage in the Customer domain.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which the shopper\'s membership in this segment is considered active and eligible for targeted promotions, personalization, and loyalty offers. May differ from assignment_date if the assignment is scheduled for a future activation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the shopper\'s membership in this segment expires and is no longer valid for targeting. Null indicates an open-ended assignment with no scheduled expiry. Supports time-bounded segmentation strategies such as seasonal or promotional cohorts.']
  geographic_market_code varchar [note: 'The geographic market or trade area code associated with this segment assignment. Used for regional segmentation strategies and market-level reporting. Follows internal market hierarchy codes aligned with store trade area definitions.']
  is_primary_segment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment assignment represents the shopper\'s primary or dominant segment when a shopper belongs to multiple segments simultaneously. True means this is the primary segment used for default personalization and offer targeting. Supports single-segment fallback logic in downstream marketing and POS systems.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the ML model or rule engine that produced this segment assignment. Enables reproducibility, model performance tracking, and rollback analysis. Format follows semantic versioning (e.g., \'v2.3.1\') or a named model run identifier. Null for manual_override and batch_import methods.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context or commentary about this segment assignment. Used by CRM analysts and data scientists to document special circumstances, exceptions, or observations not captured by structured fields. Not used for automated processing.']
  nps_score_at_assignment varchar [note: 'The shopper\'s most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) at the time of segment assignment, on a scale of 0 to 10. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to contextualize the segmentation decision. Supports analysis of whether segment membership correlates with shopper satisfaction and advocacy.']
  opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has opted out of marketing communications associated with this segment. True means the shopper should not receive outbound marketing for this segment even if the assignment is active. Distinct from data_consent_flag which governs data processing consent.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the CRM analyst or marketing manager when assignment_method is \'manual_override\'. Documents the business justification for bypassing the automated segmentation engine. Required for audit compliance when overriding ML or rule-based assignments.']
  personalization_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper\'s segment assignment qualifies them for personalized digital experiences including eCommerce storefront personalization, app recommendations, and targeted email campaigns via Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Distinct from promotion_eligibility_flag which governs price-based offers.']
  promotion_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper is eligible to receive targeted promotions, TPR (Temporary Price Reduction) offers, or ad circular inclusions based on this segment assignment. True means the shopper qualifies for segment-specific promotional targeting. Supports the Promotions Management domain and FTC advertising compliance.']
  rule_set_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific business rule set definition that triggered this assignment when assignment_method is \'rule_based\'. Enables traceability of which rule configuration produced the membership. Null for ml_model, manual_override, and batch_import methods.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'The high-level category of the segment to which the shopper is assigned. \'behavioral\' segments are based on shopping behavior patterns; \'demographic\' on age, income, or household composition; \'psychographic\' on lifestyle and values; \'transactional\' on purchase history and spend patterns; \'lifecycle\' on shopper journey stage (new, active, lapsed, churned); \'program_based\' on enrollment in specific programs such as SNAP, WIC, or pharmacy.']
  segment_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank (1 = highest priority) indicating the relative priority of this segment assignment among all active segments for the shopper. Used to resolve conflicts when multiple segments offer competing promotions or personalization rules. Lower values indicate higher priority.']
  snap_wic_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment assignment is specifically associated with a SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) or WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program participant segment. True flags the shopper as a government assistance program participant for targeted compliance reporting and eligible product promotions. Supports USDA and FTC regulatory requirements.']
  transition_trigger varchar [note: 'The business event or condition that triggered the shopper\'s transition into this segment. \'purchase_event\' indicates a qualifying transaction; \'loyalty_milestone\' indicates reaching a loyalty tier threshold; \'model_refresh\' indicates a scheduled ML model re-scoring run; \'manual_review\' indicates a CRM analyst-initiated change; \'program_enrollment\' indicates enrollment in SNAP, WIC, or pharmacy program; \'inactivity\' indicates a lapse-based re-segmentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this segment assignment record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Tracks updates such as status changes, expiry date extensions, or confidence score refreshes. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional association linking a shopper to one or more customer segments at a point in time. Captures shopper reference, segment reference, assignment date, expiry date, assignment method (rule-based, ML model, manual override), confidence score, and the model or rule version that produced the assignment. Enables time-series segment membership tracking and supports A/B testing of segmentation strategies.'
}

Table customer.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shopper preference record in the Grocery customer preference data product. Serves as the primary key for all preference-related joins and lineage tracking.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: PROMOTION: Department‑level preference allows personalized promotions and shelf‑stock decisions per department.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Personalized marketing uses shopper product preferences; linking preferences to product items enables recommendation engines and targeted promotions.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program enrollment record associated with this preference, when the preference was inferred from or is linked to loyalty program behavior. Enables correlation between loyalty tier, points activity, and preference evolution for CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) analytics.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper or household account that owns this preference record. Links to the customer master in the Customer domain.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper\'s preferred physical store location for in-store shopping, pickup, or click-and-collect fulfillment. Used by the personalization engine to surface store-specific promotions, ad circulars, and inventory availability. Links to the Store domain.']
  certification_preference varchar [note: 'Specifies a product certification standard that the shopper prefers when purchasing. Used to filter product catalog results in digital commerce and to drive assortment recommendations. Aligns with USDA organic certification and GS1 product attribute standards. Null for non-certification preference categories.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'A numeric score between 0.0000 and 1.0000 representing the system\'s confidence in the accuracy of an inferred preference. A score of 1.0000 indicates a self-declared preference with full certainty. Lower scores indicate inferred preferences with varying degrees of certainty. Used by the personalization engine to weight recommendations.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has provided explicit consent for Grocery to collect, store, and use their preference data for personalization and targeted marketing purposes. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance. When FALSE, preference data must not be used for marketing purposes.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shopper provided or last renewed consent for preference data collection and use. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the privacy policy or consent notice under which the shopper\'s preference consent was collected (e.g., \'v2.1\'). Enables compliance teams to identify shoppers who consented under older policy versions and trigger re-consent workflows when policies are updated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this preference record was first created in the system. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and GDPR data subject access request (DSAR) responses.']
  dietary_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of a dietary restriction preference. \'allergy\' indicates a medically significant food allergy. \'intolerance\' indicates a food sensitivity. \'medical\' indicates a clinically prescribed dietary requirement. \'religious\' indicates a faith-based dietary rule (e.g., kosher, halal). \'ethical\' indicates a values-based choice (e.g., vegan). \'lifestyle\' indicates a personal wellness choice (e.g., keto). Null for non-dietary preference categories.']
  ebt_eligible_filter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has enabled the EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) eligible product filter in their digital commerce experience. When TRUE, product search and browse results are filtered to show only EBT-eligible items. Supports SNAP/WIC program compliance in the digital channel.']
  email_frequency_preference varchar [note: 'The shopper\'s declared preference for how often they wish to receive promotional email communications, including ad circulars and personalized offers. Governs email suppression logic in the CRM platform. Null for non-communication preference categories.']
  inference_model_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the machine learning model used to infer this preference from purchase history or behavioral data (e.g., \'v1.3.2\'). Null for self-declared preferences. Enables model governance, A/B testing of inference models, and rollback of preferences generated by deprecated model versions.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference record is currently active and should be applied in personalization, product filtering, and marketing targeting. Set to FALSE when a shopper removes or overrides a preference.']
  is_allergy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference represents a food allergy or intolerance (e.g., nut allergy, lactose intolerance, celiac/gluten-free).
