When TRUE, this preference is treated with elevated priority in product filtering and warning displays to protect shopper safety. Aligns with FDA food allergen labeling requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this preference record was most recently modified, either by the shopper or by the inference engine. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to identify stale inferred preferences that may need re-evaluation.']
  lifestyle_diet_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the shopper\'s declared or inferred lifestyle diet pattern. Used to curate personalized product recommendations, recipe suggestions, and category affinities in the digital commerce experience. Null for non-lifestyle preference categories.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context or clarification about this preference record, entered by a customer service associate or captured from a shopper survey. Examples include specific allergen details beyond the standard type codes, or nuanced dietary requirements not covered by enumerated fields.']
  nutritional_goal_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the primary nutritional goal the shopper is pursuing. Used to filter product catalog results and surface nutritionally aligned products in digital commerce. Aligns with FDA nutrition labeling standards. Null for non-nutritional preference categories.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason explaining why a preference was overridden. Populated when preference_status is \'overridden\', typically when a self-declared preference supersedes an inferred one, or when a customer service associate corrects a preference on behalf of the shopper. Supports audit and data quality review.']
  personalization_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference record is currently eligible to be consumed by the Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization engine for product recommendations, digital commerce filtering, and targeted marketing. May be FALSE if consent is withdrawn, the preference is expired, or the confidence score is below the minimum threshold.']
  preference_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping that classifies the type of preference being recorded. Drives routing to the correct personalization engine module in Salesforce Commerce Cloud. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dietary|lifestyle|nutritional_goal|certification|shopping|communication|product_affinity — promote to reference product if additional categories are added]']
  preference_source varchar [note: 'Indicates how this preference was captured. \'self_declared\' means the shopper explicitly set it via the app or web profile. \'purchase_inferred\' means it was derived from POS transaction history. \'loyalty_inferred\' means it was derived from loyalty program behavior. \'app_behavior\' means it was inferred from digital browsing or filtering actions in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the preference record. \'active\' means the preference is in use by the personalization engine. \'inactive\' means the shopper has disabled it. \'pending_review\' means the inferred preference awaits confidence threshold validation. \'overridden\' means a self-declared preference has superseded an inferred one. \'expired\' means the preference has passed its validity window.']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Specific preference dimension within the category (e.g., \'nut_allergy\', \'gluten_free\', \'vegan\', \'keto\', \'low_sodium\', \'organic\', \'preferred_store\', \'email_frequency\', \'produce_affinity\'). Granular discriminator used by the personalization engine for product filtering and targeted marketing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nut_allergy|lactose_intolerance|gluten_free|vegan|vegetarian|keto|paleo|mediterranean|low_sodium|low_sugar|high_protein|organic|non_gmo|usda_certified|preferred_store|preferred_fulfillment|preferred_day|preferred_time|email_frequency|push_notification|category_affinity — promote to reference product]']
  preference_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The declared or inferred value for this preference dimension. For boolean preferences (e.g., nut allergy) this is \'true\' or \'false\'; for enumerated preferences (e.g., preferred fulfillment) this is the selected option (e.g., \'pickup\', \'delivery\'); for affinity scores this is a numeric string. Flexible string to accommodate all preference types.']
  preferred_fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'The shopper\'s preferred method for receiving their grocery orders. \'pickup\' refers to curbside or in-store order collection. \'click_and_collect\' refers to online order with in-store pickup. Feeds the Salesforce Commerce Cloud Order Management System (OMS) default fulfillment selection and Last Mile Delivery (LMD) routing. Null for non-shopping preference categories.']
  preferred_shopping_day varchar [note: 'The day of the week the shopper most prefers to shop, either declared via app profile or inferred from POS transaction history. Used to time promotional communications (ad circulars, push notifications) and to optimize pickup/delivery slot recommendations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preferred_shopping_time timestamp [note: 'The time-of-day window the shopper most prefers to shop, either declared or inferred from purchase history. Used to schedule push notifications and delivery/pickup slot suggestions. Complements preferred_shopping_day for precise timing of personalized outreach.']
  private_label_preference boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has a declared or inferred preference for Grocery\'s private-label (store-owned brand) products over national CPG (Consumer Packaged Goods) brands. Used to surface private-label alternatives in product recommendations and to support private-label brand development analytics.']
  product_category_affinity varchar [note: 'The product category or department (e.g., \'fresh_produce\', \'bakery\', \'dairy\', \'organic\', \'private_label\', \'pharmacy_otc\') toward which the shopper has a declared or inferred affinity. Drives category-level personalization and targeted promotions. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System item hierarchy. Null for non-affinity preference categories.']
  push_notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has opted in to receive push notifications via the Grocery mobile app. When TRUE, the shopper is eligible to receive personalized push alerts for promotions, order status updates, and loyalty rewards. Governed by mobile consent regulations.']
  push_notification_topics varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of push notification topic categories the shopper has opted into (e.g., \'promotions,order_updates,loyalty_rewards,weekly_ad\'). Allows granular control over notification content beyond the binary opt-in flag. Managed via the Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization module.']
  snap_wic_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper participates in SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) or WIC (Women Infants and Children) federal benefit programs. When TRUE, the personalization engine surfaces SNAP/WIC-eligible products and EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) payment options. Treated as confidential due to program participation sensitivity.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The digital or physical channel through which this preference was captured or inferred. Distinguishes between mobile app self-declaration, web profile updates, in-store kiosk entry, POS transaction inference, and loyalty portal updates. Used for channel attribution in preference data quality reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_app|web|in_store_kiosk|associate_entry|pos|loyalty_portal|survey — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valid_until date [note: 'The date after which this preference record is no longer valid and should be excluded from personalization logic. Null indicates an open-ended preference with no expiry. Supports temporary dietary restrictions, seasonal preferences, and time-limited program participation.']
  valid_from date [note: 'The date from which this preference record is considered valid and should be applied in personalization and product filtering. Supports time-bounded preferences such as seasonal dietary goals or temporary dietary restrictions.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive single source of truth for all shopper preferences and dietary profiles — both declared and inferred — across all dimensions. No other product in this domain stores dietary, lifestyle, or shopping preference data. Covers dietary restrictions (nut allergy, lactose intolerance, celiac/gluten-free), lifestyle diet types (vegan, vegetarian, keto, paleo, Mediterranean), nutritional goals (low sodium, low sugar, high protein), certification preferences (organic, non-GMO, USDA certified), shopping preferences (preferred store, preferred pickup vs. delivery, preferred shopping day/time), communication preferences (email frequency, push notification topics), and product category affinities. Captures preference source (self-declared via app, inferred from purchase history), confidence level, and last updated timestamp. Feeds the personalization engine in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and supports digital commerce product filtering for health-conscious shopping features.'
}

Table customer.customer_benefit_enrollment {
  customer_benefit_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each benefit program enrollment record. Primary key for the benefit_enrollment data product in the customer domain.']
  crm_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Salesforce CRM case record associated with this enrollment, if the enrollment was initiated or managed through a customer service interaction. Enables end-to-end traceability from CRM engagement to benefit enrollment outcome.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper enrolled in the benefit program. Links this enrollment to the shopper master record in the customer domain.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grocery store location where the shopper primarily redeems their benefit program. Used for store-level benefit redemption analytics, USDA FNS authorized retailer reporting, and targeted in-store program support.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Date by which the shopper must file a formal appeal with the issuing agency if the enrollment was denied or suspended. Populated when enrollment_status is denied or suspended. Supports shopper advocacy and regulatory fair hearing compliance.']
  authorized_retailer_number varchar [note: 'USDA FNS-assigned retailer authorization number for the grocery store location where the benefit is redeemable. Required for SNAP and WIC regulatory compliance reporting and EBT transaction settlement with the state processor.']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has consented to automatic recertification or renewal processing for this benefit enrollment where permitted by the issuing agency. Supports streamlined recertification workflows and reduces benefit lapse rates.']
  balance_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent synchronization of the benefit_balance field with the state EBT processor or WIC management information system. Indicates data freshness for POS eligibility decisions.']
  benefit_balance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current remaining benefit balance available on the shopper\'s EBT or WIC benefit account as of the last synchronization with the state EBT processor. Used for POS tender limit enforcement and shopper balance inquiry support.']
  benefit_card_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the EBT or WIC benefit card number, retained for customer service identification and POS receipt display purposes. Not sufficient to reconstruct the full card number.']
  benefit_card_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the shopper\'s EBT or WIC benefit card number. The actual card number is never stored; only the secure token is retained for POS tender matching and transaction reconciliation. Tokenization complies with PCI DSS and state EBT security requirements.']
  benefit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the benefit amount (typically USD for US government programs). Supports multi-currency readiness for future program expansions.']
  categorical_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper qualifies for the benefit program through categorical eligibility (i.e., automatic eligibility based on participation in another qualifying program such as TANF or SSI) rather than through standard income and resource testing. Simplifies eligibility determination and reduces documentation burden.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit enrollment record was first created in the data platform. Represents the audit trail creation event for the Silver Layer record, aligned with ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized agency-issued code indicating the reason an enrollment application was denied or an active enrollment was suspended. Populated only when enrollment_status is denied or suspended. Used for regulatory reporting and shopper appeals support. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: income_exceeds_limit|residency_not_verified|identity_not_verified|incomplete_application|duplicate_enrollment|ineligible_category|other — promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the shopper completed the benefit program enrollment process. Supports omnichannel analytics and process optimization for enrollment workflows.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shopper was officially enrolled and approved into the benefit program by the issuing agency. Represents the effective start of benefit eligibility.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known enrollment reference number assigned by the issuing government agency or program administrator. Used for cross-referencing with agency records and POS tender eligibility lookups.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the benefit program enrollment. Active indicates the shopper is currently eligible; pending indicates application submitted but not yet approved; expired indicates the benefit period has lapsed; denied indicates the application was rejected by the issuing agency; suspended indicates temporary hold by the agency.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the current benefit enrollment period expires and the shopper must re-certify or re-enroll to maintain eligibility. Null for open-ended enrollments. Critical for POS tender eligibility validation.']
  income_eligibility_level varchar [note: 'Federal Poverty Level (FPL) income bracket certified by the issuing agency at the time of enrollment. Determines benefit amount tiers and program eligibility thresholds. Expressed as a percentage range of the Federal Poverty Level per USDA FNS guidelines.']
  issuing_agency_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the government agency that issued the benefit enrollment. Aligns with USDA FNS state agency codes and enables automated agency-level regulatory reporting.']
  issuing_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or authorized body that issued and administers the benefit enrollment (e.g., state Department of Social Services, county health department, USDA FNS regional office). Used for regulatory reporting and agency reconciliation.']
  issuing_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-2 US state code of the government agency that issued the benefit enrollment. Supports state-level regulatory compliance reporting and cross-state benefit portability tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this benefit enrollment record in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance.']
  monthly_benefit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar value of government assistance benefits allocated to the shopper per calendar month. Used for POS tender limit enforcement, financial reconciliation with USDA FNS, and regulatory reporting on benefit utilization.']
  notification_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has consented to receive benefit-related notifications (e.g., expiry reminders, recertification alerts, balance updates) via their preferred communication channel. Supports CRM-driven benefit engagement campaigns.']
  pos_tender_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment record currently qualifies the shopper to use the associated benefit card as a valid tender type at the NCR POS system. Derived from enrollment_status and expiry_date but stored as an operational flag for real-time POS eligibility checks.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the government assistance or special benefit program in which the shopper is enrolled. SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women, Infants, and Children), EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer), TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families), MEDICAID. Drives POS tender eligibility rules at checkout.']
  recertification_date date [note: 'Date by which the shopper must complete recertification with the issuing agency to maintain uninterrupted benefit eligibility. Used to trigger proactive outreach and prevent benefit lapses.']
  snap_household_size varchar [note: 'Number of individuals in the shopper\'s household as certified by the issuing agency for SNAP benefit calculation purposes. Directly determines the monthly SNAP allotment amount. Applicable only when program_type is SNAP.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this benefit enrollment record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across NCR POS, Salesforce CRM, agency portal feeds, and EBT processor interfaces.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the shopper\'s eligibility documents were verified and approved by the issuing agency or authorized grocery program administrator. Establishes the audit trail for enrollment approval.']
  verification_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier pointing to the document management system record containing the eligibility verification documents submitted by the shopper (e.g., income verification, residency proof, identity documents). Supports audit trails for regulatory compliance without storing document content in the data lakehouse.']
  verification_doc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary eligibility verification document type submitted by the shopper during enrollment. Used for compliance audit reporting and document completeness checks.']
  wic_food_package_code varchar [note: 'USDA-assigned food package code designating the specific WIC food package category the shopper qualifies for (e.g., Package A for infants, Package D for women). Determines which product SKUs are WIC-eligible at POS. Applicable only when program_type is WIC.']
  wic_participant_category varchar [note: 'USDA WIC participant category classifying the shopper\'s eligibility basis within the WIC program. Determines food package entitlements and benefit amounts. Applicable only when program_type is WIC.']

  Note: 'Tracks shopper enrollment in government assistance and special benefit programs including SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women Infants and Children), and EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer). Captures program type, enrollment status (active, pending, expired, denied), enrollment date, expiry date, issuing agency, benefit card number (tokenized), monthly benefit amount, and verification document reference. Critical for POS tender eligibility and regulatory compliance reporting.'
}

Table customer.pharmacy_patient {
  pharmacy_patient_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacy patient profile record in the Grocery lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running enrollment/registration relationship between a shopper and Grocery pharmacy services.']
  pharmacist_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Pharmacy counseling assigns a pharmacist associate to each patient; required for compliance reporting and medication counseling audit.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Grocery store location where the patient prefers to fill prescriptions. Used for routing Rx notifications, staffing, and inventory planning at the pharmacy counter.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper master record in the customer domain. Links the pharmacy patient profile to the unified shopper identity. Every pharmacy patient must have a corresponding shopper record.']
  allergy_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient most recently disclosed or updated their self-reported allergy information with pharmacy staff. Nullable when allergy_flag is False. Used to prompt periodic allergy review during counseling.']
  allergy_flag boolean [note: 'Non-clinical, self-reported indicator that the patient has disclosed one or more drug or food allergies to pharmacy staff. This is a flag only — clinical allergy details reside in McKesson Pharmacy Systems and are excluded from this non-PHI profile per HIPAA compliance requirements.']
  auto_refill_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient provided or last updated consent for the auto-refill program. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance. Nullable when auto_refill_opt_in is False.']
  auto_refill_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has opted into the automatic prescription refill program. When True, eligible maintenance medications are automatically queued for refill before the supply runs out. Drives CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) logic for pharmacy inventory.']
  counseling_preference varchar [note: 'Patient\'s preferred method for receiving pharmacist counseling when picking up a new or changed prescription. \'waived\' indicates the patient has formally declined counseling. Required by OBRA-90 regulations for Medicaid patients; captured for all patients as best practice.']
  counseling_waiver_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient most recently waived pharmacist counseling. Nullable when counseling_preference is not \'waived\'. Supports compliance audit trail for OBRA-90 and State Board of Pharmacy inspections.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pharmacy patient profile record was first created in the Grocery lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record retention scheduling.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for prescription home delivery. Captured separately from the shopper\'s primary address to support patients who require delivery to a different location (e.g., workplace, caregiver address). Used by LMD (Last Mile Delivery) routing.']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for prescription home delivery (apartment, suite, unit number). Nullable. Complements delivery_address_line1 for complete delivery address specification.']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City component of the prescription home delivery address. Used for LMD (Last Mile Delivery) zone assignment and carrier routing.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the prescription home delivery address. Used for delivery zone mapping, carrier selection, and geographic analytics.']
  delivery_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the prescription home delivery address. Used for carrier zone assignment and state-level pharmacy licensing compliance verification.']
  disenrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy patient profile was deactivated or the patient relationship ended. Nullable for currently active patients. Used for record retention scheduling and compliance reporting.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the shopper was first registered as a pharmacy patient at Grocery. Marks the effective start of the pharmacy patient relationship. Used for tenure analysis and loyalty program integration.']
  guardian_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the legally authorized representative or guardian for patients who cannot consent on their own behalf (e.g., pediatric patients, patients with power of attorney arrangements). Non-PHI reference field. Nullable for adult patients without a designated representative.']
  guardian_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the authorized representative or guardian to the pharmacy patient. Used to validate authorization for prescription pickup and counseling consent. Nullable when no guardian is on file.']
  hipaa_acknowledgment_channel varchar [note: 'Method by which the patient acknowledged the HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices. Supports audit trail for HIPAA compliance. \'verbal_noted\' is used when the patient verbally acknowledged but declined to sign, per HIPAA good-faith effort documentation requirements.']
  hipaa_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient acknowledged receipt of the HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices. Nullable when hipaa_notice_acknowledged is False. Required for HIPAA compliance audit trail.']
  hipaa_notice_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has acknowledged receipt of the pharmacy\'s HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP). Required by HIPAA Privacy Rule for all new pharmacy patients. Pharmacies must make a good-faith effort to obtain written acknowledgment.']
  insurance_bin_number varchar [note: 'Six-digit Bank Identification Number (BIN) used to route pharmacy claims to the correct Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) during adjudication. Non-PHI operational identifier. Standard NCPDP field used in electronic claims processing.']
  insurance_group_number varchar [note: 'Insurance group identifier associated with the patient\'s pharmacy benefit plan. Used during claims adjudication to identify the employer group or plan sponsor. Non-PHI operational field. Standard NCPDP field.']
  insurance_pcn varchar [note: 'Processor Control Number (PCN) used in conjunction with the BIN to route pharmacy claims to the correct adjudication processor within a PBM network. Non-PHI operational field. Standard NCPDP field.']
  insurance_plan_name varchar [note: 'Name of the patient\'s primary pharmacy benefit insurance plan (e.g., \'BlueCross BlueShield Rx\', \'Aetna Pharmacy\'). Non-PHI operational field used for routing adjudication and patient communication. Detailed insurance/PBM data resides in McKesson.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Patient\'s preferred language for pharmacy communications, prescription labels, and counseling materials. Stored as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'zh-TW\'). Used to route multilingual pharmacy staff and generate translated prescription materials per FDA labeling guidance.']
  last_rx_fill_date date [note: 'Date of the patient\'s most recent prescription fill at any Grocery pharmacy location. Non-PHI operational field used for patient activity monitoring, inactive patient identification, and MedSync scheduling. Sourced from McKesson Pharmacy Systems.']
  loyalty_rx_points_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient is eligible to earn loyalty program points for qualifying prescription fills. Integrates pharmacy activity with the Grocery loyalty program managed via NCR POS Systems and CRM. Certain prescription categories (e.g., controlled substances) may be ineligible per program rules.']
  mckesson_patient_code varchar [note: 'External patient identifier assigned by McKesson Pharmacy Systems (the operational system of record for prescription management). Used to cross-reference non-PHI profile data in the lakehouse with clinical records in McKesson. Required for operational reconciliation.']
  medsync_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient is enrolled in the Medication Synchronization (MedSync) program, which aligns all of the patient\'s maintenance prescriptions to a single monthly pickup date. Improves adherence and reduces pharmacy counter traffic variability.']
  medsync_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient was enrolled in the MedSync program. Used for program tenure tracking and cohort analysis. Nullable when medsync_enrolled is False.']
  medsync_sync_date date [note: 'The designated monthly calendar date on which all synchronized prescriptions for this patient are scheduled for pickup or delivery. Nullable when medsync_enrolled is False. Used for pharmacy workload planning and inventory pre-positioning.']
  n340b_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient is eligible for the 340B Drug Pricing Program, which provides discounted outpatient drugs to qualifying patients of covered entities. Non-PHI eligibility flag used for claims routing and pricing.']
  patient_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pharmacy patient profile. \'active\' indicates the patient is actively using Grocery pharmacy services. \'inactive\' indicates no recent activity. \'on_hold\' indicates a temporary suspension of services. \'transferred\' indicates the patient has moved to another pharmacy. \'deceased\' is set upon notification of patient death for record retention compliance.']
  patient_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pharmacy patient by service type. \'retail\' is the standard in-store patient. \'mail_order\' indicates the patient primarily uses mail-order fulfillment. \'specialty\' indicates enrollment in specialty pharmacy programs for high-cost or complex medications. \'long_term_care\' indicates a patient in a care facility. \'pediatric\' indicates a minor patient with a guardian on file.']
  primary_care_physician_name varchar [note: 'Self-reported name of the patient\'s primary care physician (PCP). Used for prescription verification and physician outreach by pharmacy staff. This is a non-PHI reference field — clinical prescriber details reside in McKesson. Stored as a free-text name, not a structured provider record.']
  primary_care_physician_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the patient\'s primary care physician office. Used by pharmacy staff to verify prescriptions or coordinate care. Non-PHI operational reference field. Stored in E.164 international format.']
  rx_notification_channel varchar [note: 'Patient\'s preferred communication channel for prescription-related notifications, including ready-for-pickup alerts, refill reminders, and drug interaction notices. Drives outbound messaging routing in McKesson and NCR POS Systems.']
  rx_ready_pickup_preference varchar [note: 'Patient\'s preferred method for collecting ready prescriptions. Supports omnichannel fulfillment including in-store counter, curbside pickup (Click-and-Collect), drive-through pharmacy window, and home delivery. Drives OMS (Order Management System) routing for pharmacy fulfillment.']
  snap_benefit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has been identified as eligible for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits, which may affect OTC product eligibility at the pharmacy counter. Non-PHI flag aligned with the shopper master SNAP eligibility field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pharmacy patient profile record in the Grocery lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail.']
  wic_benefit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has been identified as eligible for WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits, which may affect eligible OTC and formula purchases at the pharmacy counter. Non-PHI flag.']

  Note: 'Non-PHI patient profile for shoppers who use Grocery pharmacy services. Captures patient reference to the shopper master, preferred pharmacy store, primary care physician name, allergy flags (non-clinical, self-reported), preferred contact method for Rx notifications, auto-refill opt-in status, medication synchronization (MedSync) enrollment, and patient counseling preference. Intentionally excludes all HIPAA-protected health information (PHI) — clinical data resides in McKesson Pharmacy Systems. Links to the shopper master via shopper_id.'
}

Table customer.cltv_profile {
  cltv_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CLTV profile record. Primary key for the cltv_profile data product in the customer domain.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper record in the customer.shopper product. Links the CLTV profile to the individual grocery shopper identity.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper\'s most frequently visited store location over the trailing 12-month period. Used to associate CLTV metrics with store-level performance and comp sales (comparable store sales) reporting.']
  avg_basket_size decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average transaction value in USD per shopping trip for the shopper over the trailing 12-month period. Computed from POS and OMS transaction data. Reflects Average Selling Price (ASP) per visit.']
  category_breadth_score varchar [note: 'Count of distinct merchandise departments (e.g., produce, deli, pharmacy, bakery, fuel) shopped by the customer over the trailing 12-month period. Higher scores indicate deeper store engagement and lower churn risk.']
  cltv_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current computed Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) in USD representing the projected net revenue this shopper is expected to generate over their remaining relationship with Grocery. Produced by the analytics pipeline on a scheduled cadence.']
  cltv_tier varchar [note: 'Value tier classification assigned to the shopper based on their CLTV estimate. Tiers are: platinum (highest value), gold, silver, bronze (lowest value). Used by CRM targeting and loyalty tier management.']
  computation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the CLTV estimate and all associated metrics were last computed by the analytics pipeline. Determines the effective date of the profile snapshot.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the CLTV profile record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  crm_contact_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for the shopper record in the Salesforce CRM platform. Used to synchronize CLTV profile data back to CRM for targeted campaign execution and personalization.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the data quality status of the CLTV profile computation. \'clean\' means all input signals were complete; \'low_transaction_volume\' means fewer than the minimum required transactions were available; \'missing_channel_data\' means one or more channel feeds were absent; \'stale\' means the profile has not been refreshed within the expected SLA window; \'excluded\' means the record was excluded from scoring.']
  delivery_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of completed last-mile delivery (LMD) orders placed by the shopper over the trailing 12-month period. Indicates omnichannel delivery engagement and is a key input to digital CLTV segmentation.']
  digital_engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score (0.00 to 100.00) measuring the shopper\'s engagement with Grocery\'s digital channels including the eCommerce storefront, mobile app, and click-and-collect services. Derived from Salesforce Commerce Cloud interaction data. Higher scores indicate stronger omnichannel engagement.']
  fuel_spend_trailing_12m decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total shopper spend in USD at Grocery fuel center locations over the trailing 12-month period. Used to assess fuel center contribution to overall CLTV and cross-category engagement.']
  loyalty_points_balance varchar [note: 'Current unredeemed loyalty points balance for the shopper as of the computation date. Sourced from the NCR POS loyalty integration. Used as a retention signal in the CLTV model.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier of the CLTV predictive model used to compute the current cltv_estimate and associated metrics (e.g., \'2.4.1\'). Enables model lineage tracking, A/B comparison, and auditability of scoring changes.']
  next_scheduled_computation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the next scheduled CLTV pipeline run is expected to refresh this profile. Used for pipeline scheduling and SLA monitoring.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from the shopper, on a scale of 0 to 10. Segmented into Promoters (9-10), Passives (7-8), and Detractors (0-6). Incorporated into CLTV model as a loyalty signal.']
  nps_segment varchar [note: 'Categorical NPS segment derived from the shopper\'s NPS score: \'promoter\' (score 9-10), \'passive\' (score 7-8), \'detractor\' (score 0-6), \'unknown\' (no survey response). Used for CRM segmentation and loyalty program targeting.']
  pharmacy_spend_trailing_12m decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total shopper spend in USD at Grocery pharmacy locations over the trailing 12-month period. Non-PHI financial metric only; no prescription or health data included. Used to assess cross-category value contribution to CLTV.']
  pickup_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of completed curbside pickup or click-and-collect orders placed by the shopper over the trailing 12-month period. Indicates omnichannel pickup engagement and is a key input to digital CLTV segmentation.']
  predicted_churn_probability decimal(5,4) [note: 'Model-generated probability (0.0000 to 1.0000) that the shopper will churn (cease purchasing) within the next 90 days. Produced alongside the CLTV estimate by the analytics pipeline. Used for proactive retention targeting in CRM.']
  predicted_next_purchase_date date [note: 'Model-predicted calendar date of the shopper\'s next expected purchase transaction. Used for personalized re-engagement campaigns and replenishment reminder targeting.']
  prior_cltv_tier varchar [note: 'The CLTV tier assigned to the shopper in the immediately preceding computation cycle. Enables tier migration tracking, loyalty program upgrade/downgrade communications, and trend analysis.']
  private_label_spend_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of the shopper\'s trailing 12-month spend attributable to Grocery\'s private-label brand products, expressed as a decimal (0.0000 to 1.0000). Higher values indicate stronger private-label affinity and margin contribution.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CLTV profile record. \'active\' indicates the profile is current and in use for CRM targeting; \'inactive\' indicates the shopper is no longer active; \'pending\' indicates awaiting first computation; \'suppressed\' indicates excluded from targeting per opt-out or compliance rules.']
  promo_redemption_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of targeted promotional offers (TPR, BOGO, ad circular) redeemed by the shopper over the trailing 12-month period, expressed as a decimal (0.0000 to 1.0000). Indicates price sensitivity and promotional responsiveness for CRM targeting.']
  recency_score varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed since the shopper\'s most recent completed purchase transaction across any channel. Lower values indicate higher recency and engagement. Key RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) input to the CLTV model.']
  snap_ebt_spend_trailing_12m decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total spend in USD processed via SNAP/EBT Electronic Benefits Transfer payment method by the shopper over the trailing 12-month period. Used for government program participation analytics and CLTV segmentation. Non-PII financial aggregate.']
  targeting_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper is currently eligible for CRM-driven targeted marketing based on their CLTV profile. Set to false if the shopper has opted out of marketing communications, is suppressed, or is flagged for compliance exclusion.']
  tier_change_date date [note: 'The date on which the shopper\'s CLTV tier last changed. Null if the shopper has never changed tiers. Used to trigger loyalty communications and track tier stability.']
  trailing_12m_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total shopper spend in USD across all channels (in-store POS, digital commerce, pharmacy) over the trailing 12-month window ending on the profile computation date. Key input to CLTV model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the CLTV profile record was most recently updated in the silver layer lakehouse, typically aligned with each pipeline computation cycle. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  visit_frequency decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average number of shopping visits per calendar month for the shopper over the trailing 12-month period, across all channels including in-store, pickup, and delivery. Used to assess engagement and predict churn.']

  Note: 'Stores computed Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) metrics and value tier classifications for each shopper. Captures current CLTV estimate, CLTV tier (platinum, gold, silver, bronze), 12-month trailing spend, average basket size, visit frequency (visits per month), category breadth score (number of distinct departments shopped), recency score (days since last purchase), and CLTV model version. Updated on a scheduled cadence from the analytics pipeline. Serves as the authoritative CLTV record for CRM targeting and loyalty tier management.'
}

Table customer.identity_resolution {
  identity_resolution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each identity resolution or deduplication event record in the Grocery customer Master Data Management (MDM) system.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the household to which the surviving golden record belongs after resolution. Populated when the resolution event results in a merge that affects household membership. Supports household-level analytics and targeted marketing.']
  prior_resolution_identity_resolution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a previous identity_resolution_id that this event supersedes, reverses, or is related to (e.g., an UNMERGE event references the original MERGE event). Enables resolution event chaining for complete MDM audit trail and lineage tracking.']
  surviving_shopper_id varchar [note: 'The shopper identifier designated as the golden record after a merge resolution. Null for linked or flagged-for-review resolutions where no merge has occurred. Supports the single view of the customer across all Grocery touchpoints.']
  address_similarity_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric similarity score (0.0000–1.0000) measuring the degree of address match between Side A and Side B records. Used in probabilistic resolution methods alongside name_similarity_score to evaluate name+address composite signals.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this identity resolution event record was first written to the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the system audit creation timestamp, distinct from resolution_timestamp which captures the business event time.']
  crm_contact_id_a varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM contact identifier for the Side A record, as sourced from Salesforce Commerce Cloud or CRM platform. Preserved for cross-system reconciliation and to support CRM deduplication workflows.']
  crm_contact_id_b varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM contact identifier for the Side B record. Preserved for cross-system reconciliation and to support downstream CRM merge/deduplication operations in Salesforce.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Categorical data quality assessment assigned to this resolution event. CLEAN = no data quality concerns; SUSPECT_DUPLICATE = additional duplicates may exist; CONFLICTING_SIGNALS = identity signals contradict each other; INCOMPLETE_PROFILE = one or both records lack sufficient attributes for high-confidence matching; SYNTHETIC = test or synthetic record detected.']
  email_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the email addresses on Side A and Side B records matched (exact or normalized) during the resolution evaluation. Supports granular analysis of which identity signals contributed to the match decision.']
  gdpr_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 under which the identity resolution processing activity is conducted. Required for compliance with privacy regulations applicable to Grocery\'s customer data processing. NOT_APPLICABLE for non-EU shoppers.']
  loyalty_id_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loyalty program identifiers (Point of Sale (POS) loyalty IDs) on Side A and Side B records matched during the resolution evaluation. Loyalty ID is a high-confidence deterministic signal in grocery retail identity resolution.']
  match_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric probability score between 0.0000 and 1.0000 representing the algorithm\'s confidence that Side A and Side B records belong to the same individual. Scores above the configured threshold trigger automatic merge; scores in the review band are flagged for manual review. Critical for data quality KPI reporting.']
  match_rule_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the matching rule set or ML model that was applied during this resolution event (e.g., \'RULE_SET_v2.3\', \'ML_MODEL_v1.0.5\'). Enables retrospective analysis of resolution quality across rule engine versions and supports model governance.']
  match_signal varchar [note: 'The primary data attribute(s) that triggered the match between Side A and Side B records. Indicates which identity signal (e.g., email, phone, loyalty ID, name+address combination) drove the resolution decision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EMAIL|PHONE|LOYALTY_ID|NAME_ADDRESS|EMAIL_PHONE|NAME_DOB|DEVICE_ID|PAYMENT_TOKEN — promote to reference product]']
  match_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'The configured confidence score threshold that was in effect at the time of this resolution event. Records the threshold value used to classify the match as auto-merge, review, or reject, enabling retrospective audit of threshold policy changes.']
  name_similarity_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric similarity score (0.0000–1.0000) measuring the degree of name match between Side A and Side B records, computed using fuzzy string matching algorithms (e.g., Jaro-Winkler, Levenshtein). Used in probabilistic resolution methods.']
  phone_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the phone numbers on Side A and Side B records matched (exact or normalized) during the resolution evaluation. Supports granular signal-level analysis of match quality.']
  pos_loyalty_id_a varchar [note: 'The NCR Point of Sale (POS) loyalty program identifier associated with the Side A record. A high-confidence deterministic identity signal in grocery retail. Preserved for audit trail and cross-system identity traceability.']
  pos_loyalty_id_b varchar [note: 'The NCR Point of Sale (POS) loyalty program identifier associated with the Side B record. Paired with pos_loyalty_id_a to evaluate loyalty ID match signal during deterministic resolution.']
  resolution_batch_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch deduplication run or scheduled job that generated this resolution event. Groups all resolution events produced in the same processing cycle for operational monitoring, reprocessing, and data quality KPI reporting.']
  resolution_channel varchar [note: 'The processing channel through which the resolution event was initiated. BATCH = overnight bulk deduplication run; REAL_TIME = triggered at point of customer interaction; MANUAL_UI = data steward action via MDM console; API = triggered by external system call; SCHEDULED = periodic reconciliation job.']
  resolution_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which a REJECTED or FLAGGED_FOR_REVIEW resolution decision should be re-evaluated. Supports periodic re-review of borderline cases as new identity signals become available. Null for permanent decisions (MERGED, LINKED).']
  resolution_method varchar [note: 'The algorithmic or procedural approach used to determine that two records represent the same shopper. DETERMINISTIC = exact match on email, phone, or loyalty ID; PROBABILISTIC = fuzzy match on name and address; MANUAL_MERGE = data steward decision; RULE_BASED = configurable business rules; ML_MODEL = machine learning classifier.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the identity resolution event. MERGED = records combined into a single golden record; LINKED = records associated but kept separate; FLAGGED_FOR_REVIEW = queued for manual data steward review; REJECTED = determined to be distinct individuals; PENDING = awaiting processing or review.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the identity resolution event was executed. Represents the principal business event time for this record. Used for MDM audit trail, data quality KPI trending, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'The category of action taken during this resolution event. MERGE = two records collapsed into one golden record; LINK = records associated without merging; UNMERGE = previously merged records separated; UNLINK = previously linked records disassociated; RECLASSIFY = resolution status changed without structural change.']
  resolved_by_user varchar [note: 'The username, service account name, or system process identifier that executed the resolution action. For manual merges, this is the data steward\'s user ID. For automated resolutions, this is the system or batch process name (e.g., \'MDM_AUTO_MERGE_JOB\'). Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  review_decision varchar [note: 'The outcome decision made by the data steward during manual review of a flagged resolution event. APPROVED = steward confirmed the match and authorized the action; REJECTED = steward determined records are distinct; ESCALATED = referred to senior steward or governance committee; DEFERRED = decision postponed pending additional information.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the data steward during manual review, documenting the rationale for their decision. Supports audit trail completeness and institutional knowledge for recurring edge cases in identity resolution.']
  review_queue_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data steward review queue or work item to which this resolution event was assigned when flagged for manual review. Null for automated resolutions that did not require human intervention. Supports workflow management and SLA tracking for MDM operations.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the data steward completed their manual review and recorded a review_decision. Null for automated resolutions. Used to calculate review SLA compliance and MDM operational KPIs.']
  reviewer_user varchar [note: 'The username of the data steward or MDM analyst who reviewed and adjudicated this resolution event when it was flagged for manual review. Null for fully automated resolutions. Distinct from resolved_by_user which captures the executor.']
  shopper_id_a varchar [note: 'Internal shopper identifier for the first (source) record participating in the identity resolution event. Represents one of the two candidate duplicate records being evaluated or merged.']
  shopper_id_b varchar [note: 'Internal shopper identifier for the second (candidate duplicate) record participating in the identity resolution event. Paired with shopper_id_a to form the resolution pair.']
  source_system_a varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated the Side A shopper record. Identifies which platform (e.g., NCR Point of Sale (POS), Salesforce CRM, eCommerce, McKesson Pharmacy, mobile app, loyalty system) contributed the first identity.']
  source_system_b varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated the Side B shopper record. Identifies which platform contributed the second (candidate duplicate) identity for comparison against Side A.']
  source_system_id_a varchar [note: 'The native identifier of the Side A record as it exists in its originating source system (e.g., NCR POS loyalty ID, Salesforce CRM contact ID, eCommerce account ID). Preserved for audit trail and cross-system traceability.']
  source_system_id_b varchar [note: 'The native identifier of the Side B record as it exists in its originating source system. Preserved for audit trail and cross-system traceability alongside source_system_id_a.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this identity resolution event record was most recently modified in the Databricks Silver Layer (e.g., when a review decision was recorded or a status was updated). Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail completeness.']

  Note: 'Tracks identity resolution and deduplication events that link or merge duplicate shopper records across source systems (NCR POS, Salesforce CRM, eCommerce platform, pharmacy system, mobile app). Captures source system A and B identifiers, resolution method (deterministic exact match on email/phone/loyalty ID, probabilistic fuzzy match on name+address, manual merge by data steward), match confidence score, resolution timestamp, resolved-by user or system, resolution status (merged, linked, flagged-for-review, rejected), and surviving record ID. Supports the golden record maintenance process, MDM audit trail, and data quality KPI reporting. Critical for ensuring a single view of the customer across all Grocery touchpoints.'
}

Table customer.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each consent record. Primary key for the consent_record data product in the customer domain.']
  previous_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior consent record that this record supersedes or replaces. Enables consent history chain reconstruction for audit and regulatory investigation purposes. Null for the initial consent record.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'The web or app session identifier active at the time the shopper submitted their consent decision. Used for digital audit trails and cross-referencing with Salesforce Commerce Cloud session logs. Null for non-digital consent sources.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper whose consent decision this record captures. Links to the customer.shopper master record.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason explaining why this consent record was created or modified. Supports audit trails and compliance investigations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shopper_request|legal_text_update|regulatory_mandate|system_migration|fraud_investigation|deceased_notification|other — promote to reference product]']
  consent_date timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the shopper granted consent for the specified consent type. Serves as the authoritative timestamp for consent activation and regulatory audit trails.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this consent record is scheduled to expire and require renewal. Null for indefinite consents. Supports re-consent campaign triggers and ensures consent currency for regulatory compliance.']
  consent_language_code varchar [note: 'The IETF BCP 47 language code representing the language in which the consent notice was presented to the shopper (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'zh-TW\'). Supports multilingual compliance and ensures consent was presented in the shopper\'s preferred language.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or touchpoint through which the shopper provided or withdrew consent. Used for compliance auditing and channel attribution. Includes digital (app, web), physical (in-store kiosk, POS), and assisted (call center, paper form) channels.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this consent record. Indicates whether the shopper has actively granted, withdrawn, or is pending confirmation of consent for the specified consent type.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'The category of consent being captured. Defines the specific communication channel or data-use purpose for which the shopper has provided or withdrawn consent. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: marketing_email|sms|push_notification|direct_mail|data_sharing_cpg|personalization|analytics — promote to reference product]']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the legal consent text or privacy notice that the shopper agreed to at the time of consent. Enables tracking of which version of terms was in effect and supports re-consent workflows when legal text changes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for the Silver Layer record. Distinct from consent_date which captures the real-world business event time.']
  crm_contact_code varchar [note: 'The external identifier for this shopper in the Salesforce CRM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the consent_record data product and the CRM platform\'s consent and suppression management modules.']
  data_sharing_partner_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) partner or third-party organization with whom shopper data may be shared under this consent record. Applicable only when consent_type is \'data_sharing_cpg\'. Null for all other consent types.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the shopper when providing or withdrawing consent. Captured for regulatory audit context and digital channel analytics. Null for non-digital consent sources. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_ios|mobile_android|desktop|tablet|kiosk|pos_terminal|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  double_opt_in_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper completed a double opt-in confirmation step (e.g., clicked a confirmation link in a verification email or SMS) for this consent record. Relevant for email and SMS marketing consent under CAN-SPAM and TCPA best practices.']
  double_opt_in_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shopper completed the double opt-in confirmation step. Null if double_opt_in_confirmed is false or not applicable for the consent type.']
  guardian_relationship varchar [note: 'Describes the relationship of the consenting adult to the minor shopper when minor_guardian_consent is true. Null for adult shoppers. Required for COPPA compliance documentation.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the device from which the shopper submitted their consent decision. Captured for regulatory proof-of-consent and fraud detection. Null for non-digital consent sources. May be considered PII under CCPA.']
  is_suppressed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shopper is currently on the suppression list for the associated consent type. When true, the shopper must be excluded from all outbound communications of the corresponding type regardless of other consent flags.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction under which this consent record is governed. Determines which privacy regulations apply (e.g., CCPA for California residents, state-level privacy laws for other states). Drives jurisdiction-specific consent logic and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US-CA|US-VA|US-CO|US-CT|US-UT|US-FED|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loyalty_program_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper has consented to the use of their loyalty program transaction data (purchase history, points activity) for personalization and targeted marketing purposes. Distinct from general analytics consent.']
  minor_guardian_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether consent was provided by a parent or legal guardian on behalf of a minor shopper. Relevant for COPPA compliance when the shopper is under 13 years of age.']
  proof_of_consent_reference varchar [note: 'An external reference identifier (e.g., document ID, screenshot hash, call recording ID, or form submission ID) that serves as auditable proof of the consent event. Supports regulatory investigations and legal hold processes.']
  re_consent_requested_date date [note: 'The date on which a re-consent request was sent to the shopper. Null if no re-consent request has been issued. Used to track re-consent campaign outreach and measure response rates.']
  re_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record requires re-confirmation from the shopper, typically triggered by a legal text version change, consent expiry, or regulatory update. Drives re-consent campaign targeting in CRM.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory framework governing this consent record. Enables framework-specific compliance reporting and audit queries. A single consent record may be governed by one primary framework even if multiple apply.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this consent record. Supports data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation. Aligns with the operational systems of record in the Grocery enterprise architecture. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|ncr_pos|sfcc|mckesson|call_center|manual|loyalty_app|web_portal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  suppression_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the suppression became active for this shopper and consent type. Used to determine when outbound communications must cease. Null when is_suppressed is false.']
  suppression_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the suppression is scheduled to expire, if applicable. Null for permanent suppressions (e.g., deceased, fraud flag). Enables automated re-activation of communication eligibility after temporary suppression periods.']
  suppression_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the shopper was added to the suppression list. Supports compliance reporting and operational triage. Null when is_suppressed is false.']
  suppression_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that triggered or originated the suppression event. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and cross-system reconciliation for compliance investigations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|ncr_pos|mckesson|sfcc|call_center|manual|legal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  suppression_type varchar [note: 'The specific communication channel or data-use category for which the shopper is suppressed. Null when is_suppressed is false. Drives suppression list filtering in outbound marketing execution systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  withdrawal_date timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the shopper withdrew or revoked consent for the specified consent type. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Required for CCPA opt-out audit trails.']

  Note: 'Authoritative single source of truth for all shopper consent decisions, privacy preferences, communication suppressions, and opt-out state. No other product in this domain stores consent or suppression data. Captures consent type (marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing with CPG partners, personalization, analytics, direct mail), consent status (granted, withdrawn, pending), consent and withdrawal dates, consent source (app, web, in-store kiosk, call center), consent version (legal text version), and device/IP context. Owns the suppression list for shoppers excluded from specific communication types due to opt-out requests, legal holds, deceased status, or fraud flags — including suppression type (global opt-out, email, SMS, direct mail, third-party sharing), suppression reason codes, effective/expiry dates, and triggering source system. Supports CCPA, CAN-SPAM, and TCPA compliance for all outbound marketing and data sharing activities.'
}

Table customer.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each recorded shopper interaction or touchpoint event across all channels. Primary key for the interaction data product.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associate or system agent who handled this interaction (call center representative, chat bot system ID, service desk associate). Nullable for self-service or automated interactions.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service case or ticket associated with this interaction, if applicable. Enables linking multiple interactions to a single service resolution workflow.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order that triggered or is associated with this interaction (e.g., post-purchase NPS survey, delivery feedback, order complaint). Nullable for non-order-related interactions.']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Point of Sale (POS) transaction associated with this interaction, when the interaction is triggered by or linked to a specific in-store purchase (e.g., post-purchase NPS, receipt-based complaint). Sourced from NCR POS Systems.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Customer service interactions (complaints, inquiries) must reference the specific product item to resolve issues and log product‑specific metrics.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper who participated in this interaction. Links to the customer.shopper master record for identity resolution and longitudinal history.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical store location associated with this interaction (e.g., in-store service desk visit, store-level NPS benchmark). Nullable for purely digital interactions.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Supplier Support Interaction logs need to associate each customer interaction with the supplier involved for issue resolution and performance tracking.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication or service channel through which the interaction occurred (e.g., phone, web chat, in-store service desk, email, mobile app). Supports omnichannel analytics and CRM segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|web_chat|in_store|email|mobile_app|sms|kiosk|social_media — promote to reference product]']
  complaint_type varchar [note: 'Specific classification of the complaint when interaction_type = \'complaint\' (e.g., \'Product Quality\', \'Pricing Error\', \'Staff Conduct\', \'Food Safety\', \'Delivery Failure\'). Supports regulatory reporting and operational improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product_quality|pricing_error|staff_conduct|food_safety|delivery_failure|pharmacy_error|billing_dispute — promote to reference product]']
  contact_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the primary reason the shopper initiated contact, as classified by the contact center or CRM system (e.g., \'ORD-001\' for order inquiry, \'PHM-003\' for pharmacy question). Enables contact reason analytics and workforce planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage, SLA measurement, and audit trail compliance.']
  crm_activity_code varchar [note: 'Source system activity or interaction record identifier from the CRM platform (Salesforce Commerce Cloud). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system of record.']
  digital_touchpoint_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific digital touchpoint associated with this interaction (e.g., app session ID, web session ID, email campaign ID). Used for digital journey analytics and attribution. Nullable for in-store or phone interactions.']
  digital_touchpoint_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the digital touchpoint type for digital channel interactions (e.g., app session, web session, email campaign, push notification). Supports omnichannel attribution and digital engagement analytics.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the interaction in seconds (e.g., call handle time, chat session length). Used for contact center efficiency metrics, average handle time (AHT) reporting, and workforce planning.']
  escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction was escalated to a supervisor, specialist team, or higher support tier. True when escalation occurred; False otherwise. Supports contact center quality and SLA reporting.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of why the interaction was escalated (e.g., \'Shopper requested supervisor\', \'Regulatory complaint\', \'Unresolved after two contacts\'). Populated only when escalated = True.']
  first_contact_resolution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper\'s issue was fully resolved during this single interaction without requiring a follow-up contact. First Contact Resolution (FCR) is a key contact center performance metric.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which a follow-up action or contact must be completed. Populated when follow_up_required = True. Used for SLA tracking and agent task management.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up action or contact is required after this interaction. Drives task creation and callback scheduling in CRM workflows.']
  interaction_category varchar [note: 'Business category classifying the root topic of the interaction (e.g., \'Order Fulfillment\', \'Pharmacy\', \'Loyalty & Promotions\', \'Product Quality\', \'Delivery\', \'Billing\'). Supports contact reason analytics and operational improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: order_fulfillment|pharmacy|loyalty_promotions|product_quality|delivery|billing|store_experience|fuel_center — promote to reference product]']
  interaction_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the interaction, derived from interaction_timestamp. Used for day-level reporting, store-level NPS benchmarking, and date-partitioned analytics queries.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the interaction event occurred (e.g., when the call was connected, when the chat session started, when the NPS survey was submitted). This is the principal business event timestamp, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the shopper interaction. Determines which supplemental fields are applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: call_center_contact|chat_session|service_desk_visit|email_open|app_session|delivery_feedback|complaint|compliment|nps_survey — promote to reference product]']
  nps_classification varchar [note: 'Derived classification of the shopper\'s NPS response: Promoter (9–10), Passive (7–8), or Detractor (0–6). Populated only for NPS survey interactions. Used for store-level NPS benchmarking and customer experience segmentation.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Shopper\'s Net Promoter Score (NPS) response on a 0–10 scale, captured exclusively for NPS survey interaction types. This is the single source of truth for NPS data in the customer domain. Null for non-NPS interactions.']
  nps_survey_trigger varchar [note: 'The business event that triggered the NPS survey to be sent to the shopper (e.g., post-purchase, post-delivery, post-pharmacy-visit, post-pickup). Enables analysis of NPS scores by customer journey stage.']
  nps_verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Open-text verbatim comment provided by the shopper as part of the NPS survey response. Captures qualitative feedback for customer experience analysis, sentiment analysis, and AI/ML text analytics. Populated only for NPS survey interactions.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the result of the interaction (e.g., \'refund issued\', \'order re-dispatched\', \'information provided\', \'complaint logged\'). Captures the actionable result for CRM and service quality reporting.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the interaction, used to drive routing and SLA targets in the contact center or CRM workflow. Critical interactions may involve safety, regulatory, or high-value shopper escalations.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this interaction, used in customer-facing communications (e.g., confirmation emails, chat transcripts). Sourced from CRM or contact center platform.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body relevant to this interaction when regulatory_flag = True (e.g., \'FDA\', \'DEA\', \'State Board of Pharmacy\', \'OSHA\', \'FTC\'). Supports compliance reporting and audit trail requirements.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction involves a regulatory or compliance matter requiring escalation or formal reporting (e.g., FDA food safety complaint, DEA pharmacy concern, HIPAA-related inquiry, OSHA workplace incident report). True triggers compliance workflow.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current resolution state of the interaction. Tracks whether the shopper\'s issue or inquiry was resolved, escalated, or left pending. Critical for customer service SLA monitoring and CRM workflow management.']
  satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Post-interaction satisfaction rating provided by the shopper, typically on a 1–5 scale (CSAT). Distinct from NPS score; captures immediate interaction-level satisfaction rather than overall brand loyalty. Null for interactions where no rating was solicited.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this interaction record originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, NCR POS, McKesson Pharmacy, eCommerce platform). Supports data lineage and multi-source reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing more granular detail within the interaction_category (e.g., within \'Delivery\': \'Late Delivery\', \'Missing Item\', \'Damaged Item\'). Enables drill-down root cause analysis.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject or topic label for the interaction (e.g., \'Missing item in delivery\', \'Pharmacy prescription inquiry\', \'Loyalty points dispute\'). Used for categorization, routing, and reporting in CRM and contact center systems.']
  survey_response_status varchar [note: 'Status of the shopper\'s response to a survey interaction: complete (all questions answered), partial (some questions answered), opted_out (shopper declined), or no_response (survey sent but not answered). Applicable to NPS and other survey interaction types.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interaction record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit trail requirements.']
  wait_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds the shopper waited before being connected to an agent or receiving a response. Used for service level agreement (SLA) monitoring and customer experience benchmarking.']

  Note: 'Captures all recorded touchpoints, interactions, and survey responses between a shopper and Grocery across channels — including NPS surveys (no other product in this domain stores NPS data). Includes interaction type (call center contact, chat session, in-store service desk visit, email open, app session, delivery feedback, complaint, compliment, NPS survey response), interaction date and time, channel, store or digital touchpoint reference, agent or system ID, interaction outcome, resolution status, and linked case or order reference. For NPS survey interactions, captures NPS score (0-10), promoter/passive/detractor classification, verbatim comment text, survey trigger event (post-purchase, post-delivery, post-pharmacy-visit), and response status (complete, partial, opted-out). Provides a longitudinal interaction history for CRM, customer service workflows, and customer experience measurement including store-level NPS benchmarking.'
}

Table customer.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer service case record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent currently assigned to the case in Salesforce Service Cloud. Used for workload balancing, agent performance reporting, and case ownership tracking.']
  escalated_to_agent_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisor or specialist agent to whom the case was escalated. Null if no escalation occurred. Supports escalation path analysis and supervisor workload reporting.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Cases triggered by compliance violations need to reference the specific compliance obligation for audit and remediation tracking.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the omnichannel order (delivery, pickup, or in-store) associated with this case, if applicable. Supports delivery issue and billing dispute case types.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Regulatory case management requires linking each case to the exact product item for recall tracking and compliance reporting.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grocery shopper who submitted or is associated with this case. Links to the customer.shopper master record.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store location associated with the case (e.g., where the incident occurred, where the purchase was made, or where the complaint originated).']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Recall Management report requires linking each case to the responsible supplier for product recalls and quality issues.']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Name of the service team or queue to which the case is assigned (e.g., Pharmacy Support, Delivery Resolution, Store Operations, Loyalty & Promotions). Supports team-level SLA and volume reporting.']
  case_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the customer complaint, inquiry, or issue as captured at case creation. May include product details, incident circumstances, and customer-stated expectations.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable case reference number assigned by Salesforce CRM Service Cloud. Used in all customer-facing communications and agent workflows (e.g., CAS-000012345678).']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer service case. Drives agent queue management, SLA monitoring, and reporting dashboards. Values: open (newly created), in_progress (agent working), escalated (elevated to supervisor or specialist), resolved (solution provided), closed (confirmed complete).']
