  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the customer service case. Drives routing, SLA assignment, and resolution workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: complaint|inquiry|refund_request|delivery_issue|product_recall_concern|pharmacy_inquiry|billing_dispute|product_quality_issue|loyalty_inquiry — promote to reference product]']
  channel varchar [note: 'The customer contact channel through which the case was originally submitted. Supports omnichannel service analytics and channel-specific resolution benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|email|chat|web|mobile_app|in_store|social_media — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was formally closed after customer confirmation or automatic closure policy. Distinct from resolution_timestamp — a case may be resolved but not yet closed pending customer acknowledgment.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Type of compensation or goodwill gesture issued to the customer as part of case resolution. Drives compensation cost reporting and policy compliance monitoring.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code of the coupon or store credit voucher issued to the customer as case compensation. Used to track redemption and prevent duplicate issuance.']
  coupon_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Face value of any coupon or promotional voucher issued to the customer as part of case resolution. Expressed in USD. Null if no coupon was issued. Supports promotions liability and compensation cost reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this case record was first ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Distinct from open_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data lineage and pipeline audit trails.']
  crm_case_code varchar [note: 'Native case record identifier from Salesforce CRM Service Cloud (the system of record). Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and the operational CRM platform.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction rating provided by the shopper via survey (typically 1–5 scale). Null if the customer did not respond to the satisfaction survey. Used for agent performance evaluation and NPS correlation analysis.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was escalated to a supervisor, specialist team, or higher support tier. Null if the case was never escalated. Used for escalation rate and time-to-escalate analytics.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assigned agent first responded to the customer after case creation. Used to measure First Response Time (FRT) against SLA targets and customer experience KPIs.']
  is_food_safety boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case involves a food safety concern (e.g., foreign object in food, illness report, temperature abuse, HACCP violation). When true, triggers mandatory escalation and regulatory notification workflows per FDA FSMA requirements.']
  is_pharmacy_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case involves a pharmacy inquiry, prescription issue, or drug-related concern. When true, the case is subject to HIPAA non-PHI handling protocols and routed to the pharmacy support team. No PHI is stored in this record.']
  open_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the customer service case was officially opened and entered into the system. Used for SLA calculation, aging reports, and volume trend analysis.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the case was created in Salesforce Service Cloud. The principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Used for SLA elapsed-time calculations and intraday volume reporting.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the case, influencing SLA targets and agent assignment. Critical cases include product recall concerns, food safety incidents, and pharmacy-related issues requiring immediate attention.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the product involved in the case as reported by the customer or agent. Captured independently of the UPC to handle cases where the product cannot be precisely identified by barcode.']
  product_upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code (UPC) or Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) of the specific product involved in the case (e.g., for product quality complaints, recall concerns, or pricing disputes). Null if no specific product is implicated.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Official FDA or USDA recall reference number associated with the case, if the case is linked to a product recall event. Null for non-recall cases. Critical for regulatory traceability and recall response reporting.']
  refund_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the refund issued to the customer as compensation for the case. Expressed in USD. Zero or null if no monetary refund was issued. Used for financial reconciliation and compensation cost tracking.']
  reopen_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this case has been reopened after a prior resolution or closure. A non-zero value indicates the initial resolution was unsatisfactory. Used for first-contact resolution (FCR) rate analysis and quality assurance.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative entered by the agent describing the actions taken to resolve the case, the outcome, and any follow-up commitments made to the customer. Used for quality assurance reviews and agent coaching.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was marked as resolved by the assigned agent or automated workflow. Used to calculate total resolution time and SLA compliance.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the underlying root cause identified during case resolution. Used for trend analysis, supplier accountability, and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product_quality|delivery_failure|pricing_error|inventory_oos|staff_conduct|system_error|vendor_issue|cold_chain_failure|shrink_related|pharmacy_dispensing — promote to reference product]']
  sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case exceeded its SLA due timestamp without resolution. Set to true when resolution_timestamp is null at sla_due_timestamp or when resolution occurred after the SLA deadline. Used for SLA compliance reporting and agent performance management.']
  sla_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target deadline by which the case must be resolved per the applicable Service Level Agreement (SLA) policy. Calculated from open_timestamp plus the SLA target hours for the assigned case type and priority.']
  snap_wic_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case involves a SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women Infants and Children), or EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) transaction dispute or eligibility inquiry. Supports government program compliance reporting.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Short, free-text summary or title of the case as entered by the customer or agent. Used for case list views, search, and initial triage in Salesforce Service Cloud.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this case record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture (CDC), and data freshness monitoring.']

  Note: 'Customer service case record tracking shopper complaints, inquiries, product quality issues, delivery problems, and billing disputes. Captures case number, case type (complaint, inquiry, refund request, delivery issue, product recall concern, pharmacy inquiry), priority, status (open, in-progress, escalated, resolved, closed), open date, resolution date, SLA due date, assigned agent, resolution notes, root cause category, and compensation issued (refund amount, coupon value). Integrates with Salesforce CRM Service Cloud case management.'
}

Table customer.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each status change event record in the shopper and wholesale account lifecycle audit trail. Primary key of this immutable history table.']
  crm_case_id varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM case or service ticket identifier associated with this status change when the change was triggered by a customer service interaction. Supports end-to-end traceability from customer complaint to account action for NPS and escalation analytics.']
  primary_status_associate_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the human user (associate, customer service representative, or administrator) who initiated the status change. Null when the change was triggered by an automated system process. Used for accountability and audit trail in compliance investigations.']
  primary_status_shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper or wholesale account record whose status changed. Links this history event to the customer master record in the customer domain.']
  reversed_event_status_history_id varchar [note: 'When reversal_flag is True, references the status_history_id of the original status change event that this record reverses or corrects. Null for non-reversal records. Maintains the immutable append-only audit trail while enabling error correction traceability.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status change required managerial or compliance approval before being applied. Supports segregation-of-duties controls for high-risk transitions such as fraud suspensions and regulatory holds.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the status change was approved by the designated approver. Null when approval_required is False or when the change is pending approval. Used for SLA tracking on approval workflows.']
  change_channel varchar [note: 'The interaction channel through which the status change was initiated. Supports omnichannel analytics and helps identify whether closures or suspensions originate from self-service digital channels, call center interactions, or automated back-office processes.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the business reason that triggered the status change. Used for compliance reporting, fraud investigation workflows, and customer service escalation routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary_closure|fraud_suspension|inactivity|reactivation|merge|duplicate_detected|deceased|regulatory_hold — promote to reference product]']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the status change beyond the standardized reason code. Supports customer service escalation notes, fraud investigation documentation, and compliance audit trails.']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the status change event occurred in the operational system. This is the authoritative business event time, distinct from the record audit timestamp. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  changed_by_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system or automated process that triggered the status change when no human user is responsible. Examples include the fraud detection engine, batch inactivity sweep, CRM automation, or account merge process. Null when changed_by_user_id is populated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|ncr_pos|sap_retail|oracle_rms|mckesson|manual|batch_inactivity|fraud_engine|merge_process — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this status history record was first written to the Databricks Silver layer. Distinct from change_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data pipeline audit, SLA monitoring, and Silver layer lineage tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_privacy_basis varchar [note: 'The legal basis under which this status change event and associated personal data is processed and retained. Required for GDPR compliance documentation. Particularly relevant for closure and erasure events where the legal basis for continued data retention must be established.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the new status becomes operationally effective. May differ from change_timestamp when status changes are scheduled in advance (e.g., a reactivation scheduled for a future date or a closure effective at end of billing period).']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the type of customer entity whose status changed. Distinguishes between B2C shoppers, B2B wholesale accounts, pharmacy patients (non-PHI), and government program participants such as SNAP and WIC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shopper|wholesale_account|pharmacy_patient|snap_participant|wic_participant — promote to reference product]']
  fraud_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this status change to an active fraud investigation case when the change_reason_code is fraud_suspension or regulatory_hold. Enables cross-referencing with the fraud management system for investigation workflows and regulatory reporting.']
  initiated_by_customer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status change was voluntarily initiated by the customer (True) or was applied by the business or an automated system without customer request (False). Critical for distinguishing voluntary closures from fraud suspensions or inactivity-driven deactivations in compliance and NPS analysis.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The account lifecycle status applied as a result of this change event. Captures the to-state in the status transition pair. Together with previous_status, defines the complete transition vector for lifecycle analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|suspended|pending|closed|merged|fraud_hold — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary free-text notes entered by the associate or system at the time of the status change. May include customer service context, escalation details, or operational observations not captured by the structured reason code. Not intended for PHI or PCI data.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer-facing notification (email, SMS, or push) was dispatched to inform the shopper of the status change. Supports compliance with consumer notification requirements and customer service follow-up tracking.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the customer notification was dispatched following the status change. Null when notification_sent is False. Used for SLA compliance tracking on customer communication obligations.']
  pharmacy_patient_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status change affects a shopper who is also registered as a pharmacy patient (non-PHI flag only). When True, triggers a notification to the McKesson pharmacy system to review active prescription refill schedules and patient communication preferences.']
  previous_loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'The Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) loyalty tier held by the shopper immediately before the status change. Captured at the time of the event to preserve the historical tier context for loyalty program compliance, reactivation offers, and NPS analysis.']
  previous_segment varchar [note: 'The marketing or behavioral shopper segment assigned to the account immediately before the status change. Preserved as a point-in-time snapshot to support segmentation drift analysis, reactivation campaign targeting, and churn modeling.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The account lifecycle status immediately before this change event was applied. Captures the from-state in the status transition pair for audit and investigation purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|suspended|pending|closed|merged|fraud_hold — promote to reference product]']
  reactivation_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is eligible for reactivation outreach following a closure or suspension event. Set to False for fraud suspensions, deceased records, or regulatory holds. Used by CRM and marketing automation to filter win-back campaign audiences.']
  reactivation_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the account is no longer eligible for reactivation, typically driven by data retention policies or regulatory requirements. Null for accounts with no reactivation expiry. Supports GDPR right-to-erasure scheduling and loyalty win-back campaign timing.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this status history record may be purged or anonymized in accordance with the applicable data retention policy. Driven by regulatory requirements (GDPR, CCPA, SOX) and the nature of the status change event. Null for records with indefinite retention requirements.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a reversal or correction of a previously applied status change. When True, the reversed_event_id field identifies the original event being corrected. Supports immutable audit trail integrity while allowing error correction.']
  snap_program_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status change affects the shopper\'s Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) / Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) eligibility or enrollment. Triggers downstream notification workflows to program administrators when True.']
  source_event_ref varchar [note: 'The native event or transaction identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce case ID, SAP change document number). Enables exact-match reconciliation between the Silver layer audit trail and the source system\'s own change log.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this status change event. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver layer and upstream operational systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|ncr_pos|sap_retail|oracle_rms|mckesson|workday_hcm|manual_entry — promote to reference product]']
  wic_program_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status change affects the shopper\'s Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) program enrollment or benefit access. Triggers downstream notification workflows to WIC program administrators when True.']

  Note: 'Immutable history of status changes for shopper and wholesale account records. Captures the entity reference, previous status, new status, change reason (voluntary closure, fraud suspension, inactivity, reactivation, merge), change timestamp, changed-by user or system, and supporting notes. Provides a full audit trail of account lifecycle events for compliance, fraud investigation, and customer service escalation workflows.'
}

Table customer.personalized_promotion_assignment {
  personalized_promotion_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the personalized_promotion_assignment association']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promotional campaign master record']
  promotion_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the redemption transaction linked to this deal']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the shopper master record']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Store where the deal is applicable or was redeemed']
  deal_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the personalized deal']
  deal_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the personalized deal']
  deal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the deal (e.g., active, expired, cancelled)']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deal (e.g., discount, bundle, loyalty‑exclusive)']
  discount_percentage decimal [note: 'Percentage discount offered in the personalized deal']
  generation_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that generated the deal (e.g., marketing engine, manual entry)']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Shopper loyalty tier applicable to the deal']
  ml_model_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the machine‑learning model used to target the shopper']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the shopper has redeemed this specific deal']
  targeting_rationale varchar [note: 'Business reason for targeting the shopper with this deal']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date when the personalized deal becomes active']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'End date when the personalized deal expires']

  Note: 'Links shoppers to promotional campaigns they are targeted by, capturing deal specifics such as discount, validity period, redemption metrics, and targeting rationale. Each record represents a single personalized deal between one shopper and one promo campaign.. Existence Justification: In Grocery, each shopper can be targeted by multiple promotional campaigns, and each campaign can be offered to many shoppers. The marketing team creates and manages personalized deal records that capture discount percentages, validity dates, redemption counts, and targeting rationale, making the relationship an operational entity that is actively created, updated, and deleted.'
}

Table product.order_header {
  order_header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for every customer purchase record across all channels (POS, online, click-and-collect, curbside pickup, last-mile delivery, pharmacy Rx fills). Primary key of the order_header entity and the single canonical identity for all customer transactions in the enterprise.']
  cashier_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce associate (cashier) who processed the POS transaction. Applicable only for in_store_pos channel type. Used for cashier performance reporting, void/return authorization auditing, and labor analytics. Null for self-checkout, online, and OMS orders.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost center assignment enables internal cost allocation and budgeting reports per order, a standard practice in grocery chains.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Links each order to its fiscal period, enabling period‑based financial statements and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_week_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail fiscal calendar week in which this order was placed. Enables alignment of sales reporting to the 4-5-4 retail fiscal calendar used for comp sales (comparable store sales) and same-store sales analysis. Derived from order_date at ingestion.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue GL account needed for GAAP sales reporting; each order must post revenue to a specific GL account, obvious to finance analysts.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer household entity for household-level purchase analytics, loyalty program household linking, and targeted promotions. Enables household basket analysis and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) calculations at the household level. Null for anonymous transactions.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Identifies the corporate legal entity owning the store for consolidation and regulatory reporting.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: REQUIRED: Loyalty reporting needs to tie each order to the shopper\'s membership to calculate points earned, tier changes, and generate the \'Points Earned per Order\' report.']
  method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: Order‑level reference to the tokenized payment method allows reporting of payment method usage per order and supports refunds to the original method.']
  order_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_channel. Business justification: Orders belong to a channel; replace the free‑text channel_type with a foreign key to order_channel for referential integrity and channel‑level analytics.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Business process: Org‑unit performance reporting requires linking each order to the responsible org unit for labor and cost allocation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center linkage supports profitability analysis by store/region; required for P&L statements per profit center.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Required for Marketing ROI report linking each order to its originating campaign; replaces denormalized promo_code.']
  rx_patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.rx_patient. Business justification: Orders containing prescriptions must be associated with the patient to satisfy HIPAA, claim reconciliation, and loyalty program integration.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer/shopper who placed or completed this order. Nullable for anonymous in-store POS transactions where no loyalty card was scanned and no account was identified.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: Required for cluster‑level sales performance reporting; each order must be attributed to its store cluster for weekly KPI dashboards.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical store or fulfillment location where the order originated or was fulfilled. Links to the store master for store-level reporting, comp sales, and same-store sales analysis.']
  age_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains age-restricted items (alcohol, tobacco, lottery tickets, certain medications) requiring cashier or delivery driver age verification at point of sale or delivery. Triggers age-check workflow in NCR POS or last-mile delivery confirmation.']
  bag_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fee charged for single-use plastic or paper bags per applicable state and local bag fee ordinances. Required for regulatory compliance reporting in jurisdictions with mandatory bag fee laws (e.g., California, Illinois). Zero in jurisdictions without bag fee requirements.']
  basket_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct line items (SKUs) in the order basket. Used for basket size analytics, express lane eligibility (e.g., 15 items or fewer), and customer segmentation. Distinct from total unit quantity which counts individual units across all line items.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the order was cancelled. Populated only when order_status = \'cancelled\'. Used for cancellation root-cause analysis, OOS (Out of Stock) reporting, and fraud detection. Values: customer_request, out_of_stock, payment_failure, fraud_hold, store_closure, system_error.']
  change_due_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of cash change to be returned to the customer when cash tender exceeds the order total. Applicable only for in-store POS and self-checkout cash transactions. Null for non-cash or digital orders. Sourced from NCR POS cash management module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order_header record was first written to the Silver layer data lakehouse. Distinct from order_timestamp (business event time) — this is the data capture audit timestamp used for data lineage, pipeline monitoring, and late-arriving data detection.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this order. Defaults to USD for domestic US grocery operations. Required for multi-currency support in international or border-region store operations.']
  delivery_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for last-mile delivery service. Applicable for online_delivery channel orders. May be zero for fee-waived orders (e.g., loyalty member benefit, promotional free delivery). Null for in-store POS, click-and-collect, and curbside pickup orders.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all discounts applied to the order, including promotional discounts (TPR — Temporary Price Reduction), loyalty discounts, coupon redemptions, and BOGO (Buy One Get One) savings. Stored as a positive value representing the reduction from subtotal.']
  ebt_snap_wic_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any government assistance program tender (EBT — Electronic Benefits Transfer, SNAP — Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program, or WIC — Women Infants and Children) was used in this transaction. Required for USDA SNAP/WIC program compliance reporting and retailer authorization audits.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date on which the order is to be fulfilled — delivered to the customer, made available for pickup, or dispensed (for pharmacy Rx). Used for delivery slot management, SLA tracking, and fulfillment capacity planning. Null for immediate in-store POS transactions.']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the order was actually fulfilled — delivered, picked up by the customer, or dispensed. Used for on-time delivery rate (OTD) measurement, last-mile delivery (LMD) SLA compliance, and customer experience analytics. Null until fulfillment is confirmed.']
  loyalty_card_scanned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer loyalty card or loyalty account was identified and scanned/linked at the time of the transaction. True enables loyalty points accrual and personalized offer redemption. False indicates an anonymous transaction with no loyalty attribution.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points earned by the customer on this transaction based on the applicable loyalty program earning rules. Zero for anonymous transactions (loyalty_card_scanned = false) or transactions where no points were awarded (e.g., fuel-only purchases).']
  oms_order_reference varchar [note: 'External order reference number assigned by the OMS (Order Management System — Salesforce Commerce Cloud) for omnichannel orders. Enables cross-system order tracing between the Silver layer canonical model and the OMS source system. Null for pure in-store POS transactions not routed through OMS.']
  order_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the order was placed or the POS transaction was initiated. Used for daily sales reporting, comp sales (comparable store sales), same-store sales analysis, and fiscal period alignment.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable order reference number presented to the customer on receipts, confirmation emails, and order tracking pages. Generated by the source system (NCR POS transaction number, Salesforce Commerce Cloud order number, or McKesson Rx order number). Unique within the source system.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order. Tracks the order from initial placement through fulfillment to completion or cancellation. POS transactions typically transition directly to a terminal state (completed or voided). OMS-originated orders traverse the full lifecycle. Values: placed, confirmed, in_fulfillment, ready, dispatched, delivered, cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: placed|confirmed|in_fulfillment|ready|dispatched|delivered|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the order was placed or the POS transaction was opened. The principal real-world business event timestamp. Used for intraday sales analytics, peak-hour staffing analysis, and order SLA measurement. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument used to tender the order. For split-tender transactions, reflects the dominant or first payment method. Full tender breakdown is captured in the order_payment child entity. Values include EBT/SNAP for government assistance program transactions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|ebt_snap|ebt_cash|gift_card|loyalty_points|mobile_pay|check — promote to reference product]']
  pharmacy_rx_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order contains one or more pharmacy prescription (Rx) line items. When true, the order is subject to HIPAA privacy requirements, DEA controlled substance regulations, and State Board of Pharmacy dispensing rules. Drives downstream routing to McKesson Pharmacy Systems.']
  register_number varchar [note: 'POS register or checkout lane identifier within the store where the transaction was processed. Applicable only for in_store_pos and self_checkout channel types. Used for lane-level performance reporting, cashier assignment, and cash reconciliation. Null for OMS-originated orders.']
  self_checkout boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was processed through a self-checkout (SCO) lane where the customer scanned and bagged their own items without cashier assistance. Used for self-checkout adoption analytics, shrink attribution, and labor efficiency reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this order record. Drives data lineage, reconciliation, and system-specific business rule application. Values: ncr_pos (in-store and self-checkout POS transactions), salesforce_commerce_cloud (online, delivery, click-and-collect, curbside orders), mckesson_pharmacy (pharmacy Rx fill orders).']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing customer-provided delivery, pickup, or preparation instructions (e.g., \'Leave at front door\', \'No substitutions\', \'Call upon arrival\'). Applicable for online delivery, click-and-collect, and curbside pickup orders. Null for in-store POS transactions.']
  substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'Customer preference indicating whether out-of-stock (OOS) items in the order may be substituted with comparable alternatives by the picker or fulfillment associate. Applicable for online delivery, click-and-collect, and curbside pickup orders. Null for in-store POS transactions.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all line item extended prices before tax, discounts, and fees are applied. Represents the gross merchandise value (GMV) of the basket prior to adjustments. Used in COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) calculations.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales tax charged on the order, calculated based on applicable state and local tax rates for taxable items in the basket. Excludes tax-exempt items (e.g., qualifying grocery staples, SNAP/EBT-eligible items). Required for tax remittance reporting and SOX financial controls.']
  tender_summary varchar [note: 'Human-readable summary of payment tender types used in this transaction (e.g., \'Credit $45.20, Loyalty Points $5.00\'). Provides a quick overview of how the order was paid without exposing sensitive payment details. Sourced from NCR POS tender records or Salesforce Commerce Cloud payment summary.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount due from the customer after applying subtotal, discounts, fees, and tax. Equals subtotal_amount minus discount_amount plus tax_amount plus any applicable fees (delivery, service). This is the amount tendered by the customer. Core metric for revenue reporting and EBITDA calculations.']
  total_unit_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of individual units (items) purchased across all line items in the order. Differs from basket_item_count which counts distinct SKUs. Used for average units per transaction (UPT) analytics and shrink analysis.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the POS or OMS transaction by its business purpose. Primarily relevant for in-store POS and self-checkout channels. Values: sale (standard purchase), return (merchandise returned for refund), void (transaction cancelled before tender completion), no_sale (cash drawer opened without a sale), exchange (item swap without net monetary change).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order_header record was last modified in the Silver layer. Tracks order status changes, payment updates, and post-transaction adjustments. Used for incremental data processing and change data capture (CDC) pipelines.']

  Note: 'Master record for every customer purchase across all channels (in-store POS, online, click-and-collect, curbside pickup, last-mile delivery, and pharmacy Rx fills). Captures order number, channel type, register/lane (for POS), cashier (for POS), transaction type (sale, return, void, no-sale for POS), order date/time, order status lifecycle (placed, confirmed, in-fulfillment, ready, dispatched, delivered, cancelled), customer reference, store reference, total amounts, subtotal, tax, tender summary, change due (for POS), basket item count, loyalty card scanned flag, loyalty points earned, promo codes applied, special instructions, self-checkout flag, EBT/SNAP/WIC tender flag, source system (NCR POS / Salesforce Commerce Cloud / McKesson), payment method summary, and OMS order reference. Single source of truth for ALL customer purchase identity in the retail-grocery enterprise — encompasses both POS transactions and OMS-originated orders in one canonical entity. No separate POS transaction entity exists; channel-specific attributes are captured via channel type discriminator and nullable POS-specific fields.'
}

Table product.product_order_line {
  product_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order line item. Primary key for the order line product.']
  age_restricted_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_restricted_sale. Business justification: Required for Age‑Restricted Sale compliance report linking each sold line to its verification record; auditors need to trace every age‑restricted SKU to the age_restricted_sale entry.']
  assortment_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_item. Business justification: Needed for planogram compliance analysis; linking each sold line to the specific assortment item enables tracking of plan execution vs actual sales.']
  assortment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_plan. Business justification: Supports assortment‑plan performance metrics; associating order lines with the originating assortment plan allows calculation of plan fulfillment rates.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Enables brand performance analytics and promotional budgeting by linking each order line to its brand master.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Order line should be linked to the department responsible for the sold item to enable departmental sales reporting.']
  drug_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_item. Business justification: Necessary for pharmacy order compliance, controlled‑substance tracking, and drug‑specific reporting.']
  fuel_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_fuel_grade. Business justification: Tracks fuel grade sales and satisfies regulatory reporting for fuel products sold at the pump.']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Supports hierarchical assortment planning and category‑level sales reporting via the item hierarchy reference.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order header that contains this line item. Links the line to its transaction header.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.po_line. Business justification: Required for traceability report linking sold items to supplier purchase order lines for recall compliance.']
  prescription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.prescription. Business justification: Line‑level tracking of prescription items enables accurate inventory deduction, claim submission, and return processing in pharmacy operations.']
  price_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_change. Business justification: Price‑change impact analysis tracks which price_change event affected a line, supporting margin and revenue impact reporting.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: When a coupon or promo code is applied, the order line should reference the specific promo offer for audit and effectiveness analysis.']
  retail_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.retail_price. Business justification: Price audit report requires linking each order line to the exact retail_price record applied, enabling compliance checks of sold price versus current price.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for SKU‑level sales reporting and inventory allocation; order lines must reference the SKU master record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Required for supplier performance reporting per line item; enables cost allocation and on‑time delivery metrics that retailers track for each SKU\'s supplier.']
  age_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item requires age verification at point of sale. Includes alcohol, tobacco, and certain over-the-counter medications.']
  bogo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was part of a Buy One Get One promotion. Used for basket analysis and promotional effectiveness reporting.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or store coupon code applied to this line item. Links to coupon campaign for redemption tracking and vendor chargeback.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order line record was first created in the system. Represents when the item was added to the cart or scanned at POS.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to this line item from promotions, coupons, loyalty rewards, or temporary price reductions (TPR). Reduces the extended price.']
  extended_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total price for the line item calculated as quantity multiplied by unit price, before discounts and taxes. Gross line amount.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which this specific line item will be fulfilled. Supports mixed-cart scenarios where different items in the same order have different fulfillment methods.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number for international product identification. Encompasses UPC, EAN, and other GS1 identifiers.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the product as it appears on the receipt and in order details. Includes brand, size, and variant information.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this line item within the parent order. Used for ordering and display purposes.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the order line item. Tracks the line through fulfillment stages from active order to final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|substituted|cancelled|out_of_stock|voided|returned|picked|packed|delivered — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned by the customer for purchasing this line item. Used for customer lifetime value (CLTV) calculation and loyalty program analytics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order line record was last modified. Tracks updates to quantity, price, status, or other line attributes.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final net amount for the line item after discounts and including taxes. This is the amount the customer pays for this line.']
  organic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is certified organic by USDA or equivalent certification body. Used for organic category reporting and compliance.']
  original_sku varchar [note: 'Original SKU that was requested by the customer before substitution occurred. Null if no substitution was made.']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is a perishable product requiring cold chain management and FIFO inventory rotation. Includes fresh produce, meat, dairy, deli, bakery, and frozen items.']
  plu varchar [note: 'Price Look-Up code used for fresh produce, deli, and bakery items that are not pre-packaged with barcodes. Typically 4-5 digit codes entered manually or via scale.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is a store-owned private label brand product. Used for private label performance analysis and margin reporting.']
  promo_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a promotional discount was applied to this line item. True if any promotion, coupon, or loyalty discount was used.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of the item ordered by the customer. Supports fractional quantities for variable-weight items such as produce, meat, and deli products.']
  return_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item represents a product return. Used for return rate analysis and inventory restocking.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the item was returned. Examples include defective, expired, customer dissatisfaction, or wrong item delivered.']
  scan_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the item at point of sale or during order picking. Indicates whether the item was scanned via handheld device, belt scanner, self-checkout, manually keyed, or captured via mobile app.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is eligible for purchase using SNAP Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT). Required for government program compliance and reporting.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a substitution for an originally requested item that was out of stock or unavailable. Common in online and click-and-collect orders.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sales tax amount calculated for this line item based on applicable tax jurisdiction and product tax code. Includes state, county, and municipal taxes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code for the item determining applicable sales tax rates. Differentiates taxable, non-taxable, and reduced-rate items such as groceries, prepared foods, and SNAP-eligible products.']
  tpr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was sold at a temporary price reduction. Used for promotional markdown analysis and ad circular tracking.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity ordered. Indicates whether the item is sold by each, weight, volume, or pack. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|lb|kg|oz|g|case|pack|dozen|gallon|liter|ml — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure before any discounts or promotions are applied. Base selling price for the item.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code scanned at Point of Sale (POS). Standard barcode identifier for CPG items.']
  void_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was voided during the transaction. Used for shrink analysis and cashier performance monitoring.']
  void_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the line item was voided. Examples include customer changed mind, pricing error, damaged item, or cashier error.']
  weight_actual decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual weight of the item for variable-weight products such as fresh produce, meat, deli, and seafood. Captured from scale at POS or during fulfillment picking.']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the actual weight field. Typically pounds or kilograms depending on regional standards.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is eligible for purchase using WIC benefits. Required for government program compliance and reporting.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a customer order representing a single SKU/UPC purchased or requested across all channels (in-store POS scan, online cart, click-and-collect). Captures line sequence number, SKU/GTIN, PLU (for produce), UPC (for POS-scanned items), item description, ordered quantity, unit of measure, unit price, extended price, discount amount, promo applied flag, substitution flag, substitution SKU, line status (active, substituted, cancelled, out-of-stock, voided, returned), weight (for variable-weight/scale items), tax code, department code, tax amount, fulfillment method per line, void flag, return flag, TPR flag, BOGO flag, coupon applied reference, and scan method (handheld, belt scanner, self-checkout, manual key). Supports both fixed-weight CPG items and variable-weight fresh/deli items. Enables basket analytics, shrink analysis, and category performance reporting at the item level across all channels.'
}

Table product.order_status_history {
  order_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each status transition record in the order lifecycle. Primary key for the immutable audit trail.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the person or system account that triggered the status transition. May be an employee ID, customer ID, system service account, or driver ID depending on actor_type.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery carrier or logistics provider involved in this status transition. Applicable for delivery-related status changes (out_for_delivery, delivered). Null for non-delivery transitions.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order header that experienced this status transition. Links this status event to the parent order entity.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location (store, distribution center, micro-fulfillment center, pharmacy) where the status transition occurred. Null for system-automated transitions without physical location context.']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'The application session or transaction identifier during which this status transition was recorded. Used for technical troubleshooting and audit trail correlation.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'The category of actor (person or system) that triggered the status transition. Examples: customer, store_associate, warehouse_picker, delivery_driver, system_automation, customer_service_rep, pharmacy_technician. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer|store_associate|warehouse_picker|delivery_driver|system_automation|customer_service_rep|pharmacy_technician — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this status history record was created in the data platform. Distinct from transition_timestamp, which represents the business event time. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the device (mobile scanner, POS terminal, handheld picker device) used to record this status transition. Used for device audit trails and operational troubleshooting.']
  dwell_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes the order spent in the previous status before this transition. Calculated as the difference between this transition_timestamp and the prior transition_timestamp. Used for cycle time analysis and bottleneck identification.']
  estimated_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The estimated date and time when the order is expected to reach final completion (delivered or ready for pickup), calculated at the time of this status transition. Updated with each transition to reflect current estimates.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying any exception or issue that occurred during this status transition. Examples: OUT_OF_STOCK, PAYMENT_DECLINED, ADDRESS_INVALID, WEATHER_DELAY, CUSTOMER_NOT_HOME. Null for normal transitions.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the exception or issue. Used for customer service investigation and root cause analysis. Null for normal transitions.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The omnichannel fulfillment method for this order. Examples: in_store (traditional POS), curbside_pickup (click-and-collect), home_delivery (last-mile delivery), ship_to_home (parcel shipment), pharmacy_pickup, locker_pickup.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude coordinate where the status transition occurred, captured from mobile device or delivery scanner. Used for delivery verification and route optimization analysis. Null if geolocation not captured.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The longitude coordinate where the status transition occurred, captured from mobile device or delivery scanner. Used for delivery verification and route optimization analysis. Null if geolocation not captured.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The status the order transitioned to. Captures the to-state in the status change event. This is the current status after the transition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|allocated|picking|picked|packed|ready_for_pickup|out_for_delivery|delivered|cancelled|refunded — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the actor at the time of status transition. Used for operational context, special instructions, or issue documentation. Null if no notes provided.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer of this status transition. Examples: email, sms, push (mobile push notification), none (no notification sent).']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification (email, SMS, push notification) was sent for this status transition. True if notification was triggered, False otherwise.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status the order was in immediately before this transition. Captures the from-state in the status change event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|allocated|picking|picked|packed|ready_for_pickup|out_for_delivery|delivered|cancelled|refunded — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status transition occurred within the SLA target window. True if transition_timestamp <= sla_target_timestamp, False otherwise. Critical for performance reporting and customer service.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The target date and time by which this status transition was expected to occur, based on the order\'s service level agreement. Used for SLA compliance tracking and exception reporting.']
  source_system_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'The unique transaction or event identifier from the source system (OMS, TMS, WMS, POS) that generated this status transition. Used for cross-system reconciliation and technical troubleshooting.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-provided tracking number or shipment identifier associated with this status transition. Used for carrier dispute management and delivery verification. Null for non-delivery transitions.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the status transition occurred. This is the business event timestamp for the status change, distinct from record creation time.']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'The specific business event or action that caused this status transition. Examples: customer_placed_order, payment_authorized, inventory_allocated, pick_started, pick_completed, pack_completed, customer_arrived (for pickup), driver_dispatched, driver_scanned, delivery_confirmed, customer_cancelled, system_timeout, exception_raised, refund_processed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_placed_order|payment_authorized|inventory_allocated|pick_started|pick_completed|pack_completed|customer_arrived|driver_dispatched|driver_scanned|delivery_confirmed|customer_cancelled|system_timeout|exception_raised|refund_processed — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  triggering_system varchar [note: 'The source system that initiated or recorded this status transition. Examples: OMS (Order Management System), TMS (Transportation Management System), WMS (Warehouse Management System), POS (Point of Sale), ECOM (eCommerce platform), MOBILE (mobile app), PHARMACY (pharmacy system), MANUAL (manual override). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OMS|TMS|WMS|POS|ECOM|MOBILE|PHARMACY|MANUAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The system process or ETL job identifier that created this record in the data platform. Used for data lineage tracking and operational support.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of every status transition for an order header throughout its lifecycle. Records the previous status, new status, transition timestamp, triggering system (OMS, TMS, WMS, POS), triggering event (customer action, store action, driver scan, system timeout), and operator/system actor. Enables SLA tracking, exception investigation, and omnichannel fulfillment performance reporting. Critical for customer service resolution and carrier dispute management.'
}

Table product.delivery_order {
  delivery_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery order record. Primary key for the delivery order entity representing the last-mile logistics leg of an online or OMS-originated order.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver or delivery associate assigned to fulfill this delivery order. Links to workforce or driver master data.']
  contact_info_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact_info. Business justification: Required for Delivery Address Verification report; each delivery order must reference a validated contact_info record for carrier compliance.']
  fulfillment_delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TMS route plan that includes this delivery. Links to JDA Transportation Management System route optimization.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order header that originated this delivery. Links the delivery leg back to the original customer order.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the delivery was completed and goods were handed off to the customer or left at the delivery location. Null if delivery not yet completed.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the carrier or delivery service provider responsible for this delivery. May represent internal fleet or third-party carrier.']
  contactless_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested contactless delivery. When true, driver should leave items at the door and minimize direct contact.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery order record was first created in the system. Represents the moment the delivery leg was initiated from the order management system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery order record.']
  delivery_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The delivery service charge amount billed to the customer for this delivery. May be zero for free delivery promotions or loyalty members.']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the driver at delivery time. May include delivery condition observations, customer interactions, or exception details.']
  delivery_order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the delivery order, used for tracking and customer communication. Typically formatted as DO- prefix followed by 10-digit sequence.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery order. Tracks progression from scheduling through final delivery or failure. Values: scheduled (delivery window assigned), assigned (driver allocated), en_route (in transit), out_for_delivery (final leg), delivered (completed successfully), failed (delivery attempt unsuccessful), returned (sent back to origin), cancelled (order cancelled before delivery). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|assigned|en_route|out_for_delivery|delivered|failed|returned|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of delivery service level. Defines the speed and method of delivery fulfillment.']
  distance_miles decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total distance in miles from the fulfillment origin (store, MFC, or DC) to the delivery destination. Used for delivery fee calculation and route analytics.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a delivery attempt failed. Populated only when delivery_status is failed or returned. Used for root cause analysis and redelivery planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_not_home|address_not_found|access_denied|weather|vehicle_issue|refused|damaged|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this delivery order record. Tracks the last status change, address correction, or driver assignment.']
  proof_of_delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method used to capture proof that the delivery was completed. Values: signature (customer signed on device), photo (driver captured delivery photo), pin (customer provided PIN code), none (no proof captured for contactless).']
  proof_of_delivery_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the stored proof of delivery artifact (signature image, photo, or PIN verification record). Used for dispute resolution.']
  reattempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of delivery attempts made for this order. Increments with each failed delivery attempt. Used to trigger customer contact or return-to-sender workflows.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who received the delivery. May differ from the order customer if delivery was accepted by household member or designee.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the delivery is scheduled to occur. Used for day-level delivery planning and customer communication.']
  scheduled_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'The ending timestamp of the scheduled delivery window communicated to the customer. Defines the latest time within the promised delivery window.']
  scheduled_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'The beginning timestamp of the scheduled delivery window communicated to the customer. Defines the earliest time the customer should expect delivery.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that originated this delivery order record. Typically OMS (Order Management System), TMS (Transportation Management System), or eCommerce platform.']
  stop_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The position of this delivery within the drivers route sequence. Used for route optimization and estimated time of arrival calculation.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery requires cold chain temperature control for perishable or frozen items. When true, vehicle must have refrigeration capability.']
  tip_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Gratuity amount provided by the customer for the delivery driver. May be pre-tip at order time or post-delivery adjustment.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the vehicle used to fulfill this delivery. May be license plate, fleet number, or vehicle asset tag.']

  Note: 'Last-mile delivery order record managing the logistics leg of an online or OMS-originated order dispatched to a customer address. Captures delivery order number, linked order header, delivery address, scheduled delivery window (start/end), actual delivery timestamp, delivery status (scheduled, assigned, en-route, delivered, failed, returned), driver/carrier reference, vehicle reference, delivery zone, distance miles, delivery fee, tip amount, contactless delivery flag, delivery instructions, proof-of-delivery method (photo/signature/PIN), and TMS route reference. Integrates with JDA TMS.'
}

Table product.pickup_appointment {
  pickup_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pickup appointment record. Primary key for curbside and click-and-collect appointment tracking.']
  dispensing_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store associate who fulfilled and dispensed the pickup order. Used for performance tracking and quality accountability.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order header associated with this pickup appointment. Links the appointment to the customer\'s order for fulfillment tracking.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who scheduled the pickup appointment. Links appointment to customer profile for notification and service history.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store location where the pickup appointment is scheduled. Identifies the fulfillment location for curbside or in-store pickup.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the pickup appointment. Used for customer communication and associate reference during fulfillment.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pickup appointment. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation. Critical for SLA (Service Level Agreement) monitoring and operational workflow management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|ready|arrived|in_progress|dispensed|completed|no_show|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of pickup method. Distinguishes between curbside delivery to vehicle, in-store pickup counter collection, or automated locker retrieval.']
  arrival_notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the customer sent an I\'m here notification via mobile app or SMS. Alerts store associates that customer has arrived.']
  cancellation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the cancellation circumstances. Provides additional context beyond the reason code for customer service and operational review.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for appointment cancellation. Used for root cause analysis and service improvement initiatives.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the appointment was cancelled by customer or system. Used for slot release and cancellation rate analytics.']
  contact_phone_number varchar [note: 'The phone number to reach the customer upon arrival or for appointment coordination. Used for real-time communication during pickup process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the pickup appointment record was first created in the system. Audit field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  customer_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the customer arrived at the pickup location. Captured via mobile app check-in or associate entry. Critical for SLA (Service Level Agreement) measurement.']
  customer_feedback varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback provided by the customer about their pickup experience. Captures qualitative insights for service improvement.']
  customer_rating varchar [note: 'The customer satisfaction rating provided after pickup completion, typically on a 1-5 scale. Used for service quality measurement and associate performance evaluation.']
  dispensing_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the order was fully dispensed to the customer and the transaction completed. Used for calculating total service time and SLA compliance.']
  dispensing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the associate began loading items into the customer\'s vehicle or handed over the order. Marks the beginning of the dispensing process.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the pickup appointment record was last updated. Audit field for tracking changes to appointment details or status.']
  order_ready_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the order was fully picked, staged, and ready for customer pickup. Triggers customer notification that order is available.']
  parking_spot_number varchar [note: 'The specific parking spot identifier where the customer parks for curbside pickup. May differ from bay number in stores with multiple spot configurations.']
  pickup_bay_number varchar [note: 'The designated curbside bay or parking spot number assigned to this appointment. Used for directing customers to the correct location and managing bay capacity.']
  ready_notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the order-ready notification (SMS, email, or app push) was sent to the customer. Used for communication audit trail.']
  reminder_notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when a reminder notification was sent to the customer about their upcoming appointment. Used for proactive customer engagement tracking.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the pickup appointment is scheduled. Used for day-level capacity planning and customer communication.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The ending timestamp of the scheduled pickup window. Defines the latest time within the appointment slot for customer arrival.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The beginning timestamp of the scheduled pickup window. Defines when the customer can arrive for their appointment.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the appointment met the SLA target. True if wait time was within target, false if exceeded. Used for compliance reporting.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'The target service time in minutes for this appointment type. Used for measuring performance against service commitments.']
  slot_capacity_used varchar [note: 'The capacity units consumed by this appointment within the time slot. Used for managing concurrent appointment limits and preventing overbooking.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Customer-provided special instructions for the pickup (e.g., trunk placement, contactless delivery, accessibility needs). Guides associate fulfillment behavior.']
  substitution_preference varchar [note: 'Customer preference for handling out-of-stock items. Determines whether substitutions are allowed and if customer approval is required.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'The temperature control requirement for this order. Determines staging location and handling procedures to maintain cold chain integrity for perishables.']
  total_bag_count varchar [note: 'The total number of bags or containers prepared for this pickup. Helps associates verify complete order transfer to customer.']
  total_item_count varchar [note: 'The total number of items in the order for this pickup appointment. Used for workload estimation and associate staffing decisions.']
  vehicle_description varchar [note: 'Description of the customer\'s vehicle including make, model, and color. Helps associates identify the correct vehicle during curbside fulfillment.']
  vehicle_license_plate varchar [note: 'The license plate number of the customer\'s vehicle. Used for positive identification during curbside pickup. Business-confidential customer information.']
  wait_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'The calculated wait time in minutes from customer arrival to dispensing completion. Key performance indicator for curbside service quality and SLA compliance.']

  Note: 'Curbside or in-store click-and-collect pickup appointment record. Captures appointment ID, linked order header, store ID, pickup bay/slot, scheduled pickup window (date, start time, end time), actual arrival timestamp, actual pickup completion timestamp, appointment status (scheduled, arrived, dispensed, no-show, cancelled), vehicle description, parking spot number, associate who dispensed, and customer notification timestamps (ready SMS, reminder). Manages curbside slot capacity and SLA compliance for click-and-collect fulfillment.'
}

Table product.order_fulfillment {
  order_fulfillment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fulfillment execution record. Primary key.']
  batch_wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch wave if this fulfillment was part of a multi-order picking wave. Used in WMS optimization for efficient picking routes.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific store, MFC, DC, or pharmacy location executing this fulfillment.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Needed for fulfillment audit to associate each order fulfillment with the inbound shipment that supplied the inventory.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order header being fulfilled. Links this fulfillment execution to the customer order.']
  pick_cart_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical picking cart or tote used by the picker. Used for equipment tracking and multi-order batch picking workflows.']
  primary_order_associate_id varchar [note: 'Workforce associate assigned to pick this order. References the associate performing the physical picking task.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Regulatory and labor reporting tracks which shift performed fulfillment; linking fulfillment to shift enables shift‑level productivity and compliance analysis.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Required for Pickup Fulfillment Assignment report: links each fulfillment record to the store that performed the pickup, enabling SLA monitoring and inventory allocation.']
  actual_fulfillment_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time in minutes from pick start to dispatch. Used to measure picker productivity and SLA compliance.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description if the fulfillment was cancelled. Examples: customer_request, inventory_unavailable, system_error, weather_delay. Populated only when fulfillment_status = cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment record was created in the system, typically when the order was released to the fulfillment queue.']
  dispatched_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was handed to the customer (curbside/in-store pickup) or to the delivery carrier for last-mile delivery.']
  fulfillment_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility executing the fulfillment. Store = retail store backroom, MFC = Micro-Fulfillment Center, DC = Distribution Center, Pharmacy = pharmacy dispensing area.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer will receive the order. Curbside Pickup = customer collects at designated parking spot, In-Store Pickup = customer collects at service desk, Home Delivery = last-mile delivery to customer address, Locker Pickup = customer retrieves from automated locker.']
  fulfillment_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for this fulfillment execution, used for tracking and communication with pickers and customers.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the fulfillment workflow. Queued = awaiting assignment, Assigned = picker assigned but not started, Picking = actively picking items, Picked = all items picked, Packing = items being packed, Packed = packing complete, Staged = ready for customer pickup or delivery dispatch, Dispatched = handed to carrier or customer, Cancelled = fulfillment aborted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: queued|assigned|picking|picked|packing|packed|staged|dispatched|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  oos_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of order lines that could not be fulfilled due to out-of-stock conditions and no acceptable substitution. Impacts customer satisfaction and fill rate metrics.']
  pack_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when packing was completed and order is ready for staging or dispatch.']
  pack_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when packing of picked items began. Used for labor tracking and process efficiency analysis.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Number of packages or bags this fulfillment was packed into. Used for customer communication and carrier handoff verification.']
  pick_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when all items were picked (or picking was completed with substitutions/OOS). Marks transition to packing stage.']
  pick_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the picker began picking items for this order. Used for labor productivity tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  picker_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the picker during fulfillment, such as item condition observations, substitution reasons, or customer communication notes.']
  quality_check_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment underwent a quality inspection before staging. True = quality check performed, False = no quality check. Used for high-value orders or training purposes.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes from order placement to fulfillment completion, based on the order type and fulfillment method. Used for performance monitoring and customer promise tracking.']
  staged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the packed order was placed in the staging area or pickup bay, ready for customer collection or carrier pickup.']
  staging_area varchar [note: 'Physical staging location identifier within the fulfillment facility (e.g., bay number, zone, cooler section) where the packed order is held for pickup or dispatch.']
  substitution_count varchar [note: 'Number of order lines where a substitute item was picked due to the original item being out of stock or unavailable.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Primary temperature control zone required for this fulfillment. Ambient = room temperature, Refrigerated = cold chain 0-4°C, Frozen = frozen storage below 0°C. Critical for fresh produce and perishable inventory management.']
  total_lines varchar [note: 'Total number of order line items in the original order that this fulfillment is responsible for picking.']
  total_lines_picked varchar [note: 'Number of order lines successfully picked (including substitutions). Used to calculate fill rate and picker productivity.']
  total_units_picked decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units picked across all lines, including substitutions. Used for labor productivity measurement (units per hour).']
  total_volume_liters decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total volume of the packed order in liters. Used for vehicle capacity planning and staging area space management.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the picked and packed order in kilograms. Used for shipping cost calculation and load planning for delivery vehicles.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fulfillment record. Used for audit trail and data freshness tracking.']
  wms_task_reference varchar [note: 'External task or wave identifier from Manhattan Associates WMS that orchestrated this fulfillment execution. Used for integration and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Fulfillment execution record linking an order header to its physical pick-pack-ship workflow in the store backroom, MFC, or DC.
