  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'The fulfillment method selected by the customer during checkout (home delivery, in-store pickup, curbside pickup).']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer began the checkout process by submitting their cart. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this checkout session record was last updated. Audit trail for record modification.']
  loyalty_card_number varchar [note: 'Loyalty card number entered or scanned during checkout for rewards and personalization.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned from this purchase, calculated during checkout.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed by the customer during this checkout session.']
  oms_order_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier assigned by the Order Management System upon successful checkout completion and order routing.']
  payment_authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the payment processor upon successful payment validation.']
  payment_method_selected varchar [note: 'Primary payment method selected by the customer during checkout. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ebt|gift_card|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_processor_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the payment gateway or processor for this checkout transaction.']
  promo_codes_applied varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of promotional codes applied during checkout for discounts or offers.']
  selected_fulfillment_slot_end timestamp [note: 'End time of the delivery or pickup time slot selected by the customer.']
  selected_fulfillment_slot_start timestamp [note: 'Start time of the delivery or pickup time slot selected by the customer.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the checkout session in seconds, from initiation to completion or abandonment.']
  session_number varchar [note: 'Business-friendly unique identifier for the checkout session, used for customer service and tracking.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checkout session. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or abandonment.']
  snap_eligible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of items in the cart that are eligible for SNAP benefits payment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or platform that captured this checkout session (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, custom eCommerce platform).']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text special instructions or notes provided by the customer for order fulfillment (e.g., delivery instructions, item preferences).']
  step_address_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer completed the delivery/pickup address step in the checkout funnel.']
  step_fulfillment_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer completed the fulfillment method and time slot selection step.']
  step_payment_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer completed the payment information entry step.']
  step_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer completed the order review and confirmation step.']
  substitution_preference varchar [note: 'Customer preference for item substitutions if ordered items are out of stock during fulfillment.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of all items in the cart before discounts, taxes, and fees are applied.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sales tax calculated and applied to the order during checkout.']
  tip_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Optional tip amount entered by the customer for delivery or pickup service.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final total amount after all discounts, taxes, fees, and tips are applied. Net amount the customer will pay.']
  total_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct line items in the cart at checkout initiation.']
  total_unit_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total quantity of all units across all items in the cart at checkout.']
  wic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether WIC benefits were selected as a payment tender during checkout.']

  Note: 'Records the checkout funnel event for a digital order, from cart submission through payment authorization. Captures checkout initiation timestamp, steps completed (address, fulfillment slot, payment), step abandonment point if applicable, applied loyalty points, coupon codes validated, EBT/SNAP tender flag, total before/after discounts, tax amount, and checkout completion status. Integrates with OMS for order routing.'
}

Table product.product_order {
  product_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store, Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), or Distribution Center (DC) assigned to fulfill this order.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Needed for order‑level analytics and campaign performance dashboards; promo_code_applied is a denormalized string.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed this digital order.']
  age_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order contains age-restricted items (alcohol, tobacco, certain medications) requiring customer age verification at fulfillment.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for order cancellation.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was cancelled, if applicable.']
  channel_source varchar [note: 'The digital channel through which the customer placed the order (web storefront, mobile app, in-store kiosk, voice assistant, chatbot, or third-party marketplace).']
  contactless_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested contactless delivery (leave at door, no signature required).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this order record was first created in the database.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this order (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  customer_feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text customer feedback or comments about the order experience.']
  customer_rating varchar [note: 'Customer satisfaction rating for the order experience on a scale of 1 to 5 stars.']
  delivery_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged for delivery or fulfillment service. Zero for in-store pickup orders.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the order including promotions, coupons, and loyalty rewards.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the order will be fulfilled and delivered to the customer. Click-and-Collect refers to in-store pickup, curbside pickup is contactless vehicle-side handoff, last-mile delivery is same-day home delivery, ship-to-home is standard shipping, and locker pickup is automated pickup point.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this order record was last updated.']
  loyalty_card_number varchar [note: 'Customer loyalty card number applied to this order for points accrual and member pricing.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Total loyalty program points earned by the customer for this order.']
  oms_order_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the Order Management System (OMS) used for routing and orchestration across fulfillment systems.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique order number displayed to customers and used for customer service inquiries. Generated by the Order Management System (OMS).']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the digital order in the fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|picking|packed|ready_for_pickup|out_for_delivery|delivered|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument type used for this order. EBT refers to Electronic Benefits Transfer for SNAP/WIC programs.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment processing for this order.']
  placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the customer submitted the order through the digital channel. This is the principal business event timestamp for the order lifecycle.']
  prescription_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order includes pharmacy prescription items requiring special handling and HIPAA compliance.']
  promised_fulfillment_date date [note: 'The date promised to the customer for order fulfillment (pickup or delivery).']
  promised_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'The end of the time window promised to the customer for order pickup or delivery.']
  promised_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'The start of the time window promised to the customer for order pickup or delivery.']
  snap_eligible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of SNAP-eligible items in the order that can be paid using Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that originated this order record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, custom eCommerce platform, mobile app backend).']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for customer-provided delivery or fulfillment instructions (e.g., gate code, preferred substitutions, contactless delivery preferences).']
  substitution_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has allowed product substitutions if ordered items are out of stock.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of all order line items before discounts, taxes, and fees.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sales tax amount charged on the order.']
  tip_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Optional gratuity amount provided by the customer for delivery or pickup service.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final total amount charged to the customer including subtotal, taxes, fees, tips, minus discounts.']
  total_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct line items (SKUs) in the order.']
  total_unit_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total quantity of all units across all line items in the order (sum of quantities).']
  wic_eligible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of WIC-eligible items in the order that can be paid using WIC vouchers or EBT.']

  Note: 'Master record for an online order placed through any digital channel (web, mobile, kiosk). Captures order number, channel source, fulfillment type (click-and-collect, curbside pickup, last-mile delivery, ship-to-home), order status, placed timestamp, promised fulfillment window, store or MFC assigned for fulfillment, total amount, payment status, and OMS routing reference. SSOT for digital order identity within the commerce domain; physical execution is owned by the order and fulfillment domains.'
}

Table product.fulfillment_slot {
  fulfillment_slot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fulfillment slot. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operations user who performed the capacity override.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Store or MFC (Micro-Fulfillment Center) where this fulfillment slot is available.']
  available_capacity varchar [note: 'Remaining capacity available for new bookings, calculated as total_capacity minus booked_count minus reserved_count.']
  booked_count varchar [note: 'Current number of confirmed orders assigned to this slot.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for why this slot was cancelled, if applicable.']
  capacity_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the slot capacity was manually adjusted by operations staff.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital commerce channel through which this slot is made available for booking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for slot fee amount.']
  cutoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Deadline timestamp after which new bookings for this slot are no longer accepted.']
  delivery_zone_code varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or service area code for delivery slots, used for routing and capacity planning.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Type of fulfillment service this slot supports: click-and-collect pickup, curbside pickup, last-mile delivery (LMD), or pharmacy prescription pickup.']
  holiday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this slot falls on a holiday or high-demand promotional period.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this slot record was last updated.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for manual capacity override, if applicable.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Customer segment or priority level that has preferential access to this slot.']
  reserved_count varchar [note: 'Number of slots currently held in reservation (not yet confirmed) during checkout sessions.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier or speed commitment associated with this slot.']
  slot_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this slot was closed to new bookings, either due to reaching capacity or manual closure.']
  slot_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this slot record was first created in the system.']
  slot_date date [note: 'Calendar date for which this fulfillment slot is scheduled.']
  slot_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the fulfillment window in minutes.']
  slot_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fulfillment slot window ends.']
  slot_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for booking this slot, if applicable.']
  slot_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or special instructions related to this slot.']
  slot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the slot, used in customer-facing displays and operational tracking.']
  slot_published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this slot was made visible and bookable to customers.']
  slot_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fulfillment slot window begins.']
  slot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fulfillment slot indicating availability for new bookings.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that created or manages this slot record, such as OMS (Order Management System) or Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  surge_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether surge or dynamic pricing is applied to this slot due to high demand.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control requirement for this slot, relevant for perishable and frozen goods fulfillment.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders that can be assigned to this slot.']

  Note: 'Scheduled time windows available for click-and-collect, curbside pickup, or last-mile delivery fulfillment at a given store or MFC. Captures slot date, start/end time, fulfillment type, location, total capacity (max orders), booked count, available capacity, cutoff time for new bookings, and slot status (open, full, closed, cancelled). Also manages individual slot reservations linking a checkout session or order to a specific slot, including reservation timestamp, reservation status (held, confirmed, released, expired), hold expiry timestamp, and release reason. Drives the slot selection UI in checkout and manages capacity contention during high-demand periods such as holidays and promotional events. This is the commerce-layer view of slot availability and reservation for the digital shopping experience; physical fulfillment execution is owned by the fulfillment domain.'
}

Table product.slot_reservation {
  slot_reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the slot reservation record. Primary key.']
  fulfillment_slot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fulfillment slot being reserved.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order associated with this slot reservation.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopper who made the reservation.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store location where the fulfillment slot is located.']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Digital session identifier linking the reservation to the customer\'s checkout session for analytics and troubleshooting.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reservation was cancelled by the customer or system.']
  capacity_units_reserved varchar [note: 'Number of capacity units consumed by this reservation within the slot\'s total capacity. Used for slot inventory management.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The digital channel through which the slot reservation was made.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reservation transitioned from held to confirmed status, typically after successful payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this record was first created in the data platform.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the slot fee amount.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to make the reservation, used for fraud detection and customer experience analytics.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'The method of order fulfillment associated with this slot reservation.']
  hold_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The length of time in seconds that the slot was held before confirmation or expiration. Used for checkout flow optimization.']
  hold_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a held reservation will automatically expire if not confirmed. Used to manage slot inventory contention during high-demand periods.']
  holiday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserved slot falls on a holiday or peak shopping period, used for capacity planning and pricing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this record was most recently updated in the data platform.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer about the reservation status.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a confirmation or reminder notification was sent to the customer for this reservation.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Customer priority level for slot allocation, used during high-demand periods to prioritize loyal or premium customers.']
  release_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason why the slot reservation was released or cancelled. Used for operational analytics and slot inventory optimization.']
  release_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the slot was released, providing additional context beyond the reason code.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the slot was released back to available inventory.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the slot reservation, used for customer communication and tracking.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the slot reservation. Held indicates temporary hold during checkout, confirmed indicates successful booking, released indicates slot returned to inventory, expired indicates hold timeout, cancelled indicates customer cancellation.']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the slot reservation was initially created. Principal business event timestamp.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The speed or priority tier of the fulfillment service for this reservation.']
  slot_date date [note: 'The calendar date of the reserved fulfillment slot.']
  slot_end_time timestamp [note: 'The ending time of the reserved fulfillment window.']
  slot_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged for reserving this specific slot, if applicable. May vary based on demand, time, or service level.']
  slot_start_time timestamp [note: 'The beginning time of the reserved fulfillment window.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this slot reservation record, used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Customer-provided notes or special requests related to the slot reservation and fulfillment.']
  surge_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether surge pricing was applied to this slot reservation due to high demand or holiday periods.']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking a digital order or checkout session to a specific fulfillment slot. Captures reservation timestamp, reserved slot reference, shopper identity, order reference, reservation status (held, confirmed, released, expired), hold expiry timestamp, and release reason if applicable. Manages slot inventory contention during high-demand periods such as holiday or promotional events.'
}

Table product.payment_event {
  payment_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_event']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original authorization transaction for capture, void, or refund events. Links related payment events in the transaction chain.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital commerce order that initiated this payment event. Links payment event to the order header in the digital shopping flow.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who initiated this payment. Links to the customer master for analytics and personalization.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital storefront where this payment event occurred. Links to the commerce platform configuration.']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the customer\'s digital session during which this payment event occurred. Used for conversion funnel analysis.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Approval code returned by the payment processor or issuing bank when the transaction is authorized. Used for settlement reconciliation.']
  avs_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code from the Address Verification System indicating whether the billing address provided matches the address on file with the card issuer.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Card network or brand for card-based payments (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover). Null for non-card tender types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment event record was first created in the commerce system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  cvv_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code indicating whether the CVV/CVC security code provided matches the card issuer\'s records. Used for fraud prevention.']
  decline_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason the transaction was declined by the processor or issuer (insufficient funds, invalid card, fraud suspicion, etc.).']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the device used to initiate the payment. Used for fraud detection and device fingerprinting.']
  ebt_balance_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining balance on the EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) card after this transaction. Null for non-EBT transactions.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent by automated fraud detection systems.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by fraud detection system indicating the likelihood this transaction is fraudulent. Higher scores indicate higher risk.']
  gift_card_balance_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining balance on the gift card after this transaction. Null for non-gift-card tender types.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Internet Protocol address of the device that initiated the payment transaction. Used for fraud detection and geolocation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment event record was last updated. Tracks changes to transaction status or settlement information.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed as part of this payment transaction. Null if no points were used.']
  merchant_account_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the merchant account used to process this transaction. Multiple accounts may be used for different brands or regions.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel or interface through which the payment was initiated (web storefront, mobile app, mobile web, in-store kiosk, call center).']
  payment_gateway varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the payment gateway or processor that handled this transaction (e.g., Stripe, Adyen, Cybersource).']
  payment_token_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the tokenized payment instrument in the payment domain. The payment domain is the authoritative SSOT for tokenization and PCI compliance.']
  processor_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code returned by the payment gateway or processor indicating the outcome of the transaction attempt (approved, declined, error codes).']
  processor_response_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable message from the payment processor describing the transaction outcome or reason for decline.']
  processor_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment processor or gateway. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  retry_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which retry attempt this is for the same payment. First attempt is 1, subsequent retries increment.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the transaction was settled and funds were transferred. Null for unsettled transactions.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Status of funds settlement with the payment processor. Indicates whether the transaction has been finalized and funds transferred.']
  snap_eligible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Portion of the transaction amount that qualifies for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits. Null for non-SNAP transactions.']
  tender_type varchar [note: 'Payment instrument type used for this transaction. Includes credit card, debit card, EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer), SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women Infants and Children Program), loyalty points, or gift card.']
  three_d_secure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3D Secure authentication (Verified by Visa, Mastercard SecureCode) was used for this transaction. Enhances security and shifts liability.']
  three_d_secure_version varchar [note: 'Version of 3D Secure protocol used (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 2.1). Null if 3D Secure was not used.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of this payment event. For authorizations and captures, this is the gross amount; for refunds, this is the amount being returned.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment transaction event in its lifecycle. Tracks progression from initiation through settlement or failure.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the payment event was initiated in the digital commerce flow. Primary business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment event: authorization (hold funds), capture (settle funds), void (cancel authorization), refund (return funds), partial_refund (return portion), or reversal (undo transaction).']
  wic_eligible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Portion of the transaction amount that qualifies for WIC (Women Infants and Children Program) benefits. Null for non-WIC transactions.']

  Note: 'Commerce-layer record of each payment authorization, capture, void, or refund event initiated through the digital shopping flow. Captures transaction type, amount, currency, tender type (credit, debit, EBT, SNAP, WIC, loyalty points, gift card), authorization code, processor response code, transaction timestamp, digital order reference, payment token reference (FK to payment domain), and settlement status. This product exists solely for checkout UX state management, order confirmation display, and digital channel conversion analytics. The payment domain is the authoritative SSOT for payment processing lifecycle, tokenization, PCI compliance, and settlement — commerce does not duplicate that responsibility.'
}

Table product.search_query {
  search_query_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each product search query event executed by a shopper on a digital storefront.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authenticated shopper who executed the search. Null for guest users.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical store selected by the shopper for fulfillment context, if applicable to the search session.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital storefront where the search was executed.']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the shopper session during which the search was performed, enabling session-level analytics and journey tracking.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'The A/B test variant or experiment group assigned to this search event, enabling controlled testing of search algorithms and merchandising strategies.']
  added_to_cart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the shopper added any product to their cart as a result of this search, measuring search conversion effectiveness.']
  autocomplete_suggestion varchar [note: 'The autocomplete suggestion selected by the shopper, if applicable.']
  autocomplete_used_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the shopper selected a suggestion from the autocomplete dropdown rather than typing the full query.']
  clicked_position varchar [note: 'The position (rank) of the clicked product in the search results list, used to measure relevance quality.']
  clicked_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of the first product clicked by the shopper from the search results, if any.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this search query record was created in the system.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the shopper to perform the search.']
  filter_brand_applied varchar [note: 'The brand filter applied by the shopper to narrow search results, if any.']
  filter_category_applied varchar [note: 'The product category filter applied by the shopper to narrow search results, if any.']
  filter_dietary_applied varchar [note: 'Dietary or nutritional filters applied by the shopper (e.g., organic, gluten-free, vegan, low-sodium) to narrow search results.']
  filter_price_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum price threshold applied by the shopper as a filter, if any.']
  filter_price_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum price threshold applied by the shopper as a filter, if any.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the device from which the search was executed, used for fraud detection and geographic analytics.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'The locale or language setting of the shopper at the time of search (e.g., en-US, es-MX), affecting search relevance and merchandising.']
  normalized_query_string varchar [note: 'The search query after normalization processing (lowercased, stemmed, stop-words removed) used for relevance matching and analytics.']
  personalization_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether personalized ranking or filtering was applied to the search results based on shopper profile or behavior.']
  query_refined_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the shopper refined or modified the search query after viewing initial results, signaling potential relevance issues.']
  query_string varchar [note: 'The raw search text entered by the shopper, capturing the exact keywords or phrases used to find products.']
  refined_query_string varchar [note: 'The subsequent search query entered by the shopper if they refined their original search.']
  result_clicked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the shopper clicked on any product from the search results, measuring search effectiveness.']
  result_count varchar [note: 'The total number of product results returned by the search engine for this query.']
  search_channel varchar [note: 'The digital channel through which the search was executed (e.g., web browser, mobile app, mobile web, in-store kiosk).']
  search_engine_version varchar [note: 'The version of the search engine or algorithm used to process this query, enabling A/B testing and performance tracking.']
  search_method varchar [note: 'The method used by the shopper to initiate the search (e.g., text input, voice search, barcode scan, image search).']
  search_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'The time in milliseconds taken by the search engine to return results, used for performance monitoring.']
  search_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the shopper executed the search query.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'The sort order selected by the shopper to organize search results (e.g., relevance, price ascending, price descending, newest arrivals). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: relevance|price_low_to_high|price_high_to_low|newest|best_selling|customer_rating|alphabetical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The source system or platform that captured this search event (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, custom eCommerce platform).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The user agent string of the browser or application used to perform the search, providing device and browser details.']
  zero_results_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the search returned no results, critical for identifying search relevance gaps and merchandising opportunities.']

  Note: 'Captures each product search event executed by a shopper on a digital storefront. Records query string, storefront, session reference, timestamp, result count returned, zero-results flag, applied filters (category, brand, dietary), sort order selected, and whether the shopper clicked a result or refined the query. Supports search relevance tuning, zero-result remediation, and digital merchandising decisions.'
}

Table product.product_recommendation {
  product_recommendation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product recommendation record. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category to which the recommended product belongs.']
  merchandising_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandising or promotional campaign associated with this recommendation, if applicable.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order in which the recommended product was purchased, if conversion occurred.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shopper to whom the recommendation was presented.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital storefront where the recommendation was displayed.']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the digital browsing session during which the recommendation was presented.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant or experiment group to which this recommendation belongs, enabling testing of recommendation strategies.']
  add_to_cart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper added the recommended product to their cart (True if added, False otherwise).']
  add_to_cart_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the recommended product was added to the cart, if applicable.']
  algorithm varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the personalization algorithm or strategy used to generate the recommendation.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The brand name of the recommended product.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The digital channel through which the recommendation was delivered to the shopper.']
  click_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper clicked on the recommended product (True if clicked, False otherwise).']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shopper clicked on the recommendation, if applicable.']
  context varchar [note: 'The page or context within the digital storefront where the recommendation was displayed.']
  control_group_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recommendation was part of a control group in an A/B test (True for control, False for treatment).']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper purchased the recommended product (True if purchased, False otherwise).']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the recommended product was purchased, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this recommendation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the display price.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the shopper when the recommendation was presented.']
  display_position varchar [note: 'The ordinal position where the recommendation was displayed on the page (1 for first position, 2 for second, etc.).']
  display_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The price of the recommended product as displayed to the shopper at the time of the recommendation.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the recommendation is no longer considered valid or relevant for tracking purposes.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude coordinate of the shoppers location at the time of the recommendation, if available.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The longitude coordinate of the shoppers location at the time of the recommendation, if available.']
  impression_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the recommendation was displayed to the shopper.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the shopper at the time of the recommendation impression, used for fraud detection and analytics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this recommendation record was last updated.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the machine learning model or recommendation engine that generated this recommendation.']
  promotion_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a promotional discount was applied to the recommended product at the time of display.']
  recommendation_number varchar [note: 'Business-friendly reference number for the recommendation event, used for tracking and reporting.']
  recommendation_source varchar [note: 'The origin or system that generated the recommendation, distinguishing between automated and manual recommendations.']
  recommendation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recommendation indicating shopper interaction level.']
  recommendation_type varchar [note: 'The category or methodology of the recommendation strategy employed.']
  recommended_product_name varchar [note: 'The display name of the recommended product as shown to the shopper.']
  recommended_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of the product that was recommended to the shopper.']
  score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Confidence or relevance score assigned by the recommendation engine, indicating predicted likelihood of shopper interest.']
  time_on_page_seconds varchar [note: 'The number of seconds the shopper spent on the page where the recommendation was displayed.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string captured at the time of the recommendation impression.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a product recommendation presented to a shopper on a digital storefront, generated by the personalization engine. Captures recommendation context (homepage, PDP, cart, checkout), recommended SKU, recommendation algorithm/model version, display position, impression timestamp, click flag, add-to-cart flag, and conversion flag. Enables A/B testing of recommendation strategies and measurement of digital merchandising lift.'
}

Table product.order_status_event {
  order_status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order_status_event']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the specific actor (associate ID, shopper ID, system process ID, or carrier ID) who triggered this status transition.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery carrier or logistics provider associated with this status event. Applicable for delivery and shipping fulfillment channels.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital order that experienced this status transition. Links to the parent order header.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'The store, Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), or Distribution Center (DC) where this status transition occurred.']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'The application session identifier during which this status transition was recorded. Used for technical troubleshooting and audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this event record was first created in the system. This is the system record creation time, distinct from the business event_timestamp.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the mobile device or terminal that recorded this status transition event. Used for audit and troubleshooting.']
  dwell_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes the order spent in the previous status before this transition. Used for operational efficiency analysis and bottleneck identification.']
  estimated_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The estimated date and time when the order will reach its final delivered or ready_for_pickup state. Updated dynamically as the order progresses.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the status transition event occurred in the order lifecycle. This is the business event time, not the system record creation time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The specific status transition type that occurred. Represents the new state the order moved into during this event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: placed|confirmed|picking_started|ready_for_pickup|out_for_delivery|delivered|cancelled|refund_initiated — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'The standardized code representing the type of exception that occurred during this status transition. Null if no exception occurred.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exception or issue that occurred during this status transition. Null if no exception occurred.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition was associated with an operational exception or issue requiring attention.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The order fulfillment method associated with this status event. Indicates whether the order is for curbside pickup, last-mile delivery (LMD), or ship-to-home.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The geographic latitude coordinate where this status transition occurred. Captured from mobile devices for delivery events.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The geographic longitude coordinate where this status transition occurred. Captured from mobile devices for delivery events.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'The type of fulfillment location where this status transition occurred. MFC refers to Micro-Fulfillment Center, DC refers to Distribution Center.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The order status after this transition event. Matches the event_type in most cases.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text notes or comments recorded by the actor at the time of this status transition. Used for operational context and issue documentation.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the shopper of this status transition. Push refers to mobile app push notification.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification was successfully sent for this status transition event. Used for shopper app real-time tracking.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer notification was sent for this status event. Null if no notification was sent.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The order status immediately before this transition event. Null for the initial \'placed\' event.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition occurred within the SLA target window. Used for operational SLA breach detection and performance monitoring.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The target date and time by which this status transition should have occurred to meet the service level agreement commitment to the customer.']
  sla_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'The difference in minutes between the actual event timestamp and the SLA target timestamp. Negative values indicate early completion, positive values indicate SLA breach.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or code of the operational system that generated this status event record. Examples include OMS (Order Management System), WMS (Warehouse Management System), TMS (Transportation Management System).']
  source_system_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'The unique transaction or event identifier from the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility. Applicable for out_for_delivery and delivered events.']
  triggered_by varchar [note: 'The actor type that initiated this status transition. Indicates whether the event was automated or human-initiated.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log of each status transition for a digital order, supporting shopper notifications and operational SLA tracking. Captures event type (placed, confirmed, picking_started, ready_for_pickup, out_for_delivery, delivered, cancelled, refund_initiated), event timestamp, triggered_by (system, associate, shopper), store or MFC location, and notification sent flag. Enables real-time order tracking in the shopper app and SLA breach detection.'
}

Table product.channel_config {
  channel_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for channel_config']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital catalog assigned to this channel. Determines which products are available for browsing and purchase.']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price book used for this channel. Determines pricing rules and promotional pricing logic.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital storefront that this channel configuration applies to. Links to the storefront master data.']
  accepted_tender_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment tender types accepted on this channel (e.g., credit_card, debit_card, gift_card, paypal, apple_pay, google_pay, ebt, snap, wic).']
  age_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether age verification is required for purchases on this channel (e.g., for alcohol, tobacco, pharmacy items).']
  api_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Base API endpoint URL for this channel. Used by mobile apps and integrations to connect to backend services.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the digital channel (e.g., WEB_US, IOS_APP, ANDROID_APP, KIOSK).']
  channel_config_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the digital channel configuration.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the digital channel (e.g., Web Storefront, iOS Mobile App, Android Mobile App, In-Store Kiosk).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the digital channel platform type.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Customer support email address for inquiries related to this channel.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Customer support phone number for inquiries related to this channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel configuration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for pricing and transactions on this channel (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  ebt_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel accepts Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) payments for government assistance programs.']
  feature_toggles varchar [note: 'JSON or comma-separated list of feature flags and their enabled/disabled state for this channel (e.g., enable_voice_search=true, enable_ar_preview=false). Drives runtime behavior.']
  fulfillment_methods_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fulfillment methods available on this channel (e.g., delivery, pickup, curbside, in_store, ship_to_home). Drives OMS routing logic.']
  guest_checkout_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers can complete purchases without creating an account (guest checkout) on this channel.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel configuration record was last updated.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the digital channel was first made available to customers.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Locale identifier for language and regional formatting (e.g., en_US, es_MX, fr_CA). Drives localization behavior.']
  loyalty_program_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program integrated with this channel. Null if no loyalty integration.']
  loyalty_program_integrated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loyalty program is integrated and active on this channel, allowing customers to earn and redeem points.']
  maximum_cart_item_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of distinct line items (SKUs) allowed in a single cart on this channel. Enforces cart size limits.']
  minimum_age_required varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to use this channel or purchase age-restricted items. Typically 18 or 21 depending on jurisdiction and product category.']
  minimum_order_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum order subtotal required for checkout on this channel. Used to enforce order minimums for delivery or pickup.']
  personalization_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personalized product recommendations and content are enabled on this channel.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Operating system or platform on which the channel operates (iOS, Android, Web Desktop, Web Mobile, Windows, Linux).']
  promotion_engine_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion engine is active on this channel, allowing application of discounts, coupons, and promotional offers.']
  search_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether product search functionality is enabled on this channel.']
  sla_target_delivery_hours varchar [note: 'Target time in hours for order delivery from order placement to customer receipt for this channel. Used for service level commitments.']
  sla_target_order_processing_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for order processing from submission to fulfillment assignment for this channel. Used for performance monitoring.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel accepts SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) payments.']
  substitution_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether product substitutions are allowed by default on this channel when items are out of stock during fulfillment.']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the digital channel will be or was decommissioned. Null for active channels with no planned sunset.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code for calculating sales tax on transactions in this channel. Used for tax compliance.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the channel (e.g., America/New_York, America/Los_Angeles). Used for scheduling and time display.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel accepts WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program vouchers or EBT.']

  Note: 'Configuration master for each digital channel (web, iOS app, Android app, kiosk), capturing feature toggles, supported fulfillment methods, minimum order amounts, accepted tender types, loyalty integration flags, age-verification requirements, SNAP/EBT acceptance flag, maximum cart item count, and channel-specific SLA targets. Drives runtime behavior of the Salesforce Commerce Cloud storefront and OMS routing logic.'
}

Table product.product_item {
  product_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product item. Primary key for the product_item entity.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.brand']
  created_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Item Master Data creation audit requires tracking which associate entered the new product_item; used in compliance and merchandising performance reports.']
  fuel_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_fuel_grade. Business justification: Fuel grade is a product attribute for fuel items; FK enables lookup.']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Product items belong to a hierarchy; linking provides parent classification.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Tracks which legal entity owns/liabilities for a product, needed for entity‑level financial reporting and compliance.']
  plu_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plu_code. Business justification: PLU codes are managed centrally; replace string with FK.']
  primary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Sourcing teams designate a primary supplier per item for purchase order generation and cost negotiations.']
  age_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item requires age verification at point-of-sale (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, certain pharmacy items).']
  allergen_information varchar [note: 'List of major allergens contained in or potentially cross-contaminated with the product (e.g., milk, eggs, peanuts, tree nuts, soy, wheat, fish, shellfish). Required for FDA labeling compliance.']
  case_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of sellable units contained in one case. Used for warehouse receiving, putaway, and replenishment planning.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Unit cost paid to the supplier or manufacturer. Used for margin calculation and financial reporting.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the item was manufactured or grown. Required for customs and labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this item record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this item record was discontinued or became inactive. Null for currently active items.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this item record became active and available for ordering and sale.']
  fda_labeling_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item requires FDA-compliant nutrition facts labeling and ingredient disclosure.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number assigned by GS1 for international product identification. May be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits depending on packaging level.']
  haccp_classification varchar [note: 'HACCP risk classification for food safety management. Identifies critical control points for temperature, handling, and contamination prevention.']
  height_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the item package in inches. Used for planogram design and space allocation.']
  ingredient_statement varchar [note: 'Complete list of ingredients in descending order by weight. Required for FDA-regulated food products.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Full business description of the sellable item as it appears in merchandising systems and customer-facing channels.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the item in the product catalog. Determines availability for ordering and replenishment.']
  item_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the item by department or operational area. Determines handling, storage, and regulatory requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this item record was last updated in the system.']
  length_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the item package in inches. Used for planogram design and space allocation.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or supplier who produces the item. Used for vendor management and sourcing.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to purchase this item. Typically 18 or 21 for age-restricted products.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified organic by USDA or equivalent certifying body.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Size or quantity of the product package (e.g., 12 oz, 1 gallon, 6-pack). Used for merchandising and pricing.']
  plu_code varchar [note: 'Price Look-Up code used primarily for fresh produce and bulk items at point-of-sale. Typically 4 or 5 digits.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is a store-owned private-label brand product rather than a national CPG brand.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the item remains sellable from receipt date. Critical for perishable inventory management and shrink reduction.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal Stock Keeping Unit code used for inventory management, replenishment, and warehouse operations. Unique within the retailer\'s system.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is eligible for purchase using SNAP/EBT benefits.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this item master data originates (e.g., Oracle Retail, SAP MM).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this item in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  suggested_retail_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manufacturer\'s suggested retail price for the item. Used as a reference for pricing and promotional planning.']
  taxable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sales tax applies to this item at point-of-sale. Tax treatment varies by jurisdiction and item category.']
  temperature_class varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature classification for the item. Critical for cold chain management and HACCP compliance.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for inventory tracking and ordering. Standard units include each, case, pound, ounce, gallon, liter, kilogram, gram. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pound|ounce|gallon|liter|kilogram|gram — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code used for point-of-sale scanning and inventory tracking. Primary barcode identifier for CPG products.']
  usda_grade varchar [note: 'USDA quality grade for meat, poultry, dairy, and produce items (e.g., Prime, Choice, Select for beef; Grade A for eggs).']
  weight_pounds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the item in pounds. Used for shipping cost calculation and transportation planning.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is approved for purchase under the WIC program.']
  width_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the item package in inches. Used for planogram design and space allocation.']

  Note: 'Core master record and single source of truth for every sellable item in the Grocery catalog — CPG products, private-label brands, fresh produce, pharmacy drug items, and fuel grades. Stores the authoritative item description, brand reference, pack size, unit of measure, item status (active/discontinued/seasonal), item type (grocery/produce/pharmacy/fuel), temperature class (ambient/refrigerated/frozen), HACCP classification, FDA labeling flag, organic certification flag, USDA grade, private-label indicator, and source system reference. Extended by sku (identification codes and sellable units), drug_item (pharmacy attributes), fuel_grade (fuel center attributes), item_attribute (extensible regulatory/compliance flags), and nutritional_info (FDA nutrition/allergen data). Sourced from Oracle Retail Item Management and SAP MM Material Master.'
}

Table product.sku {
  sku_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SKU record. Primary key.']
  created_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: SKU generation is performed by a merchandising associate; tracking creator supports audit of SKU assignments and error resolution.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent item master record in the product catalog. Links SKU to the base product definition.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: SKU master data includes a default supplier to streamline PO creation and inventory replenishment.']
  alcohol_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is an alcoholic beverage requiring age verification and special licensing. True for beer, wine, and spirits.']
  barcode_type varchar [note: 'Type of barcode symbology used for this SKU. Determines scanning compatibility and data capacity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UPC-A|UPC-E|EAN-13|EAN-8|GTIN-14|GS1-128|GS1 DataBar — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  case_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of selling units contained in a case pack for receiving and distribution. Used for warehouse replenishment and DSD deliveries.']
  catch_weight_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is sold by variable weight with price calculated at time of sale. True for meat, seafood, deli, and produce items sold by weight.']
  check_digit varchar [note: 'Single-digit checksum calculated from the barcode to ensure scanning accuracy. Validates UPC, EAN, and GTIN integrity at POS and receiving.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of produce or bulk commodity type per IFPS standards. Used for PLU assignment and produce category management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SKU record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and new item tracking.']
  ean_code varchar [note: '13-digit EAN-13 barcode identifier used internationally. Superset of UPC, used for global product identification and cross-border trade.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU is discontinued or removed from active assortment. Null for ongoing items. Used for markdown planning and inventory liquidation.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU becomes active and available for sale. Used for new item introductions and seasonal assortment planning.']
  fda_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is subject to FDA labeling and safety regulations. True for food, beverage, dietary supplements, and OTC drugs.']
  gs1_company_prefix varchar [note: 'GS1-assigned company prefix portion of the GTIN, identifying the brand owner or manufacturer. Used for barcode validation and supplier identification.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit GTIN identifier used for case and pallet level tracking in supply chain and distribution. Includes packaging hierarchy information.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, or transportation. True for items with flammable, corrosive, or toxic components.']
  inner_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of selling units contained in an inner pack within a case. Used for shelf replenishment and inventory control.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SKU record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  organic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is certified organic per USDA standards. True for products meeting organic certification requirements. Corresponds to PLU codes starting with 9.']
  plu_code varchar [note: '4 or 5-digit PLU code used for fresh produce, bulk items, and variable-weight products. Follows IFPS standard. 5-digit codes starting with 9 indicate organic products.']
  primary_barcode_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this barcode is the primary identifier for POS scanning. True if this is the default barcode, False for alternate barcodes.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is a store-owned brand product. True for private label, False for national brands and CPG products.']
  retailer_item_number varchar [note: 'Internal item number assigned by the retailer for merchandising and procurement purposes. May differ from SKU code in multi-location or multi-pack scenarios.']
  selling_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for customer-facing sales transactions. Determines how the item is priced and sold at POS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EACH|POUND|OUNCE|KILOGRAM|GRAM|LITER|GALLON|CASE|PACK — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the product remains sellable from receipt date. Critical for perishable inventory management, markdown planning, and FIFO rotation.']
  size_grade varchar [note: 'Size or grade classification for produce and fresh items. Examples: Small, Medium, Large, Extra Large, Grade A. Used for pricing and quality differentiation.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Retailer-assigned unique alphanumeric code representing a specific item-pack-location combination for inventory and sales tracking. This is the primary business identifier used across POS, WMS, and merchandising systems.']
  sku_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SKU. Active items are available for sale and replenishment. Inactive items are temporarily unavailable. Discontinued items are permanently removed from assortment.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is eligible for purchase using SNAP benefits (EBT). True for qualifying food items per USDA guidelines.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage and transportation temperature zone for this SKU. Critical for cold chain management and warehouse slotting.']
  tobacco_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is a tobacco product requiring age verification and regulatory compliance. True for cigarettes, cigars, and vaping products.']
  upc_code varchar [note: '12-digit UPC-A barcode identifier used for POS scanning and inventory tracking. Primary barcode for most CPG products in North America.']
  variable_weight_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU has variable weight for inventory tracking purposes. True for items where each unit may have different weight.']
  variety varchar [note: 'Specific variety or sub-type of the commodity. Examples: Gala apples, Roma tomatoes, Russet potatoes. Used for produce differentiation and customer selection.']
  vendor_item_number varchar [note: 'Supplier or manufacturer assigned item number used for ordering, receiving, and EDI transactions. Critical for cross-referencing purchase orders and invoices.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is approved for WIC program purchases. True for items meeting WIC nutritional and category requirements.']

  Note: 'Retailer-assigned Stock Keeping Unit representing a unique item-pack-location combination for inventory and sales tracking. Stores SKU code, parent item reference, retailer item number, vendor item number, case pack quantity, inner pack quantity, selling unit of measure, catch-weight flag, variable-weight flag, shelf life days, and SKU status (active/inactive/discontinued). Serves as the authoritative single source of truth for all item identification codes including UPC-A, UPC-E, EAN-13, GTIN-14, GS1 DataBar barcodes (with barcode type, barcode value, GS1 company prefix, check digit, primary barcode flag, effective date range) and PLU codes (IFPS standard PLU, commodity type, variety, organic flag per 9-prefix convention, size/grade). Bridges the item master to store-level assortment, inventory positions, and POS transactions. Supports POS scanning, receiving, EDI transactions, manual PLU entry for produce and bulk items, and multi-barcode lookup for items with multiple packaging configurations. Sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System and IFPS.'
}

Table product.item_hierarchy {
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each node in the product classification hierarchy. Primary key.']
  owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Hierarchy ownership determines planogram and space allocation; associate owner is tracked for labor planning and audit.']
  parent_node_item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent node in the hierarchy tree. Null for top-level department nodes. Enables recursive hierarchy traversal and roll-up calculations.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead vendor responsible for category management, assortment recommendations, and planogram design for this hierarchy node. Typically assigned at category or subcategory level.']
  age_restriction_years varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to purchase items in this hierarchy node. Applies to alcohol, tobacco, and other age-restricted products. Enforced at POS with ID verification.']
  average_unit_retail_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average retail price per unit for items in this hierarchy node. Used in pricing strategy, competitive analysis, and ASP (Average Selling Price) reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dsd_category_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are typically delivered via DSD (Direct Store Delivery) rather than through DC (Distribution Center). Affects receiving processes and vendor management.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node was discontinued or retired. Null for active nodes. Used for historical reporting and category lifecycle analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node became active and available for use in assortment planning, reporting, and SKU assignment. Supports temporal hierarchy analysis.']
  endcap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are suitable for endcap (end-of-aisle display) placement. Used in planogram design and promotional space allocation.']
  fda_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are subject to FDA (Food and Drug Administration) regulation for food safety, labeling, or nutritional information disclosure.']
  gmroi_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) percentage for this hierarchy node. Used in merchandise planning and performance evaluation. Calculated as (Gross Margin / Average Inventory Cost) * 100.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the hierarchy tree. Typically: 1=Department, 2=Category, 3=Subcategory, 4=Segment, 5=Subsegment. Supports drill-down navigation and roll-up aggregation.']
  hierarchy_level_name varchar [note: 'Business name for the hierarchy level. Standard retail taxonomy labels used in category management and merchandise planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing.']
  leaf_node_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is a leaf (has no children). True for lowest-level nodes that directly contain SKUs (Stock Keeping Units). Used to optimize query performance.']
  minimum_facings varchar [note: 'Minimum number of product facings (product fronts visible on shelf) required for items in this hierarchy node. Used in planogram compliance and shelf stocking guidelines.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this hierarchy node. Used for audit trail and accountability in category management changes.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the hierarchy node. Used in planograms, assortment planning, and financial reporting. Examples: DEPT01, CAT-DAIRY, SUBCAT-MILK.']
  node_depth varchar [note: 'Distance from root node (0 for root). Used for hierarchy traversal algorithms and display indentation in user interfaces.']
  node_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the hierarchy node including scope, product types included, and merchandising guidelines.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the hierarchy node. Examples: Fresh Produce, Dairy Products, Organic Milk, Private Label Snacks.']
  node_path varchar [note: 'Full path from root to current node using node codes separated by forward slashes. Example: DEPT01/CAT-DAIRY/SUBCAT-MILK. Supports fast ancestor queries and breadcrumb navigation.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hierarchy node. Active nodes are used in assortment planning and reporting. Inactive nodes are retained for historical analysis.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node require USDA organic certification. Drives supplier qualification, labeling requirements, and premium pricing strategies.']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are perishable (fresh produce, dairy, meat, bakery). Drives cold chain requirements, FIFO (First In First Out) handling, and shrink management.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy node contains primarily private-label (store-owned brand) products. Used for brand mix analysis and margin optimization.']
  promotional_category_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy node is frequently featured in ad circulars, BOGO (Buy One Get One) promotions, and TPR (Temporary Price Reduction) campaigns. Used in promotional planning.']
  replenishment_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard replenishment cycle in days for items in this hierarchy node. Used in CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) and DC (Distribution Center) planning.']
  seasonality_pattern varchar [note: 'Primary seasonality pattern for demand in this hierarchy node. Used in seasonal assortment planning, inventory forecasting, and promotional calendar development.']
  shrink_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected shrink (inventory loss from theft, spoilage, or damage) rate percentage for this hierarchy node. Used in inventory valuation, loss prevention planning, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are eligible for purchase using SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) or EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) benefits. Required for POS (Point of Sale) transaction processing.']
  space_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of total store shelf space allocated to this hierarchy node. Used in planogram design and space productivity analysis. Sum of all categories should equal 100% at store level.']
  target_gross_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage for this hierarchy node. Used in pricing decisions, vendor negotiations, and financial planning. Calculated as ((Retail Price - Cost) / Retail Price) * 100.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone for storing and displaying items in this hierarchy node. Drives cold chain logistics, WMS (Warehouse Management System) slotting, and HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) compliance.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are eligible for purchase using WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits. Required for POS transaction processing and state reporting.']

  Note: 'Product classification hierarchy defining the multi-level taxonomy used for category management, merchandise planning, and financial reporting. Stores each node in the hierarchy (department, category, subcategory, segment, subsegment) with node code, node name, hierarchy level number, parent node reference, category captain vendor, GMROI target, and space allocation percentage. Supports planogram design, assortment optimization, ad circular planning, and COGS roll-up by category. Sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System.'
}

Table product.upc_barcode {
  upc_barcode_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the UPC barcode record. Primary key for the barcode registry.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU that this barcode represents. Links barcode to the master product catalog.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier who provided this barcode. Used for DSD receiving and vendor barcode compliance tracking.']
  barcode_image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the stored barcode image file used for label printing and visual verification. Supports planogram and shelf label generation.']
  barcode_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the barcode. Active barcodes are scannable at POS and receiving. Recalled barcodes trigger alerts.']
  barcode_type varchar [note: 'The barcode symbology standard used for this code. Determines the encoding format and scanning requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UPC-A|UPC-E|EAN-13|EAN-8|GTIN-14|GS1-128|GS1 DataBar — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  barcode_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The numeric barcode value as encoded in the barcode symbol. Supports UPC-A (12 digits), UPC-E (8 digits), EAN-13 (13 digits), and GTIN-14 (14 digits).']
  check_digit varchar [note: 'The calculated check digit at the end of the barcode used to validate barcode accuracy during scanning. Computed using GS1 modulo-10 algorithm.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this barcode record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date this barcode is discontinued and should no longer be accepted. Null for active barcodes with no planned discontinuation.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date this barcode becomes active and scannable in POS and warehouse systems. Supports phased product launches and barcode transitions.']
  gs1_company_prefix varchar [note: 'The GS1-assigned company prefix portion of the barcode that identifies the brand owner or manufacturer. Used for vendor identification and EDI transactions.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'The standardized Global Trade Item Number assigned by GS1. May be GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14 depending on product packaging level.']
  is_age_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product requires age verification at POS (alcohol, tobacco, pharmacy items). Triggers ID check prompt.']
  is_coupon_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this barcode is eligible for manufacturer or store coupon redemption. Used for POS coupon validation.']
  is_pharmacy_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this barcode is for a pharmacy prescription or over-the-counter drug item requiring special handling and compliance tracking.']
  is_primary_barcode boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary barcode for the SKU. Used when a product has multiple barcodes for different packaging configurations.']
