  price_tier varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of fuel grade pricing from lowest to highest (e.g., 1 for Regular, 2 for Mid-Grade, 3 for Premium). Used for pricing strategy and margin management.']
  pump_button_color varchar [note: 'Standard color coding for fuel grade selection buttons on dispensers (e.g., green for Regular, yellow for Mid-Grade, red for Premium, black for Diesel). Supports consistent customer experience across fuel centers.']
  pump_display_label varchar [note: 'Customer-facing label text displayed on fuel pump dispensers and signage. Includes grade name, octane rating, and ethanol content per FTC labeling requirements.']
  reformulated_gasoline_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the fuel meets EPA reformulated gasoline (RFG) requirements for ozone non-attainment areas. True for RFG-compliant fuels sold in designated metropolitan areas.']
  renewable_fuel_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the fuel grade qualifies as a renewable fuel under EPA Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS). True for ethanol blends, biodiesel, and other renewable fuels.']
  specific_gravity decimal(6,4) [note: 'Ratio of fuel density to water density at standard temperature. Used for volume-to-weight conversions in inventory reconciliation and delivery verification.']
  sulfur_content_ppm varchar [note: 'Sulfur concentration in parts per million. EPA regulations mandate ultra-low sulfur diesel (ULSD) at 15 ppm maximum. Required for emissions compliance reporting.']
  summer_blend_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the fuel grade is formulated for warm weather with lower Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP) to reduce evaporative emissions. Seasonal blend used from June through September.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials transportation (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline, UN1202 for diesel). Required for shipping documentation and emergency response.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for the fuel grade. Used for Point of Sale (POS) transaction processing and inventory tracking at fuel centers.']
  wholesale_product_code varchar [note: 'Supplier or terminal product code used for wholesale fuel procurement and Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) transactions. Links to vendor catalog and supply chain systems.']
  winter_blend_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the fuel grade is formulated for cold weather performance with higher Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP). Seasonal blend used from November through March in most regions.']

  Note: 'Fuel grade master defining the types of fuel sold at Grocery fuel centers. Stores fuel grade code, fuel grade name (Regular Unleaded, Mid-Grade, Premium, Diesel, E85), octane rating, ethanol blend percentage, EPA fuel type classification, pump display label, price tier, and active status. Supports fuel center pump configuration, EPA emissions compliance reporting, and fuel pricing management. Referenced by fuel inventory and fuel pricing domains.'
}

Table product.item_substitution {
  item_substitution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item substitution relationship record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the category manager or merchandising manager who approved this substitution relationship.']
  primary_product_item_id varchar [note: 'The original item that is out of stock or unavailable. References the item master catalog.']
  allergen_match_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the substitute item must have the same allergen profile as the primary item. Critical for food safety and customer health. True enforces allergen matching; False allows different allergen profiles.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this substitution relationship in the governance workflow. Draft indicates initial creation; pending_review awaits category manager approval; approved is active; rejected was denied; suspended is temporarily inactive; expired has passed its effective end date.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this substitution relationship was approved by the authorized user.']
  auto_substitute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this substitution can be applied automatically by the Order Management System (OMS) or Computer Assisted Ordering (CAO) system without manual intervention. True enables automatic substitution; False requires manual review.']
  brand_match_preference varchar [note: 'Indicates the brand matching requirement for substitution: exact (same brand only), private_label (store-owned brand preferred), national_brand (Consumer Packaged Goods brand preferred), or any (brand agnostic).']
  category_match_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the substitute item must belong to the same product category as the primary item. True enforces category matching; False allows cross-category substitution.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates which fulfillment channels this substitution rule applies to: all channels, in-store only, online only, pickup (click-and-collect), delivery (last mile delivery), or curbside pickup.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this substitution relationship record was first created in the system.']
  customer_acceptance_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of times customers accepted this substitution when offered (accepted substitutions divided by total offers). Used to measure customer satisfaction and optimize substitution rules.']
  customer_consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether explicit customer approval is required before making this substitution. True means the customer must approve the substitute before fulfillment; False means the substitution can be made automatically.']
  dietary_restriction_match_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the substitute item must match dietary restrictions of the primary item (e.g., organic, gluten-free, kosher, halal, vegan). True enforces dietary attribute matching; False allows different dietary profiles.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this substitution relationship expires and should no longer be used. Null indicates an open-ended relationship with no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this substitution relationship becomes active and can be used for order fulfillment and replenishment decisions.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this substitution was most recently applied in an order fulfillment or replenishment transaction.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this substitution relationship record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for category managers to document special instructions, business rationale, customer communication guidelines, or other relevant information about this substitution relationship.']
  pharmacy_therapeutic_equivalent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specific to pharmacy items indicating whether the substitute drug is therapeutically equivalent to the primary drug per FDA Orange Book standards. True means the substitute has the same active ingredient, strength, dosage form, and route of administration.']
  prescription_required_match_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator for pharmacy items indicating whether the substitute must have the same prescription requirement status as the primary item (both Rx or both OTC). True enforces matching; False allows substitution between prescription and over-the-counter items.']
  price_variance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable percentage difference in price between the primary item and substitute item. Positive values allow higher-priced substitutes; negative values allow lower-priced substitutes. For example, 15.00 means the substitute can cost up to 15% more.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the order of preference when multiple substitutes exist for the same primary item. Lower numbers indicate higher priority (1 = first choice, 2 = second choice, etc.).']
  size_match_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage variance in product size or quantity between the primary item and substitute item. For example, 10.00 means the substitute can be up to 10% larger or smaller than the original item.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or process that created this substitution rule: merchandising system, eCommerce platform, Computer Assisted Ordering (CAO), manual entry by category manager, or category management tool.']
  substitution_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the primary business reason for establishing this substitution relationship: out-of-stock (OOS), discontinued item, seasonal availability, product recall, quality issue, pricing strategy, or promotional campaign. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oos|discontinued|seasonal|recall|quality|pricing|promotion — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  substitution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the substitution relationship indicating whether the substitute is an equivalent product, an upgrade (higher quality or price), a downgrade (lower quality or price), similar product, alternative brand, or generic equivalent.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times this substitution has been applied in actual order fulfillment or replenishment transactions. Used for effectiveness tracking and optimization.']

  Note: 'Approved item substitution relationships defining which items can replace others during out-of-stock (OOS) scenarios in eCommerce order picking, CAO (Computer Assisted Ordering) replenishment, and in-store fulfillment. Stores primary item, substitute item, substitution type (equivalent/upgrade/downgrade), priority rank, channel applicability (in-store/online/pickup/delivery), customer consent required flag, size-match tolerance, brand-match preference, and effective date range. Critical for omnichannel order fulfillment SLA compliance and customer satisfaction. Sourced from category management and eCommerce fulfillment teams.'
}

Table product.item_vendor {
  item_vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item-vendor association record.']
  procurement_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Procurement ownership of vendor contracts is assigned to an associate; needed for spend analysis and supplier performance dashboards.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the item (SKU) in this vendor relationship.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier providing this item.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master supply agreement or contract governing this item-vendor relationship. Links to vendor contract management system.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the vendor cost (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where this item is manufactured or grown by this vendor. Required for customs, labeling, and country-of-origin marketing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this item-vendor relationship record was first created in the system.']
  dsd_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor delivers directly to stores, bypassing the distribution center. Common for bread, dairy, snacks, and beverages.']
  edi_transaction_set_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EDI transaction sets supported by this vendor for this item (e.g., 850 Purchase Order, 856 Advance Ship Notice, 810 Invoice). Blank if vendor does not support EDI.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this item-vendor relationship expires or is terminated. Null indicates an open-ended relationship.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this item-vendor relationship becomes active and can be used for purchase order creation.']
  item_vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this item-vendor sourcing relationship. Blocked status prevents new purchase orders; discontinued indicates vendor no longer supplies this item.']
  last_cost_update_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor cost was last updated in the system. Used for cost change tracking and margin analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this item-vendor relationship record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  last_purchase_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order placed with this vendor for this item. Used for vendor activity analysis and relationship management.']
  last_receipt_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods receipt from this vendor for this item at any distribution center or store. Used for vendor performance tracking.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The smallest quantity that can be ordered from this vendor for this item, expressed in the vendor\'s unit of measure.']
  order_multiple decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity increment in which this item must be ordered (e.g., case pack size, pallet quantity). Orders must be in multiples of this value.']
  primary_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the primary or preferred source for this item. Used in automated replenishment and purchase order generation.']
  promotional_allowance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard promotional discount percentage offered by this vendor for this item during promotional periods. Used in temporary price reduction (TPR) planning.']
  return_authorization_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a return merchandise authorization (RMA) is required from this vendor before returning defective or unsold items.']
  slotting_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time or recurring fee paid by the vendor for shelf space allocation for this item. Common in new item introductions and category resets.']
  vendor_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'The current negotiated cost per vendor unit of measure. Used for purchase order pricing and gross margin calculations. Subject to contract terms and promotional allowances.']
  vendor_item_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s internal catalog or part number for this item, used in purchase orders and EDI transactions.']
  vendor_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from purchase order submission to expected delivery at the distribution center or store. Critical for replenishment planning and safety stock calculations.']
  vendor_pack_size varchar [note: 'Number of consumer units (eaches) contained in the vendor\'s shipping unit (case or pallet). Used for unit conversion between vendor ordering and store selling units.']
  vendor_quality_certification varchar [note: 'Quality certifications held by the vendor for this item (e.g., USDA Organic, Non-GMO Project, Fair Trade, HACCP, SQF). Pipe-separated list if multiple certifications apply.']
  vendor_reliability_rating varchar [note: 'Performance rating for this vendor on this item, based on fill rate, on-time delivery, and quality metrics. Used in vendor selection and category captain decisions.']
  vendor_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used by the vendor for ordering and invoicing this item. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|pound|kilogram|liter|gallon|dozen|box — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vendor_upc varchar [note: 'The UPC or GTIN assigned by the vendor for this item, may differ from retailer\'s UPC in case of private label or vendor-specific packaging.']

  Note: 'Association between items and their approved vendors/suppliers, capturing the sourcing relationship. Stores vendor reference, item reference, vendor item number, vendor UPC, primary vendor flag, vendor lead time days, minimum order quantity, order multiple, EDI transaction set supported, DSD indicator, and effective date range. Supports purchase order creation, replenishment planning, and vendor performance management. Sourced from SAP MM Purchasing Info Record and Oracle Retail.'
}

Table product.recall {
  recall_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product recall record. Primary key.']
  initiated_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Recall process requires identifying the associate who initiated the recall; essential for incident investigation and regulatory reporting.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Recalls are tied to the specific product affected; FK provides direct association.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Recall management reports must identify the responsible supplier for regulatory notifications and corrective action tracking.']
  affected_gtin varchar [note: '14-digit GTIN of the affected product. Global standard identifier for supply chain and distribution tracking.']
  affected_lot_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of affected lot or batch numbers. Used to identify specific production runs subject to recall.']
  affected_product_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the affected product including brand name, product name, package size, and any distinguishing characteristics to aid in identification.']
  affected_states varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of US state codes where the recalled product was distributed. Used for targeted store notification and consumer alerts.']
  affected_upc varchar [note: '12-digit UPC barcode of the affected product. Used for POS system identification and automated inventory removal.']
  classification varchar [note: 'FDA recall classification indicating severity of health risk. Class I: life-threatening or serious injury risk. Class II: temporary or reversible adverse health consequences. Class III: unlikely to cause adverse health consequences.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory agency officially closed the recall case. Indicates successful completion of all regulatory requirements.']
  completed_date date [note: 'Date when all recall activities (store removal, consumer notification, disposition) were completed. Precedes regulatory closure.']
  consumer_notification_date date [note: 'Date when consumers were notified of the recall through public communication channels.']
  consumer_notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify consumers of the recall. Multiple methods may be used; this field captures the primary notification channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Press Release|Email|SMS|In-Store Signage|Website|Social Media|Loyalty App — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field.']
  disposition_instructions varchar [note: 'Instructions for handling recalled product. Destroy: dispose of product. Return to Vendor: ship back to supplier. Hold for Inspection: quarantine pending agency review. Dispose per HACCP: follow food safety protocols.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of product distribution. Indicates whether the recall affects national, regional, state-level, or local distribution.']
  estimated_financial_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial impact of the recall including product cost, disposal costs, labor, and potential liability. Measured in USD. Business-confidential financial data.']
  fda_enforcement_report_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official FDA enforcement report for this recall. Provides access to the public regulatory record.']
  health_hazard_evaluation varchar [note: 'FDA health hazard evaluation summary describing the potential health risk to consumers, including severity, likelihood of occurrence, and affected populations.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Date the recall was officially initiated by the agency or manufacturer. Marks the start of the recall lifecycle.']
  initiating_agency varchar [note: 'Regulatory agency or entity that initiated the recall. FDA: food/drug/cosmetic. USDA: meat/poultry. CPSC: consumer product safety. DEA: controlled substances. Manufacturer: voluntary firm-initiated.']
  internal_case_number varchar [note: 'Internal case tracking number assigned by the retailer for recall management and cross-functional coordination across supply chain, store operations, and legal teams.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall record was last modified in the data platform. Audit trail field.']
  loyalty_matched_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether loyalty program purchase history was used to identify and directly notify customers who purchased the recalled product.']
  manufacturer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the manufacturer contact person. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  manufacturer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer contact person responsible for coordinating the recall. Used for vendor communication and coordination.']
  manufacturer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the manufacturer contact person. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  product_removed_date date [note: 'Date when the recalled product was physically removed from store shelves and inventory. Marks completion of store-level recall execution.']
  production_date_end date [note: 'End date of the production date range for affected items. Defines the latest production date of recalled products.']
  production_date_start date [note: 'Start date of the production date range for affected items. Defines the earliest production date of recalled products.']
  quantity_recalled decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total quantity of product units subject to recall. Measured in units appropriate to the product (cases, pounds, bottles, etc.).']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity recalled field. Defines how the recalled quantity is measured.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reason for the recall, including the specific defect, contamination, mislabeling, or health hazard that triggered the action.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Official recall number assigned by the initiating agency (FDA, USDA, CPSC, DEA, or manufacturer). Serves as the external business identifier for regulatory reporting and cross-agency coordination.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recall. Tracks progression from initiation through store notification, product removal, completion, and regulatory closure.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Type of recall action. Voluntary: firm-initiated removal. Mandatory: agency-ordered removal. Market Withdrawal: removal of product not subject to FDA enforcement action.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated this recall record (e.g., Oracle Retail, SAP, Manual Entry).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this recall record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  store_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when store operations were notified of the recall. Critical for tracking compliance with pull-from-shelf execution timelines.']

  Note: 'Product recall and market withdrawal record managing the end-to-end recall lifecycle per FDA 21 CFR Part 7 and USDA FSIS directives. Stores recall number (FDA/USDA/manufacturer-assigned), recall classification (Class I life-threatening / Class II reversible health risk / Class III no health risk), recall type (voluntary/mandatory/market withdrawal), initiating agency (FDA/USDA/CPSC/DEA), affected item references, affected lot/batch numbers, affected production date range, distribution scope, recall reason, health hazard evaluation summary, recall status (initiated/in-progress/store-notified/product-removed/completed/closed), store notification timestamp, consumer notification method, and disposition instructions (destroy/return-to-vendor/hold). Supports FDA recall compliance, store operations pull-from-shelf execution, customer notification for loyalty-matched purchases, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table product.item_regulatory_flag {
  item_regulatory_flag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item regulatory flag record. Primary key.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product item to which this regulatory flag applies.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing audit trail information, compliance review findings, or historical changes to the regulatory flag status.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique certification or approval number issued by the certifying body for traceability and audit purposes.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or government agency that issued the certification or approval (e.g., USDA, FDA, State Board of Pharmacy, third-party certifier).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory compliance flag indicating whether the certification or approval is active, expired, suspended, pending, revoked, or under review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory flag record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  digital_channel_display_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory flag should be displayed in digital commerce channels (website, mobile app) for customer filtering and product discovery. True if displayable, False otherwise.']
  disclosure_statement varchar [note: 'Standardized text or statement required for regulatory disclosure on labels, receipts, or digital channels (e.g., \'Bioengineered Food\', \'USDA Organic\').']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory flag or certification becomes effective and can be used for compliance purposes at POS and in digital channels.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory flag or certification expires and is no longer valid for compliance purposes. Nullable for flags without expiration.']
  flag_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory compliance flag or certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: snap_eligible|wic_approved|ebt_eligible|organic_certified|non_gmo|gluten_free_certified|kosher|halal|fair_trade|usda_grade|fda_bioengineered_disclosure|allergen_free — promote to reference product]']
  label_disclosure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory flag requires mandatory disclosure on product labels or packaging (e.g., FDA bioengineered food disclosure). True if disclosure required, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory flag record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or recertification audit to ensure ongoing regulatory adherence.']
  pos_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory flag enables special transaction eligibility at POS systems (e.g., SNAP/EBT/WIC payment acceptance). True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or administrative jurisdiction where this regulatory flag applies (e.g., USA, California, European Union). Uses 3-letter country codes or state abbreviations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated this regulatory flag data (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System, SAP Retail MM).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this regulatory flag record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  supporting_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to supporting documentation, certificates, or audit reports that validate the regulatory flag or certification.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory flag or certification was last verified or audited by the certifying body or internal compliance team.']

  Note: 'Regulatory compliance flags and certifications associated with items. Stores flag type (SNAP-eligible, WIC-approved, EBT-eligible, organic-certified, non-GMO, gluten-free-certified, kosher, halal, fair-trade, USDA-grade, FDA-bioengineered-disclosure), certifying body, certification number, effective date, expiration date, and compliance status. Supports EBT/SNAP/WIC transaction eligibility at POS, FDA bioengineered food disclosure compliance, and digital channel filtering.'
}

Table product.item_packaging {
  item_packaging_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item packaging specification record. Primary key.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent product item for which this packaging specification applies. Links to the authoritative product catalog.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or manufacturer who provides this packaged item. Used for vendor performance tracking and supply chain management.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product was manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations and trade compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this packaging specification record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  deposit_amount decimal(5,2) [note: 'The refundable deposit amount charged for returnable containers such as bottles and cans, measured in USD. Applicable in states with bottle bill legislation. Null for non-deposit items.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this packaging specification is no longer valid for use. Null indicates currently active with no planned end date. Supports time-variant packaging changes and historical tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this packaging specification becomes active and valid for use in the supply chain. Supports time-variant packaging changes and historical tracking.']
  gross_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the packaging level including product and all packaging materials, measured in pounds. Used for freight calculations and warehouse capacity planning.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'The GS1 Global Trade Item Number assigned to this specific packaging level. GTIN-8, GTIN-12 (UPC), GTIN-13 (EAN), or GTIN-14 (case/pallet) format. Used for scanning, EDI transactions, and supply chain traceability.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packaging contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and transportation compliance per DOT regulations.']
  height_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'The vertical dimension of the packaging unit measured in inches. Used for warehouse slotting, transportation planning, and planogram design.']
  hi varchar [note: 'The number of layers stacked vertically on a pallet in the Ti-Hi pallet configuration. Used for warehouse receiving, DC slotting, and transportation planning. Null for non-pallet packaging levels.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this packaging specification record was most recently updated in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  length_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'The longest dimension of the packaging unit measured in inches. Used for warehouse slotting, transportation planning, and planogram design.']
  max_stack_height varchar [note: 'The maximum number of units that can be safely stacked vertically. Used for warehouse safety compliance and space planning. Null if not stackable.']
  net_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the product contents only, excluding packaging materials, measured in pounds. Used for nutritional labeling and FDA compliance.']
  packaging_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical level of packaging in the distribution chain. Each represents the consumer unit, inner pack is a sub-case grouping, case is the standard shipping unit, pallet is the warehouse unit, display shipper is a retail-ready merchandising unit, and master case is an outer shipping container.']
  packaging_material_type varchar [note: 'The primary material composition of the packaging at this level. Used for sustainability reporting, recycling programs, and environmental compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrugated_cardboard|plastic|glass|metal|wood|composite|flexible_film — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  packaging_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this packaging specification. Active indicates current use in supply chain, inactive indicates temporarily out of use, discontinued indicates permanently retired, pending approval indicates awaiting vendor or regulatory approval, seasonal indicates used only during specific periods.']
  recyclable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packaging material is recyclable through standard municipal recycling programs. Supports sustainability reporting and consumer labeling requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record from which this packaging specification was sourced (e.g., SAP MM, Oracle Retail, Manhattan WMS). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this packaging record in the source operational system. Used for data reconciliation and traceability back to the system of record.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging level can be safely stacked on top of identical units during storage and transportation. Used for warehouse space optimization and load planning.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized Tariff Schedule classification code for this packaged item. Used for customs clearance, duty calculation, and international trade compliance.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging level requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation (cold chain). Applicable to perishables, frozen foods, and pharmacy items.']
  ti varchar [note: 'The number of cases per layer on a pallet in the Ti-Hi pallet configuration. Used for warehouse receiving, DC slotting, and transportation planning. Null for non-pallet packaging levels.']
  unit_quantity varchar [note: 'The number of base units (each) contained within this packaging level. For example, a case may contain 24 eaches, a pallet may contain 60 cases.']
  upc varchar [note: 'The 12-digit UPC barcode for this packaging level, primarily used at point-of-sale in North America. Subset of GTIN-12.']
  vendor_item_number varchar [note: 'The suppliers internal item identifier for this packaging configuration. Used for purchase orders, EDI transactions, and cross-reference with vendor catalogs.']
  volume_cubic_feet decimal(10,3) [note: 'The total cubic volume of the packaging unit measured in cubic feet. Calculated from length, width, and height. Used for warehouse capacity planning and transportation optimization.']
  width_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'The width dimension of the packaging unit measured in inches. Used for warehouse slotting, transportation planning, and planogram design.']

  Note: 'Packaging specification record for each item defining the physical packaging hierarchy and dimensions. Stores packaging level (each/inner pack/case/pallet/display shipper), GTIN per level, unit quantity per level, gross weight, net weight, length, width, height, volume, packaging material type, recyclable flag, deposit amount (bottle deposit), and Ti-Hi (pallet configuration: tiers x height). Supports WMS receiving, DC slotting, transportation planning, and sustainability reporting.'
}

Table product.ab_test_experiment {
  ab_test_experiment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the A/B test experiment.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the experiment.']
  iterated_from_ab_test_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ab_test_experiment (iterated_from_ab_test_experiment_id)']
  last_modified_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who last modified the experiment.']
  ab_test_experiment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the experiment.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of sales channels (e.g., online, mobile, in‑store) included in the experiment.']
  confidence_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum confidence level (percentage) required to declare a winner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the experiment record was created.']
  customer_segment_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of customer segment identifiers targeted by the experiment.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment is scheduled to end.']
  experiment_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the experiment.']
  experiment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the experiment.']
  experiment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the experiment is a simple A/B test or a multivariate test.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'Statement of the hypothesis being tested.']
  is_statistically_significant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the result met the confidence threshold.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the experiment.']
  product_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product SKUs or categories targeted by the experiment.']
  result_metric_confidence_interval varchar [note: 'Confidence interval for the result metric value (e.g., 95% CI).']
  result_metric_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Observed value of the target metric for the winning variant.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment is scheduled to start.']
  statistical_test varchar [note: 'Statistical test applied to determine significance of results.']
  storefront_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of storefront identifiers where the experiment is applied.']
  target_metric varchar [note: 'Primary metric used to evaluate experiment performance.']
  target_metric_goal decimal(10,4) [note: 'Goal value for the target metric (e.g., 0.05 for a 5% conversion lift).']
  traffic_allocation varchar [note: 'JSON string mapping variant identifiers to traffic allocation percentages.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the experiment record.']
  variant_count varchar [note: 'Number of variants defined in the experiment, including the control.']
  variant_definitions varchar [note: 'JSON string describing each variant\'s configuration (e.g., UI changes, pricing adjustments).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the experiment definition, incremented on each change.']
  winner_determined_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the winning variant was identified.']
  winning_variant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the variant determined as the winner after experiment conclusion.']

  Note: 'Master record for A/B and multivariate test experiments run on digital storefronts. Captures experiment name, hypothesis, variant definitions, traffic allocation percentages, target metric (conversion rate, AOV, click-through), start/end dates, storefront scope, status (draft, running, paused, concluded), and winning variant determination. Supports data-driven optimization of the digital shopping experience including recommendation algorithms, search ranking, checkout flows, and promotional placements.'
}

Table product.order_channel {
  order_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each order origination channel. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup for channel classification.']
  parent_order_channel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on order_channel (parent_order_channel_id)']
  allowed_payment_methods varchar [note: 'List of payment methods accepted for this channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|gift_card|mobile_wallet|cash|check|ebt|snap|wic|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay — promote to reference product]']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used internally to identify the channel (e.g., POS, WEB, MOB).']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the order channel (e.g., In‑Store POS, Web Store, Mobile App).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the channel based on interaction mode.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code used for monetary limits on the channel.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the channel was retired or disabled (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the channel became operational.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel is currently enabled for order capture.']
  is_third_party boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel is operated by an external marketplace partner.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the channel record.']
  max_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value allowed for a single order on this channel.']
  min_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary value required for an order to be accepted via this channel.']
  order_channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the channel\'s purpose, capabilities, and typical use cases.']
  requires_age_verification boolean [note: 'True if the channel must verify customer age for restricted items.']
  requires_prescription boolean [note: 'True if the channel can process pharmacy prescription orders.']
  routing_rule_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the routing rule set that governs order flow for this channel.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that defines this channel (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail).']
  supported_fulfillment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of fulfillment methods the channel can route to.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of order origination channels (in-store POS, web, mobile app, kiosk, phone, third-party marketplace) with channel-specific configuration and routing rules.'
}

Table product.order_hold {
  order_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the order hold record.']
  order_assigned_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate responsible for handling the hold.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this hold is applied.']
  primary_order_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the fraud review.']
  product_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order line that is subject to the hold.']
  superseded_order_hold_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on order_hold (superseded_order_hold_id)']
  hold_age_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the hold is due to an age‑restricted item.']
  hold_age_verified_flag boolean [note: 'True when age verification has been successfully completed.']
  hold_age_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when age verification was completed.']
  hold_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount placed on hold (e.g., pending payment amount).']
  hold_applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold was initially placed on the order.']
  hold_channel_type varchar [note: 'Channel through which the order was placed that generated the hold.']
  hold_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  hold_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the hold amount.']
  hold_expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold is scheduled to expire if not released.']
  hold_expiry_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the hold expired without release.']
  hold_expiry_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the expiry reason.']
  hold_fraud_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the manual fraud review process.']
  hold_fraud_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud review decision was recorded.']
  hold_fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by the fraud engine for this hold.']
  hold_inventory_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when inventory confirmation was performed.']
  hold_inventory_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'True if inventory availability has been confirmed for the held items.']
  hold_last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hold record.']
  hold_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by staff regarding the hold.']
  hold_notification_channel varchar [note: 'Channel used to notify the customer about the hold.']
  hold_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'True if a notification about the hold was sent to the customer.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the hold, used in communications and reporting.']
  hold_payment_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when payment verification was completed.']
  hold_payment_verified_flag boolean [note: 'True when payment verification (e.g., card auth) has succeeded.']
  hold_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the hold for processing urgency.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold.']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the hold was applied.']
  hold_related_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the related SKU involved in the hold.']
  hold_related_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the product that triggered the hold, if applicable.']
  hold_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold was manually or automatically released.']
  hold_sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold met its SLA target.']
  hold_sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for the hold to be resolved.']
  hold_sla_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'Difference in minutes between actual resolution time and SLA target (positive = over, negative = under).']
  hold_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the hold (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Retail).']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of the hold reason, such as fraud review, payment verification, age‑restricted item validation, inventory confirmation, or other.']

  Note: 'Records order holds applied for fraud review, payment verification, age-restricted item validation, or inventory confirmation before fulfillment release.'
}

Table product.gs1_sync {
  gs1_sync_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each GS1 data synchronization record.']
  prior_gs1_sync_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on gs1_sync (prior_gs1_sync_id)']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product item being synchronized.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external trading partner (vendor or supplier) involved in the synchronization.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for a batch of synchronization messages sent together.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the synchronized item meets applicable regulatory requirements.']
  correlation_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate related synchronization messages across systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the synchronization record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_format varchar [note: 'Serialization format of the synchronization payload.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the synchronized data ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended records).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the synchronized data becomes effective for downstream systems.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing any error encountered during synchronization.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the error associated with the synchronization attempt.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction of the regulatory requirement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the synchronization record.']
  message_ref varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier for the synchronization message.']
  payload_type varchar [note: 'Category of data contained in the payload (e.g., product_data, pricing_data).']
  payload_version varchar [note: 'Version of the data payload format used in the synchronization message.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the synchronization record within the data pool.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Governing body responsible for the compliance aspect (e.g., FDA, USDA, DEA).']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of retry attempts made after a failed synchronization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the internal system that originated the synchronization message (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Retail).']
  sync_action varchar [note: 'Type of synchronization operation performed on the item data.']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the synchronization record.']
  sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the synchronization event occurred.']
  target_system varchar [note: 'Name of the external system or data pool that receives the synchronization message.']

  Note: 'GS1 Global Data Synchronization Network (GDSN) data pool synchronization records tracking item data publication, subscription, and synchronization status between Grocery and CPG trading partners per GS1 standards.'
}

Table product.product_assortment {
  product_assortment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the assortment association']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product_item']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store_location']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the product is removed from the store']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the product becomes available at the store']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assortment relationship between product_item and store_location. It captures the period a product is available at a specific store.. Existence Justification: Each product_item can be stocked at many store_location records, and each store_location carries many product_item records. The business actively manages the assortment of items per store, tracking when a product becomes available and when it is removed using effective start and end dates.'
}

Table product.promo_sku_assignment {
  promo_sku_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the promo_sku_assignment association']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotional offer']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to SKU']
  discount_override_amount decimal [note: 'Specific monetary discount applied to this SKU within the offer']
  discount_override_percentage decimal [note: 'Specific percentage discount applied to this SKU within the offer']
  facing_count varchar [note: 'Number of facings allocated to the SKU for the promotion']
  maximum_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity of the SKU eligible for the offer']
  planogram_placement_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating shelf placement rules for the SKU under this offer']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Order in which the SKU is considered when multiple offers compete']
  required_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity of the SKU that must be purchased for the offer to apply']
  substitution_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a substitute SKU may be used if the primary SKU is out of stock']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of promotional offers to SKUs. It captures per‑SKU offer terms such as quantity limits, discount overrides, and placement priorities, enabling the business to manage and query which items participate in each promotion.. Existence Justification: In Grocery, marketing teams actively create and maintain a list of which SKUs are eligible for each promotional offer, and a single SKU can participate in multiple offers over time. Conversely, each promotional offer can include many SKUs. The association carries its own attributes such as required quantity and discount overrides, making it an operational entity.'
}

Table product.inventory_allocation {
  inventory_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the inventory_allocation association']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SKU']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the storage location']
  available_quantity varchar [note: 'Units available for allocation (on_hand - reserved)']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the SKU batch at the location']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier for traceability']
  on_hand_quantity varchar [note: 'Current on‑hand units of the SKU at the location']
  reserved_quantity varchar [note: 'Units reserved for pending orders at the location']
  temperature_celsius decimal [note: 'Recorded temperature of the location for the SKU']

  Note: 'Represents the placement of a SKU in a specific storage location, capturing quantities, lot, expiration, and temperature information that belong to the SKU‑location relationship.. Existence Justification: A SKU can be stocked in many physical storage locations across stores and distribution centers, and each storage location can hold many different SKUs. The business actively manages this placement, tracking on‑hand, reserved, and available quantities, lot numbers, expiration dates, and temperature per SKU‑location pair.'
}

Table product.product_order_line2 {
  product_order_line2_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the digital order line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category hierarchy node for this item, enabling category-level sales analysis and assortment planning.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order header that contains this line item. Links this line to the overall order transaction.']
  picker_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store associate or picker who selected and prepared this line item for fulfillment. Used for performance tracking and quality accountability.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: order_line must reference the product_item it represents; adds child-to-parent relationship']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotion or promotional campaign applied to this line item, enabling tracking of promotional effectiveness and discount attribution.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store, distribution center, or micro-fulfillment center from which this line item was picked and fulfilled.']
  age_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product has age restrictions requiring customer age verification at the time of delivery or pickup, such as alcohol or tobacco products.']
  bogo_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this line item was part of a buy-one-get-one promotional offer.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'The specific coupon code applied to this line item for discount purposes. Used for tracking coupon redemption and promotional campaign performance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this order line record was first created in the order management system, representing when the customer added this item to their digital cart and confirmed the order.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which this line item is priced and billed.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The retail department or category code to which this product belongs, used for merchandising analytics, inventory management, and financial reporting.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total dollar amount of discounts applied to this line item, including promotional discounts, coupons, temporary price reductions, and loyalty rewards.']
  extended_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total price for this line item calculated as quantity multiplied by unit price, before discounts and taxes. Represents the gross line amount.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number that uniquely identifies the product internationally. Can be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits depending on product type and packaging level.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the order, used for ordering and display purposes. Determines the sequence in which items appear on the order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this individual line item within the order lifecycle. Tracks the line from order confirmation through final delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|picking|picked|packed|shipped|delivered|cancelled|refunded — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  net_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The final net amount for this line item after applying all discounts and adding taxes. This is the amount the customer pays for this specific line.']
  organic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is certified organic according to USDA organic standards. Important for customer preference tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is perishable and requires special handling, temperature control, or expedited fulfillment to maintain quality and food safety.']
  picked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this line item was physically picked from inventory by the fulfillment associate. Critical for fulfillment SLA tracking and operational efficiency analysis.']
  picker_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the store associate or picker regarding this line item, such as product condition, substitution rationale, or special handling instructions.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is a store-owned private label brand rather than a national or regional consumer packaged goods brand.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the product as displayed to the customer during order placement and in order confirmation communications.']
  quantity_confirmed decimal(10,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of the item that was confirmed for fulfillment after inventory validation. May differ from quantity ordered due to stock availability or picking accuracy.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of the item that the customer originally requested in their order. This is the initial order quantity before any substitutions or out-of-stock adjustments.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit code that uniquely identifies the product within the retailer\'s inventory system. Primary product identifier for internal operations.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is eligible for purchase using SNAP benefits (formerly food stamps). Critical for government assistance program compliance.']
  substituted_product_name varchar [note: 'The name of the substitute product provided to the customer. Used for customer communication and order accuracy tracking.']
  substituted_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of the substitute product that was provided when the original item was unavailable. Null if no substitution occurred.']
  substitution_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a substitution was made, such as out-of-stock, product discontinued, or quality concerns.']
  substitution_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this line represents the original item ordered, a substituted item, an out-of-stock item, or a substitution that the customer declined.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total sales tax amount calculated and applied to this line item based on the product tax code and delivery location tax jurisdiction.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'The required storage and handling temperature zone for this product: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (cold chain), or frozen. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.']
  tpr_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this line item was sold at a temporary promotional price reduction at the time of order placement.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the product quantity is measured, such as each, pound, kilogram, gallon, or liter. Critical for accurate pricing and inventory management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|lb|oz|kg|g|gal|qt|pt|l|ml|dozen|case — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The price per single unit of the product at the time of order placement, before any line-level discounts or promotions are applied.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code barcode identifier used for point-of-sale scanning and product identification across retail systems.']
  weight_actual decimal(10,3) [note: 'The actual weight of the item picked and fulfilled, particularly important for variable-weight items like produce, meat, and deli products sold by weight.']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the actual weight field, such as pounds, ounces, kilograms, or grams.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is eligible for purchase using WIC program benefits. Required for WIC program compliance and reporting.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the line items linking product_item and order_header. It captures quantity, unit price, discount, tax, net amount, and line status for each product in an order.. Existence Justification: An order can contain many product items and a single product item can appear in many orders. Each occurrence is recorded as an order line that stores quantity, pricing, discounts, tax, net amount, and line status. Business users create, update, and delete these order lines as part of the order fulfillment process.'
}

Table product.pick_assignment {
  pick_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the pick_assignment association']
  assigned_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate responsible for the pick']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SKU']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Wave']
  actual_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity actually picked for the SKU in the wave']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the pick within the wave']
  target_quantity varchar [note: 'Planned quantity of the SKU to be picked in the wave']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the pick task (e.g., planned, in‑progress, completed)']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantities (e.g., each, case)']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a SKU to a picking wave in the fulfillment process, capturing quantities, status, and responsible associate for each allocation.. Existence Justification: In Grocery fulfillment, a SKU can be allocated to many picking waves over time, and each wave contains many SKUs. The allocation is managed through pick‑assignment records that capture quantities, status, and responsible associate, and these records are created, updated, and deleted by operations staff.'
}

Table product.line_item {
  line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the line_item association']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the payment transaction containing the line']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the product_item being sold']
  discount_amount decimal [note: 'Discount applied to this line item']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product sold in this line']
  tax_amount decimal [note: 'Tax amount calculated for this line item']
  total_price decimal [note: 'Final total price for this line after discounts and taxes']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'Price per unit of the product at the time of sale']

  Note: 'Represents the association between a product_item and a payment_transaction. Each record captures the quantity of the product sold, the unit price, discounts, taxes and total price for that line in the transaction.. Existence Justification: Each payment transaction can include multiple product items, and each product item can be sold in many different payment transactions over time. The link between them is captured as a line‑item record that stores quantity, price and discount information, which is created, updated, and deleted by the sales and inventory processes.'
}

Table store.store_location {
  store_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical retail location. Primary key for the store_location entity. This is the single source of truth (SSOT) for store identity across the enterprise and the anchor entity for all store-domain relationships.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Cash handling and bank reconciliation require each store to reference its dedicated bank account.']
  banner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.banner. Business justification: Store locations have a textual banner_name; adding store_location.banner_id FK creates a proper reference to the banner master table, enables consistent reporting, and removes the redundant banner_nam']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Store‑level expense reporting requires linking each store to its primary cost center for P&L and budget variance analysis.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dc_facility. Business justification: REQUIRED: Store replenishment planning needs the primary DC assignment to calculate inbound logistics; experts expect a dc_facility_id on each store for routing and capacity planning.']
  district_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.district. Business justification: Each store belongs to a district; adding district_id creates the hierarchical link without redundancy.']
  format_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_format. Business justification: Store format is a master reference; replace string format_type with FK to store_format for consistency and normalization.']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_gateway. Business justification: Required for POS settlement reporting: each store is assigned a payment gateway that processes its transactions, needed for daily settlement batch generation and compliance audits.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Daily sales posting uses a default GL account per store; linking enables automated journal entry generation.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Tax and regulatory filings require each store to be linked to its legal entity for jurisdictional compliance.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Pricing zone assignment per store drives zone‑specific price strategies; required for Price Zone Performance Report and zone‑based price optimization.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability dashboards aggregate sales by profit center; each store must be assigned to a profit center for store‑wide profit reporting.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Required for store‑level loyalty program settings used in earn‑rate calculations, store‑specific promotions, and compliance reporting; a store manager configures the program per store.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.region. Business justification: Allows direct regional performance reporting and inventory allocation per store without traversing district hierarchy.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: REQUIRED: Assortment planning assigns each store to a store cluster for category mix, planogram templates, and performance reporting; linking store_location to store_cluster enables cluster‑based asso']
  accepts_snap_ebt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store is authorized to accept SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits via EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer). Impacts payment processing, regulatory compliance, and customer accessibility.']
  accepts_wic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store is authorized to accept WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits. Impacts product assortment, POS configuration, and regulatory compliance.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the store location. Used for customer navigation, delivery routing, and regulatory reporting. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite number, building identifier, or cross-street. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  backroom_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Square footage of storage and receiving areas behind the sales floor. Used for inventory capacity planning, replenishment strategy, and operational efficiency analysis.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the store is located. Used for geographic analysis, market segmentation, and regulatory compliance.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date this store location permanently ceased operations. Null for active stores. Used for historical analysis, lease obligation tracking, and asset disposition planning.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Used for international operations, currency handling, and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this store location record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and compliance reporting.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for store-level communication. Used for customer inquiries, vendor coordination, and internal communications. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: '24/7 emergency contact number for the store location. Used for after-hours incidents, security alerts, and facility emergencies. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  gross_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total building square footage including sales floor, backroom, offices, restrooms, and mechanical spaces. Used for facility management, lease agreements, and property tax calculations.']
  has_bakery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store includes an in-store bakery. Impacts food safety compliance, production scheduling, and fresh product assortment.']
  has_deli boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store includes a deli department with prepared foods. Impacts food safety compliance (HACCP), staffing, and merchandising strategy.']
  has_floral boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store includes a floral department. Impacts perishable inventory management, vendor relationships, and seasonal merchandising.']
  has_fuel_center boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store location includes a fuel center. Impacts environmental compliance (EPA), pricing strategy, and loyalty program integration.']
  has_meat_department boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store includes a full-service meat department with butchers. Impacts USDA compliance, cold chain management, and staffing.']
  has_organic_section boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store includes a dedicated organic and natural foods section. Impacts merchandising strategy, vendor relationships, and customer segmentation.']
  has_pharmacy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store location includes a licensed pharmacy department. Impacts regulatory compliance (DEA, State Boards of Pharmacy, HIPAA), staffing requirements, and service offerings.']
  has_seafood boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store includes a fresh seafood department. Impacts cold chain management, HACCP compliance, and specialized staffing requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this store location record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit trails.']
  last_remodel_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent major remodel or renovation. Used to track capital investment cycles, assess store modernization status, and plan future remodel schedules.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the store location. Used for mapping, proximity analysis, delivery routing, and trade area modeling.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the store location. Used for mapping, proximity analysis, delivery routing, and trade area modeling.']
  opening_date date [note: 'The date this store location first opened for business. Used for calculating store maturity, comp sales eligibility (typically requires 12+ months of operation), and anniversary planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the store location. Determines whether the store is open for business, undergoing changes, or closed. Critical for inventory allocation, workforce scheduling, and customer communication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|temporarily_closed|permanently_closed|under_construction|under_remodel|pre_opening|seasonal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership structure of the store location. Impacts financial reporting, capital expenditure planning, and operational control.']
  parking_spaces_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer parking spaces available at this location. Impacts site selection, customer convenience, and omnichannel pickup capacity.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary customer-facing phone number for the store. Used for customer inquiries, order status, and pharmacy consultations. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or postal code for the store location. Used for delivery zone mapping, demographic analysis, and trade area definition. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  primary_store_manager_associate_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.associate']
  selling_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Square footage of customer-accessible sales floor area. Excludes backroom, offices, and non-selling spaces. Used for sales productivity metrics (sales per square foot), space planning, and merchandising allocation.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code (e.g., CA, TX, ON). Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory compliance, and regional reporting.']
  store_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the store location, often including neighborhood or landmark identifiers (e.g., \'Downtown Main Street\', \'Riverside Plaza\'). Used for customer communication and internal reference.']
  store_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the store location. Used in all customer-facing communications, signage, and operational systems. Typically a 4-6 digit numeric code assigned sequentially or by region.']
  supports_curbside_pickup boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store offers curbside pickup service for online orders (click-and-collect). Impacts omnichannel fulfillment strategy, parking allocation, and staffing.']
  supports_home_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store serves as a fulfillment center for home delivery orders. Impacts last mile delivery (LMD) operations, inventory allocation, and order management system (OMS) integration.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the store location (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago). Used for scheduling, reporting, and operational coordination across regions.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical retail location operated by Grocery, including supermarkets, pharmacies, fuel centers, and micro-fulfillment centers (MFCs). Captures store number, banner name, format type (superstore, neighborhood market, express), ownership type (owned/leased), gross and selling square footage, opening date, remodel date, closure date, operational status, primary phone number, email, and emergency contact information. This is the SSOT for store identity across the enterprise and the anchor entity for all store-domain relationships. Every cross-domain reference to a physical store resolves to this product.'
}

Table store.format {
  format_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the store format. Primary key.']
  annual_revenue_target_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual revenue target for stores of this format, in USD. Used for comp sales performance tracking and store clustering.']
  annual_revenue_target_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual revenue target for stores of this format, in USD. Used for comp sales performance tracking and store clustering.']
  bakery_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format include an in-store bakery department.']
  capital_investment_typical decimal(15,2) [note: 'Typical total capital investment required to build or convert a store to this format, in USD. Used for new store budgeting and format conversion ROI analysis.']
  checkout_lane_count_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of POS (Point of Sale) checkout lanes in stores of this format.']
  checkout_lane_count_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of POS (Point of Sale) checkout lanes in stores of this format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this store format record was first created in the system.']
  deli_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format include a deli department with prepared foods and sliced meats.']
  department_count_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of merchandise departments typically found in stores of this format.']
  department_count_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of merchandise departments typically found in stores of this format.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this store format definition was discontinued or superseded. Null for currently active formats.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this store format definition became effective and available for use in store planning and operations.']
  endcap_count_typical varchar [note: 'Typical number of endcap display locations in stores of this format. Endcaps are high-visibility end-of-aisle promotional displays.']
  format_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the store format type used for strategic segmentation and portfolio analysis.']
  format_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the store format (e.g., SS for Superstore, NM for Neighborhood Market, EXP for Express). Used as a business identifier in operational systems and reporting.']
  format_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the store format including its purpose, target customer segment, and key differentiators.']
