  format_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the store format (e.g., Superstore, Neighborhood Market, Express Convenience, Warehouse Club, Fuel-Only).']
  format_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the store format in the portfolio. Active formats are in production use; pilot formats are being tested; discontinued formats are no longer used for new stores.']
  fresh_produce_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format carry fresh produce departments with perishable inventory.']
  fuel_center_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format typically include an attached fuel center.']
  gondola_count_typical varchar [note: 'Typical number of gondola shelving units in stores of this format. Gondolas are the primary shelving units in store aisles.']
  headcount_typical varchar [note: 'Typical total employee headcount for stores of this format, used for labor planning and budgeting.']
  home_delivery_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format support last-mile delivery (LMD) services to customer homes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this store format record was last updated in the system.']
  meat_seafood_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format include fresh meat and seafood departments with butcher services.']
  mfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format include or are co-located with a Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC) for automated online order fulfillment.']
  online_pickup_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format support click-and-collect online order pickup services (curbside or in-store).']
  operating_hours_typical varchar [note: 'Typical daily operating hours for stores of this format (e.g., 6am-11pm, 24/7). Used for labor scheduling and customer communication.']
  parking_spaces_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customer parking spaces typically provided for stores of this format.']
  parking_spaces_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of customer parking spaces required for stores of this format, used for site selection and zoning compliance.']
  pharmacy_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format typically include a full-service pharmacy department.']
  planogram_template_family varchar [note: 'Identifier for the planogram template family used by this store format. Planograms are visual merchandise display plans that define product placement on shelves and fixtures.']
  self_checkout_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores of this format typically include self-checkout capabilities.']
  selling_area_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total square footage allocated to customer-facing selling area (excluding backroom, receiving, offices). Used for space productivity analysis.']
  sku_count_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) typically carried in stores of this format. Used for assortment planning and inventory management.']
  sku_count_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) typically carried in stores of this format. Used for assortment planning and inventory management.']
  sku_count_typical varchar [note: 'Typical or average number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) carried in stores of this format. Used as the baseline for assortment planning.']
  square_footage_max varchar [note: 'Maximum total retail square footage for stores in this format, measured in square feet. Used for site selection and capital planning.']
  square_footage_min varchar [note: 'Minimum total retail square footage for stores in this format, measured in square feet. Used for site selection and capital planning.']
  square_footage_typical varchar [note: 'Typical or average retail square footage for stores in this format, measured in square feet. Used as the baseline for new store planning and format conversion analysis.']
  staffing_model_template varchar [note: 'Identifier for the workforce staffing model template used for stores of this format, defining typical headcount, roles, and scheduling patterns.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of store formats used across the Grocery banner portfolio (e.g., Superstore 100K+ sqft, Neighborhood Market 40-60K sqft, Express/Convenience 10-15K sqft, Warehouse Club, Fuel-Only). Defines format-level attributes such as typical square footage range, standard department count and mix, default service capability set, planogram template family, typical SKU count range, and staffing model template. Used for store clustering, comp sales grouping, assortment planning, new store capital budgeting, and format conversion analysis.'
}

Table store.region {
  region_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the region. Primary key.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary distribution center serving this region for replenishment and supply chain operations.']
  division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent division or banner under which this region operates.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Region pricing zone determines zone‑wide pricing rules for all stores in the region; used in Regional Pricing Performance and budgeting.']
  annual_ebitda_target_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned annual EBITDA target for the region in US dollars, representing operational profitability goal.']
  annual_revenue_target_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned annual revenue target for the region in US dollars, used for P&L accountability and performance tracking.']
  comp_sales_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this region is included in comp sales (same-store sales) performance reporting and analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this region record was first created in the system.']
  district_count varchar [note: 'Total number of districts currently assigned to this region.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this region was closed, consolidated, or otherwise ceased operations. Null for active regions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this region became active and operational within the organizational hierarchy.']
  geographic_boundary_definition varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic coverage of the region, typically listing states, metro areas, or counties included (e.g., \'NY, NJ, CT, PA eastern counties\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or historical notes about the region.']
  primary_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code representing the primary or largest state within the region\'s geographic boundary (e.g., NY, CA, TX).']
  promotional_calendar_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the promotional calendar or ad circular schedule applicable to this region.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the region for operational use and reporting (e.g., NE, SW, MW01).']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the region (e.g., Northeast Region, Southwest Region).']
  region_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the region: active (operational), inactive (closed or suspended), planned (future region), consolidating (merging into another region).']
  region_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the region based on its organizational purpose: geographic (standard market region), operational (distribution-focused), strategic (growth/pilot region), or test (sandbox region).']
  regional_office_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the regional headquarters or administrative office.']
  regional_office_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address for the regional headquarters (suite, floor, building).']
  regional_office_city varchar [note: 'City where the regional headquarters or administrative office is located.']
  regional_office_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the regional headquarters.']
  regional_office_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the regional headquarters location.']
  regional_office_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the regional headquarters location.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Total number of stores (all formats) currently operating within this region.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Primary time zone for the region\'s operations (e.g., EST, CST, MST, PST).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this region record was last modified.']
  workforce_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of employees (full-time and part-time) assigned to stores and facilities within this region.']

  Note: 'Geographic and organizational hierarchy node representing a regional grouping of districts and stores under a Regional Vice President. Captures region code, region name, VP assignment (employee reference), geographic boundary definition (state/metro groupings), parent division or banner, number of districts, total store count, and active/inactive status. Serves as the primary roll-up level for comp sales reporting, regional pricing zones, promotional planning, workforce organizational structure, and P&L accountability.'
}

Table store.district {
  district_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the district organizational hierarchy node. Primary key.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary DC (Distribution Center) that services the majority of stores in this district. Used for supply chain planning and replenishment routing.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent region in the organizational hierarchy. Districts roll up to regions for executive oversight and strategic planning.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the district office or administrative location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, or building information for the district office. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  annual_sales_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned annual sales revenue target for the district in USD. Used for budget planning, performance evaluation, and incentive compensation calculations.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the district office is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the district office location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this district record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  district_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the district, used in operational reporting and external communications. Typically alphanumeric format assigned by corporate planning.']
  district_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the district, often reflecting geographic coverage or regional designation (e.g., \'Metro Atlanta District\', \'Northern California District\').']
  district_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the district. Active districts have full operational authority; inactive districts are closed or suspended; consolidating districts are merging with others; restructuring districts are undergoing boundary or leadership changes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current district configuration will end or ended. Null for currently active configurations. Used for temporal hierarchy management and historical reporting.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current district configuration became effective. Used for time-based hierarchy queries and historical analysis of organizational changes.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for district-level communication and operational correspondence.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date when the district was officially established in the organizational hierarchy. Used for tenure analysis and historical reporting.']
  geographic_coverage_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic area covered by this district, including cities, counties, or regional boundaries. Used for territory planning and market analysis.']
  labor_budget_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total budgeted labor hours per week across all stores in the district. Used for workforce planning, scheduling optimization, and labor cost management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special considerations, or operational context about the district. Used for qualitative information not captured in structured fields.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of district performance based on composite metrics including Comp Sales (Comparable Store Sales), Shrink (Inventory Loss), GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment), and NPS (Net Promoter Score). Used for resource allocation and incentive compensation.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the district office or district manager. Used for operational communication and escalation.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code (ZIP code in USA) for the district office location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  shrink_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target shrink rate as a percentage of sales for the district. Shrink represents inventory loss from theft, spoilage, or damage. District managers are accountable for maintaining shrink below this threshold.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the district office location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active stores currently assigned to this district. Typically ranges from 8 to 15 stores per district for effective span of control.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Primary time zone for the district, used for scheduling, reporting cutoffs, and operational coordination. Follows IANA time zone database naming convention. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: America/New_York|America/Chicago|America/Denver|America/Los_Angeles|America/Phoenix|America/Anchorage|Pacific/Honolulu — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate selling square footage across all stores in the district. Used for productivity analysis, sales per square foot benchmarking, and capacity planning.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the user who last modified this district record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this district record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data freshness monitoring.']

  Note: 'Organizational hierarchy node below region, grouping a set of stores (typically 8-15) under a District Manager. Captures district code, district name, district manager employee reference, parent region reference, store count, geographic coverage description, performance tier classification, and active status. Used for operational oversight, labor scheduling aggregation, shrink accountability, weekly sales performance benchmarking, and district manager performance evaluation.'
}

Table store.department {
  department_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the department. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned as the department manager responsible for operations, staffing, and performance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Department‑level expense allocation uses a dedicated cost center for detailed cost tracking.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where this department is located.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead vendor designated as category captain for this department, responsible for category management insights and assortment recommendations.']
  backroom_capacity_cubic_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total storage capacity in cubic feet allocated to this department in the backroom area.']
  backroom_cooler_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the department has dedicated refrigerated storage in the backroom for cold chain inventory management.']
  backroom_freezer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the department has dedicated frozen storage in the backroom for frozen product inventory management.']
  backroom_storage_zone varchar [note: 'Designated backroom storage area identifier assigned to this department for inventory staging and replenishment.']
  cooler_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of refrigerated display doors allocated to this department for temperature-controlled products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this department record was first created in the system.']
  department_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the department (e.g., Produce, Bakery, Deli, Pharmacy, Frozen Foods, General Merchandise).']
  department_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the department, typically a 2-4 digit code used in store operations and reporting.']
  department_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the department within the store.']
  department_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the department based on operational characteristics: perishable (fresh produce, meat, dairy), center_store (shelf-stable goods), service (deli, bakery, floral), pharmacy (prescription and OTC medications), fuel (fuel center operations).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this department configuration ceased to be effective, null for currently active departments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this department configuration became effective in the store.']
  endcap_count varchar [note: 'Number of end-of-aisle display positions (endcaps) allocated to this department for promotional merchandise.']
  fifo_lane_count varchar [note: 'Number of FIFO rotation lanes configured in the backroom storage zone to ensure proper inventory rotation and minimize spoilage.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code assigned to this department for financial accounting and cost allocation.']
  gondola_count varchar [note: 'Number of gondola shelving units allocated to this department for product display.']
  haccp_classification varchar [note: 'HACCP food safety classification indicating whether the department handles critical control point products requiring enhanced food safety monitoring and documentation.']
  labor_standard_hours_per_week decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard weekly labor hours allocated to this department based on sales volume and operational complexity, used for workforce scheduling and labor budgeting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this department record was last updated in the system.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this department carries USDA organic certified products requiring special handling and segregation.']
  planogram_family varchar [note: 'Planogram template family or category assigned to this department for visual merchandise display planning and fixture layout standardization.']
  pos_department_code varchar [note: 'Department code used in POS transaction processing and sales reporting systems for revenue attribution.']
  private_label_focus_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this department has a strategic focus on private label (store-owned brand) product assortment and merchandising.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center code assigned to this department for profitability analysis and management reporting.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Primary inventory replenishment method used for this department: CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering), manual ordering, DSD (Direct Store Delivery), or cross-dock distribution.']
  shrink_budget_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target shrink rate (inventory loss from theft, spoilage, or damage) expressed as a percentage of sales, used for loss prevention planning and performance evaluation.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this department are generally eligible for purchase using SNAP/EBT benefits.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total sales floor area allocated to this department in square feet, used for space productivity analysis and planogram planning.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Primary temperature control zone classification for products in this department, critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this department are generally eligible for purchase using WIC benefits.']

  Note: 'Operational department within a store such as Produce, Bakery, Deli, Pharmacy, Floral, Frozen Foods, or General Merchandise. Captures department number, name, type classification (perishable, center-store, service), assigned manager, allocated square footage, gondola count, endcap count, cooler door count, planogram family, shrink budget target, HACCP classification, and backroom storage zone configuration (capacity, cooler/freezer flags, FIFO lane count). Supports labor scheduling, category performance reporting, replenishment planning, food safety compliance, and fixture inventory tracking.'
}

Table store.operating_hours {
  operating_hours_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operating hours record. Primary key for this entity.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location to which these operating hours apply. Links to the store master data.']
  bakery_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the in-store bakery department closes for fresh baked goods service. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if bakery is not available at this location.']
  bakery_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the in-store bakery department opens for fresh baked goods service. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if bakery is not available at this location.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for store closure when is_closed is True. Holiday for recognized holidays, weather for severe weather events, emergency for unplanned incidents, renovation for planned maintenance, staffing for labor shortages, inventory for physical inventory counts, special_event for community events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: holiday|weather|emergency|renovation|staffing|inventory|special_event — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this operating hours record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_service_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the customer service desk closes for returns, money orders, lottery, and other services. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if customer service desk is not staffed separately.']
  customer_service_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the customer service desk opens for returns, money orders, lottery, and other services. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if customer service desk is not staffed separately.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'The day of the week for recurring weekly operating hours schedules. Used in conjunction with or independently from effective_date for standard weekly patterns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deli_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the in-store deli department closes for fresh food service. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if deli is not available at this location.']
  deli_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the in-store deli department opens for fresh food service. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if deli is not available at this location.']
  delivery_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'The latest time by which same-day or next-day delivery orders must be placed for fulfillment from this store location. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Supports last mile delivery (LMD) operations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date for which these operating hours are effective. Used for date-specific overrides such as holidays or special events.']
  fuel_center_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the fuel center closes for customer fueling. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null indicates fuel center is closed or not available at this location.']
  fuel_center_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the fuel center opens for customer fueling. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null indicates fuel center is closed or not available at this location.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'The name of the holiday when schedule_type is holiday. Examples include Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Years Day, Independence Day. Used for customer communication and workforce scheduling.']
  is_24_hour_store boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store operates 24 hours per day. True if store never closes, False if store has defined open and close times.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is completely closed on this date or day of week. True if closed all day, False if open for any period.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this operating hours record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about these operating hours. May include reasons for changes, special instructions, or customer communication details.']
  online_ordering_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether online ordering for pickup or delivery is available during these operating hours. True if omnichannel order fulfillment is active, False if disabled.']
  operating_hours_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this operating hours record. Active indicates currently in effect, inactive for historical records, pending for future scheduled changes, superseded when replaced by a newer record.']
  pharmacy_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the in-store pharmacy closes for prescription services. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null indicates pharmacy is closed or not available at this location.']
  pharmacy_lunch_break_end varchar [note: 'The end time of the pharmacy lunch break closure period. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Required by some State Boards of Pharmacy for single-pharmacist locations.']
  pharmacy_lunch_break_start varchar [note: 'The start time of the pharmacy lunch break closure period. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Required by some State Boards of Pharmacy for single-pharmacist locations.']
  pharmacy_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the in-store pharmacy opens for prescription services. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null indicates pharmacy is closed or not available at this location.']
  pickup_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'The latest time when curbside or in-store pickup orders can be collected by customers. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Supports omnichannel click-and-collect fulfillment slot availability.']
  pickup_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'The earliest time when curbside or in-store pickup orders can be collected by customers. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Supports omnichannel click-and-collect fulfillment slot availability.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for resolving conflicts when multiple operating hours records apply to the same store and date. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Date-specific overrides typically have priority 1, standard weekly schedules have priority 10.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when these operating hours were published to customer-facing channels. Used for audit trail and customer communication tracking.']
  published_to_website boolean [note: 'Indicates whether these operating hours have been published to the customer-facing store locator and website. True if visible to customers, False if internal only.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operating hours schedule. Standard represents normal weekly hours, holiday represents holiday overrides, special_event for promotional events, temporary for short-term changes, seasonal for recurring seasonal adjustments, emergency for unplanned closures or modifications.']
  senior_hours_end_time timestamp [note: 'The end time for dedicated senior citizen and vulnerable population shopping hours. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if no senior hours are offered.']
  senior_hours_start_time timestamp [note: 'The start time for dedicated senior citizen and vulnerable population shopping hours. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null if no senior hours are offered.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this operating hours data. Examples include Oracle Retail, SAP Retail, or proprietary store management systems.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'The name of the special event when schedule_type is special_event. Examples include Grand Opening, Anniversary Sale, Community Day. Used for promotional planning and extended hours justification.']
  store_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the main store closes to customer shopping. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null indicates store is closed for the day.']
  store_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time when the main store opens for customer shopping. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation. Null indicates store is closed for the day.']

  Note: 'Scheduled and actual operating hours for each store location by day of week and date, including holiday overrides. Captures open time, close time, pharmacy hours, fuel center hours, pickup window hours, and special event hours. Supports omnichannel order fulfillment slot availability, customer-facing store locator, and workforce scheduling.'
}

Table store.service_capability {
  service_capability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service capability record. Primary key.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where this service capability is offered.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the service capability at this store location. Active means the service is currently available to customers.']
  average_fulfillment_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time in minutes required to fulfill a customer order or transaction using this service capability. Used for customer communication and capacity planning.']
  capability_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping for the service capability to support filtering and reporting.']
  capability_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of service capability (e.g., CURBSIDE_PICKUP, HOME_DELIVERY, PHARMACY_DRIVE_THRU, COINSTAR, WESTERN_UNION, STARBUCKS_KIOSK, SUSHI_BAR, FLORAL_CUSTOM, EBT_SNAP_WIC, LOTTERY, MONEY_ORDER, BOTTLE_RETURN).']
  capability_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service capability, including what it offers and any special conditions or limitations.']
  capability_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service capability for customer-facing display and internal reference.']
  capacity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum concurrent capacity for this service capability (e.g., maximum number of pickup orders that can be processed simultaneously, number of drive-thru lanes, kiosk stations). Null if no specific limit applies.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the capacity limit (e.g., orders per hour, concurrent customers, transaction slots).']
  compliance_certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff must complete specific compliance training or certification to operate this service capability (e.g., HIPAA training for pharmacy, food safety certification for food service).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service capability record was first created in the system.']
  customer_facing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability should be displayed to customers in store locator tools, mobile apps, and marketing materials.']
  daily_capacity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of service transactions or orders that can be fulfilled in a single day. Used for omnichannel order routing and capacity planning.']
  display_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for ordering service capabilities in customer-facing displays. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this service capability was or will be discontinued at the store location. Null indicates the service is ongoing with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this service capability became or will become available at the store location.']
  equipment_description varchar [note: 'Description of the specialized equipment or infrastructure required to support this service capability, including make, model, or configuration details.']
  equipment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialized equipment or infrastructure is required to deliver this service capability (e.g., refrigerated pickup lockers, drive-thru window, kiosk hardware).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment or infrastructure for this service capability was installed at the store location.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance or inspection was performed on the equipment or infrastructure supporting this service capability.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service capability record was most recently modified.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory license or permit expires and must be renewed to continue offering this service capability.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory license or permit number authorizing the store to offer this service capability. Confidential business information.']
  license_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory license or permit is required to offer this service capability (e.g., pharmacy license, lottery license, money transmitter license).']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next scheduled maintenance or inspection is due for the equipment or infrastructure supporting this service capability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about this service capability, including special instructions, known issues, or temporary conditions.']
  omnichannel_routing_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability is integrated with the Order Management System (OMS) for automated omnichannel order routing and fulfillment assignment.']
  oms_capability_code varchar [note: 'Code used by the Order Management System to identify and route orders to this service capability. Must match OMS configuration.']
  operating_hours_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the operating hours for this service capability if different from store hours (e.g., Pharmacy: Mon-Fri 9am-9pm, Sat 9am-6pm, Sun 10am-6pm).']
  operating_hours_same_as_store_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability operates during the same hours as the store itself, or has different operating hours.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue from this service capability that is shared with a vendor partner. Confidential business information used for financial reporting and vendor management.']
  seasonal_end_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal service capability typically ends each year. Null if not seasonal.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability is offered seasonally rather than year-round (e.g., holiday gift wrapping, seasonal outdoor plant sales).']
  seasonal_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal service capability typically begins each year. Null if not seasonal.']
  sla_target_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Target service level agreement time in minutes for fulfilling customer orders or transactions using this service capability. Used for performance monitoring and customer expectations.']
  staffing_requirement_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of full-time equivalent employees required to operate this service capability during standard operating hours. Used for labor planning and scheduling.']
  vendor_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party vendor or partner that provides or supports this service capability (e.g., Coinstar, Western Union, Starbucks, Instacart). Null if operated directly by the retailer.']

  Note: 'Catalog of service capabilities available at a store location, such as Curbside Pickup, Home Delivery, Click-and-Collect, Pharmacy Drive-Through, Coinstar, Western Union, Starbucks Kiosk, Sushi Bar, Floral Custom Orders, EBT/SNAP/WIC acceptance, lottery sales, money orders, and bottle return. Each record defines the capability type, activation status, capacity limits (e.g., max concurrent pickup orders), staffing requirements, equipment dependencies, and effective date range. Enables omnichannel order routing, customer-facing store locator feature display, labor planning, and site selection analysis.'
}

Table store.mfc_profile {
  mfc_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the micro-fulfillment center profile record. Primary key.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store location with which this MFC is co-located or adjacent. Links the MFC to its parent store for operational and reporting purposes.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Robotics vendor contracts are managed per fulfillment center; linking enables tracking of vendor performance, costs, and compliance for each store\'s MFC.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the MFC became operational and began fulfilling customer orders. Used for capacity planning and performance tracking.']
  ambient_zone_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has an ambient temperature zone for storing shelf-stable products (dry goods, canned items, non-perishables).']
  average_inventory_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling average percentage of inventory records that match physical counts during cycle counts. Key metric for operational excellence.']
  average_order_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling average percentage of orders fulfilled without errors (wrong items, missing items, damaged items). Critical customer satisfaction metric.']
  average_order_fulfillment_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean time in minutes from order receipt to order ready for pickup or dispatch. Critical SLA metric for omnichannel fulfillment.']
  average_pick_time_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean time in seconds to pick a single item from inventory. Key performance indicator for MFC efficiency and throughput modeling.']
  chilled_zone_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has a refrigerated zone for storing chilled products (dairy, fresh produce, deli items) typically maintained at 32-40°F.']
  cold_chain_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has automated temperature monitoring systems for chilled and frozen zones to ensure cold chain integrity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MFC profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  daily_order_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customer orders the MFC can fulfill in a 24-hour period under normal operating conditions. Used for order routing and capacity planning.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the MFC ceased operations, if applicable. Null for active MFCs. Used for historical analysis and capacity planning.']
  food_safety_inspection_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score from the most recent food safety inspection, typically on a 0-100 scale. Higher scores indicate better compliance.']
  frozen_zone_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has a frozen storage zone for frozen foods, ice cream, and other products requiring temperatures at or below 0°F.']
  fulfillment_service_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fulfillment services supported by this MFC (e.g., curbside pickup, home delivery, ship-from-store, same-day delivery). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: curbside_pickup|home_delivery|ship_from_store|same_day_delivery|next_day_delivery|scheduled_delivery|locker_pickup — promote to reference product]']
  haccp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has achieved HACCP certification for food safety management, required for facilities handling perishable foods.']
  last_food_safety_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent food safety inspection by regulatory authorities (FDA, USDA, or state health department). Used for compliance tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MFC profile record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  mfc_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the MFC, used in operational systems and reporting. Format: MFC followed by 6 digits.']
  mfc_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the MFC, typically including geographic or store reference for easy identification by operations teams.']
  mfc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MFC based on its operational model: automated (fully robotic picking and packing), semi-automated (hybrid human and robotic), or manual (human-only fulfillment).']
  number_of_packing_stations varchar [note: 'Count of workstations where picked orders are packed into bags or boxes for customer delivery or pickup. Used for labor planning.']
  number_of_picking_stations varchar [note: 'Count of workstations where associates or robots pick items into customer orders. Key metric for throughput capacity planning.']
  number_of_robots varchar [note: 'Total count of robotic units (picking robots, transport robots, sorting robots) deployed in the MFC. Zero for manual facilities.']
  oms_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC is integrated with the enterprise Order Management System for real-time order routing and fulfillment orchestration.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Daily end time for MFC operations in 24-hour HH:MM format. Used for order routing and labor scheduling.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time for MFC operations in 24-hour HH:MM format. Used for order routing and labor scheduling.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MFC indicating whether it is actively fulfilling orders, temporarily offline, being built, or permanently closed.']
  peak_hourly_order_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders the MFC can process in a single hour during peak demand periods. Critical for real-time order routing decisions.']
  picking_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Floor area dedicated to order picking operations in square feet. Used for productivity analysis and space utilization metrics.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC uses RFID technology for inventory tracking and order accuracy verification.']
  robotics_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the robotics or automation technology provider for automated or semi-automated MFCs (e.g., AutoStore, Dematic, Ocado). Null for manual MFCs.']
  sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique SKUs stocked in the MFC at any given time. Indicates assortment breadth and inventory complexity.']
  total_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the MFC facility in square feet, including all operational zones, staging areas, and support spaces.']
  total_tote_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of totes (storage containers) that the MFC can hold at any given time. Key metric for inventory capacity planning.']
  twenty_four_hour_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC operates continuously 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. True for round-the-clock facilities.']
  wms_instance_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific WMS instance or tenant serving this MFC. Used for technical integration and troubleshooting.']
  wms_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the WMS platform integrated with this MFC (e.g., Manhattan Associates, Blue Yonder, SAP EWM). Used for system integration and support.']

  Note: 'Master profile for a Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC) co-located with or adjacent to a store. Captures MFC ID, associated store location, MFC type (automated/manual), total tote capacity, daily order throughput capacity, temperature zone configuration (ambient, chilled, frozen), WMS system integration reference, activation date, and operational status. Supports omnichannel fulfillment routing and capacity planning.'
}

Table store.remodel_project {
  remodel_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the store remodel, expansion, or refresh project.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location undergoing remodel or expansion.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when remodel project was completed and store resumed normal operations.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred on the remodel project in USD. Tracked for budget variance analysis and capital planning accuracy.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when remodel construction or implementation activities commenced.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the remodel project was formally approved by executive leadership or capital planning committee.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the remodel project was cancelled or terminated before completion.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the remodel project was cancelled (e.g., budget constraints, store closure decision, market conditions, permitting issues).']
  capital_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total approved capital budget allocated for the remodel project in USD. Includes construction, equipment, fixtures, and technology costs.']
  closure_end_date date [note: 'Date when store reopens to customers after remodel-related closure period.']
  closure_start_date date [note: 'Date when store closure begins if full closure is required for the remodel project.']
  comp_sales_exclusion_end_date date [note: 'Date when the store is reintroduced into comp sales calculations after remodel completion and stabilization period.']
  comp_sales_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store should be excluded from comp sales (same-store sales) calculations during the remodel period due to operational disruption. True means exclude from comp sales reporting; false means include in comp sales.']
  comp_sales_exclusion_start_date date [note: 'Date when the store begins to be excluded from comp sales calculations due to remodel disruption.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary general contractor or construction firm responsible for executing the remodel project.']
  contractor_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or contract identifier for the contractor agreement associated with this remodel project.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this remodel project record was first created in the system.']
  departments_impacted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of store departments affected by the remodel project (e.g., produce, deli, bakery, pharmacy, fuel center, front end).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the remodel project is planned and budgeted (e.g., 2024).']
  inspection_completion_date date [note: 'Date when final building inspection was completed and approved by local authorities, allowing store to resume operations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this remodel project record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments about the remodel project for internal tracking and communication.']
  payback_period_months varchar [note: 'Expected number of months for the remodel investment to be recovered through incremental sales and margin improvements.']
  permit_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the building permit was issued by local authorities for the remodel project.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Local government building permit number issued for the remodel construction work.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for remodel project to be completed and store to resume normal operations.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for remodel project to begin construction or implementation activities.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Relative priority ranking of this remodel project within the annual capital plan portfolio. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  project_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal project manager or construction manager responsible for overseeing the remodel project.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the remodel project for business communication and reporting.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique project number for tracking and reference in capital planning systems.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the remodel project. Planning indicates initial scoping and budgeting; approved means capital budget has been authorized; in_progress indicates active construction; on_hold reflects temporary suspension; completed means project is finished and store is operational; cancelled indicates project was terminated before completion.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the remodel project scope. Full remodel includes complete store renovation; department refresh targets specific departments (produce, pharmacy, deli); technology upgrade focuses on POS, self-checkout, or digital signage; ADA compliance addresses accessibility requirements; expansion adds square footage; format conversion changes store format (e.g., conventional to fresh format).']
  roi_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target return on investment percentage for the remodel project, used in capital planning and project approval decisions.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the remodel project scope, including specific work to be performed, fixtures to be replaced, technology to be installed, and layout changes.']
  square_footage_after varchar [note: 'Total retail square footage of the store after the remodel or expansion project is completed.']
  square_footage_before varchar [note: 'Total retail square footage of the store before the remodel or expansion project.']
  store_closure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store must be fully closed to customers during the remodel period. True means full closure; false means store remains open with partial operations.']

  Note: 'Tracks store remodel, expansion, or refresh projects including project ID, store location, project type (full remodel, department refresh, technology upgrade, ADA compliance), planned start date, planned completion date, actual completion date, capital budget, contractor reference, and project status. Supports capital planning, store closure scheduling, and comp sales exclusion flagging during remodel periods.'
}

Table store.site_selection {
  site_selection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the site selection evaluation record. Primary key for the site selection entity.']
  format_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_format. Business justification: Site selection records need to specify the intended store format; FK provides this relationship.']
  annual_lease_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated annual lease cost in US dollars including base rent and common area maintenance (CAM). Null if the site is a purchase.']
  approval_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the site selection approval workflow. Tracks progression through the capital allocation and real estate decision process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_review|feasibility_study|due_diligence|executive_review|approved|rejected|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  candidate_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the candidate site location.']
  candidate_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information for the candidate site.']
  candidate_city varchar [note: 'City name for the candidate site location.']
  candidate_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the candidate site location.']
  candidate_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the candidate site location.']
  candidate_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the candidate site location.']
  cannibalization_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0-100) representing the estimated risk that the new store will negatively impact sales at existing nearby stores. Higher scores indicate greater cannibalization risk.']
  competitor_count_within_trade_area varchar [note: 'Number of direct competitor stores within the defined trade area. Used for competitive intensity assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the site selection record was first created in the system. Used for pipeline tracking and audit trail.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date when the final go/no-go decision was made for the candidate site. Null if still under evaluation.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Final decision outcome for the site selection evaluation. Approved sites move to the store opening pipeline.']
  distance_to_nearest_competitor_miles decimal(6,2) [note: 'Distance in miles from the candidate site to the nearest competitor store. Used for competitive positioning analysis.']
  distance_to_nearest_own_store_miles decimal(6,2) [note: 'Distance in miles from the candidate site to the nearest existing company-owned store. Used to assess cannibalization risk and market coverage gaps.']
  environmental_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of Phase I and Phase II environmental site assessments. Required for EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) compliance and risk mitigation, particularly for fuel center sites.']
  environmental_issues_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether environmental concerns or contamination were identified during site assessment. True if issues exist that require remediation or further investigation.']
  estimated_annual_sales_volume decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected annual sales revenue in US dollars for the candidate location based on trade area analysis and comparable store performance. Used for ROI (Return on Investment) and capital allocation decisions.']
  estimated_build_out_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected cost in US dollars for construction, fixtures, equipment, and store build-out. Used for total capital investment calculation.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing qualitative observations, risks, opportunities, and strategic considerations for the site evaluation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the site selection record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the candidate site in decimal degrees. Used for proximity analysis and trade area mapping.']
  lease_or_purchase_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the site will be leased, purchased, or developed as a build-to-suit arrangement. Impacts capital requirements and financial modeling.']
  lease_term_years varchar [note: 'Length of the proposed lease term in years. Null if the site is a purchase. Used for lease obligation tracking and financial planning.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the candidate site in decimal degrees. Used for proximity analysis and trade area mapping.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region designation for the candidate site. Used for regional portfolio planning and market penetration analysis.']
  parking_spaces_count varchar [note: 'Number of available parking spaces at the candidate site. Critical for customer convenience and local zoning compliance.']
  permitting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of building and operational permits required for store opening. Includes construction, occupancy, and health department permits.']
  planned_square_footage varchar [note: 'Total planned retail and operational square footage for the candidate store location.']
  projected_opening_date date [note: 'Target date for store opening if the site is approved. Used for capital planning and operational readiness scheduling.']
  purchase_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Proposed purchase price in US dollars for the site and property. Null if the site is a lease.']
  real_estate_broker varchar [note: 'Name of the real estate broker or firm representing the site opportunity. Used for vendor relationship tracking.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the candidate site in the real estate pipeline. Used for tracking and reference across real estate and capital planning systems.']
  target_store_format varchar [note: 'Planned store format type for the candidate location. Determines size, assortment, and service capabilities. MFC (Micro-Fulfillment Center) represents automated fulfillment facilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: supermarket|hypermarket|neighborhood_market|express|pharmacy|fuel_center|mfc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trade_area_household_density decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of households per square mile within the trade area. Indicates market density and potential customer base.']
  trade_area_median_income decimal(12,2) [note: 'Median household income in US dollars within the trade area. Key demographic indicator for assortment planning and pricing strategy.']
  trade_area_population varchar [note: 'Total population count within the defined trade area radius for the candidate site. Used for demand forecasting and market sizing.']
  traffic_count_daily varchar [note: 'Average daily vehicle traffic count on the primary road adjacent to the candidate site. Key visibility and accessibility metric.']
  zoning_status varchar [note: 'Current status of zoning approval for the intended retail use. Critical gate for site viability and timeline planning.']

  Note: 'Site selection evaluation record for candidate new store locations in the real estate pipeline. Captures site ID, candidate address and coordinates, target store format, trade area demographics (population, median income, household density), estimated annual sales volume, proximity to existing stores and competitors, cannibalization risk score, lease/purchase terms summary, zoning and permitting status, environmental assessment status, approval stage, and decision date. Supports real estate strategy, new store opening pipeline management, and capital allocation decisions.'
}

Table store.location_event {
  location_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for location_event']
  primary_location_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initially reported or logged the event. Links to employee master data.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the event occurred. Links to the store master data.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor or contractor involved in the event response or resolution (e.g., HVAC repair company, security firm, cleaning service). Null if no external vendor was involved.']
  affected_departments varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of store departments impacted by the event (e.g., Produce, Meat, Deli, Pharmacy, Bakery, Frozen Foods, Dairy). Used to assess scope of operational disruption.']
  comp_sales_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event should trigger an adjustment to comparable store sales (comp sales) reporting. True if the event significantly impacted sales and should be excluded from comp sales calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this event record was first created in the system. Represents the initial logging of the event.']
  customer_communication_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer-facing communication was issued regarding the event (e.g., store closure notice, service disruption alert). True if communication was sent.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the event in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamp. Used for operational impact analysis and SLA tracking.']
  estimated_sales_impact_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact on store sales revenue in US dollars due to the event. Used for insurance claims, comp sales adjustments, and financial reporting. Negative values indicate lost revenue.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the event type for reporting and analysis purposes. Operational events affect store operations, regulatory events involve compliance inspections, safety events involve hazards, emergency events require immediate response, promotional events are customer-facing, and maintenance events involve planned repairs.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the store event ended or is scheduled to end. Null if the event is ongoing or end time is not yet determined.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique event number assigned to this location event for tracking and reference purposes. Format: EVT-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the store event began or is scheduled to begin. Represents the actual or planned start of the event occurrence.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the store event. Tracks progression from initial scheduling through resolution and closure.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the store event. Indicates the nature of the operational event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grand_opening|temporary_closure|power_outage|refrigeration_failure|health_inspection|osha_visit|fire_marshal_inspection|bomb_threat|active_shooter_lockdown|community_event|remodel|weather_closure|emergency_closure|equipment_failure|pest_control|security_incident — promote to reference product]']
  follow_up_action_items varchar [note: 'List of follow-up actions, preventive measures, or corrective tasks identified as a result of the event. Used for continuous improvement and risk mitigation.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the event impact on store operations. Critical events cause complete store closure or major safety hazards, major events significantly disrupt operations, minor events have limited impact, and informational events are for record-keeping only.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of a regulatory inspection event. Passed indicates full compliance, failed indicates violations found, conditional indicates minor issues requiring follow-up, and pending indicates results not yet finalized.']
  insurance_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for any insurance claim filed as a result of the event. Used to track financial recovery and claim status.']
  media_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event received media attention or press coverage. True if the event was reported in news media or social media.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes, comments, or observations about the event that do not fit into other structured fields. Used for supplementary context and documentation.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required notifications were sent to corporate headquarters, regional management, or regulatory agencies. True if notifications were completed per protocol.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event notification was sent to relevant stakeholders. Used for compliance tracking and response time analysis.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or governing body involved in the event (e.g., FDA, USDA, DEA, State Board of Pharmacy, OSHA, EPA, Fire Marshal, Health Department). Applicable for inspection and compliance events.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes describing how the event was resolved, actions taken, and final outcome. Captures the response and remediation activities performed.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the underlying cause of the event. Used for trend analysis and preventive action planning.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause of the event. Provides context for understanding why the event occurred and what factors contributed.']
  store_closure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event resulted in a complete store closure. True if the store was closed to customers during the event, False otherwise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this event record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of violations or deficiencies identified during a regulatory inspection event. Zero indicates no violations found.']
  violation_details varchar [note: 'Detailed description of violations or deficiencies identified during a regulatory inspection, including citation codes and corrective action requirements.']

  Note: 'Record of significant operational events at a store location such as grand openings, temporary closures (weather, emergency, remodel), power outages, refrigeration failures, health department inspections, OSHA visits, fire marshal inspections, bomb threats, active shooter lockdowns, or special community events. Captures event type, start datetime, end datetime, duration, impact severity (critical/major/minor), affected departments, estimated sales impact, root cause category, resolution notes, and follow-up action items. Supports operational continuity planning, insurance claims, comp sales adjustment flagging, and regulatory compliance reporting.'
}

Table store.health_inspection {
  health_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the health inspection record. Primary key for the health inspection entity.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the health inspection was conducted.']
  closure_end_date date [note: 'Date when the store or department was permitted to reopen after corrective actions were verified and approved by the inspecting agency.']
  closure_ordered boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inspecting agency ordered immediate closure of the store or specific departments due to critical food safety violations posing imminent health hazard. True indicates closure ordered, False indicates no closure.']
  closure_start_date date [note: 'Date when the store or department closure became effective due to health violations.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which all corrective actions must be completed to address violations identified during the inspection.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions required to address violations, including specific steps, responsible parties, and timelines for remediation.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective actions are required to address violations identified during the inspection. True indicates corrective actions needed, False indicates no actions required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the health inspection record was first created in the system.']
  critical_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical violations identified during the inspection. Critical violations are food safety issues that pose immediate risk to public health (e.g., improper food temperatures, cross-contamination, inadequate handwashing).']
  cross_contamination_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for preventing cross-contamination between raw and ready-to-eat foods, proper separation of food preparation areas, and equipment sanitation.']
  departments_inspected varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of store departments included in the inspection scope (e.g., Deli, Bakery, Meat, Seafood, Produce, Pharmacy, Prepared Foods, Grocery).']
  facility_maintenance_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for physical facility maintenance including floors, walls, ceilings, lighting, ventilation, and equipment condition.']
  food_temperature_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for proper food storage and holding temperatures. Critical for preventing bacterial growth and foodborne illness.']
  haccp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of compliance with HACCP food safety management system principles. HACCP is a systematic preventive approach to food safety addressing biological, chemical, and physical hazards. Compliant indicates full adherence, non-compliant indicates violations, not-applicable indicates HACCP not required for this location, partial indicates some HACCP elements in place.']
  handwashing_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for proper handwashing facilities, employee hand hygiene practices, and personal cleanliness standards.']
  inspecting_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or health department that conducted the inspection (e.g., County Health Department, FDA, USDA).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the health inspection was conducted at the store location.']
  inspection_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the inspection in minutes, from start to completion.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique inspection number assigned by the inspecting agency or internal tracking system.']
  inspection_report_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the full inspection report document stored in the document management system or public health department website.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the health inspection. Scheduled indicates future inspection, in-progress indicates active inspection, completed indicates inspection finished, passed indicates no critical violations, failed indicates critical violations found, pending-review indicates awaiting final determination, cancelled indicates inspection did not occur. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in-progress|completed|passed|failed|pending-review|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of health inspection conducted. Routine inspections are scheduled periodic reviews, follow-up inspections verify corrective actions, complaint-driven inspections respond to customer or employee reports, pre-opening inspections occur before new store launch, special inspections address specific concerns, and reinspections verify compliance after violations.']
  inspector_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or badge number of the health inspector assigned by the inspecting agency.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the health inspector who conducted the inspection.']
  letter_grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade assigned to the inspection based on overall score, if applicable in the jurisdiction (A=excellent, B=good, C=satisfactory, D=marginal, F=failing).']
  non_critical_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of non-critical violations identified during the inspection. Non-critical violations are food safety issues that do not pose immediate risk but require correction (e.g., minor equipment maintenance, documentation gaps).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or comments from the inspector or store management regarding the inspection, violations, or corrective actions.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the inspection representing overall food safety compliance. Scoring scale varies by jurisdiction (e.g., 0-100 scale where higher is better, or deduction-based scoring).']
  pest_control_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for pest prevention and control measures, including evidence of rodents, insects, or other pests.']
  posted_at_store boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inspection results or grade card has been posted at the store location as required by local regulations. True indicates posted, False indicates not posted.']
  public_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inspection results are subject to public disclosure or posting requirements per local health department regulations. True indicates public disclosure required, False indicates confidential.']
  reinspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for follow-up reinspection to verify that corrective actions have been completed and violations have been resolved.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a follow-up reinspection is required to verify corrective actions have been completed. True indicates reinspection needed, False indicates no reinspection required.']
  score_scale varchar [note: 'Description of the scoring scale used for the overall score (e.g., 0-100 scale, A-F letter grade, pass/fail, deduction-based).']
  total_violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of violations (critical and non-critical combined) identified during the inspection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the health inspection record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Record of food safety and health department inspections conducted at a store location. Captures inspection ID, inspection date, inspecting agency (local health department, FDA, USDA), inspection type (routine, follow-up, complaint-driven), overall score, critical violation count, non-critical violation count, HACCP compliance status, corrective action plan, and re-inspection date. Supports FDA/USDA regulatory compliance and HACCP program management.'
}

Table store.lease {
  lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lease']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location covered by this lease agreement.']
  base_rent_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly or annual base rent amount in US dollars before additional charges.']
  base_rent_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which base rent payments are due.']
  cam_charges_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Common Area Maintenance charges in US dollars covering shared facility costs such as landscaping, parking lot maintenance, and common utilities.']
  co_tenancy_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease includes a co-tenancy clause that provides rent relief or termination rights if anchor tenants vacate the property.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement becomes effective and rent obligations begin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was first created in the system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digitally stored lease agreement document for reference and audit purposes.']
  early_termination_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease agreement includes an early termination clause allowing tenant to exit before expiration date.']
  early_termination_penalty_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty amount in US dollars that tenant must pay if exercising early termination clause.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Business category or industry segment protected under the exclusivity provision (e.g., grocery, pharmacy, fuel).']
  exclusivity_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease includes an exclusivity clause preventing landlord from leasing to competing businesses in the same property.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement was signed by both landlord and tenant.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement term ends unless renewed or extended.']
  guarantor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity or individual providing financial guarantee for the lease obligations.']
  insurance_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual insurance premium amount in US dollars that tenant is responsible for under the lease terms.']
  landlord_contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary contact person name at the landlord organization for lease administration.']
  landlord_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for landlord contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  landlord_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the landlord or property owner entity that holds title to the leased premises.']
  landlord_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for landlord contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  lease_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known lease agreement number or reference code used by landlord and tenant for identification.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lease agreement.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease agreement type for accounting and operational purposes.']
  leased_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total square footage of the leased premises as specified in the lease agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional lease terms, special conditions, or administrative notes.']
  property_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the leased property street address. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the leased property street address (suite, unit, building). Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_city varchar [note: 'City where the leased property is located. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the leased property is located.']
  property_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the leased property. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the leased property is located. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_tax_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual property tax amount in US dollars that tenant is responsible for under the lease terms.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to lease expiration that tenant must provide written notice to exercise renewal option.']
  renewal_option_count varchar [note: 'Number of renewal option periods available to the tenant after the initial lease term expires.']
  renewal_option_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each renewal option period if exercised by the tenant.']
  rent_escalation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual percentage rate at which base rent increases if escalation type is percentage-based.']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Type of rent escalation clause defining how base rent increases over the lease term.']
  security_deposit_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Security deposit amount in US dollars held by landlord to cover potential damages or unpaid rent.']
  sox_disclosure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease agreement requires disclosure in financial statements under Sarbanes-Oxley Act compliance requirements.']
