  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Real estate lease record for store locations that are leased rather than owned. Captures lease ID, store location, landlord entity name, lease commencement date, lease expiration date, renewal option terms, base rent amount, CAM (Common Area Maintenance) charges, rent escalation schedule, co-tenancy clauses, exclusivity provisions, and lease status. Supports real estate portfolio management, SOX financial disclosure, and capital vs. operating lease classification.'
}

Table store.energy_management {
  energy_management_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each energy management record.']
  prior_period_energy_management_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on energy_management (prior_period_energy_management_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store to which the energy data applies.']
  average_demand_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average demand over the reporting period, in kilowatts.']
  carbon_emission_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated CO₂ emissions associated with the energy consumption, in kilograms.']
  consumption_amount decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of energy consumed during the period.']
  consumption_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the consumption amount.']
  consumption_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference between actual consumption and target.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cost per consumption unit (e.g., USD per kWh).']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of the energy consumed, expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score (0‑1) indicating the quality of the measurement data.']
  energy_efficiency_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the store\'s energy efficiency based on internal benchmarks.']
  energy_management_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the energy record.']
  energy_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of energy for the measured consumption.']
  epa_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is required for EPA environmental reporting.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the meter was last calibrated.']
  load_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Ratio of average demand to peak demand, indicating utilization efficiency.']
  maintenance_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent maintenance activity on the meter.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain the energy reading.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the energy measurement was recorded.']
  meter_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the energy meter or sub‑meter that generated the reading.']
  meter_location varchar [note: 'Physical location of the meter within the store (e.g., HVAC zone, refrigeration room).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the measurement.']
  peak_demand_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum demand recorded during the period, expressed in kilowatts.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting period for the measurement.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period for the measurement.']
  reading_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reading is a direct measurement or an estimate.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal or calendar period label (e.g., 2023-Q1) for regulatory reporting.']
  sensor_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the energy sensor.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the measurement (e.g., WMS, utility provider).']
  sustainability_target_flag boolean [note: 'True if the record is compared against a sustainability target.']
  target_consumption_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Energy consumption target for the reporting period, in kilowatt‑hours.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  utility_type varchar [note: 'Category of utility being measured (e.g., HVAC, refrigeration, lighting).']

  Note: 'Store energy consumption monitoring and management records tracking HVAC, refrigeration, lighting, and utility usage by store location for sustainability reporting and cost optimization.'
}

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the banner.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual store locations that belong to this banner.']
  target_customer_group varchar [note: 'Primary customer demographic group the banner is designed to attract.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the banner based on its market positioning and ownership model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the banner record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for banner. Referenced by banner_id.'

Table inventory.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each stock position record.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Category‑Level Stock Position Reporting requires each stock position to be tied to its product category for aggregated inventory metrics.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_center. Business justification: Store performance dashboard combines grocery stock positions with fuel center sales; linking each stock position to its fuel center enables per‑store profitability analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Holding cost allocation ties each stock position to a cost center for budgeting and profitability analysis per store.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Stock position is physically located within a store department; linking enables department‑level inventory visibility.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.item.item_id — Core inventory-to-product join enabling stock queries by product attributes (category, brand, vendor). Fundamental to replenishment, OOS analysis, and shrink reporting.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: PHARMACY STOCK AUDIT: ties each stock position to the pharmacy location for drug inventory compliance and daily stock‑reconciliation reports.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotional Allocation: inventory positions are earmarked for a campaign to ensure shelf space and display compliance.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key referencing the product (SKU) whose inventory is tracked.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Link stock_position to its physical storage location for precise inventory location tracking.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'FK to store.store_location.store_location_id — Links stock positions to physical store locations for store-level inventory visibility, replenishment triggers, and OOS alerting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT PLANNING: Stock position reports need primary supplier to calculate lead times and order allocation per SKU.']
  available_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units that can be sold to customers (on‑hand minus reserved).']
  cold_chain_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when temperature‑controlled items meet required temperature range.']
  estimated_lost_sales_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of sales lost due to the OOS condition.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the product expires; applicable to perishable goods.']
  fifo_lifo_indicator varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the SKU is managed using First‑In‑First‑Out or Last‑In‑First‑Out rotation.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units currently being moved between locations (e.g., from DC to store) and not yet on‑hand.']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is a perishable item requiring special handling.']
  last_cycle_count_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity recorded during the most recent cycle count.']
  last_cycle_count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent physical cycle count for this SKU at the location.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the location as a retail store, distribution center (DC), or micro‑fulfillment center (MFC).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of perishable or regulated items.']
  on_hand_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current physical units of the SKU available on the shelf or floor at the location.']
  on_order_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units ordered from suppliers but not yet received at any location.']
  oos_detection_method varchar [note: 'Technique by which the OOS condition was identified.']
  oos_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the OOS event expressed in hours.']
  oos_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SKU was restocked and the OOS condition ended (null if still OOS).']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is currently out of stock at the location.']
  oos_root_cause varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for the OOS event.']
  oos_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the out‑of‑stock condition began.']
  perishable_category varchar [note: 'Sub‑category of perishable items, used for handling and pricing rules.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when this stock position record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to this stock position record.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units allocated to open orders, transfers, or replenishment signals and therefore not sellable.']
  shrink_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units lost due to theft, spoilage, damage, or administrative error.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Most recent temperature reading for the location or pallet, in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the temperature_celsius value was recorded.']
  upc_code varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode identifier for the product, used for POS scanning and inventory reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Real-time and near-real-time on-hand stock position for every SKU at every location (store, DC, MFC). Captures on-hand quantity, reserved quantity, available-to-sell quantity, in-transit quantity, on-order quantity, and comprehensive OOS tracking: OOS status flag, OOS start timestamp, OOS end timestamp (when restocked), OOS duration in hours, root cause category (supplier OOS, DC OOS, store receiving delay, shrink, demand spike), estimated lost sales value, and OOS detection method (POS scan failure, CAO signal, manual audit). This is the authoritative SSOT for current inventory levels and out-of-stock events across the enterprise, fed by SAP MM goods movements and Manhattan WMS inventory control. Supports OOS monitoring and alerting, CAO replenishment signal generation, omnichannel availability checks, and store operations performance measurement.'
}

Table inventory.goods_movement {
  goods_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each inventory movement event.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who approved the adjustment or movement.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Internal movements are charged to a cost center to track transfer costs and support internal charge‑back reporting.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Goods movement often occurs between departments; linking provides departmental movement tracking.']
  destination_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Location (store, DC, MFC) to which inventory is added.']
  primary_goods_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Location (store, DC, MFC) from which inventory is removed.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the SKU or product being moved.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Goods movement (inbound/outbound) is audited against the PO that generated the movement for traceability and audit.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: TRACEABILITY: Inbound goods movements must capture the supplying vendor for compliance and supplier performance audits.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text or coded reason for an inventory adjustment (e.g., shrink, recount, donation).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer batch number for items that require batch tracking.']
  cost_at_movement decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of the inventory at the time of movement, used for financial valuation.']
  cost_method varchar [note: 'Costing methodology applied to the inventory item at the time of movement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the movement record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount.']
  cycle_count_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity difference identified during a cycle count.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source document (e.g., material document number, WMS transaction ID).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the product expires; used for perishable inventory control.']
  goods_movement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the movement record.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to value inventory for this movement (FIFO, LIFO, average cost, or standard cost).']
  is_cycle_count_correction boolean [note: 'True when the movement reflects a correction from a cycle‑count process.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the movement was entered manually rather than via automated interface.']
  is_physical_inventory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the movement is part of a physical inventory count process.']
  is_reserved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quantity was reserved for a pending order at the time of movement.']
  is_shrink boolean [note: 'True when the movement records inventory loss (theft, spoilage, damage).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of perishable or regulated items.']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing why the movement occurred (e.g., purchase receipt, customer return, internal transfer).']
  movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory movement was posted in the source system.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Category of inventory movement: receipt, issue, transfer, adjustment, correction, or other.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric amount of inventory moved, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized items (e.g., electronics, high‑value assets).']
  shrink_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized cause of shrinkage when is_shrink is true.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the movement record.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the movement complied with cold‑chain temperature requirements.']
  temperature_reading decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the time of movement for temperature‑sensitive items.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the temperature reading (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).']
  transaction_document_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable document number shown on transaction prints and reports.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram, pound, case, pallet).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the movement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every inventory movement event — receipts, issues, transfers, adjustments (including cycle-count corrections, damage write-offs, donation dispositions, and recall removals), returns, and write-offs — across all locations. Each record captures movement type (GR, GI, transfer, adjustment, cycle-count-correction), quantity, unit of measure, originating document reference, posting timestamp, and for adjustments: reason code, approving manager, adjustment value at cost, and supporting documentation reference. Sourced from SAP MM material documents and Manhattan WMS transactions. Provides the full audit trail for stock position changes, supports FIFO/LIFO rotation tracking, and serves as the controlled, auditable record for all inventory quantity changes including authorized corrections. This is the single source of truth for all inventory quantity change events.'
}

Table inventory.shrink_record {
  shrink_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each shrink record.']
  food_safety_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_violation. Business justification: When shrink is caused by a food safety violation, the violation must be recorded and linked to the shrink record for traceability and regulatory reporting.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the product (SKU) that experienced shrink.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Shrink analysis per PO enables supplier chargebacks, loss accounting, and regulatory reporting of inventory loss.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link shrink_record to the stock_position it originates from, consolidating shrink events with the affected position.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the shrink occurred.']
  compliance_flag_haccp boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shrink event relates to a HACCP (food safety) issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shrink record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the estimated cost.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of inventory that cannot be salvaged.']
  disposition_method varchar [note: 'How the lost inventory was handled after identification.']
  donation_recipient varchar [note: 'Name of the charitable organization or entity receiving the donated inventory.']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the lost inventory based on standard cost.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the shrink event.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability, especially for perishable items.']
  markdown_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the markdown price became effective.']
  markdown_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the markdown is no longer valid.']
  markdown_original_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard retail price of the item before markdown was applied.']
  markdown_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction from original price (e.g., 25.00 for 25%).']
  markdown_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Reduced price applied to the item for markdown disposition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional details or observations.']
  quantity_lost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of inventory lost, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  recorded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the shrink event was captured in the source system.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational unit accountable for the shrink event.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial identifier for high‑value or regulated items.']
  shrink_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the shrink event for reporting.']
  shrink_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause of the inventory loss.']
  shrink_event_number varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this line to the parent shrink event batch.']
  shrink_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the shrink record.']
  temperature_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the time of the event.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the temperature reading.']
  temperature_violation boolean [note: 'True if temperature readings indicated a cold‑chain breach leading to spoilage.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity lost (e.g., each, kilogram, liter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this shrink record.']

  Note: 'Operational record of inventory shrink events and loss-mitigation dispositions. Captures shrink cause (theft known/unknown, spoilage, damage, administrative error, vendor short-ship), quantity lost, estimated cost of shrink, shrink category, responsible department, and disposition method and outcome — including markdowns (clearance, near-expiry, seasonal, damage markdowns with original price, markdown price, markdown percentage, effective/expiry dates), donations, and disposal. For markdown dispositions: tracks quantity marked down, sell-through outcome, and recovered value. Supports GMROI analysis, loss prevention programs, HACCP compliance for perishable spoilage, shrink reduction programs, and perishable inventory management. Sourced from store shrink entry workflows in SAP MM and Oracle Retail.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each cycle count event.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who performed the physical count.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific location (aisle, shelf, bin) within the store.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the associate who approved the count.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the count was approved.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the count (full inventory, cycle count, spot check, or ad‑hoc).']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the count event, used for tracking and reference.']
  count_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for initiating the count.']
  count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the physical count was performed.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the count was performed manually or by an automated system.']
  counted_quantity varchar [note: 'Physical quantity recorded by the associate during the count.']
  cycle_count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the count event.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the product lot, if applicable.']
  is_out_of_stock boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is considered out‑of‑stock after the count.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of perishable or regulated items.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the associate performing the count.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cycle count record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cycle count record.']
  reserved_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the SKU reserved for open orders at count time.']
  shrink_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the variance is classified as shrink (loss).']
  shrink_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause of shrink when shrink_flag is true.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Retailer-assigned identifier for the product being counted.']
  system_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity that the inventory system recorded for the SKU at the time of the count.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the counted item met cold‑chain temperature requirements.']
  temperature_reading_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the time of count for temperature‑sensitive items.']
  tolerance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the variance exceeds predefined tolerance thresholds.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for the quantity (e.g., each, case, pallet, kilogram).']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode number for the product.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference between counted and system quantities.']
  variance_quantity varchar [note: 'Difference between counted_quantity and system_quantity (counted – system).']
  variance_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary impact of the quantity variance based on standard cost.']

  Note: 'Scheduled and ad-hoc physical inventory count events at the SKU-location level. Captures count date, count method (full, cycle, spot), counted quantity, system quantity at time of count, variance quantity, variance value, count status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, approved), and the associate who performed the count. Drives inventory accuracy KPIs and triggers adjustment postings when variances exceed tolerance thresholds. Sourced from Manhattan WMS cycle count module and SAP MM physical inventory.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_signal {
  replenishment_signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each replenishment signal record.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Category Replenishment Signal Aggregation groups signals by category to drive category‑wide ordering decisions.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Demand signal generated by an active campaign, used for automated replenishment planning.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the SKU for which the signal is generated.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the signal applies.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: AUTOMATED ORDERING: Replenishment signals must identify the designated supplier to generate correct purchase orders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the signal record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  demand_forecast_date date [note: 'Date for which the demand forecast quantity applies.']
  demand_forecast_units varchar [note: 'Projected units of demand for the SKU‑location over the forecast horizon that triggered the signal.']
  forecast_accuracy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical accuracy of the demand forecast that generated this signal, expressed as a percentage.']
  inventory_on_hand varchar [note: 'Quantity of units physically available at the location at signal generation time.']
  inventory_reserved varchar [note: 'Units already allocated to existing orders or transfers and therefore unavailable for new demand.']
  inventory_shrink varchar [note: 'Units lost due to theft, spoilage, or damage recorded at the time of signal.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the signal requires immediate attention due to risk of stockout or high‑margin item.']
  is_oos boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is currently out of stock at the location.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of days from order placement to receipt of goods for the SKU at this location.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by planners or system about the signal.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the signal for planning and execution urgency.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory level at which the signal is triggered; when on‑hand falls below this value.']
  reorder_point_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reorder point value was last calculated or updated.']
  replenishment_signal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the signal: pending, converted to purchase order, or cancelled.']
  safety_stock_level varchar [note: 'Buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply lead‑time.']
  signal_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the replenishment signal was created by the system.']
  signal_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the signal was automatically generated by the demand engine or manually overridden by a planner.']
  signal_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the signal record to support updates and re‑evaluations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that produced the signal, e.g., Blue Yonder demand planning or Oracle Retail Replenishment.']
  suggested_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of units recommended to order to bring inventory back to target levels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the signal record.']

  Note: 'CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) replenishment signals generated for each SKU-location combination when on-hand stock falls below reorder point or safety stock threshold. Captures signal type (auto-generated, manual override), suggested order quantity, reorder point at time of signal, safety stock level, lead time days, signal status (pending, converted-to-PO, cancelled), and the demand forecast that triggered the signal. Integrates Blue Yonder Demand Planning output with Oracle Retail Replenishment.'
}

Table inventory.oos_event {
  oos_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the OOS event record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the OOS record was generated automatically by a system process or entered manually by a user.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Out‑of‑stock events trigger compliance obligations to report inventory shortages to health authorities; linking enables obligation tracking and audit.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Purchase order that was expected to deliver the SKU but contributed to the OOS.']
  replenishment_signal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Computer‑Assisted Ordering (CAO) signal that triggered the OOS detection.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link oos_event to the stock_position to associate out‑of‑stock events with the exact inventory record.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the specific location (store number, DC code, or MFC ID).']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier responsible for the product when root cause is supplier OOS.']
  actual_sales_qty decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units actually sold during the OOS period (typically zero).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the OOS event record was first created in the data lake.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'How the OOS was identified: POS scan failure, CAO replenishment signal, or manual audit.']
  duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the out‑of‑stock event expressed in hours (difference between end and start timestamps).']
  estimated_lost_sales_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of sales lost due to the OOS condition, expressed in the store\'s currency.']
  forecasted_demand_qty decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected quantity of the SKU expected to sell during the OOS window based on demand planning.']
  is_resolved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OOS event has been closed (true) or is still open (false).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Type of location where the OOS occurred: store, distribution center (dc), or micro‑fulfillment center (mfc).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments providing additional context or observations about the OOS event.']
  oos_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the item was replenished and the OOS condition ended.']
  oos_severity varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the OOS event based on expected sales loss and product importance.']
  oos_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the inventory quantity first reached zero and the OOS event began.']
  quantity_after varchar [note: 'On‑hand quantity of the SKU after the OOS was resolved (post‑replenishment).']
  quantity_before varchar [note: 'On‑hand quantity of the SKU immediately before it reached zero.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary reason for the OOS event, classified into standard categories.']
  shrink_type varchar [note: 'Category of inventory loss if the OOS root cause is shrinkage.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the product (SKU) that experienced the out‑of‑stock condition.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if temperature‑controlled items remained within required range during the OOS period; otherwise false.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the OOS event record.']

  Note: 'Out-of-Stock (OOS) event records capturing when a SKU at a specific location transitions to zero available-to-sell quantity. Tracks OOS start timestamp, OOS end timestamp (when restocked), OOS duration in hours, root cause category (supplier OOS, DC OOS, store receiving delay, shrink, demand spike), estimated lost sales value, and whether the OOS was detected via POS scan failure, CAO signal, or manual audit. Critical for store operations performance and vendor fill-rate accountability.'
}

Table inventory.lot_batch {
  lot_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each lot or batch record.']
  food_safety_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_violation. Business justification: Lot or batch failures that cause food safety violations are recorded; linking the batch to the violation supports recall management and regulatory documentation.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, store, or micro‑fulfillment center where the lot is stored.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier that provided the lot.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned batch code used for traceability across the supply chain.']
  best_by_date date [note: 'Recommended date for optimal quality consumption.']
  cold_chain_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot must be stored and transported under temperature‑controlled conditions.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating where the product was produced.']
  days_to_expiry varchar [note: 'Calculated number of days remaining until the expiration date at the time of check.']
  entry_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature recorded when the lot entered the cold chain.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product must not be sold or consumed.']
  lot_batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lot within inventory management.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the lot by the manufacturer or supplier.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the lot for handling and compliance purposes.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the product to which this lot or batch belongs.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured or produced.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount of product units contained in the lot.']
  quarantine_end_date date [note: 'Date the lot exited quarantine status.']
  quarantine_start_date date [note: 'Date the lot entered quarantine status.']
  recall_date date [note: 'Date the lot was officially recalled from the supply chain.']
  recall_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why the lot was recalled.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the lot entered the warehouse or store receiving area.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lot record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the lot record.']
  rotation_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the rotation compliance was verified.']
  rotation_checked_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who performed the rotation compliance check.']
  rotation_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the lot was rotated according to policy during the most recent check.']
  rotation_method varchar [note: 'Policy used to rotate inventory: First‑In‑First‑Out, First‑Expired‑First‑Out, or Last‑In‑First‑Out.']
  rotation_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order of the lot within the chosen rotation method at its current location.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram, pound, liter).']
  usda_fda_certified_flag boolean [note: 'True if the lot complies with USDA or FDA certification requirements.']

  Note: 'Lot and batch master records for perishable, regulated, and traceable inventory items. Captures lot number, batch number, production date, expiration date, best-by date, FIFO/FEFO sequence number, country of origin, USDA/FDA certification flags, cold chain entry temperature, current lot status (active, quarantined, recalled, expired, consumed), and comprehensive rotation compliance tracking: rotation method (FIFO, FEFO — First Expired First Out), current rotation sequence at each location, days-to-expiry at time of check, rotation compliance flag (whether stock was rotated per policy), rotation check date, and checking associate. Essential for FIFO/LIFO/FEFO rotation enforcement, HACCP compliance, fresh produce quality management, spoilage reduction, perishable rotation audit, and FDA/USDA food safety traceability. Sourced from SAP MM batch management.'
}

Table inventory.cold_chain_log {
  cold_chain_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cold_chain_log']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who logged any manual corrective action.']
  battery_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Remaining battery level of the sensor at time of reading.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the action performed to resolve the excursion (e.g., re‑stock, equipment repair).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the log record was first created in the system.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sensor reading was captured.']
  excursion_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the out‑of‑range event in seconds.']
  excursion_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the condition returned to within thresholds.']
  excursion_flag boolean [note: 'True when temperature or humidity exceeds configured thresholds.']
  excursion_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the first out‑of‑range condition was detected.']
  haccp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reading complies with HACCP food‑safety requirements.']
  humidity_deviation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Difference between measured humidity and configured threshold.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured relative humidity as a percentage.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category of the physical location hosting the zone.']
  max_temp_threshold_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Configured upper bound for acceptable temperature in Celsius.']
  max_temp_threshold_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Configured upper bound for acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit.']
  min_temp_threshold_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Configured lower bound for acceptable temperature in Celsius.']
  min_temp_threshold_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Configured lower bound for acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the reading.']
  reading_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment of the sensor reading.']
  reading_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record captures temperature, humidity, or both.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the temperature/humidity sensor that generated the reading.']
  sensor_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the sensor.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_deviation_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Difference between measured temperature and nearest threshold in Celsius.']
  temperature_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured temperature in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the log record.']

  Note: 'Temperature and humidity monitoring log for cold chain inventory across refrigerated/frozen storage zones in stores, DCs, and MFCs. Captures sensor readings at defined intervals, zone identifier, temperature reading (°F/°C), humidity reading, min/max thresholds, excursion flag, excursion duration, corrective action taken, and HACCP compliance status. Supports EPA refrigerant management compliance and FDA cold chain requirements for perishable and pharmaceutical inventory.'
}

Table inventory.storage_location {
  storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each storage location.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center that contains the location.']
  mfc_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the micro‑fulfillment center (MFC) where the location resides.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: CONTROLLED‑SUBSTANCE COMPLIANCE: maps storage locations used for drug storage to the pharmacy they serve, required for DEA audit and temperature‑monitoring reports.']
  planogram_slot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planogram slot that defines product placement for this location.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Location‑level campaign assignment: stores map locations to campaigns for end‑cap and aisle displays.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle identifier within the store or warehouse where the location resides.']
  available_units varchar [note: 'Units available for new allocation (on‑hand minus reserved).']
  bay varchar [note: 'Bay or section within an aisle.']
  bin varchar [note: 'Bin or slot identifier on the shelf.']
  capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual items the location can hold.']
  capacity_volume_m3 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum volume in cubic meters that the location can accommodate.']
  capacity_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in kilograms that the location can safely store.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage location record was first created in the system.']
  current_onhand_units varchar [note: 'Real‑time count of items physically present in the location.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the location became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the location is scheduled to be decommissioned or retired (null if indefinite).']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Numeric floor level where the location resides (e.g., 0 for ground floor).']
  is_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location meets accessibility standards for disabled personnel.']
  is_security_restricted boolean [note: 'True for locations with heightened security controls (e.g., pharmacy vault, fuel tank).']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is equipped with active temperature control.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent cycle count performed for the location.']
  last_inventory_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the latest inventory reconciliation event.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'External business code or identifier used by the retailer to reference the location (e.g., SKU‑like code).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the storage location used in operational screens and reports.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category of the location such as sales floor, backroom, cooler, freezer, pharmacy vault, fuel tank, or MFC bin. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_floor|backroom|cooler|freezer|pharmacy_vault|fuel_tank|mfc_bin — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the location.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'True when the location has zero available units.']
  reserved_units varchar [note: 'Units allocated to pending orders but not yet picked.']
  shelf varchar [note: 'Shelf identifier on the bay.']
  shrink_units varchar [note: 'Units lost due to theft, spoilage, or damage.']
  storage_location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the location.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for the location.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for the location.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature classification of the location for compliance with cold‑chain requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the storage location record.']
  zone_classification varchar [note: 'Operational zone label used for routing and labor planning.']

  Note: 'Master record of all physical storage locations within stores, DCs, and MFCs where inventory is held. Captures location type (sales floor, backroom, cooler, freezer, pharmacy vault, fuel tank, MFC bin), aisle, bay, shelf, bin coordinates, planogram slot reference, maximum capacity (units and weight), temperature zone classification, and active status. This is the SSOT for inventory location topology used by WMS putaway, picking, and replenishment workflows.'
}

Table inventory.stock_reservation {
  stock_reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each inventory reservation record.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Reservation for limited‑quantity offers: reservation records must reference the specific promo offer they protect.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (or shopper) whose order triggered the reservation.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location (store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center) where inventory is reserved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reservation record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  demand_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating demand document (sales order, transfer order, production order, or OMS allocation).']
  demand_document_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of document that generated the reservation.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time after which the reservation is automatically released if not fulfilled.']
  is_auto_reservation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reservation was generated automatically by system rules (true) or manually by an operator (false).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of location holding the reserved stock.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special handling instructions.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier (e.g., reservation code) used in operational screens and reports.']
  reservation_source varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the reservation record.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reservation indicating allocation progress.']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact moment the reservation was created in the source system (e.g., SAP MM or OMS).']
  reservation_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the demand source that triggered the reservation: customer order, inter‑store transfer, production requirement, or automated replenishment.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory units reserved for the demand document.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the reserved quantity (e.g., each, kilogram, liter, case).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reservation (e.g., status change, quantity adjustment).']

  Note: 'Records of inventory quantities reserved against specific demand documents — customer orders (click-and-collect, delivery), transfer orders, and production requirements. Captures reservation type, reserved quantity, reservation status (open, partially-fulfilled, fulfilled, cancelled), expiry timestamp, and the originating demand document reference. Prevents double-allocation of inventory across omnichannel order fulfillment and DC replenishment workflows. Sourced from SAP MM reservations and OMS allocation.'
}

Table inventory.transfer_order {
  transfer_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the transfer order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Transfer orders incur internal costs; associating each order with a cost center enables charge‑back and profitability tracking.']
  destination_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location (store, DC, MFC) receiving the transferred inventory.']
  primary_transfer_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the transfer order.']
  primary_transfer_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location (store, DC, MFC) from which inventory is being transferred.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the inventory was actually received.']
  approved_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of inventory approved for transfer after validation.']
  carrier_mode varchar [note: 'Transportation mode used for the transfer.']
  compliance_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a compliance document (e.g., FDA, USDA) attached to the transfer.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with moving the inventory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the transfer cost.']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination location.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the transfer should be completed.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'True if the transfer is a cross‑dock operation without storage.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'True if the transfer is processed with expedited handling.']
  is_fifo_rotation boolean [note: 'True if the items follow a First‑In‑First‑Out rotation policy.']
  is_lifo_rotation boolean [note: 'True if the items follow a Last‑In‑First‑Out rotation policy.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the status field was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or special instructions.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer order is high priority.']
  received_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of inventory received at the destination location.']
  requested_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of inventory initially requested for transfer.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of inventory actually shipped from the source location.']
  shrink_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity lost due to theft, spoilage, or damage during transfer.']
  shrink_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause of the shrinkage recorded for the transfer.']
  source_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source location.']
  temperature_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of temperature compliance checks for the transfer.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True if the transferred items require temperature-controlled handling.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'The business date on which the transfer order was initiated.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible reference number for the transfer order, used in communications and documentation.']
  transfer_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transfer order.']
  transfer_order_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer is internal (within company) or external (to third‑party).']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business driver for the inventory movement.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantities expressed in the transfer order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the transfer order record.']

  Note: 'Inter-location inventory transfer orders authorizing movement of stock between stores, from DC to store, from DC to MFC, or between DCs. Captures source location, destination location, requested quantity, approved quantity, shipped quantity, received quantity, transfer reason (replenishment, rebalancing, recall, seasonal), transfer status (created, approved, in-transit, received, closed), and expected delivery date. Sourced from Manhattan WMS transfer order management and SAP MM stock transport orders.'
}

Table inventory.stock_adjustment {
  stock_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each stock adjustment record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who approved the stock adjustment.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Financial posting of inventory adjustments requires linking each adjustment to a GL account for accurate expense/revenue reporting.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Adjustment due to promotion: markdowns or corrections linked to the offer that triggered the change.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link stock_adjustment directly to the stock_position it adjusts, enabling audit of adjustments per position.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the adjustment took place.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: ADJUSTMENT AUDIT: Adjustments caused by supplier issues (quality, recall) require linking to the responsible supplier for KPI tracking.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the adjustment (e.g., ADJ‑20230915‑001).']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code describing why the adjustment was made.']
  adjustment_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the adjustment reason.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment physically occurred in the store or warehouse.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was formally approved.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard cost of one unit of the product at the time of adjustment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  cycle_count_flag boolean [note: 'True if the adjustment originated from a cycle‑count variance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment becomes effective for reporting purposes.']
  inventory_status_after varchar [note: 'Stock status after the adjustment is applied.']
  inventory_status_before varchar [note: 'Stock status prior to the adjustment.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the location (e.g., store, DC, MFC).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations entered by the adjusting personnel.']
  quantity_adjusted decimal(12,2) [note: 'Signed quantity change; positive for additions, negative for reductions.']
  shrink_flag boolean [note: 'True if the adjustment is recorded as shrink (theft, spoilage, damage).']
  sku varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier to which the adjustment applies.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the original SAP MM document or other source supporting the adjustment.']
  stock_adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the adjustment.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the adjustment is related to a temperature‑control violation.']
  total_adjusted_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Monetary impact of the adjustment (quantity_adjusted * cost_per_unit).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity adjusted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the adjustment record.']

  Note: 'Authorized inventory adjustment records correcting on-hand quantities outside of normal goods movement flows. Captures adjustment reason code (cycle count variance, system correction, damage write-off, donation, recall removal), quantity adjusted (positive or negative), adjusted value at cost, approving manager, adjustment date, and reference to supporting documentation. Provides a controlled audit trail for all non-transactional stock changes. Sourced from SAP MM inventory adjustment postings.'
}

Table inventory.reorder_policy {
  reorder_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each reorder policy record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Reorder policies are budgeted against cost centers to control inventory replenishment spend.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Link to the demand forecast record that supplies expected sales for the SKU‑location.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product (SKU) to which this reorder policy applies.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the policy is enforced.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: SUPPLIER‑SPECIFIC POLICY: Reorder points and safety stock are often defined per supplier for a SKU, needed for procurement analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  economic_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Calculated optimal order size that minimizes total holding and ordering costs.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes binding and may be used for replenishment calculations.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the policy ceases to be active; null indicates an open‑ended policy.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy parameters were last reviewed or recalibrated.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of calendar days from order placement to receipt at the location.']
  maximum_stock_level varchar [note: 'Upper bound on on‑hand inventory to avoid over‑stocking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the policy configuration.']
  order_multiple varchar [note: 'Standard case‑pack or pallet quantity that orders must be rounded to.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the policy, often used in operational reports and communications.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the policy logic governing how reorder signals are generated.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number to track changes to the policy over time.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority used when multiple policies compete for the same SKU‑location.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory level (units) at which a replenishment signal is triggered.']
  reorder_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate reorder signals: static min‑max, demand‑driven, or vendor‑managed.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'How often the policy parameters are evaluated and potentially updated.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Buffer stock kept to protect against demand variability and supply delays.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or last maintained the policy (e.g., Oracle Retail, Blue Yonder).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the policy.']

  Note: 'SKU-location level replenishment policy parameters governing CAO and manual reorder behavior. Captures reorder point (units), safety stock quantity, maximum stock level, economic order quantity (EOQ), order multiple (case pack), lead time days, review cycle (daily, weekly), replenishment method (min-max, demand-driven, vendor-managed), demand forecast reference, and policy effective/expiry dates. Managed in Oracle Retail Replenishment and Blue Yonder Demand Planning. Drives replenishment signal generation and integrates demand forecast inputs for dynamic threshold calculation.'
}

Table inventory.recall_hold {
  recall_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each recall hold record.']
  food_safety_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_violation. Business justification: Recall holds are often initiated due to identified food safety violations; linking provides traceability from hold to the specific violation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product (SKU) that is subject to the hold.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the hold is applied.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor responsible for the affected product.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the affected product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall hold record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  disposition_instruction varchar [note: 'Action to be taken with the held product.']
  hold_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was resolved or released.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the recall hold, used for tracking and communication with regulators and suppliers.']
  hold_resolution_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the hold was resolved or released.']
  hold_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold became effective.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recall hold based on regulatory or internal criteria.']
  is_shrink boolean [note: 'True if the hold is due to inventory shrink (theft, spoilage, damage).']
  is_temperature_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the held product meets required cold‑chain temperature specifications.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier associated with the affected product batch.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the hold.']
  quantity_on_hold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount of product units placed on hold.']
  recall_agency varchar [note: 'Regulatory agency that issued the recall or hold.']
  recall_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the recall severity.']
  recall_hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold.']
  recall_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause for the recall or hold.']
  regulatory_notification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the regulatory notice (e.g., FDA recall notice).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the hold record (e.g., SAP, Oracle, WMS).']
  temperature_reading_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the time of hold for temperature‑sensitive items.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity on hold.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recall hold record.']

  Note: 'Inventory hold and recall management records for SKUs subject to FDA/USDA product recalls, supplier quality holds, or internal quality quarantine actions. Captures hold type (FDA Class I/II/III recall, supplier voluntary recall, internal quarantine), affected lot/batch range, quantity on hold, hold status (active, released, disposed), regulatory notification reference, disposition instructions (return-to-vendor, destroy, donate), and hold resolution date. Critical for FDA food safety compliance and USDA meat/poultry recall protocols.'
}

Table inventory.perishable_rotation {
  perishable_rotation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each perishable rotation record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who executed the rotation check.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Perishable Rotation Compliance per Category links rotation events to categories for regulatory and freshness reporting.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU/lot being evaluated for rotation.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the rotation check occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rotation record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  days_to_expiry_at_check varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days remaining until the lot expires at the moment of the check.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded snapshot of key fields captured at the time of the rotation check for downstream processing.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the event; fixed value \'rotation_check\' for this product.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the lot is required to be sold or removed.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number assigned by the supplier for traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the associate regarding the rotation check.']
  rotation_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rotation check was performed.']
  rotation_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot was rotated according to the defined policy (true = compliant).']
  rotation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to rotate inventory: FIFO (First‑In‑First‑Out), FEFO (First‑Expired‑First‑Out), or LIFO (Last‑In‑First‑Out).']
  rotation_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the lot within the rotation order at the location.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the record (e.g., Manhattan WMS, SAP MM).']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the temperature reading at the time of check met cold‑chain requirements.']
  temperature_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured temperature of the product at the time of rotation check.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the temperature reading (Celsius or Fahrenheit).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rotation record.']

  Note: 'FIFO/LIFO rotation tracking records for perishable and short-shelf-life inventory. Captures rotation method (FIFO, FEFO — First Expired First Out), current rotation sequence for each lot at each location, days-to-expiry at time of record, rotation compliance flag (whether stock was rotated per policy), rotation check date, and associate who performed the rotation check. Supports HACCP compliance, fresh produce quality management, and reduction of spoilage shrink.'
}

Table inventory.receiving_record {
  receiving_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inbound receiving record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: REQUIRED: Receiving audit logs need the associate who performed the receipt; this is essential for compliance and traceability reports.']
  food_safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_inspection. Business justification: Each received shipment is inspected for safety; associating the inspection result with the receiving record ensures compliance verification and auditability.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Receiving costs (duties, freight) are posted to GL accounts; the link supports AP integration and financial reporting.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Receiving ties each receipt to its PO for invoice matching, compliance reporting, and supplier performance tracking.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where receipt occurred.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplying vendor.']
  accepted_quantity varchar [note: 'Units approved for inventory after quality and compliance checks.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier used for manufacturing or production tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receiving record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for monetary values.']
  cycle_count_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity adjustment applied during a cycle count correction.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary discounts applied to the receipt (e.g., promotional or volume discounts).']
  dsd_vendor_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier used for Direct Store Delivery vendors that may differ from the master vendor ID.']
  expected_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of units expected on the purchase order or transfer.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product must not be sold or consumed.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to value inventory for this receipt (FIFO, LIFO, or Average).']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice number associated with the receipt for three‑way matching.']
  is_cycle_count_correction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the receipt was entered to correct a prior cycle count.']
  is_physical_inventory boolean [note: 'True if the receipt is part of a physical inventory count process.']
  is_shrink boolean [note: 'True if the receipt includes shrink (theft, spoilage, damage).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of perishable or regulated items.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net payable amount after discounts; used for three‑way matching.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by the receiver.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms associated with the vendor invoice.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order reference that initiated the inbound shipment.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the receipt (e.g., MIGO document number).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the goods were physically received and logged.']
  received_quantity varchar [note: 'Total units physically counted at receipt, before any rejections.']
  receiving_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the receiving record.']
  receiving_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the goods arrived: distribution center sourced, direct store delivery, or inter‑location transfer.']
  refused_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity the DSD vendor refused to accept back (e.g., for damaged pallets).']
  refused_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the DSD vendor\'s refusal.']
  rejected_quantity varchar [note: 'Units rejected due to damage, spoilage, or non‑conformance.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text or coded reason why items were rejected.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for the delivery to arrive at the receiving location.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial identifier for serialized items.']
  shrink_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for shrinkage observed at receipt.']
  sign_off_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee who signed off on the receipt.']
  temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured temperature of the product at receipt for cold‑chain compliance.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recorded temperature met the product\'s required range.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the temperature reading (Celsius or Fahrenheit).']
  total_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the received goods before discounts or adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the receiving record.']

  Note: 'Inbound inventory receiving records for all goods arriving at stores and DCs — including DC-sourced replenishment, Direct Store Delivery (DSD) from vendor-managed distributors, and inter-location transfers. Captures receiving type (DC-sourced, DSD, transfer), PO or invoice reference, vendor/carrier, DSD vendor identifier, scheduled and actual delivery dates, expected/received/accepted/rejected quantities with rejection reasons, refused quantities with reason for DSD, temperature at receipt for cold chain items, backdoor receiver sign-off, payment terms reference, and receiving status (scheduled, partial, complete, discrepant). Sourced from Manhattan WMS receiving module and SAP MM goods receipt (MIGO). Supports DSD vendor reconciliation, accounts payable three-way match, store-level inventory updates, and DSD-specific audit workflows. This is the single source of truth for all inbound inventory receipts regardless of delivery channel.'
}

Table inventory.markdown_event {
  markdown_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for markdown_event']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the markdown event.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Markdowns affect revenue accounts; linking to a GL account enables proper expense posting and margin analysis.']
  markdown_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.markdown. Business justification: Required for the Markdown Audit Report that ties each inventory markdown event to its pricing markdown record for compliance and margin analysis.']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.planogram. Business justification: Markdown Impact Analysis per planogram links markdown events to the specific planogram to assess shelf‑level price changes.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the stock‑keeping unit being marked down.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the markdown occurred.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the markdown event received final approval.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier associated with the inventory units being marked down.']
  cost_at_markdown decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the SKU at the time of markdown, used for margin analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the markdown record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price values (e.g., USD).']
  disposition_outcome varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the markdown inventory (e.g., sold, donated, disposed, returned).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the markdown price becomes active.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the markdown was applied to inventory.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the SKU, relevant for perishable items.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the markdown is no longer valid (nullable if indefinite).']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the markdown was entered manually rather than generated automatically.']
  markdown_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the markdown event.']
  markdown_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction from original price to markdown price.']
  markdown_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Reduced price applied to the SKU during the markdown event.']
  markdown_type varchar [note: 'Category describing why the markdown was applied (e.g., clearance, near‑expiry, seasonal, damage, shrink).']
  markdown_version varchar [note: 'Version counter for the markdown record to support audit of changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the markdown.']
  original_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard retail price of the SKU before any markdown.']
  quantity_marked_down varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU that were subject to the markdown.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Internal code indicating the business reason for the markdown (e.g., slow_moving, overstock, promotional).']
  shrink_type varchar [note: 'Category of inventory loss associated with the markdown (e.g., theft, spoilage, damage).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the markdown record (e.g., SAP, Oracle).']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the markdown item met cold‑chain temperature requirements at the time of markdown.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the markdown record.']

  Note: 'Inventory markdown events applied to slow-moving, near-expiry, or clearance stock to accelerate sell-through and reduce shrink. Captures markdown type (clearance, near-expiry, seasonal, damage), original retail price, markdown price, markdown percentage, quantity marked down, markdown effective date, markdown expiry date, and disposition outcome (sold, donated, disposed). Supports perishable inventory management, shrink reduction programs, and GMROI optimization.'
}

Table inventory.stock_snapshot {
  stock_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for stock_snapshot']
  assortment_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_item. Business justification: Assortment Performance Dashboard joins inventory snapshot with assortment items to evaluate on‑hand stock vs planogram placement.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link each stock_snapshot to the stock_position it captures for easy navigation between snapshot and current position.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical location (store, distribution center, micro‑fulfillment center) where the inventory is held.']
  available_quantity varchar [note: 'Units available for sale (on‑hand minus reserved).']
  cold_chain_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if temperature‑controlled items met compliance at snapshot time.']
  estimated_lost_sales_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of sales lost due to the out‑of‑stock event.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the product expires, if applicable.']
  fifo_lifo_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory is managed using FIFO or LIFO rotation.']
  in_transit_quantity varchar [note: 'Units that have been shipped to the location but not yet received.']
  inventory_cost_at_snapshot decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost value of the on‑hand inventory at snapshot time.']
  inventory_retail_value_at_snapshot decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total retail (selling price) value of the on‑hand inventory at snapshot time.']
