  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to value inventory cost at snapshot (e.g., standard, moving average, FIFO, LIFO).']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is classified as perishable.']
  last_cycle_count_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity recorded during the last cycle count.']
  last_cycle_count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent physical cycle count for this SKU‑location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the snapshot.']
  on_hand_quantity varchar [note: 'Total units of the SKU physically present at the location at snapshot time.']
  on_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Units ordered from suppliers but not yet shipped to the location.']
  oos_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the out‑of‑stock event in hours.']
  oos_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the out‑of‑stock condition ended, if resolved.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU was out of stock at the snapshot time.']
  oos_root_cause varchar [note: 'Primary reason for the out‑of‑stock condition (enumerated).']
  oos_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the out‑of‑stock condition began, if applicable.']
  perishable_category varchar [note: 'Category describing the type of perishable product.']
  reserved_quantity varchar [note: 'Units of the SKU that are allocated to open orders or reservations and not available for new sales.']
  shrink_quantity varchar [note: 'Units lost due to theft, spoilage, damage, or administrative adjustments.']
  snapshot_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the snapshot record was initially created in the data lake.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory snapshot was captured (EOD/EOW).']
  snapshot_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the snapshot record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the snapshot data (e.g., SAP MM, Manhattan WMS).']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature of the storage environment at snapshot time.']
  temperature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the temperature reading was taken.']

  Note: 'Point-in-time historical snapshots of stock positions captured at end-of-day (EOD) or end-of-week (EOW) for each SKU-location combination. Captures snapshot timestamp, on-hand quantity, reserved quantity, in-transit quantity, on-order quantity, inventory value at cost (using current valuation method), and inventory value at retail. Enables period-over-period inventory trend analysis, shrink calculation by comparing successive snapshots, book-to-physical reconciliation, and financial inventory valuation reporting for SOX compliance. Distinct from real-time stock_position as it represents frozen historical states used for audit and financial close.'
}

Table inventory.valuation {
  valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each inventory valuation record.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the valuation batch or run that groups multiple valuation lines.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Valuation entries must be posted to GL accounts to reflect inventory asset values and COGS in financial statements.']
  prior_layer_valuation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on valuation (prior_layer_valuation_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the stock keeping unit being valued.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the inventory resides.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any manual or system adjustment to the valuation.']
  compliance_flag_haccp boolean [note: 'True if the inventory item complies with HACCP food‑safety standards.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier for financial reporting.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost assigned to a single unit of inventory based on the selected valuation method.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the valuation record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the inventory item must not be sold.']
  financial_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier (e.g., FY2025Q1) used for GL posting.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the valuation amount has been manually adjusted.']
  is_physical_inventory boolean [note: 'True if the record reflects a physical inventory count rather than a system estimate.']
  lcm_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Adjustment amount applied when market value is lower than cost, per GAAP/IFRS LCM rules.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the valuation batch.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the inventory batch.']
  lower_of_cost_or_market_flag boolean [note: 'True if the lower‑of‑cost‑or‑market rule was applied to this record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or audit notes related to the valuation.']
  obsolescence_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reserve for inventory that may become obsolete or unsellable.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Fiscal period end date associated with this valuation snapshot.']
  perishable_category varchar [note: 'Category describing the type of perishable product.']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is classified as perishable.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(15,3) [note: 'Physical count of units available for sale at the valuation moment.']
  shrink_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial reserve for anticipated inventory shrink (theft, spoilage, damage).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP MM, Oracle Retail, Manhattan WMS).']
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the valuation record meets Sarbanes‑Oxley compliance requirements.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if temperature readings for the item are within defined cold‑chain limits.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost of the on‑hand quantity (quantity_on_hand * cost_per_unit).']
  total_retail_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate retail price of the on‑hand quantity for gross‑margin reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the valuation record.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date on which the inventory valuation is performed.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate inventory cost (e.g., FIFO, LIFO, weighted average, retail inventory method, standard cost).']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the valuation record.']

  Note: 'Inventory cost layer and valuation records supporting GAAP/IFRS financial reporting. Captures valuation method (weighted average cost, FIFO, LIFO, retail inventory method), cost per unit, total inventory value at cost, total inventory value at retail, lower-of-cost-or-market (LCM) adjustments, shrink reserve accruals, obsolescence reserve, valuation date, and period-end book-to-physical reconciliation. Supports SOX-compliant inventory valuation for monthly/quarterly close, COGS calculation, gross margin reporting, and external audit evidence. Integrates with SAP MM material valuation and finance domain GL postings.'
}

Table inventory.pick {
  pick_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the pick association']
  product_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the order line receiving the pick']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the stock position being picked']
  pick_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the pick was performed']
  quantity_picked decimal [note: 'Amount of inventory units taken from the stock position for this order line']

  Note: 'This association captures each pick event linking a stock_position to an order_line. It records the quantity taken from a specific inventory location and the time of the pick, enabling audit of inventory consumption per order line.. Existence Justification: An order_line can be fulfilled from multiple stock_position records (different locations or batches) and each stock_position can serve many order_line records across orders. The picking process records the quantity taken and the timestamp for each stock_position‑order_line pair, creating a distinct record for every pick.'
}

Table inventory.lot_allocation {
  lot_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the LotAllocation association']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lot_batch']
  product_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the order_line']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation was performed']
  quantity_allocated decimal [note: 'Quantity of the product allocated from the lot to the order line']

  Note: 'Represents the allocation of inventory lot/batch to a specific order line for traceability and recall management. Each record links one lot_batch to one order_line and captures allocation-specific data.. Existence Justification: A lot_batch can be allocated to many order_line records (multiple sales) and a single order_line can be fulfilled from multiple lot_batches when the quantity exceeds a single lot. The allocation is actively managed in the system for traceability, with attributes such as quantity_allocated and allocation_timestamp recorded for each allocation.'
}

Table inventory.temperature_zone {
  temperature_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for temperature_zone']
  parent_temperature_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on temperature_zone (parent_temperature_zone_id)']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the zone meets regulatory temperature and humidity compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was created in the master reference system.']
  humidity_max_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the relative humidity range for the zone, expressed as a percentage.']
  humidity_min_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the relative humidity range for the zone, expressed as a percentage.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the temperature zone is currently active and usable in operations.']
  monitoring_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if continuous temperature monitoring is required for this zone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the zone, such as special handling instructions.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the temperature range maintained in the zone, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the temperature range maintained in the zone, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the zone record.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the temperature zone in operational systems.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the temperature zone (e.g., Cold Storage, Frozen Bay, Ambient Shelf).']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Category of temperature control applied to the zone.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for temperature_zone. Referenced by zone_id.'
}

Table order.order_delivery_route {
  order_delivery_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery route. Primary key for this entity.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver assigned to execute this delivery route.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center, store, or micro-fulfillment center from which this delivery route originates.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the driver completed this delivery route.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the driver began executing this delivery route.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the carrier or fleet operator responsible for executing this delivery route.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier executing the route, distinguishing between internal fleet operations and external carrier partnerships.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery route record was first created in the system.']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'The date on which this delivery route was dispatched or scheduled for execution.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the route was dispatched to the driver, marking the start of route execution.']
  dispatch_window_end timestamp [note: 'The end of the time window during which this route batch was dispatched for delivery.']
  dispatch_window_start timestamp [note: 'The start of the time window during which this route batch was dispatched for delivery.']
  estimated_fuel_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated fuel cost in USD for completing this delivery route based on planned mileage and current fuel rates.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated labor cost in USD for the driver to complete this delivery route based on planned duration and wage rates.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery route record was last updated in the system.']
  planned_end_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when the driver is scheduled to complete this delivery route.']
  planned_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when the driver is scheduled to begin executing this delivery route.']
  route_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions for the driver regarding this delivery route, such as traffic alerts or access restrictions.']
  route_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the delivery route, externally known and used for operational tracking and driver assignment.']
  route_optimization_algorithm varchar [note: 'The name or version of the route optimization algorithm used by JDA TMS to generate this route plan.']
  route_sequence_version varchar [note: 'Version number tracking route resequencing events, incremented each time the stop sequence is modified during execution.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery route indicating its operational state.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery route based on the type of fulfillment service being provided.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The delivery service level commitment associated with this route, defining customer expectations for delivery speed.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'The temperature control requirement for this delivery route, critical for cold chain compliance in grocery delivery.']
  total_actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual duration in minutes taken to complete this delivery route from start to finish.']
  total_actual_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual distance in miles traveled during the execution of this delivery route, captured from vehicle telematics or GPS.']
  total_completed_stops varchar [note: 'The actual number of delivery stops successfully completed on this route.']
  total_failed_stops varchar [note: 'The number of delivery stops that failed or could not be completed on this route.']
  total_order_count varchar [note: 'The total number of customer orders included in this delivery route across all stops.']
  total_package_count varchar [note: 'The total number of packages or totes loaded onto the vehicle for this delivery route.']
  total_planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total planned duration in minutes for completing this delivery route, including drive time and stop dwell time.']
  total_planned_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total distance in miles planned for this delivery route based on route optimization algorithms.']
  total_planned_stops varchar [note: 'The total number of delivery stops planned for this route at the time of route creation.']
  total_volume_cubic_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total volume in cubic feet of all packages and orders on this delivery route.']
  total_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total weight in pounds of all packages and orders on this delivery route.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle assigned to this delivery route.']

  Note: 'Optimized delivery route plan and its constituent stops, generated by JDA TMS for a batch of delivery orders assigned to a single driver/vehicle for a given dispatch window. At route level: captures route ID, dispatch date, vehicle ID, driver reference, total planned stops, total planned miles, total planned duration, actual start/end timestamps, route status (planned, in-progress, completed, partial), and carrier/fleet type. At stop level (embedded detail): captures stop sequence, linked delivery order, planned/actual arrival and departure times, stop status (pending, completed, failed, skipped), failure reason code, proof-of-delivery reference, and dwell time in minutes. Enables route efficiency analysis, stop-level SLA tracking, driver performance, failed delivery root cause analysis, route resequencing, and LMD cost management. Stop-level detail is modeled within this entity as route and stops share a lifecycle and are always managed together.'
}

Table order.catalog_item {
  catalog_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for catalog_item']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.brand']
  department_id varchar [note: 'FK to store.department']
  age_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item requires age verification at checkout (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, certain medications).']
  average_customer_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average customer rating for this item on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00, calculated from customer reviews.']
  category_path varchar [note: 'Hierarchical category path for navigation and filtering (e.g., Grocery > Dairy > Milk > Whole Milk).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this digital catalog item record was first created in the system.']
  digital_availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of the item in the digital catalog. Controls whether the item can be added to cart.']
  digital_discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date this item was or will be removed from the digital catalog. Null if still active.']
  digital_display_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The price displayed to customers on the digital storefront. May differ from store-specific pricing.']
  digital_item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed product description for the digital catalog, including features, benefits, ingredients, and usage instructions.']
  digital_item_title varchar [note: 'The customer-facing title displayed on the digital storefront. Optimized for online presentation and may differ from physical product name.']
  digital_launch_date date [note: 'The date this item was first made available in the digital catalog.']
  ebt_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item can be purchased using Electronic Benefits Transfer payment methods.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number assigned by GS1 for worldwide product identification. May be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits.']
  hero_image_alt_text varchar [note: 'Alternative text for the hero image, used for accessibility (screen readers) and SEO purposes.']
  hero_image_url varchar [note: 'Primary product image URL displayed prominently on product detail pages and search results.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this digital catalog item record was last updated.']
  loyalty_points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base loyalty points earned when purchasing this item. Default is 1.0.']
  minimum_age_required varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to purchase this item. Null if no age restriction applies.']
  online_exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is available exclusively through digital channels and not sold in physical stores.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item carries USDA Organic certification.']
  package_size varchar [note: 'The size or quantity of the package as displayed to customers (e.g., 12 oz, 1 gallon, 6-pack).']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is perishable and requires temperature-controlled handling and expedited delivery.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the digital display price.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is a store-owned brand (private label) product rather than a national Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) brand.']
  promotion_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item can be included in promotional campaigns and discounts.']
  search_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases used to improve internal site search relevance and product discoverability.']
  seo_meta_description varchar [note: 'HTML meta description tag content that appears in search engine results. Typically 150-160 characters.']
  seo_meta_title varchar [note: 'HTML meta title tag content optimized for search engine results pages. Typically 50-60 characters.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The physical product SKU that this digital catalog item represents. Links the digital presentation layer to the physical product domain.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is eligible for purchase using SNAP Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) benefits.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The source system that provided this digital catalog item data (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail).']
  substitution_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item can be substituted with an alternative product during order fulfillment if out of stock.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage and transportation temperature zone for this item. Critical for cold chain logistics.']
  thumbnail_image_url varchar [note: 'Smaller thumbnail image URL used in product listings, carousels, and grid views.']
  total_review_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer reviews submitted for this item.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which this item is sold (e.g., each, pound, ounce, gallon, liter).']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for the item, used for barcode scanning and product identification.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is eligible for purchase using WIC program benefits.']

  Note: 'Individual SKU-level entry within the digital catalog, capturing online-specific attributes such as digital item title, hero image URL, alt text, SEO metadata, search keywords, substitution eligibility, age-restricted flag, SNAP/EBT eligibility flag, WIC eligibility flag, and digital availability status. Bridges the physical product domain SKU to the commerce presentation layer.'
}

Table supply.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for shipment, if known at PO creation. May be vendor-selected for FOB origin terms.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for posting PO expenses to the appropriate cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Essential for GL posting of purchase order line amounts to the correct account in financial statements.']
  primary_purchase_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchasing agent or buyer responsible for creating and managing this purchase order.']
  primary_purchase_store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location where goods should be delivered. Can be a distribution center (DC), store, or micro-fulfillment center (MFC) depending on PO type.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Needed to allocate PO costs to profit centers for profitability reporting and performance dashboards.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign or ad circular associated with this purchase order, if promotional_flag is true.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom goods or services are being purchased.']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total vendor allowances, rebates, or promotional funding applied to this purchase order. Reduces net cost and impacts GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) calculation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was approved by authorized personnel, triggering transmission to vendor. Null if still in draft status.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for purchase order cancellation, used for vendor performance analysis and process improvement.']
  cancelled_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active orders.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was administratively closed after all goods were received and invoices matched. Marks end of PO lifecycle.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order contains temperature-sensitive perishable items requiring cold chain logistics (refrigerated or frozen transport and storage). Critical for fresh produce, dairy, meat, and frozen goods.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by the vendor for delivery via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) acknowledgment or manual confirmation. May differ from requested date due to vendor capacity or lead time constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this purchase order record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this purchase order (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  edi_transmission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order was transmitted to the vendor via EDI 850 transaction set (true) or via manual methods such as email or fax (false).']
  edi_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the EDI 850 purchase order transaction was transmitted to the vendor. Null if edi_transmission_flag is false.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Freight or shipping charges associated with this purchase order. May be vendor-paid, prepaid-and-add, or collect depending on Incoterms.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and vendor. FOB (Free On Board), CIF (Cost Insurance Freight), EXW (Ex Works), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), DAP (Delivered At Place), FCA (Free Carrier).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this purchase order record was last updated. Tracks changes to status, amounts, dates, or other fields throughout the PO lifecycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for special instructions, handling requirements, or additional context for the vendor or receiving team. May include delivery window constraints, quality specifications, or packaging requirements.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was created and issued to the vendor. Principal business event timestamp for procurement cycle measurement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms with the vendor, expressed as discount/days or net days (e.g., 2/10_net_30 means 2% discount if paid within 10 days, otherwise net due in 30 days; net_30 means payment due in 30 days with no discount).']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the purchase order, typically formatted as PO followed by numeric sequence. Used for vendor communication and three-way match with goods receipt and invoice.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order. Tracks progression from draft through approval, vendor confirmation, shipment, receipt, and closure. Supports three-way match validation and exception management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|confirmed|in_transit|partially_received|fully_received|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order based on fulfillment method and business purpose. Standard for regular DC delivery, DSD (Direct Store Delivery) for vendor-direct shipments, cross-dock for immediate transfer without storage, promotional for ad-driven buys, emergency for urgent replenishment, blanket for long-term agreements with release schedules.']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order is associated with a promotional event or ad circular, requiring special handling, display, and timing coordination.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the buyer requests delivery of goods to the ship-to location. Used for replenishment planning and vendor performance measurement.']
  rfid_tracking_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order shipment will be tracked using RFID tags for real-time visibility and automated receiving at DC or store.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for delivery of goods from vendor to ship-to location. Impacts lead time and freight cost.']
  slotting_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time or recurring fee charged to vendor for shelf space allocation, particularly for new item introductions. Revenue offset against procurement cost.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this purchase order, calculated based on ship-to location tax jurisdiction and product tax categories.']
  temperature_range_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for cold chain shipments. Null if cold_chain_required_flag is false.']
  temperature_range_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for cold chain shipments. Null if cold_chain_required_flag is false.']
  total_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the purchase order including all line items, before taxes and freight charges. Sum of (ordered_quantity × unit_cost) across all lines.']
  total_po_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Grand total value of the purchase order including line items, taxes, and freight. Used for budget tracking and three-way match validation.']
  vendor_acknowledgment_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the purchase order via EDI 855 or other communication method.']
  vendor_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when vendor acknowledgment was received. Used to measure vendor responsiveness and confirm order acceptance.']

  Note: 'Master record for all purchase orders issued to vendors, encompassing both header-level details (vendor, buyer, order type, delivery window, ship-to location, payment terms, lifecycle status) and line-level detail (SKU, ordered quantity, unit cost, pack size, allowances, line status, confirmed ship date). Supports regular, DSD, cross-dock, and promotional PO types. Enables three-way match with goods receipt and vendor invoice. Sourced from SAP MM and Oracle Retail Merchandising System.'
}

Table supply.po_line {
  po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order line item. Primary key for the po_line product.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the purchasing employee who created or is responsible for this PO line.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the merchandise category or commodity group for this item. Used for spend analysis and category management.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent purchase order header. Links this line item to its containing purchase order document.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the SKU being ordered on this line. Links to the product master for item details.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the distribution center, store, or facility where this line will be received.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier providing this item. Denormalized from header for line-level vendor analysis.']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_item. Business justification: PO line must reference the exact vendor catalog item for pricing, compliance, and traceability; experts expect a vendor_item_id FK.']
  allowance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total promotional allowances, rebates, or discounts applied to this line. Reduces net cost to retailer.']
  allowance_type varchar [note: 'Category of allowance applied to this line. Determines accounting treatment and vendor settlement process.']
  case_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual units (eaches) contained in one case. Used for converting between case and each quantities.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Vendor-confirmed date when this line item will be delivered to the destination. Used for receiving dock scheduling.']
  confirmed_ship_date date [note: 'Vendor-confirmed date when this line item will ship. Received via EDI 855 PO acknowledgment or vendor portal.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which this line item expense is allocated. Used for departmental P&L and budget tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order line was originally created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Logistics method for delivering this line. Warehouse=DC receiving, DSD=Direct Store Delivery, Cross-Dock=no storage, Drop-Ship=vendor to customer.']
  extended_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total line cost calculated as ordered quantity multiplied by unit cost. Base amount before allowances and freight.']
  freight_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Freight or transportation charges allocated to this line item. May be actual or estimated based on shipment method.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for posting the cost of this line item. Determines financial statement classification.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 standard global trade item number for the product. May be GTIN-8, GTIN-12 (UPC), GTIN-13 (EAN), or GTIN-14 format.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, labeling, and transportation compliance.']
  invoiced_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity invoiced by the vendor for this line. Used for three-way match reconciliation (PO, receipt, invoice).']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the item being ordered. Captured at time of PO creation for reference and receiving verification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order line was last updated. Tracks most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the purchase order. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items on the PO document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order line. Tracks progression from creation through receiving and closure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions for this line item. May include receiving instructions, quality requirements, or vendor communications.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the item ordered on this line. Expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is USDA organic certified. Used for merchandising, labeling, and regulatory compliance.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Consumer-facing pack size description (e.g., 12 oz, 1 gallon, 500g). Used for merchandising and shelf labeling.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is a store-owned private label brand versus a national brand CPG product.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity received to date for this line. Updated as goods receipts are posted in the WMS.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the retailer requests delivery of this line item. Used for supply planning and vendor scheduling.']
  srp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vendor\'s suggested retail price for the item. Used as a reference for pricing decisions and margin analysis.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction or rate code applicable to this line item. Used for sales tax calculation and compliance reporting.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature control zone for this item during transportation and storage. Critical for cold chain compliance.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Agreed cost per unit of measure from the vendor. Used for invoice matching and COGS calculation. Excludes allowances and discounts.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the ordered quantity. EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, LB=Pound, KG=Kilogram, GAL=Gallon, LTR=Liter, DZ=Dozen, BX=Box. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|GAL|LTR|DZ|BX — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit UPC barcode identifier for the item. Used for scanning at receiving and point-of-sale.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each SKU/item on a purchase order. Captures ordered quantity, unit of measure, agreed cost per unit, SRP, GTIN/UPC, item description, pack size, case pack quantity, allowances, line status, and confirmed ship date. Each line maps to a single SKU and is the atomic unit for receiving reconciliation and invoice matching in SAP MM.'
}

Table supply.inbound_shipment {
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound shipment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for delivering this shipment.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location (DC or store) where the shipment is to be received.']
  food_safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_inspection. Business justification: Required: Inbound shipment inspection report needed for food safety compliance; experts expect each inbound shipment to be linked to a food safety inspection record.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that authorized this shipment from the vendor.']
  receiving_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: DC receipt process logs which associate performed receipt for labor tracking and compliance.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier originating this shipment.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the origin location (vendor facility, supplier DC, or cross-dock) from which the shipment departed.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the shipment arrived at the destination dock and was checked in.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date the shipment departed from the origin location.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-provided advance ship notice number transmitted via EDI 856 to notify the DC or store of the pending shipment.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued bill of lading number serving as the legal document for the shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inbound shipment record was first created in the system (typically when ASN was received).']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment is designated for cross-dock operations (transfer without storage).']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Type of destination facility receiving the shipment: Distribution Center, Store, Cross-Dock, or Micro-Fulfillment Center.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any receiving discrepancies, damages, or quality issues identified during inspection.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total freight cost for this shipment in USD.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Freight payment terms defining who pays for transportation: Prepaid (vendor pays), Collect (buyer pays), Third-Party, FOB-Origin, or FOB-Destination.']
  haccp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment met all HACCP food safety requirements during transit and receiving.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inbound shipment record was last modified.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for receiving and putaway: Standard (normal flow), High (expedited), Urgent (same-day), Critical (immediate).']
  receiving_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receiving process was completed and all items were putaway or staged.']
  receiving_discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any discrepancies (quantity variance, damage, quality issues) were identified during receiving.']
  receiving_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receiving process began (unloading and inspection started).']
  rfid_tracked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment was tracked using RFID technology for real-time visibility.']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date the shipment is scheduled to arrive at the destination location.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the shipment arrival at the destination dock, used for dock scheduling and labor planning.']
  scheduled_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date the shipment was scheduled to depart from the origin location.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the trailer to ensure shipment integrity during transit.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound shipment: Planned (ASN received), In-Transit (departed origin), Arrived (at destination dock), Receiving (being unloaded), Received (fully processed), or Cancelled.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment method: Standard warehouse delivery, Direct Store Delivery (DSD), Cross-Dock transfer, Emergency replenishment, or Inter-facility Transfer.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment maintained required temperature throughout transit (True) or experienced temperature excursions (False).']
  temperature_max_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for temperature-controlled shipments to maintain product integrity.']
  temperature_min_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for temperature-controlled shipments to maintain product integrity.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Cold chain temperature requirement for this shipment: Ambient (room temperature), Refrigerated (33-40°F), Frozen (0°F or below), or Controlled (specific range).']
  total_case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cases (cartons) included in this shipment across all line items.']
  total_cube_ft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic feet, used for capacity planning and freight optimization.']
  total_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in this shipment.']
  total_unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual units (eaches) included in this shipment across all SKUs.']
  total_weight_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in pounds, including packaging.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trailer or container used to transport the shipment.']

  Note: 'Tracks vendor-initiated shipments (ASN — Advance Ship Notice) from origin to DC or store, encompassing both shipment-level details (carrier, BOL, trailer, origin, destination, scheduled/actual arrival, temperature requirement, status) and line-level detail (SKU, shipped quantity, lot number, expiration date, country of origin, HACCP compliance, pallet configuration). Integrates with JDA TMS and Manhattan Associates WMS for receiving reconciliation.'
}

Table supply.shipment_line {
  shipment_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shipment_line']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order line that this shipment line fulfills. Used for receiving reconciliation in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent inbound shipment (ASN header) that contains this line item.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU being shipped. Links to the product master for item details.']
  cases_per_layer varchar [note: 'Number of cases in each layer of the pallet. Used for pallet configuration and receiving verification.']
  cases_per_pallet varchar [note: 'Number of cases stacked on each pallet. Standard pallet configuration for this SKU.']
  catch_weight_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is a catch-weight product (variable weight, such as meat or produce). True if catch weight, False otherwise.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product was manufactured or grown. Required for customs and labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment line record was first created in the system.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line is designated for cross-dock operations (transfer without storage). True if cross-dock, False otherwise.']
  cube_per_unit decimal(10,3) [note: 'Cubic volume of one unit. Used for warehouse space allocation and transportation planning.']
  cube_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for cubic volume (cubic feet, cubic meters, cubic inches).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit cost and extended cost.']
  damage_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units damaged during shipment or receiving. Used for shrink tracking and vendor claims.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line is part of a direct store delivery (bypassing DC). True if DSD, False if DC delivery.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-by date. Critical for perishable inventory management and FIFO rotation.']
  extended_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost for this line (unit cost × shipped quantity). Used for invoice reconciliation and financial reporting.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 standard global trade item number (UPC/EAN) for the product. Used for barcode scanning and item identification.']
  haccp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment line meets HACCP food safety standards. True if compliant, False otherwise.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the product being shipped. Includes brand, size, and key attributes.']
  layers_per_pallet varchar [note: 'Number of layers (tiers) of cases on each pallet. Used for pallet building and warehouse stacking.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the shipment. Used for ordering and identification of individual line items.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer lot or batch number for traceability. Critical for recalls and quality control, especially for perishables and pharmacy items.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculation and quality tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment line record was last updated.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is USDA organic certified. True if certified, False otherwise.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Number of individual units (eaches) contained in one case or pack. Used for case-to-each conversion.']
  pallet_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the pallet containing this line item. Used for warehouse putaway and RFID tracking.']
  pallet_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of pallets for this line item. Used for warehouse space planning and cross-dock operations.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality inspection for this line. Required for perishables, pharmacy items, and high-value goods.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity received and verified at the DC or store. May differ from shipped quantity due to damage or discrepancies.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this line item was physically received and verified at the DC or store.']
  receiving_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the receiving process for this line item in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier for automated tracking and inventory visibility. Used for real-time supply chain monitoring.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the item shipped by the vendor. Used for receiving reconciliation against ordered quantity.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature zone for the product. Critical for cold chain logistics and HACCP compliance.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit paid to the vendor. Used for inventory valuation and COGS calculation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the shipped quantity (each, case, pallet, layer, inner pack, weight, volume). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|layer|inner_pack|pound|kilogram|liter|gallon — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for the item. Standard barcode identifier used in North American retail.']
  weight_per_unit decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of one unit in the specified unit of measure. Used for freight calculation and warehouse capacity planning.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for weight (pounds, kilograms, ounces, grams).']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU within an inbound shipment (ASN line). Captures GTIN/UPC, item description, shipped quantity, unit of measure, pack size, lot number, expiration date, country of origin, HACCP compliance flag, temperature zone, and pallet/layer configuration. Used for receiving reconciliation against PO lines in Manhattan Associates WMS.'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods receipt transaction. Primary key.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center (DC) or store where the goods were physically received.']
  food_safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_inspection. Business justification: Required: Goods receipt must be validated by a food safety inspection; the link enables traceability of inspection outcomes to receipt transactions.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment or advanced ship notice (ASN) associated with this receipt.']
  primary_goods_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or store employee who performed the physical receipt and inspection.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this goods receipt is recorded.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom the goods were received.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'The bill of lading number provided by the carrier, serving as the shipping document and receipt for the goods in transit.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transportation carrier or logistics provider that delivered the shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this goods receipt record was first created in the system.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this receipt is part of a cross-dock operation where goods are transferred directly from inbound to outbound without storage (True) or follows standard receiving and putaway (False).']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'The physical dock door or receiving bay where the shipment was unloaded.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this receipt is a direct store delivery from a vendor bypassing the distribution center (True) or a standard DC receipt (False).']
  gr_document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known goods receipt document number generated by the ERP or WMS system (e.g., SAP MIGO document number).']
  inspection_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required quality inspection has been completed (True) or is still pending (False).']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt requires formal quality inspection before acceptance into inventory (True) or can be received without inspection (False).']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of the quality inspection: PASSED (all items accepted), FAILED (all items rejected), PARTIAL (some accepted, some rejected), PENDING (inspection not yet complete), NOT_REQUIRED (no inspection needed).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this goods receipt record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  osd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt contains any overage, shortage, or damage discrepancies (True) or was received as expected (False).']
  osd_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the nature and details of any overage, shortage, or damage issues identified during receipt.']
  osd_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the OS&D discrepancy: OVERAGE (more received than ordered), SHORTAGE (less received than ordered), DAMAGE (damaged goods), MULTIPLE (combination of issues), NONE (no discrepancy).']
  packing_slip_number varchar [note: 'The packing slip or delivery note number provided by the vendor, listing the contents of the shipment.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the goods receipt was posted to inventory and financial ledgers.']
  putaway_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the putaway process for moving received goods from the receiving area to storage locations: NOT_STARTED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, BLOCKED.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the goods were physically received at the location.']
  receipt_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the receiving personnel regarding any special conditions, observations, or issues during the receipt process.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt: DRAFT (not yet started), IN_PROGRESS (receiving in process), COMPLETED (physically received but not posted), POSTED (posted to inventory), CANCELLED (receipt cancelled).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the goods receipt transaction was initiated or completed.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receipt transaction: PO (purchase order receipt), DSD (direct store delivery), RETURN (vendor return), TRANSFER (inter-location transfer), CROSS_DOCK (cross-dock operation), SAMPLE (vendor sample).']
  rfid_scanned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID tags were scanned during the receipt process for automated item tracking (True) or manual receipt was performed (False).']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security seal was found intact upon receipt (True) or broken/missing (False).']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'The security seal number affixed to the trailer or container, verified at receipt to ensure shipment integrity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this goods receipt transaction: SAP_MM (SAP Materials Management), MANHATTAN_WMS (Manhattan Associates WMS), ORACLE_RETAIL (Oracle Retail), MANUAL (manual entry).']
  temperature_at_receipt decimal(5,2) [note: 'The measured temperature (in Celsius) of the shipment at the time of receipt, critical for cold chain compliance and perishable goods.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment temperature was within the acceptable range per cold chain requirements (True) or out of compliance (False).']
  total_accepted_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'The aggregate quantity of all items accepted into inventory after quality inspection, summed across all line items.']
  total_received_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'The aggregate quantity of all items physically received in this goods receipt transaction, summed across all line items.']
  total_rejected_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'The aggregate quantity of all items rejected due to damage, quality issues, or discrepancies, summed across all line items.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'The trailer or container identification number used for the inbound shipment.']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt of merchandise at a DC or store against a purchase order or inbound shipment, encompassing both receipt-level details (date/time, dock door, receiver, temperature, GR document number) and item-level detail (received/accepted/rejected quantities by SKU, rejection reason, lot number, expiration date, putaway assignment). Captures OS&D (overage/shortage/damage) at both aggregate and item level. Sourced from SAP MM MIGO and Manhattan Associates WMS.'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt_line {
  goods_receipt_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods receipt line item. Primary key for line-level receiving detail.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate who physically received and counted this line. Supports accountability and productivity tracking.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: REQUIRED: Goods receipt analysis by category supports quality and compliance reporting across stores.']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Needed for Cost of Goods Received posting, tying each receipt line to the cost_price record for accurate financial and margin reporting.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent goods receipt header document. Links this line to the overall receiving event.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order line that this receipt fulfills. Enables PO-to-receipt reconciliation.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Target store for cross-dock shipment. Populated when goods are received at DC for immediate transfer to store.']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_item. Business justification: Goods receipt validation requires linking received items to the vendor\'s catalog entry; a vendor_item_id FK is essential for quality and audit.']
  accepted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity accepted into inventory after quality inspection. Represents units available for putaway and sale.']
  asn_line_number varchar [note: 'Line number from the vendor\'s advance shipping notice. Links physical receipt to pre-notification for cross-dock and receiving planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this goods receipt line record was first created. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line is designated for cross-dock operations (transfer without storage). Drives expedited handling workflow.']
  cube_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the received quantity. Essential for warehouse space utilization and transportation planning.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for cost amounts. Supports multi-currency procurement and financial reporting.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this receipt bypassed the DC and was delivered directly to store by vendor. Identifies DSD workflow.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product should not be sold or consumed. Essential for perishable inventory management and shrink prevention.']
  extended_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost for this line (accepted quantity multiplied by unit cost). Represents inventory value added by this receipt line.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global standard identifier for trade items. Supports international supply chain traceability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this record was last updated. Tracks data change history for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the goods receipt document. Determines ordering of line items.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s batch or lot identifier for traceability. Critical for recalls, FIFO rotation, and food safety compliance.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured or produced. Supports shelf-life calculations and quality tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations recorded during receiving. Captures exceptions, damage details, or special handling instructions.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity originally ordered on the purchase order for this line item. Baseline for receipt variance analysis.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets received for this line item. Supports dock-to-stock efficiency tracking and handling resource planning.']
  putaway_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when putaway task was completed and goods were placed in storage location. Marks end of receiving-to-stock cycle.']
  putaway_task_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse task created to move received goods to storage. Links receiving to putaway execution.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line requires quality inspection before acceptance. Determines routing to QC hold area.']
  quality_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of quality inspection process. Determines whether goods are accepted into inventory or rejected.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity physically received and counted during the receiving process. Includes both accepted and rejected units.']
  received_temperature decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature of the product at time of receipt in Celsius. Critical for cold chain validation and food safety compliance.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this line was physically received and recorded in the system. Primary business event timestamp for receiving cycle time analysis.']
  receiving_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of this receipt line. Tracks workflow progression from dock to putaway completion.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity rejected during receiving due to quality issues, damage, or discrepancies. Triggers vendor return or disposal workflow.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why units were rejected. Enables root cause analysis of receiving issues and vendor performance tracking.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique RFID tag identifier attached to the received goods. Enables automated tracking and real-time inventory visibility.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal product identifier for the item being received. Primary product reference for inventory management.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse location where the received goods are assigned for putaway. Determines physical placement in DC or backroom.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature classification for cold chain compliance. Determines handling and storage requirements.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the received quantity is expressed. Determines how inventory is counted and tracked.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Standard barcode identifier scanned during receiving. Used for automated item identification and verification.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of the received quantity in kilograms. Used for freight reconciliation and capacity planning.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU received in a goods receipt event. Captures received quantity by UOM, accepted vs. rejected quantity, rejection reason code, lot number, expiration date, storage location assignment, putaway task reference, and unit cost at receipt. Enables item-level receiving accuracy tracking and shrink attribution.'
}

Table supply.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the replenishment order. Primary key for internal DC-to-store or DC-to-DC transfer orders generated by Blue Yonder CAO/demand planning or manually by buyers.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to deliver the order. May be internal fleet or third-party carrier.']
  destination_location_store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store or DC receiving the replenishment inventory. Can be a retail store, MFC, or downstream DC.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.node. Business justification: REQUIRED: Replenishment execution assigns a fulfillment node (DC) for picking/shipping; node performance reports depend on this link.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: Pharmacy inventory replenishment orders require linking to the pharmacy location for stock planning, fill‑rate analysis, and regulatory audit.']
  primary_replenishment_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the buyer or planner who created or approved the replenishment order. Applicable for manual and override orders.']
  primary_replenishment_store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or warehouse from which inventory is being transferred. Typically a DC in the supply chain network.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Needed for Promotional Replenishment Planning process that creates replenishment orders based on active promo campaigns.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment that includes this replenishment order. Multiple orders may be consolidated into a single shipment for transportation efficiency.']
  transport_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation route assigned for delivery. Used for multi-stop route optimization and last-mile delivery planning.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the order was received at the destination location. Used to measure delivery SLA performance and calculate fill rate timing.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date when the order was shipped from the source DC. Used to measure on-time shipment performance and calculate actual transit time.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order requires management approval before fulfillment. True for high-value orders, emergency orders, or orders exceeding allocation thresholds.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order was approved. Used for approval cycle time analysis and compliance tracking.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for order cancellation. Populated only when order_status is cancelled. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains perishable items requiring temperature-controlled transportation and storage. True if any line item requires refrigeration or freezing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and order age analysis.']
  cross_dock_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is eligible for cross-dock operations, where inventory is transferred directly from inbound to outbound without intermediate storage. Used for flow-through optimization.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination location. Store represents retail locations, DC represents distribution centers, MFC represents micro-fulfillment centers for omnichannel orders.']
  fill_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered quantity that was successfully shipped, calculated as (total_shipped_quantity / total_ordered_quantity) * 100. Key supply chain KPI for measuring DC fulfillment performance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, exceptions, or context about the replenishment order. Used for communication between buyers, planners, and fulfillment teams.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the replenishment order was created in the system. Represents the business event timestamp for order initiation.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the replenishment order, used for tracking and communication across supply chain systems.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the replenishment order in the fulfillment workflow. Tracks progression from creation through delivery and enables exception management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|allocated|picking|packed|shipped|in_transit|received|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the replenishment order based on trigger and business purpose. Auto-replenishment orders are system-generated based on CAO algorithms, manual orders are buyer-initiated, emergency orders address stockouts, seasonal orders support seasonal demand, promotional orders support ad circulars, and new store orders support store openings.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for order fulfillment and transportation routing. Critical priority is used for emergency stockout situations, high priority for fast-moving SKUs, normal for standard replenishment, low for slow-moving items.']
  replenishment_trigger varchar [note: 'The business rule or algorithm that initiated the replenishment order. CAO forecast uses demand planning algorithms, min-max threshold triggers when inventory falls below reorder point, safety stock maintains buffer inventory, manual override is buyer-initiated, promotional event supports ad circulars, seasonal demand addresses seasonal patterns.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Target date by which the destination location requires the replenishment inventory to arrive. Used for transportation planning and SLA tracking.']
  rfid_tracking_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is tracked using RFID technology for real-time visibility and automated receiving. True if RFID tags are applied at case or pallet level.']
  scheduled_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date for the order to be shipped from the source DC. Calculated based on requested delivery date and transit time.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special handling requirements such as fragile items, hazardous materials, or delivery time windows. Communicated to warehouse and transportation teams.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature control classification for the order. Ambient for shelf-stable products, refrigerated for fresh produce and dairy, frozen for frozen foods, multi-temp for mixed orders requiring multiple temperature zones.']
  total_cube_volume decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the order in cubic feet or cubic meters. Used for trailer utilization optimization and cross-dock planning.']
  total_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKU line items included in the replenishment order. Used for picking complexity assessment and labor planning.']
  total_ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all line items in the replenishment order, measured in eaches or cases depending on line-level unit of measure. Used for capacity planning and transportation optimization.']
  total_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets required to ship the order. Used for transportation capacity planning and dock scheduling.']
  total_received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all line items received at the destination location. May differ from shipped quantity due to damage, loss, or receiving discrepancies.']
  total_shipped_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all line items actually shipped from the source DC. May differ from ordered quantity due to inventory availability or allocation adjustments.']
  total_weight decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of the order in pounds or kilograms. Used for transportation cost calculation and carrier selection.']

  Note: 'Store or DC replenishment orders generated by Blue Yonder CAO/demand planning or manually by buyers, encompassing both order-level details (type, source, destination, trigger, status) and line-level detail (SKU, ordered quantity, suggested vs. override quantity, fill rate, line status). Covers auto-replenishment, manual, and emergency order types. Distinct from purchase orders — these are internal DC-to-store transfer orders.'
}

Table supply.replenishment_line {
  replenishment_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the replenishment line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: REQUIRED: Replenishment planning reports need each line tied to a product category for category‑level forecasting and allocation.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the distribution center receiving this replenishment line. Used when replenishing DC inventory rather than store inventory.']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent replenishment order header. Links this line to its parent order.']
  retail_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.retail_price. Business justification: Required for Replenishment Pricing Snapshot report, linking each replenishment line to the exact retail price used for margin analysis.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the store receiving this replenishment line. The target location for inventory delivery.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the vendor supplying this item. Links to the vendor master for supplier information.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date the shipment was received at the destination. Used for on-time delivery metrics and inventory availability.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date the shipment departed from the source location. Used for lead time analysis and performance tracking.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why this replenishment line was cancelled. Used for root-cause analysis of order cancellations.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this replenishment line was cancelled. Null if the line has not been cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this replenishment line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts. Typically USD for domestic operations.']
  extended_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost for this line. Calculated as ordered_quantity * unit_cost. Used for purchase order valuation.']
  fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered quantity that was fulfilled. Calculated as (shipped_quantity / ordered_quantity) * 100. Key metric for supplier performance.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number for the product. 14-digit GTIN used for global supply chain identification.']
  is_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line requires temperature-controlled cold chain logistics for perishable or frozen products.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line is processed through cross-dock operations without storage at the distribution center.']
  is_dsd boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line is fulfilled via Direct Store Delivery from vendor to store, bypassing the distribution center.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this replenishment line record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the replenishment order. Used for ordering and referencing specific lines.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the replenishment line. Tracks progression from creation through fulfillment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|ordered|shipped|received|cancelled|backordered — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ordered_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual quantity ordered for this line. May differ from suggested quantity due to manual overrides or business rules.']
  override_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Manual override quantity if the user changed the suggested order quantity. Null if no override was applied.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the suggested quantity was overridden. Used for root-cause analysis of forecast accuracy issues.']
  override_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the reason for quantity override. Provides additional context beyond the reason code.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Number of eaches per case or inner pack. Used to convert between different units of measure.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level for this replenishment line. Determines processing and shipping urgency for out-of-stock situations.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the product being replenished. Includes brand, size, and key attributes.']
  received_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual quantity received at the destination location. May differ from shipped quantity due to damage or discrepancies.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the replenishment is requested to arrive at the destination. Used for planning and scheduling.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier for tracking this shipment through the supply chain. Used for real-time visibility and inventory accuracy.']
  scheduled_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date for shipment from the source location. Used for transportation planning and coordination.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual quantity shipped by the vendor or distribution center. May differ from ordered quantity due to availability constraints.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the product being replenished. The unique item identifier used across the retail system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated this replenishment line. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  suggested_order_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'System-calculated recommended order quantity from Blue Yonder Demand Planning. The optimal quantity based on forecasting algorithms.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone for storage and transportation. Critical for cold chain compliance and product quality.']
