  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for this replenishment line. Used for inventory valuation and cost of goods sold calculations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the ordered quantity. Defines whether quantity is in eaches, cases, pallets, or other packaging units.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code barcode identifier for the item. Standard 12-digit UPC-A format used for scanning and identification.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each SKU on a replenishment order. Captures SKU, ordered quantity, unit of measure, pack size, suggested order quantity from Blue Yonder, override quantity, override reason, fill rate at fulfillment, and line status. Enables replenishment accuracy analysis and OOS root-cause investigation.'
}

Table supply.dc_transfer {
  dc_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution center transfer transaction. Primary key for the dc_transfer product.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for moving the shipment between origin and destination.']
  created_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Transfer requests are initiated by an associate; needed for labor cost allocation and audit trail.']
  destination_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the target facility to which inventory is being transferred. Can reference a distribution center or store location.']
  fulfillment_delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_route. Business justification: REQUIRED: DC transfer is scheduled as a delivery route; route KPI dashboards need the transfer‑to‑route association.']
  load_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consolidated load or shipment that includes this transfer. Multiple transfers may be combined into a single load for transportation efficiency.']
  origin_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source facility from which inventory is being transferred. Can reference a distribution center or store location.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse picking wave that generated this transfer. Used to group transfers for efficient picking and loading operations.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual arrival date at the destination location when the shipment was received. Null until delivery occurs.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual departure date from the origin location when the shipment left the facility. Null until shipment departs.']
  actual_transit_hours varchar [note: 'Actual duration in hours from departure to arrival. Used for carrier performance analysis and transit time optimization. Null until delivery complete.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the transfer was confirmed as received and put away at the destination location. Marks completion of the transfer lifecycle. Null until confirmation occurs.']
  contains_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the transfer shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and carrier certification per DOT regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the transfer record was first created in the warehouse management system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  damage_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether product damage was reported during receiving inspection. Triggers claims process and carrier performance review.']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination facility. DC is distribution center, store is retail location, MFC is micro-fulfillment center, cross-dock is flow-through facility.']
  estimated_transit_hours varchar [note: 'Planned duration in hours for the shipment to travel from origin to destination. Used for delivery date calculation and carrier performance measurement.']
  freight_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total transportation cost for the transfer shipment in US dollars. Includes carrier charges, fuel surcharges, and accessorial fees. Used for supply chain cost allocation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the transfer record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional transfer instructions, special handling requirements, or operational comments. Used for communication between origin, carrier, and destination.']
  on_time_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the transfer was delivered by the scheduled delivery date. Used for carrier scorecard and supply chain performance metrics.']
  origin_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the origin facility. DC is distribution center, store is retail location, MFC is micro-fulfillment center, cross-dock is flow-through facility.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification for the transfer affecting processing sequence and transportation mode selection. Standard is normal flow, expedited is accelerated, urgent is same-day target, critical is immediate action required.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order if the transfer is fulfilling a store replenishment order or allocation. Links transfer to demand planning.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the transfer shipment uses RFID tags for automated tracking and receiving. Enables real-time visibility and reduces manual scanning.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned arrival date at the destination location. Used for receiving planning and labor scheduling.']
  scheduled_ship_date date [note: 'Planned departure date from the origin location. Used for transportation planning and carrier scheduling.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the trailer to ensure shipment integrity during transit. Verified at receiving to detect tampering.']
  shortage_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether quantity shortages were identified during receiving verification. Triggers inventory reconciliation and carrier investigation.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Cold chain requirement for the transfer shipment. Ambient is room temperature, refrigerated is 32-40F, frozen is below 0F, multi-temp contains mixed zones. Critical for perishable inventory compliance.']
  total_cases varchar [note: 'Total number of cases or cartons included in the transfer shipment. Used for detailed inventory tracking and receiving verification.']
  total_cube_cf decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume of the transfer shipment in cubic feet. Used for trailer utilization optimization and space planning.']
  total_pallets varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in the transfer shipment. Used for capacity planning and receiving resource allocation.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total number of individual units or eaches included in the transfer shipment. Represents the most granular inventory count.']
  total_weight_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight of the transfer shipment in pounds. Used for freight costing, carrier capacity planning, and compliance with weight restrictions.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or container used for the transfer shipment. Used for tracking and yard management.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'The business date when the transfer transaction was initiated or scheduled. This is the principal event date for the transfer, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the transfer transaction, used for tracking and reference across systems and with carriers.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the transfer. Allocation is planned distribution, rebalancing is inventory optimization, emergency is urgent replenishment, recall is product withdrawal, seasonal is assortment change, promotion is event-driven, new-store is initial stocking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: allocation|rebalancing|emergency|recall|seasonal|promotion|new-store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transfer transaction. Planned indicates scheduled but not started, picking indicates warehouse fulfillment in progress, loaded indicates staged on carrier, in-transit indicates shipment en route, delivered indicates arrival at destination, confirmed indicates receiving complete, cancelled indicates transaction voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|picking|loaded|in-transit|delivered|confirmed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer operation indicating the movement pattern and handling requirements. Cross-dock indicates flow-through without storage, pick-and-ship is standard DC fulfillment, store-to-store is lateral rebalancing, dc-to-dc is network redistribution, emergency is expedited replenishment, recall is product withdrawal.']

  Note: 'Records inter-DC and DC-to-store stock transfer movements including cross-dock flow-through and store-to-store rebalancing operations. Captures transfer type (cross-dock, pick-and-ship, store-to-store, DC-to-DC), origin location, destination location, transfer date, carrier, trailer number, total pallets, total cases, transfer reason (allocation, rebalancing, emergency, recall, seasonal), priority level, and transfer status (planned, picking, loaded, in-transit, delivered, confirmed). Managed in Manhattan Associates WMS and SAP WM. Enables inventory movement visibility and network optimization.'
}

Table supply.direct_store_delivery {
  direct_store_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for direct_store_delivery']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: REQUIRED: DSD delivery performance dashboards aggregate metrics by category, so each delivery must reference its category.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: DSD deliveries must be tied to a specific pharmacy location for inventory tracking, compliance reporting, and POS integration.']
  receiving_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the store associate who received and verified the DSD delivery. Used for accountability and training purposes.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the vendor delivering merchandise directly to the store, bypassing the distribution center network. Common DSD vendors include bread, snack, and beverage suppliers.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the DSD delivery was received at the store. Used for on-time delivery performance measurement and vendor compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DSD delivery record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the DSD delivery transaction. Supports multi-currency operations for international vendors.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the DSD delivery transaction, typically assigned by the vendor or receiving system. Used for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DSD delivery transaction. Tracks progression from scheduling through final reconciliation or dispute resolution.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the DSD delivery invoice. Includes promotional allowances, volume discounts, and vendor rebates.']
  discrepancy_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any quantity or quality discrepancies identified during DSD delivery receipt. Used for vendor dispute resolution and claims processing.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a discrepancy was identified between the delivered quantity and the invoice quantity. Triggers dispute resolution workflow.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Driver\'s license number for the delivery driver. Captured for security verification and compliance with store access policies.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the delivery driver who executed the DSD delivery. Used for accountability, quality tracking, and security verification.']
  invoice_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount for the DSD delivery before any adjustments or discounts. Represents the vendor\'s billed amount for the merchandise delivered.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice number associated with the DSD delivery. Used for accounts payable processing and financial reconciliation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DSD delivery record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount due for the DSD delivery after applying taxes, discounts, and adjustments. Represents the final payment obligation to the vendor.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used to settle the DSD delivery invoice. Common methods include check, electronic funds transfer (EFT), credit terms, cash on delivery (COD), or vendor account.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms for the DSD delivery, such as net 30, net 60, or 2/10 net 30. Defines the payment due date and any early payment discounts.']
  pos_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSD delivery has been integrated with the POS system for immediate inventory and cost updates. Critical for real-time inventory accuracy.']
  pos_integration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DSD delivery was integrated with the POS system. Used for inventory accuracy tracking and system reconciliation.']
  quality_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSD delivery passed quality inspection at the time of receipt. Used for vendor performance tracking and product quality assurance.']
  quality_issue_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any quality issues identified during DSD delivery receipt. Includes details on damaged goods, expired products, or specification non-compliance.']
  receiving_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured by the receiving associate during DSD delivery receipt. Includes observations, special handling instructions, or operational comments.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DSD delivery was fully reconciled between received quantities, invoice amounts, and POS integration. Marks completion of the DSD workflow.']
  route_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned route identifier for the delivery driver\'s scheduled stops. Used for delivery sequencing and driver performance tracking.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for the DSD delivery to arrive at the store location. Used for store labor planning and receiving dock scheduling.']
  scheduled_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the DSD delivery arrival, including time of day. Enables precise receiving dock coordination and labor allocation.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in the DSD delivery invoice. Used for sales tax reconciliation and financial reporting.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSD delivery met required temperature control standards for cold chain products. Critical for food safety compliance and quality assurance.']
  temperature_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading (in Fahrenheit) recorded at the time of DSD delivery receipt. Used for cold chain compliance verification and quality control.']
  total_case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cases delivered in the DSD transaction. Used for receiving dock capacity planning and inventory volume tracking.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKUs included in the DSD delivery. Used for receiving complexity assessment and labor planning.']
  total_unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual units (eaches) delivered across all SKUs in the DSD delivery. Used for detailed inventory tracking and shrink analysis.']
  vendor_delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-provided delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the DSD delivery. Used for cross-reference and dispute resolution.']

  Note: 'Direct Store Delivery records for vendor-delivered merchandise bypassing the DC network. Captures DSD vendor, delivery date/time, store location, delivery route number, driver name, total SKUs, total cases, invoice number, payment method (check, EFT, credit), receiving associate, temperature compliance flag, product category (bread, snacks, beverages, dairy), and DSD status (scheduled, delivered, reconciled, disputed). Common for bread, snack, and beverage vendors. Integrates with POS for immediate inventory and cost update.'
}

Table supply.transport_route {
  transport_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transportation route. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier or logistics provider responsible for executing this route. Links to vendor or carrier master data.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or facility where the route originates. Links to the facility or warehouse master data.']
  planner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Route planning is assigned to a specific associate; needed for accountability and performance reporting.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Required: Certain transport routes require regulatory filings (e.g., hazardous material permits); linking routes to filings supports compliance reporting.']
  backhaul_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route includes backhaul operations, where the trailer picks up goods from vendors or stores on the return trip to the DC. True if backhaul is planned; false otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was first created in the JDA TMS system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cross_dock_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route supports cross-dock operations, where goods are transferred directly from inbound to outbound trailers without intermediate storage. True if cross-dock is allowed; false otherwise.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this route configuration was or will be retired or replaced. Null for currently active routes. Used for route lifecycle management and historical tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this route configuration became or will become active. Used for route lifecycle management and historical tracking.']
  estimated_transit_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected total time to complete the route including driving time, stop time, and rest breaks, measured in hours. Used for scheduling and on-time delivery tracking.']
  fill_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of trailer capacity utilization for this route, used for load optimization and efficiency tracking. Represents the percentage of orders fulfilled completely on each route execution.']
  fuel_surcharge_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Fuel surcharge rate applied to this route, typically expressed as a percentage or per-mile amount. Adjusted periodically based on diesel fuel price indices.']
  gps_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GPS tracking is enabled for real-time location monitoring of the trailer or truck on this route. True if GPS devices are installed; false otherwise.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route is certified and equipped to transport hazardous materials. True if carrier and equipment meet DOT HAZMAT requirements; false otherwise.']
  last_executed_date date [note: 'Date when this route was most recently executed. Used for route utilization analysis and performance tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was last updated in the JDA TMS system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, instructions, or comments about the route. May include driver instructions, seasonal considerations, or operational alerts.']
  on_time_delivery_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time delivery performance for this route, used for carrier performance evaluation and SLA compliance. Typically 95-98% for grocery retail.']
  operating_days varchar [note: 'Days of the week on which this route operates, typically stored as comma-separated day codes (e.g., MON,WED,FRI) or day-of-week flags.']
  rfid_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID technology is used to track shipments on this route. True if RFID tags are applied to pallets or cases for real-time visibility; false otherwise.']
  route_cost_fixed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed cost component for executing this route, independent of distance or load. Includes dispatch fees, administrative overhead, and minimum carrier charges.']
  route_cost_per_mile decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard cost per mile for this route, used for freight cost allocation and budgeting. Includes fuel, driver wages, maintenance, and carrier fees.']
  route_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this route is executed. Daily routes run every day; weekly routes run on specific days; bi-weekly routes run every two weeks; monthly routes run once per month; on-demand routes are triggered by need; seasonal routes operate during specific periods.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the route, typically indicating origin and destination or geographic coverage.']
  route_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the route, used in operational communications and dispatch systems. Managed in JDA TMS.']
  route_optimization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route is subject to dynamic optimization algorithms in JDA TMS. True if stop sequence and timing can be adjusted by the system; false if route is fixed.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route. Active routes are in regular operation; inactive routes are temporarily not in use; suspended routes are on hold pending review; planned routes are designed but not yet operational; archived routes are historical records.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route based on its operational purpose. DC-to-store routes deliver from distribution center to retail locations; inter-DC routes transfer between distribution centers; DSD (Direct Store Delivery) routes bypass DC; cross-dock routes involve immediate transfer without storage; backhaul routes return empty trailers or pick up vendor goods; milk-run routes serve multiple stops in sequence.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Standard time of day when the route is scheduled to depart from the origin DC, stored as HH:MM format (e.g., 06:00 for 6 AM departure). Used for dispatch planning and driver scheduling.']
  special_handling_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special handling, loading, or delivery requirements for this route (e.g., liftgate required, inside delivery, appointment required, security escort).']
  stop_count varchar [note: 'Total number of delivery or pickup stops on this route, excluding the origin DC. Used for route complexity assessment and driver workload planning.']
  temperature_max_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in Fahrenheit for temperature-controlled routes. Used for cold chain monitoring and compliance verification.']
  temperature_min_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in Fahrenheit for temperature-controlled routes. Used for cold chain monitoring and compliance verification.']
  temperature_zone_required varchar [note: 'Temperature control requirement for this route. Ambient for room temperature; refrigerated for 32-40°F; frozen for below 0°F; multi-temp for trailers with multiple zones; controlled for specific temperature ranges. Critical for cold chain compliance.']
  total_route_distance_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance of the route from origin to final destination, including all intermediate stops, measured in miles. Used for freight cost calculation and driver scheduling.']
  trailer_capacity_cases varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cases that can be loaded on the trailer for this route, used when shipments are measured in case count rather than pallets.']
  trailer_capacity_cubic_feet decimal(12,2) [note: 'Volumetric capacity of the trailer in cubic feet, used for cube optimization and load planning.']
  trailer_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Maximum number of standard pallets that can be loaded on the trailer for this route. Typically 26-28 pallets for a 53-foot trailer.']
  trailer_capacity_weight_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the trailer in pounds, constrained by DOT regulations and vehicle specifications. Typically 40,000-45,000 lbs for a loaded trailer.']
  trailer_type varchar [note: 'Type of trailer equipment required for this route. Dry van for ambient goods; refrigerated for cold chain; flatbed for oversized items; tanker for liquids; intermodal for rail/truck combination; multi-temp for mixed temperature zones.']

  Note: 'Master record for DC-to-store and inter-DC transportation routes and their execution, encompassing route definition (origin DC, stop sequence, mileage, carrier, trailer type, capacity, frequency), individual stop details (stop location, arrival window, pallet/case count, special handling), and load plan execution (trailer assignment, departure time, load sequence, temperature zones, dispatch status). Managed in JDA TMS. Enables route optimization, freight cost allocation, and carrier performance tracking.'
}

Table supply.route_stop {
  route_stop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for route_stop']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center if this stop is at a DC. Null for non-DC stops.']
  driver_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Each stop is executed by a driver associate; link required for driver scheduling and safety audits.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: Route stops flagged for pharmacy deliveries require explicit pharmacy location linkage for routing optimization and performance metrics.']
  transport_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent transport route to which this stop belongs.']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle arrived at this stop. Null if stop not yet reached.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle departed from this stop. Null if stop not yet completed.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation number or reference for the scheduled appointment at this stop. Null if no appointment required.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Number of cases (cartons) to be delivered or picked up at this stop.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop involves temperature-controlled (refrigerated or frozen) cargo requiring cold chain logistics (True = cold chain required, False = ambient).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route stop record was first created in the system.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Specific dock door or bay number at the stop location where unloading should occur.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop is a Direct Store Delivery (DSD) from vendor directly to store, bypassing the DC (True = DSD, False = standard DC-to-store delivery).']
  estimated_unload_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes required to unload cargo at this stop, based on volume and handling requirements.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the stop failed or was skipped (e.g., location closed, refused delivery, access denied, weather delay). Null if stop completed successfully.']
  fuel_center_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop includes fuel delivery to a fuel center or gas station (True = fuel delivery, False = no fuel).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop involves hazardous materials requiring special handling and regulatory compliance (True = HAZMAT present, False = no HAZMAT).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route stop record was last updated.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets to be delivered or picked up at this stop.']
  pharmacy_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop includes pharmacy prescription or pharmaceutical inventory delivery requiring DEA compliance (True = pharmacy delivery, False = general merchandise).']
  requires_appointment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop requires a pre-scheduled appointment for delivery or pickup (True = appointment required, False = no appointment needed).']
  rfid_scan_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID scanning is required at this stop for inventory tracking and verification (True = RFID scan required, False = no RFID requirement).']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date the vehicle is scheduled to arrive at this stop.']
  scheduled_arrival_time_end timestamp [note: 'End of the scheduled arrival time window for this stop (latest acceptable arrival time).']
  scheduled_arrival_time_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the scheduled arrival time window for this stop (earliest acceptable arrival time).']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Planned time the vehicle is scheduled to depart from this stop after completing unload/load activities.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements at this stop (e.g., pharmacy drop, fuel center delivery, refrigerated unload, rear dock only, appointment required).']
  stop_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the stop location.']
  stop_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, dock door) for the stop location.']
  stop_city varchar [note: 'City name of the stop location.']
  stop_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the stop location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  stop_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the stop location in decimal degrees.']
  stop_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility at this stop: store (retail location), DC (Distribution Center), MFC (Micro-Fulfillment Center), vendor (supplier location), cross_dock (transfer facility), or return_center (reverse logistics facility).']
  stop_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the stop location in decimal degrees.']
  stop_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the stop location.']
  stop_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this stop within the route (1 = first stop, 2 = second stop, etc.).']
  stop_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code of the stop location.']
  stop_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this route stop in its lifecycle: scheduled (planned but not started), in_transit (vehicle en route), arrived (vehicle at location), unloading (active unload), completed (stop finished successfully), skipped (stop bypassed), or failed (stop could not be completed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_transit|arrived|unloading|completed|skipped|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stop_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the stop activity: delivery (drop-off only), pickup (pick-up only), or delivery_and_pickup (both activities at this stop).']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control zone for cargo at this stop: ambient (no temperature control), refrigerated (33-40°F), frozen (0°F or below), or multi_temp (multiple zones).']
  total_cube_cubic_feet decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume in cubic feet of all cargo for this stop.']
  total_weight_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight in pounds of all cargo for this stop.']

  Note: 'Individual stop details within a transport route, representing each store or DC on a delivery run. Captures stop sequence number, stop location (store or DC), scheduled arrival window, scheduled departure time, estimated unload time, pallet count for stop, cases for stop, special handling instructions (e.g., pharmacy drop, fuel center delivery), and stop status. Managed in JDA TMS.'
}

Table supply.load_plan {
  load_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trailer load plan. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver assigned to transport this load.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center originating this outbound shipment.']
  transport_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assigned transportation route for this load plan.']
  accessorial_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total accessorial charges (detention, lumper fees, fuel surcharge, etc.) for this load in USD.']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the trailer arrived at the destination, used for delivery performance measurement and receiving coordination.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the trailer departed from the distribution center, used for on-time performance tracking and freight audit.']
  ambient_pallets varchar [note: 'Number of pallets containing ambient temperature products (dry goods) loaded on this trailer.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the bill of lading document associated with this shipment, serving as the legal contract between shipper and carrier.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the transportation carrier assigned to this load plan.']
  carrier_scac varchar [note: 'Four-letter code uniquely identifying the carrier responsible for transporting this load, as defined by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this load plan record was first created in the transportation management system.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this load was assembled using cross-dock operations (transfer without storage) for fast-moving inventory.']
  dispatched_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the load plan was officially dispatched and the driver was authorized to depart, marking the transition from loading to in-transit status.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dock door assigned for loading this trailer at the distribution center.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the driver assigned to this load plan, used for dispatch and delivery confirmation.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total freight transportation charge for this load in USD, used for carrier payment and freight cost allocation.']
  frozen_pallets varchar [note: 'Number of pallets containing frozen products requiring frozen temperature control (typically 0°F or below).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this load contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and DOT placarding per 49 CFR regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this load plan record was last updated, used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  load_plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the load plan, externally visible and used for tracking and communication with carriers and warehouse operations.']
  load_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the load plan indicating its progress through the outbound fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|loading|loaded|dispatched|in_transit|delivered|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  load_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order number for this load plan within the daily dispatch schedule, used for dock door assignment and loading prioritization.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the load based on transportation mode and delivery pattern (FTL, LTL, dedicated route, or pool distribution).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing special instructions, delivery requirements, or operational notes related to this load plan.']
  planned_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp when the trailer is expected to arrive at the destination store or facility.']
  planned_departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the trailer is planned to depart from the distribution center.']
  planned_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp when the trailer is planned to depart from the distribution center, used for dock scheduling and carrier coordination.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned progressive number used for shipment tracking and freight bill matching in the freight audit process.']
  refrigerated_pallets varchar [note: 'Number of pallets containing refrigerated products requiring cold chain temperature control (typically 32-40°F).']
  rfid_tracked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this load contains RFID-tagged pallets or cases for real-time inventory visibility and automated receiving.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the security seal applied to the trailer door after loading, used for tamper detection and cargo security verification.']
  stop_count varchar [note: 'Total number of delivery stops on this route, used for multi-stop route optimization and driver scheduling.']
  temperature_zone_type varchar [note: 'Primary temperature control zone classification for this load, indicating whether the trailer is single-zone or multi-temperature equipped.']
  total_cube_cf decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the load in cubic feet, used for trailer utilization analysis and capacity optimization.']
  total_freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total combined freight and accessorial cost for this load in USD, used for freight spend analysis and carrier performance evaluation.']
  total_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned mileage for this route, used for fuel cost estimation and driver compensation calculation.']
  total_pallets varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets loaded onto the trailer for this shipment, used for capacity planning and load verification.']
  total_weight_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight of the load in pounds, used for freight rating, carrier billing, and DOT compliance verification.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer assigned to this load plan, used for equipment tracking and yard management.']

  Note: 'Trailer load plan generated by JDA TMS for each outbound DC shipment. Captures load plan number, assigned route, trailer number, carrier SCAC, driver assignment, planned departure date/time, total pallets, total weight, total cube, temperature zones loaded (ambient, refrigerated, frozen), load sequence, and load plan status (planned, loaded, dispatched, delivered). Enables freight audit and carrier performance tracking.'
}

Table supply.vendor_lead_time {
  vendor_lead_time_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor lead time record. Primary key for this operational planning parameter.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system that last updated this lead time parameter. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary carrier used for this vendor-item-destination lane. Links to carrier master for routing and performance tracking.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Internal item identifier linking to the product master. References the specific product for which lead time applies.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination DC, store, or MFC receiving the shipment. Determines where the lead time applies.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor supplying the item. Links to the vendor master in the vendor domain.']
  case_pack_size varchar [note: 'Number of units per case pack from the vendor. Defines the vendor shipping unit for this item.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature-controlled logistics are required for this vendor-item combination. True for perishable items requiring refrigeration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead time record was first created in the system. Establishes the origin point for this parameter configuration.']
  cross_dock_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor-item can bypass DC storage and be cross-docked directly to stores. True enables flow-through distribution.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Type of destination location for the shipment. DC = Distribution Center, MFC = Micro-Fulfillment Center.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this lead time parameter becomes active. Start of the validity period for this lead time configuration.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this lead time parameter expires. End of the validity period. Null indicates no expiration.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter Incoterm code defining responsibility for shipping costs and risk transfer. Examples: FOB, CIF, DDP.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead time record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  lead_time_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this lead time parameter. Active = in use for replenishment calculations, Inactive = not currently used, Suspended = temporarily disabled, Under Review = being evaluated for changes.']
  lead_time_tier varchar [note: 'Service level tier for the lead time. Standard = normal replenishment, Expedited = faster service, Emergency = urgent fulfillment, DSD = Direct Store Delivery bypass DC.']
  lead_time_variability_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard deviation of lead time in days. Measures consistency of vendor delivery performance. Used to calculate safety stock.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units that must be ordered from the vendor for this item. Vendor-imposed constraint on order size.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or special instructions related to this vendor lead time configuration. Used for context that does not fit structured fields.']
  order_multiple varchar [note: 'Order quantity must be a multiple of this value. Ensures orders align with vendor case pack or pallet configurations.']
  order_to_ship_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of days from purchase order placement to vendor shipment departure. Vendor processing time component of total lead time.']
  reorder_point_units varchar [note: 'Inventory level in units that triggers automatic replenishment. Calculated from lead time, demand forecast, and safety stock.']
  rfid_tracking_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID tags are used to track shipments from this vendor for this item. Enables real-time supply chain visibility.']
  safety_stock_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of days of safety stock coverage calculated based on lead time and variability. Buffer inventory to protect against stockouts.']
  seasonal_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base lead time during seasonal periods. Values >1.0 extend lead time, <1.0 reduce it. Used for holiday or peak season adjustments.']
  seasonal_period_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the seasonal period for which the adjustment factor applies. Examples: HOLIDAY, SUMMER, BACKTOSCHOOL.']
  ship_to_receipt_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of days from vendor shipment departure to receipt at destination location. Transit time component of total lead time.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the item. The specific product code for which lead time is tracked.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that provided this lead time data. Examples: BYDP (Blue Yonder Demand Planning), ORMS (Oracle Retail Merchandising System), MANUAL.']
  total_lead_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total end-to-end lead time from PO placement to receipt at destination. Sum of order-to-ship and ship-to-receipt days. Used to calculate reorder points.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for shipments from this vendor to the destination. Impacts transit time and cost.']
  vendor_fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of orders fulfilled completely by the vendor for this item. Historical performance metric used to adjust lead time parameters.']

  Note: 'Operational lead time parameters by vendor-item-location combination used by Blue Yonder demand planning to calculate reorder points and safety stock. Captures vendor ID, item/SKU, destination DC or store, order-to-ship days, ship-to-receipt days, total lead time days, minimum order quantity, order multiple, case pack size, effective/expiration dates, lead time tier (standard, expedited, emergency), and seasonal adjustment factors. Drives automated replenishment calculations and PO timing. Distinct from vendor master (vendor domain) — this is supply-chain operational planning data.'
}

Table supply.demand_forecast {
  demand_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for demand_forecast']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand planner responsible for reviewing and approving this forecast. Links to the workforce or employee master data.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being forecasted. Links to the item master data.']
  promotional_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional event or campaign driving the promotional lift in this forecast period. Links to the promotions master data.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store or DC (Distribution Center) for which the forecast is generated. Supports both store-level and DC-level forecasting.']
  baseline_forecast_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The baseline demand forecast in units, excluding promotional lift. Represents normal demand patterns based on historical trends and seasonality.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'A score between 0 and 1 indicating the quality of input data used for forecast generation. Factors include completeness of historical sales, POS (Point of Sale) data accuracy, and inventory data reliability.']
  demand_variability_coefficient decimal(7,4) [note: 'The coefficient of variation for historical demand, measuring demand volatility. Higher values indicate more variable demand patterns requiring higher safety stock.']
  forecast_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the forecast was approved by the demand planner and released for use in replenishment planning.']
  forecast_bias_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The historical forecast bias as a percentage, indicating systematic over-forecasting (positive) or under-forecasting (negative). Used for forecast accuracy monitoring.']
  forecast_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'A confidence score between 0 and 1 indicating the reliability of the forecast. Higher scores indicate greater confidence based on historical accuracy and data quality.']
  forecast_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the forecast was initially generated by the Blue Yonder Demand Planning system.']
  forecast_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'The number of weeks into the future this forecast covers from the period start date. Typical horizons range from 4 to 52 weeks.']
  forecast_lower_bound_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The lower bound of the forecast confidence interval in units, representing the pessimistic scenario for demand.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'The method used to generate the forecast. Statistical methods include time series models, machine learning uses advanced algorithms, manual override indicates planner adjustments, hybrid combines multiple methods, and consensus represents collaborative planning.']
  forecast_model_name varchar [note: 'The specific forecasting model or algorithm used to generate the forecast (e.g., ARIMA, exponential smoothing, neural network, ensemble model).']
  forecast_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the forecast period. Typically represents the end of a week for weekly forecasts.']
  forecast_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the forecast period. Typically represents the beginning of a week for weekly forecasts.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the forecast. Draft forecasts are under review, approved forecasts are validated by planners, published forecasts are released to replenishment systems, superseded forecasts are replaced by newer versions, and rejected forecasts are not used.']
  forecast_upper_bound_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The upper bound of the forecast confidence interval in units, representing the optimistic scenario for demand.']
  forecast_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the forecast for the same item-location-period combination. Increments with each revision to support forecast history and audit trails.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the forecast was last modified, either by system recalculation or manual planner override.']
  mean_absolute_percentage_error decimal(7,4) [note: 'The Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) for this item-location combination, measuring historical forecast accuracy. Lower values indicate better accuracy.']
  new_product_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast is for a new product introduction with limited or no historical sales data. True indicates a new product.']
  override_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the planner explaining the rationale for manual forecast overrides or adjustments.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code when a planner manually overrides the system-generated forecast. Captures business context for forecast adjustments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotion|stockout|weather|seasonality|new_product|discontinuation|market_trend|competitive_action|supply_constraint|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promotional_lift_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The incremental demand units expected from promotional activities such as BOGO (Buy One Get One), TPR (Temporary Price Reduction), or ad circular features.']
  safety_stock_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The recommended safety stock level in units to buffer against forecast variability and supply chain uncertainty. Used in replenishment calculations.']
  seasonal_index decimal(7,4) [note: 'The seasonal index applied to the forecast for this period, representing the expected demand variation due to seasonality. A value of 1.0 represents average demand, above 1.0 indicates peak season, below 1.0 indicates off-season.']
  total_forecast_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total demand forecast in units, combining baseline forecast and promotional lift. This is the primary forecast value used for replenishment planning.']
  trend_factor decimal(7,4) [note: 'The trend factor applied to the forecast, representing the expected growth or decline in demand over time. Positive values indicate growth, negative values indicate decline.']

  Note: 'Demand and supply forecasts generated by Blue Yonder Demand Planning at the item-location-week level. Captures forecast period, item/SKU, location (store or DC), baseline forecast units, promotional lift units, total forecast units, forecast confidence score, forecast method (statistical, ML, manual override), last updated timestamp, and planner ID. Used to drive replenishment orders and DC allocation. Operational planning data — not an analytics aggregate.'
}

Table supply.allocation_plan {
  allocation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the allocation plan record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise buyer or planner responsible for this allocation plan.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise category or department for this allocated item.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center from which inventory will be allocated to stores.']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the visual merchandise display plan (planogram) that defines shelf placement and facing count for this allocated item.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) or product being allocated across stores.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional event driving this allocation, if applicable (e.g., BOGO, TPR, ad circular feature).']
  actual_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual percentage of store orders completely fulfilled after allocation execution, calculated post-completion for performance measurement.']
  ad_circular_week varchar [note: 'ISO week identifier for the ad circular promotional flyer period this allocation supports (format: YYYY-Www).']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or strategy used to distribute inventory: proportional to store size, store tier-based, historical sales-based, forecast-driven, manual override by category captain, or equal distribution.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the merchandise planner or buyer providing context, special instructions, or business rationale for this allocation plan.']
  allocation_strategy_code varchar [note: 'System code representing the specific allocation algorithm configuration used by Blue Yonder.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Category of allocation plan indicating the business driver: promotional (BOGO, TPR), constrained supply, seasonal launch, new item introduction, standard replenishment, or ad circular support.']
  allocation_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for allocated quantities: each (individual units), case (vendor case pack), pallet (full pallet), or inner pack (intermediate pack size).']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocated item requires temperature-controlled logistics (fresh produce, frozen, dairy, meat, pharmacy).']
  constrained_supply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation is for limited or constrained inventory requiring equitable distribution across stores.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation plan record was first created in the system.']
  cross_dock_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allocated inventory can be cross-docked (transferred without storage) at the DC for faster store delivery.']
  endcap_display_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation includes inventory for end-of-aisle promotional displays (endcaps) in addition to regular shelf stock.']
  fifo_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FIFO inventory rotation must be enforced during allocation fulfillment to manage perishable goods and expiration dates.']
  fill_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of store orders that should be completely fulfilled by this allocation plan, used to measure allocation effectiveness.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation plan record was last modified, tracking changes through the plan lifecycle.']
  max_store_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity each store can receive based on shelf capacity, backroom space, or planogram facing constraints.']
  min_store_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity each store should receive to maintain shelf presence and avoid out-of-stock (OOS) conditions.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any manual overrides applied to system-generated allocation recommendations, documenting planner judgment.']
  plan_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation plan was approved by the merchandise planner or category manager.']
  plan_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when all store allocations in the plan were fulfilled and the plan was marked complete.']
  plan_created_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation plan was initially created in the Blue Yonder system.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the allocation plan, typically generated by Blue Yonder Allocation Optimization system.']
  plan_released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation plan was released to the warehouse management system for fulfillment execution.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the allocation plan: draft (being created), approved (ready for execution), released (sent to WMS), in progress (actively fulfilling), completed (all stores fulfilled), or cancelled.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority for fulfilling this allocation plan, affecting warehouse picking and shipping sequence.']
  rfid_tracked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocated item is tracked using RFID technology for real-time inventory visibility and shrink reduction.']
  seasonal_event varchar [note: 'Seasonal or holiday event associated with this allocation (e.g., Thanksgiving, Back to School, Summer BBQ).']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Number of stores included in this allocation plan.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for allocated inventory: ambient (room temp), refrigerated (33-40°F), frozen (0°F or below), or controlled room temperature (pharmacy).']
  total_allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of units allocated across all stores in this plan.']
  total_available_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of the item available at the source DC at the time the allocation plan was created.']

  Note: 'DC-to-store allocation plans generated by Blue Yonder Allocation Optimization for constrained or promotional inventory, encompassing both plan-level details (item, source DC, allocation type, method, status) and store-level allocation detail (destination store, allocated quantity, min/max, store tier, override, fulfillment status). Enables equitable distribution of limited supply and store-level fill rate measurement.'
}

Table supply.allocation_detail {
  allocation_detail_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the allocation detail line item. Primary key for store-level allocation records.']
  allocation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent allocation plan that this detail line belongs to. Links to the header-level allocation strategy.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'User who approved this allocation detail for release. Captures accountability for allocation decisions.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Distribution center or warehouse from which this allocation will be fulfilled. Identifies the origin facility for the shipment.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment that fulfilled this allocation detail. Links allocation planning to physical shipment execution.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Product being allocated to the store. References the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) in the allocation.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Destination store receiving the allocated inventory. Identifies the specific retail location for this allocation line.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date the allocation was received at the destination store. Used to measure on-time delivery performance.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date the allocation was shipped from the distribution center. Used to measure on-time shipment performance.']
  allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'Calculated quantity allocated to this store based on allocation rules and store tier. Represents the system-recommended allocation amount.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate the allocated quantity for this store. Defines the business rule applied during allocation planning.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this allocation detail line. Tracks progression from planning through fulfillment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation detail was approved for fulfillment. Marks the transition from planning to execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation detail record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the final allocated quantity that was successfully received at the store. Calculated as (received_quantity / final_allocated_quantity) * 100. Key metric for allocation effectiveness.']
  final_allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'Final quantity to be shipped to the store. Equals override_quantity if present, otherwise equals allocated_quantity. Used for fulfillment execution.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Status of physical fulfillment for this allocation line. Indicates whether the allocated quantity has been picked, shipped, and received at the store.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation detail record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the allocation plan. Orders allocation detail records within the parent plan.']
  maximum_allocation_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be allocated to this store based on shelf capacity, backroom space, or inventory policy. Ceiling constraint for allocation.']
  minimum_allocation_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be allocated to this store to meet presentation standards or planogram requirements. Floor constraint for allocation.']
  override_quantity varchar [note: 'Manual override quantity entered by planner or merchandiser. Replaces the system-calculated allocated quantity when business judgment requires adjustment.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the allocation was manually overridden. Captures business justification for deviating from system recommendation.']
  override_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for the allocation override. Provides additional context beyond the reason code.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Number of units per case or pack. Used to convert case quantities to eaches for inventory tracking.']
  planned_arrival_date date [note: 'Expected date for the allocation to arrive at the destination store. Used for store labor planning and receiving dock scheduling.']
  planned_ship_date date [note: 'Target date for shipping this allocation from the distribution center. Used for transportation planning and store receiving scheduling.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Fulfillment priority for this allocation detail. CRITICAL=immediate need (OOS recovery), HIGH=promotional support, NORMAL=standard replenishment, LOW=backfill.']
  received_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual quantity received and confirmed at the destination store. May differ from shipped quantity due to damage or discrepancies.']
  shipped_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual quantity shipped from the distribution center to the store. May differ from final allocated quantity due to inventory availability.']
  store_tier varchar [note: 'Performance tier classification of the destination store. Used to prioritize allocation quantities based on store sales volume and strategic importance (A=highest, F=lowest).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for allocated quantities. EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, LB=Pound, KG=Kilogram.']
  variance_quantity varchar [note: 'Difference between final allocated quantity and received quantity. Positive values indicate over-shipment, negative values indicate under-shipment.']

  Note: 'Store-level line items within an allocation plan specifying the exact quantity allocated to each store. Captures allocation plan reference, destination store, allocated quantity, minimum allocation, maximum allocation, store tier, override quantity, override reason, and fulfillment status. Enables store-level allocation tracking and fill rate measurement.'
}

Table supply.dc_facility {
  dc_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution center or micro-fulfillment center facility.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  ambient_capacity_cubic_ft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cubic feet of ambient (room temperature) storage capacity for non-perishable inventory.']
  average_daily_outbound_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average number of cases or pallets shipped outbound per day for store replenishment and customer fulfillment.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the distribution center is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center was permanently closed or decommissioned. Null for active facilities.']
  cold_chain_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified for cold chain logistics and temperature-controlled supply chain operations for perishable goods.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the distribution center location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution center facility record was first created in the system.']
  cross_dock_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility supports cross-dock operations (transfer of goods from inbound to outbound without storage).']
  dc_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the distribution center used across operational systems and reporting. Typically a short alphanumeric code.']
  dc_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the distribution center or micro-fulfillment center facility.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Total number of dock doors available for inbound receiving and outbound shipping operations.']
  dsd_receiving_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution center receives DSD shipments directly from vendors for consolidation and redistribution to stores.']
  erp_location_code varchar [note: 'Location identifier in the enterprise ERP system (SAP, Oracle) used for financial posting and inventory accounting.']
  facility_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the distribution center facility in its lifecycle.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution center by operational model: full-service DC (receiving, storage, picking, shipping), cross-dock (transfer without storage), MFC (micro-fulfillment center for last-mile delivery), cold storage (refrigerated/frozen only), ambient-only, or perishable-focused.']
  frozen_capacity_cubic_ft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cubic feet of frozen storage capacity for frozen goods inventory (typically 0°F or below).']
  haccp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility maintains HACCP certification for food safety and hazard prevention in handling perishable and food products.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution center facility record was most recently modified.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution center for routing and logistics optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center for routing and logistics optimization.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center facility first became operational and began receiving and shipping inventory.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays (Monday-Friday) in format HH:MM-HH:MM (e.g., 06:00-22:00). Used for scheduling inbound shipments and outbound loads.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends (Saturday-Sunday) in format HH:MM-HH:MM. May differ from weekday hours or be closed.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified for handling and storing USDA organic products with required segregation and handling protocols.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the distribution center address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  primary_service_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market area primarily served by this distribution center (e.g., Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West Coast).']
  refrigerated_capacity_cubic_ft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cubic feet of refrigerated storage capacity for temperature-controlled perishable inventory (typically 32-40°F).']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility is equipped with RFID infrastructure for automated inventory tracking and shipment verification.']
  sap_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP ERP plant code assigned to this distribution center for materials management and logistics execution in SAP MM/WM modules.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the distribution center location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  store_count_served varchar [note: 'Number of retail store locations actively served by this distribution center for replenishment.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the distribution center location used for scheduling and operational hours (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago).']
  total_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total building square footage of the distribution center facility including all storage, operational, and administrative areas.']
  twenty_four_hour_operation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week for continuous receiving and shipping.']
  wms_system_instance varchar [note: 'Identifier for the WMS system instance deployed at this distribution center. References Manhattan Associates WMS or other warehouse management platform.']

  Note: 'Master record for Distribution Centers and Micro-Fulfillment Centers (MFCs) in the Grocery supply network. Captures DC code, DC name, address, facility type (full-service DC, cross-dock, MFC, cold storage), total square footage, dock door count, refrigerated capacity (cubic feet), frozen capacity, ambient capacity, WMS system instance, SAP plant code, operating hours, and facility status. Distinct from store master (owned by store domain).'
}

Table supply.supply_supplier_contract {
  supply_supplier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier contract. Primary key.']
  primary_supply_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the procurement buyer or category manager responsible for negotiating and managing this contract.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: REQUIRED: Store‑level supplier contract compliance reports require linking each contract to the specific store it governs; this FK enables per‑store contract performance tracking.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor master record. Links to the vendor who is party to this supply agreement.']
  allowance_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Negotiated allowance or discount percentage applied to invoice amounts. May represent volume rebates, promotional allowances, or early payment discounts.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews for another term if neither party provides termination notice by the renewal deadline.']
  category_captain_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor serves as the category captain, providing category management insights, planogram recommendations, and assortment optimization guidance.']
  chargeback_policy varchar [note: 'Detailed policy governing vendor chargebacks for non-compliance events including late shipments, short fills, mislabeling, EDI errors, and HACCP violations. Defines chargeback amounts, dispute process, and resolution timelines.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature-controlled logistics (cold chain) are required for all shipments under this contract. Enforced for perishable goods, frozen foods, and pharmacy products.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the signed contract document stored in the document management system. Provides audit trail and legal reference.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the supplier contract for easy identification and reference.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the supplier contract. Used in procurement documents, EDI transactions, and vendor communications.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supplier contract. Active contracts govern current procurement operations. Suspended contracts are temporarily inactive. Expired contracts have passed their end date. Terminated contracts were ended early.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract governing the procurement relationship. Blanket PO for recurring orders, spot buy for one-time purchases, DSD for Direct Store Delivery agreements, private label for store-owned brand sourcing, consignment for vendor-owned inventory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system. Audit trail for contract lifecycle tracking.']
  cross_dock_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shipments under this contract are eligible for cross-dock operations (transfer without DC storage). Requires vendor to meet strict ASN accuracy and delivery window compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this contract (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  dispute_resolution_process varchar [note: 'Agreed process for resolving contract disputes including escalation paths, mediation requirements, and arbitration clauses. May reference specific legal jurisdictions.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract governs Direct Store Delivery operations, where vendor delivers directly to stores bypassing the DC. Common for bread, beverages, snacks, and magazines.']
  edi_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EDI transaction compliance is mandatory under this contract. True means vendor must submit ASN (856), invoices (810), and other documents via EDI. False allows manual or alternative submission methods.']
  edi_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required EDI transaction set codes (e.g., 850 for PO, 856 for ASN, 810 for Invoice, 997 for Functional Acknowledgment). Defines the EDI compliance scope.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms expire. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal or termination workflows as the date approaches.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms become binding and procurement operations under this agreement may begin.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Freight payment and liability terms. FOB Origin means buyer pays freight and assumes risk at vendor location. FOB Destination means vendor pays freight and assumes risk until delivery. Prepaid means vendor pays and may bill back. Collect means buyer pays carrier directly.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern contract interpretation and enforcement. Typically the state or country where the retailer is headquartered.']
  haccp_compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vendor must maintain HACCP certification and provide HACCP documentation for shipments. Critical for fresh produce, meat, dairy, and perishable goods suppliers.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was last updated. Tracks amendments, status changes, and data corrections.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from purchase order submission to expected delivery at DC or store. Used in demand planning and replenishment forecasting.']
  minimum_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually required fill rate percentage. Vendor must fulfill at least this percentage of ordered quantity to avoid chargeback penalties. Monitored against actual shipment performance.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required per purchase order under this contract. Used in CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) and replenishment planning systems.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary value required per purchase order to meet contract terms. Enforced in procurement systems to avoid order rejection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contract terms, special conditions, or operational notes not captured in structured fields.']
  on_time_delivery_sla_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually required on-time delivery percentage. Vendor must meet scheduled delivery windows at least this percentage of the time to avoid penalties. Tracked via TMS and WMS receiving timestamps.']
  organic_certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vendor must maintain USDA Organic certification for products supplied under this contract. Enforced for organic private-label and branded organic SKUs.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms governing invoice settlement. Examples: Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30 (2% discount if paid within 10 days, otherwise net 30).']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract governs sourcing of private-label (store-owned brand) products. Private-label contracts often include exclusivity clauses and quality control requirements.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming shipments under this contract require mandatory quality inspection at receiving before putaway. Common for fresh produce, meat, and private-label goods.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before contract expiration that renewal or termination notice must be provided. Triggers workflow alerts in procurement systems.']
  return_policy varchar [note: 'Terms governing product returns, including acceptable reasons (damage, expiration, overstock), timeframes, and restocking fees. Critical for managing shrink and vendor chargebacks.']
  rfid_tracking_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vendor must apply RFID tags to pallets or cases for automated tracking through the supply chain. Supports inventory visibility and shrink reduction initiatives.']
  slotting_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time or recurring fee paid by vendor for shelf space allocation and new item introduction. Common in category captain and promotional agreements.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to terminate the contract before its natural expiration. Protects both parties from abrupt supply disruption.']
