  prescriber_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the prescriber in the pharmacy system.']
  relationship_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the prescriber\'s relationship strength with Grocery pharmacies based on prescription volume and engagement (tier_1 = high volume, tier_2 = medium volume, tier_3 = low volume).']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Primary medical specialty or practice area of the prescriber (e.g., Family Medicine, Internal Medicine, Cardiology, Pediatrics, Psychiatry, Dentistry).']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued medical license number authorizing the prescriber to practice medicine in the jurisdiction.']
  state_license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code indicating the jurisdiction that issued the medical license.']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Name suffix or professional designation (e.g., Jr, Sr, II, III, MD, DO, NP, PA, DDS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Jr|Sr|II|III|IV|MD|DO|PharmD|NP|PA|DDS|DMD — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  taxonomy_code varchar [note: 'Ten-digit alphanumeric code that classifies the prescriber\'s type, classification, and specialization. Used in HIPAA transactions.']

  Note: 'Master record for licensed prescribers (physicians, NPs, PAs, dentists) who write prescriptions filled at Grocery pharmacies. Captures NPI (National Provider Identifier), DEA registration number, DEA schedules authorized, prescriber name, specialty, practice address, state license number, license expiration date, e-prescribing (eRx) capability flag, and preferred contact method. Used for Rx validation, DEA compliance, and prescriber relationship management.'
}

Table pharmacy.controlled_substance_log {
  controlled_substance_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each controlled substance transaction log entry. Primary key for the controlled substance log.']
  drug_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug. Business justification: Controlled substance log should reference drug master for consistency.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pharmacy store location where the controlled substance transaction occurred.']
  prescription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prescription record for dispense transactions. Null for non-dispense transactions (receive, destroy, theft/loss).']
  rx_patient_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the patient receiving the controlled substance for dispense transactions. Null for non-dispense transactions. Protected under HIPAA.']
  biennial_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is part of the DEA-mandated biennial (every two years) physical inventory count of all controlled substances.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this controlled substance log record was first created in the data warehouse. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  dea_form_number varchar [note: 'Unique DEA form number or serial number for Form 222, 41, or 106 submissions. Null for routine dispense transactions.']
  dea_form_type varchar [note: 'DEA form reference for controlled substance transactions: Form 222 (Schedule II order), Form 41 (destruction), Form 106 (theft or loss report). Null for routine dispense transactions.']
  dea_schedule varchar [note: 'DEA controlled substance schedule classification: Schedule II (high abuse potential, accepted medical use), III (moderate abuse potential), IV (low abuse potential), or V (lowest abuse potential).']
  destruction_method varchar [note: 'Method used to destroy the controlled substance (e.g., reverse distributor, on-site incineration, authorized collector). Required for destroy transactions and DEA Form 41 reporting.']
  destruction_witness_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized witness present during controlled substance destruction. Required for DEA Form 41 compliance.']
  distributor_dea_number varchar [note: 'DEA registration number of the distributor or manufacturer for receive transactions.']
  distributor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the wholesale distributor or manufacturer for receive transactions. Null for dispense, destroy, theft/loss transactions.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned expiration date of the controlled substance lot. Critical for inventory management and patient safety.']
  inventory_reconciliation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is an inventory adjustment or reconciliation entry to correct discrepancies identified during physical counts or audits.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Distributor invoice or packing slip number for receive transactions. Null for dispense, destroy, theft/loss transactions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this controlled substance log record was last updated in the data warehouse. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency notified of the theft or loss incident.']
  law_enforcement_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether local law enforcement was notified of the theft or loss incident. Required for DEA Form 106 compliance.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer lot or batch number of the controlled substance for traceability and recall purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the controlled substance transaction for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  pharmacist_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued pharmacist license number of the pharmacist who processed the transaction.']
  pharmacist_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the pharmacist who processed and verified the controlled substance transaction.']
  pharmacist_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier of the pharmacist who processed the controlled substance transaction.']
  prescriber_dea_number varchar [note: 'DEA registration number of the prescriber authorized to prescribe controlled substances. Required for Schedule II-V dispense transactions.']
  prescriber_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier of the prescriber for dispense transactions. Null for non-dispense transactions.']
  quantity_in decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of controlled substance units added to inventory (for receive, return-to-stock transactions). Null for dispense, destroy, theft/loss transactions.']
  quantity_out decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of controlled substance units removed from inventory (for dispense, destroy, theft/loss transactions). Null for receive transactions.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the controlled substance quantity (e.g., tablet, capsule, mL, gram, patch).']
  running_balance decimal(12,3) [note: 'Cumulative on-hand inventory balance of the controlled substance after this transaction. Critical for perpetual inventory tracking and DEA audit reconciliation.']
  rx_number varchar [note: 'Pharmacy-assigned prescription number for dispense transactions. Null for non-dispense transactions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the pharmacy management system that originated this controlled substance log entry (e.g., McKesson Pharmacy Systems).']
  theft_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the circumstances surrounding theft or loss of controlled substances. Required for DEA Form 106 reporting.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Date when the controlled substance transaction occurred. Critical for DEA compliance reporting and biennial inventory reconciliation.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the controlled substance transaction was recorded in the pharmacy system.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of controlled substance transaction: dispense (prescription fill), receive (inventory receipt from distributor), return (patient or distributor return), destroy (disposal), theft_loss (inventory loss event), or adjustment (inventory correction).']

  Note: 'DEA-mandated transactional log tracking all controlled substance (Schedule II–V) dispensing, receiving, returns, and destruction events at the pharmacy. Captures transaction type (dispense, receive, return, destroy, theft/loss), DEA schedule, drug NDC, quantity in/out, running balance, transaction date, pharmacist NPI, DEA Form reference (222, 41, 106), and biennial inventory reconciliation flag. Critical for DEA compliance reporting and State Board of Pharmacy audits.'
}

Table pharmacy.patient_medication_profile {
  patient_medication_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the patient medication profile record.']
  primary_patient_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmacist assigned as the primary care provider for this patient for MTM (Medication Therapy Management) services.']
  rx_patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmacy patient for whom this medication profile is maintained.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary pharmacy store location where the patient typically fills prescriptions.']
  active_medication_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active medications currently prescribed to the patient.']
  adherence_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for medication non-adherence based on PDC scores and fill patterns.']
  allergy_alerts_count varchar [note: 'Number of documented drug allergies or adverse reactions on file for this patient.']
  auto_refill_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has opted into automatic prescription refill services.']
  chronic_medication_count varchar [note: 'Number of chronic maintenance medications the patient is taking (e.g., for diabetes, hypertension, cholesterol).']
  controlled_substance_count varchar [note: 'Number of active controlled substance prescriptions (DEA Schedule II-V) in the patient\'s profile.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the patient medication profile record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_preference varchar [note: 'Patient\'s preferred method for receiving filled prescriptions.']
  drug_interaction_alerts_count varchar [note: 'Number of active drug-drug interaction alerts flagged in the patient\'s current medication profile.']
  estimated_annual_fill_count varchar [note: 'Projected number of prescription fills for this patient over a 12-month period, used for pharmacy workflow planning and inventory management.']
  hipaa_consent_date date [note: 'Date when the patient signed the HIPAA authorization form. Null if no consent on file.']
  hipaa_consent_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed HIPAA authorization form is on file allowing disclosure of protected health information.']
  last_adherence_calculation_date date [note: 'Date when adherence metrics (PDC, MPR) were last calculated for this patient.']
  last_fill_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent prescription fill for this patient.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the profile record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the patient medication profile record was last updated.']
  last_mtm_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent comprehensive medication review conducted as part of MTM services.']
  medsync_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient is enrolled in the Medication Synchronization program to align all prescription refills to a single monthly pickup date.']
  medsync_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient enrolled in the MedSync program. Null if never enrolled.']
  medsync_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the patient\'s MedSync program enrollment.']
  medsync_target_day varchar [note: 'Target day of the month (1-31) when all synchronized medications are scheduled for pickup. Null if not enrolled in MedSync.']
  mtm_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient meets eligibility criteria for MTM services based on medication count, chronic conditions, and drug spend.']
  mtm_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient is actively enrolled in MTM services.']
  mtm_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient enrolled in MTM services. Null if never enrolled.']
  next_mtm_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next comprehensive medication review. Null if not enrolled in MTM.']
  next_scheduled_fill_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled prescription refill based on current medication regimen and days supply.']
  overall_adherence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite medication adherence score across all chronic medications, calculated as percentage (0.00-100.00).']
  pdc_cholesterol decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of Days Covered adherence metric for cholesterol medications (statins), calculated as percentage (0.00-100.00). Used for Medicare Part D Star Ratings.']
  pdc_diabetes decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of Days Covered adherence metric for diabetes medications, calculated as percentage (0.00-100.00). Used for Medicare Part D Star Ratings.']
  pdc_hypertension decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of Days Covered adherence metric for hypertension medications, calculated as percentage (0.00-100.00). Used for Medicare Part D Star Ratings.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Patient\'s preferred method for pharmacy communications including refill reminders and adherence outreach.']
  profile_end_date date [note: 'Date when the patient medication profile was closed or transferred. Null for active profiles.']
  profile_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical notes and observations about the patient\'s medication history, adherence patterns, or special handling requirements.']
  profile_start_date date [note: 'Date when the patient medication profile was first established at this pharmacy.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the patient medication profile.']

  Note: 'Consolidated medication history and adherence management record for a pharmacy patient, capturing all active and historical medications including Rx fills, OTC medications self-reported, and medication adherence metrics (PDC — Proportion of Days Covered, MPR — Medication Possession Ratio). Tracks therapy start/stop dates, prescriber, indication, and Medication Synchronization (MedSync) program enrollment including sync date (target monthly pickup day), enrolled medications, program status (active, paused, cancelled), assigned pharmacy technician, and estimated annual fill count. Supports MTM (Medication Therapy Management) services, Star Ratings compliance for Medicare Part D, patient retention through adherence programs, and pharmacy workflow planning.'
}

Table pharmacy.clinical_service {
  clinical_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pharmacy clinical service record. Primary key.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pharmacy store location where this service is offered. Null if service is available at all pharmacy locations. Used for store-specific service availability.']
  adverse_event_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adverse events from this service must be reported to FDA VAERS or other regulatory bodies. True for immunizations and high-risk procedures.']
  age_restriction_maximum varchar [note: 'Maximum patient age in years eligible to receive this service. Null if no maximum age restriction. Used for patient eligibility screening.']
  age_restriction_minimum varchar [note: 'Minimum patient age in years eligible to receive this service. Null if no minimum age restriction. Used for patient eligibility screening.']
  appointment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patients must schedule an appointment for this service. False for walk-in services. True for services requiring preparation or extended time.']
  clinical_protocol_document_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the clinical protocol or standard operating procedure document governing administration of this service. Used for pharmacist training and compliance.']
  commercial_insurance_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is typically covered by commercial insurance plans. Actual coverage varies by payer and plan.']
  consent_form_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed patient consent form is required before administering this service. True for services with clinical risk or HIPAA implications.']
  consent_form_template_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the consent form template document used for this service. Null if no consent required.']
  cpt_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit CPT billing code used for insurance reimbursement and revenue tracking. Assigned by the American Medical Association for standardized medical procedure coding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this clinical service record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  hcpcs_code varchar [note: 'HCPCS Level II code for services and supplies not covered by CPT codes. Used for Medicare and Medicaid billing.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this clinical service record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this clinical service record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  liability_insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional professional liability insurance coverage is required for pharmacists administering this service. True for high-risk clinical services.']
  medicaid_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is covered under state Medicaid programs. Coverage may vary by state.']
  medicare_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is covered under Medicare Part B or Part D. Used for patient eligibility determination and billing.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'NDC for vaccine or medication administered as part of the clinical service. Applicable for immunizations and injectable medications. Format: 5-4-2 segments.']
  online_scheduling_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patients can schedule this service through the online portal or mobile app. Used for digital commerce integration.']
  patient_out_of_pocket_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical patient copay or self-pay amount in USD for this service. Used for patient communication and point-of-sale transactions.']
  pharmacist_certification_required varchar [note: 'Specific certification or credential required for the pharmacist to administer this service (e.g., Immunization Certification, Diabetes Care Specialist, MTM Certificate, Tobacco Cessation Specialist). Null if no special certification required beyond RPh license.']
  pharmacist_training_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of continuing education hours required for pharmacist to be qualified to provide this service. Used for workforce training planning.']
  quality_measure_tracked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is tracked for quality measures and clinical outcomes reporting. True for services with CMS quality reporting requirements.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'State Board of Pharmacy or DEA approval number authorizing the pharmacy to provide this clinical service. Required for controlled services. Null if no special approval required.']
  reimbursable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is eligible for insurance reimbursement. True if billable to insurance payers, False if patient self-pay only.']
  seasonal_end_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal service ends. Null if not a seasonal service. Used for inventory and staffing planning.']
  seasonal_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is offered seasonally (e.g., flu shots in fall/winter). True for seasonal services, False for year-round services.']
  seasonal_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal service becomes available. Null if not a seasonal service. Used for inventory and staffing planning.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Internal unique code identifying the clinical service offering. Used for operational tracking and service catalog management.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the clinical service, including what is provided, patient benefits, and clinical outcomes. Used for patient communication and marketing materials.']
  service_discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when this clinical service was discontinued or retired. Null if service is still active. Used for historical reporting.']
  service_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected duration of the clinical service appointment in minutes. Used for scheduling and resource planning.']
  service_launch_date date [note: 'Date when this clinical service was first introduced and made available to patients. Used for service lifecycle tracking and performance analysis.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the clinical service (e.g., Influenza Vaccination, Medication Therapy Management Consultation, Blood Pressure Screening).']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clinical service offering. Active services are available for scheduling. Inactive services are not offered. Suspended services are temporarily unavailable. Pending approval services are awaiting clinical or regulatory approval. Discontinued services are permanently retired.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of clinical service offered. Immunization includes vaccines and injections. MTM includes comprehensive medication reviews. Health screening includes blood pressure, cholesterol, glucose testing. Point-of-care testing includes rapid diagnostic tests. Smoking cessation includes counseling programs. Chronic disease management includes diabetes, hypertension, asthma programs.']
  standard_reimbursement_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average reimbursement amount in USD for this service across all payers. Used for revenue forecasting and financial planning. Null if not reimbursable.']

  Note: 'Master record for pharmacy-administered clinical services offered at Grocery pharmacy locations. Captures service type (immunization, MTM consultation, health screening, point-of-care testing, smoking cessation, diabetes management), service name, CPT billing code, duration, required pharmacist certification, reimbursable flag, payer coverage, and service availability by store. Supports clinical services program management and revenue tracking.'
}

Table pharmacy.clinical_service_encounter {
  clinical_service_encounter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical service encounter record. Primary key.']
  clinical_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.clinical_service. Business justification: An encounter records the delivery of a specific clinical service; linking to clinical_service provides service definition and pricing.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmacy store location where the clinical service was delivered.']
  prescription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated prescription record if the clinical service was related to a prescription (e.g., medication therapy management). Nullable for services not tied to a specific prescription.']
  rx_patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient who received the clinical service. Links to pharmacy patient master data.']
  administering_pharmacist_license_number varchar [note: 'State pharmacy license number of the administering pharmacist. Required for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  administering_pharmacist_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the pharmacist who delivered the clinical service. Used for documentation and patient communication.']
  administering_pharmacist_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the pharmacist who administered or delivered the clinical service. Validated against workforce domain pharmacist credential records for license and certification compliance.']
  adverse_event_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the adverse event or reaction experienced by the patient. Used for clinical review and safety reporting.']
  adverse_event_documented_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an adverse event or reaction was documented following the clinical service. Triggers quality review and potential VAERS reporting.']
  adverse_event_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the documented adverse event. Used for risk assessment and regulatory reporting.']
  billing_cpt_code varchar [note: 'CPT code used for billing and reimbursement of the clinical service. Maps the service to standardized healthcare procedure codes for insurance claims.']
  billing_cpt_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the CPT code used for billing. Provides clarity for billing and audit purposes.']
  clinical_outcome_documented_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinical outcome of the service was documented in the patient record. Used for quality assurance and clinical governance.']
  clinical_outcome_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the clinical outcome, patient response, and any follow-up recommendations. Supports continuity of care and quality improvement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the clinical service encounter record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']
  encounter_date date [note: 'The date on which the clinical service encounter occurred. Primary business event date for reporting and compliance.']
  encounter_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the clinical service encounter, externally visible and used for tracking and reference.']
  encounter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clinical service encounter. Tracks the encounter from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  encounter_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the clinical service encounter was initiated. Used for detailed scheduling and workflow analysis.']
  iis_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the immunization encounter was reported to the state Immunization Information System registry. Required for public health surveillance.']
  iis_report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the immunization data was transmitted to the state IIS registry. Used for compliance tracking.']
  insurance_claim_number varchar [note: 'Claim number submitted to the insurance payer for reimbursement of the clinical service. Used for claim tracking and reconciliation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the clinical service encounter record was last updated. Used for audit trails and change tracking.']
  patient_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which patient consent was obtained for the clinical service. Nullable if consent was not required or not obtained.']
  patient_consent_obtained_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether informed patient consent was obtained prior to delivering the clinical service. Required for HIPAA compliance and clinical documentation.']
  payer_type varchar [note: 'Type of payer responsible for reimbursement of the clinical service. Used for revenue analysis and payer mix reporting.']
  reimbursement_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount reimbursed or expected to be reimbursed for the clinical service by the payer. Used for revenue recognition and financial reporting.']
  reimbursement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reimbursement amount. Typically USD for US-based operations.']
  service_subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed subtype or specific variant of the clinical service (e.g., flu vaccine, COVID-19 vaccine, comprehensive medication review). Provides granular classification.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of clinical pharmacy service delivered during the encounter. Categorizes the service for billing, quality metrics, and operational reporting.']
  vaccine_administered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinical service involved vaccine administration (immunization). Used to filter immunization encounters for registry reporting.']
  vaccine_dose_number varchar [note: 'Sequential dose number in a multi-dose vaccine series (e.g., 1 for first dose, 2 for second dose). Used for immunization schedule tracking.']
  vaccine_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vaccine lot administered. Ensures compliance with product safety and efficacy standards.']
  vaccine_lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer lot number of the vaccine administered. Required for traceability, adverse event reporting, and recall management.']
  vaccine_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the pharmaceutical manufacturer of the vaccine administered. Required for adverse event reporting and supply chain tracking.']
  vaccine_name varchar [note: 'Brand or generic name of the vaccine administered. Used for patient communication and clinical documentation.']
  vaccine_ndc varchar [note: 'National Drug Code of the vaccine product administered. Required for immunization registry reporting and inventory tracking. Nullable for non-immunization services.']
  vaccine_route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Anatomical route by which the vaccine was administered. Required for clinical documentation and immunization registry reporting.']
  vaccine_site_of_administration varchar [note: 'Specific anatomical site where the vaccine was administered (e.g., left deltoid, right thigh). Used for clinical documentation.']
  vaers_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an adverse event report was submitted to the FDA VAERS system. Required for serious adverse events following immunization.']
  vaers_report_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by VAERS for the submitted adverse event report. Used for tracking and follow-up.']
  vis_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CDC Vaccine Information Statement was provided to the patient or guardian prior to vaccine administration. Federally mandated for all vaccines covered by the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program.']
  vis_publication_date date [note: 'Publication date of the VIS document provided to the patient. Required for federal compliance and audit trails.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each patient encounter where a clinical pharmacy service was delivered. Captures encounter date, service type, administering pharmacist NPI (validated against workforce domain pharmacist credential records), patient consent obtained flag, clinical outcome documented, vaccine lot number and expiration (for immunizations), VIS (Vaccine Information Statement) provided flag, billing CPT code, reimbursement amount, adverse event documentation, and VAERS (Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System) report flag. Supports immunization registry reporting (state IIS integration), clinical billing, and pharmacy quality metrics. Pharmacist credential and license validation is a cross-domain dependency on the workforce domain.'
}

Table pharmacy.pharmacy_location {
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pharmacy location. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for financial reporting of pharmacy operating expenses per cost center; profit & loss statements allocate pharmacy costs to the appropriate cost center.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Needed for consolidating pharmacy financial results under the correct legal entity for statutory reporting and tax compliance.']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.planogram. Business justification: Pharmacy aisles use planograms; this FK enables compliance tracking, planogram version control, and automated replenishment for pharmacy shelves.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Required for Pharmacy Pricing Zone assignment used in daily price calculation reports and regulatory price compliance per location.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Store Promotion Planning assigns campaigns to specific pharmacy locations, supporting localized marketing calendars and performance reporting.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: Cluster‑wide assortment planning includes pharmacy sites; linking location to its store cluster supports cluster inventory allocation and space planning reports.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent retail store location where this pharmacy is housed. Links pharmacy to the broader store operations.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier_site. Business justification: LOGISTICS: Map each store to its primary supplier site to optimize delivery routing, lead‑time monitoring, and performance scorecards.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the pharmacy location. Required for regulatory filings, DEA registration, and state board documentation.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite number, building identifier, or floor. Optional field for additional location detail.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the pharmacy is located. Required for regulatory filings and geographic reporting.']
  clia_waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the CLIA waiver certificate. CLIA certificates must be renewed every two years. Null if no waiver held.']
  clia_waiver_number varchar [note: 'CLIA waiver certificate number authorizing the pharmacy to perform point-of-care testing such as flu tests, strep tests, and blood glucose monitoring. Null if no waiver held.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the pharmacy location permanently ceased operations. Null for active pharmacies. Used for historical analysis and patient transfer tracking.']
  controlled_substance_vault_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy has a DEA-compliant controlled substance storage vault that meets security and access control requirements. True if certified, False otherwise.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the pharmacy location. Determines applicable national regulatory framework.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacy location record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  dea_registration_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the DEA registration. DEA registrations must be renewed every three years. Critical for compliance tracking and renewal planning.']
  dea_registration_number varchar [note: 'DEA registration number authorizing the pharmacy to dispense controlled substances. Format: two letters followed by seven digits.']
  dispensing_system_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the pharmacy dispensing system endpoint. Used for McKesson system integration and prescription routing.']
  drive_through_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacy location offers drive-through prescription pickup service. True if available, False otherwise.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the pharmacy location. Used for business communication, regulatory correspondence, and vendor coordination.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for receiving prescriptions from prescribers and processing prior authorizations. Critical for HIPAA-compliant prescription transmission.']
  immunization_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacy location is certified to administer immunizations and vaccinations. True if certified, False otherwise.']
  last_state_board_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent state board of pharmacy inspection. Used for compliance tracking and inspection readiness planning.']
  max_prescription_volume_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of prescriptions this pharmacy location can safely process per day based on staffing, equipment, and physical capacity. Used for workload balancing and capacity planning.']
  mckesson_site_code varchar [note: 'McKesson system site identifier for this pharmacy location. Used for inventory management, ordering, and claims processing integration.']
  next_state_board_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Expected or scheduled date for the next state board of pharmacy inspection. Used for proactive compliance preparation.']
  npi varchar [note: 'Ten-digit National Provider Identifier assigned by CMS for healthcare billing and identification. Required for pharmacy claims processing.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the pharmacy location first opened for business and began dispensing prescriptions. Used for tenure analysis and historical reporting.']
  operating_hours_saturday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for Saturday. Format: HH:MM-HH:MM. May differ from weekday hours.']
  operating_hours_sunday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for Sunday. Format: HH:MM-HH:MM. May differ from weekday and Saturday hours.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for Monday through Friday. Format: HH:MM-HH:MM (e.g., 09:00-21:00). Used for customer communication and staffing planning.']
  pharmacist_in_charge_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued pharmacist license number for the pharmacist-in-charge. Required for state board reporting and inspection documentation.']
  pharmacist_in_charge_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the licensed pharmacist designated as pharmacist-in-charge responsible for day-to-day operations and regulatory compliance at this location.']
  pharmacy_director_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the licensed pharmacist designated as the pharmacy director responsible for overall pharmacy operations and regulatory compliance.']
  pharmacy_director_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the pharmacy director. Required for regulatory filings and compliance documentation.']
  pharmacy_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the pharmacy location as registered with state authorities and displayed to customers.']
  pharmacy_number varchar [note: 'Internal business identifier for the pharmacy location used in operational systems and reporting.']
  pharmacy_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the pharmacy location in its lifecycle.']
  pharmacy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of pharmacy service model and capabilities offered at this location.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the pharmacy location. Used for patient inquiries, prescription transfers, and prescriber communication.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or postal code for the pharmacy location. Used for geographic analysis, network adequacy reporting, and regulatory filings.']
  state_board_license_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the state pharmacy license. Renewal frequency varies by state. Critical for operational compliance and inspection readiness.']
  state_board_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued pharmacy license number required to operate and dispense prescriptions. Format varies by state jurisdiction.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code where the pharmacy is located. Determines applicable state board jurisdiction and regulatory requirements.']
  twenty_four_hour_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacy operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. True if 24-hour operation, False otherwise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacy location record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Grocery pharmacy location capturing pharmacy-specific regulatory credentials and operational configuration distinct from the store domain\'s general location profile. Includes pharmacy NPI, DEA registration number and expiration, State Board of Pharmacy license number and expiration, pharmacy director name and NPI, pharmacist-in-charge designation, operating hours, pharmacy type (full-service, drive-through, 24-hour, specialty), dispensing system identifier (McKesson integration endpoint), CLIA waiver number (for point-of-care testing), controlled substance vault certification status, and maximum prescription volume capacity. Supports DEA registration renewal tracking, State Board inspection readiness, and pharmacy network adequacy reporting.'
}

Table pharmacy.rems_enrollment {
  rems_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the REMS enrollment record. Primary key.']
  drug_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug. Business justification: REMS enrollment tracks specific drug; link to drug master and remove duplicated fields.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pharmacy store location where the REMS enrollment is managed and dispensing occurs.']
  rx_patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient enrolled in the REMS program. Links to rx_patient master record.']
  authorization_code_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current dispensing authorization code expires and a new code must be obtained.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the patient with REMS monitoring and documentation requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the REMS enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  dea_schedule varchar [note: 'DEA controlled substance schedule classification of the REMS medication, if applicable.']
  dispensing_authorization_code varchar [note: 'Unique authorization code or token issued by the REMS program that must be verified before each dispensing event.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient or prescriber was enrolled in the REMS program.']
  enrollment_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the REMS enrollment expires and must be renewed to continue dispensing authorization.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Indicates how the REMS enrollment was initiated (pharmacy, prescriber, patient, or transfer from another pharmacy).']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the REMS enrollment. Active indicates compliant and authorized to dispense; inactive, suspended, or expired indicates non-compliance or lapsed enrollment.']
  last_compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance verification or monitoring activity (e.g., pregnancy test, lab result, clinical assessment).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the REMS enrollment record was last updated or modified.']
  next_compliance_check_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next required compliance check or monitoring activity is due.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the REMS enrollment, compliance issues, or special circumstances.']
  patient_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient signed the REMS acknowledgment and informed consent form.']
  patient_acknowledgment_signed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has signed the required REMS acknowledgment and informed consent form.']
  patient_education_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has completed required REMS education and counseling about medication risks and safe use.']
  patient_education_date date [note: 'Date when the patient completed required REMS education and counseling.']
  prescriber_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the prescriber completed REMS certification training.']
  prescriber_certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the prescriber REMS certification expires and must be renewed.']
  prescriber_certification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the prescriber has completed required REMS training and certification to prescribe the high-risk medication.']
  prescriber_npi varchar [note: 'Ten-digit National Provider Identifier of the prescriber enrolled in the REMS program.']
  program_administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party organization or entity that administers the REMS program on behalf of the drug manufacturer.']
  program_administrator_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the REMS program administrator for enrollment support and compliance inquiries.']
  program_administrator_website varchar [note: 'Website URL for the REMS program administrator where enrollment forms, training materials, and compliance resources are available.']
  rems_coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the pharmacy staff member or REMS coordinator responsible for managing this enrollment.']
  rems_program_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or identifier for the REMS program assigned by FDA or program administrator.']
  rems_program_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the FDA-mandated REMS program (e.g., iPLEDGE for isotretinoin, Clozapine REMS, TIRF REMS Access for fentanyl products).']
  required_monitoring_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required patient monitoring activities (e.g., lab tests, pregnancy tests, clinical assessments) as mandated by the REMS program.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Primary risk category that necessitates REMS enrollment (e.g., teratogenic risk, addiction potential, serious adverse events, restricted distribution).']
  transfer_source_pharmacy_ncpdp varchar [note: 'Seven-digit NCPDP number of the pharmacy from which the REMS enrollment was transferred, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking patient and prescriber enrollments in FDA-mandated REMS (Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy) programs for high-risk medications (e.g., isotretinoin iPLEDGE, clozapine REMS, opioid REMS). Captures REMS program name, enrolled drug NDC, enrollment date, enrollment status, required monitoring intervals, last compliance check date, patient acknowledgment form signed flag, and prescriber certification status. Required for FDA REMS compliance and dispensing authorization.'
}

Table pharmacy.pharmacy_supplier_contract {
  pharmacy_supplier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the pharmacy_supplier_contract association']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the pharmacy location']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the contract (e.g., Active, Pending, Expired)']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Agreed delivery lead time for this pharmacy‑supplier contract']

  Note: 'This association represents the contractual relationship between a pharmacy location and a supplier. Each record stores the lead time and status specific to that pharmacy‑supplier pairing, enabling the business to manage and query supply agreements.. Existence Justification: Each pharmacy location can enter into contracts with multiple suppliers, and each supplier can serve many pharmacy locations. The contract captures operational details such as lead time and contract status, and it is actively created, updated, and terminated by business users. This relationship is therefore a true many‑to‑many business entity.'
}

Table pharmacy.payer {
  payer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payer']
  parent_payer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payer (parent_payer_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the payer\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the payer\'s mailing address, if applicable.']
  cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount payable by the payer per contract period.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the payer\'s address.']
  claim_submission_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for submitting claims to the payer.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the payer with regulatory requirements.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for payer communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for payer communications.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the contractual agreement with the payer.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the payer\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payer record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for reimbursements.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the payer.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the payer relationship becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the payer relationship ends or is scheduled to terminate; null if open‑ended.']
  external_payer_code varchar [note: 'Code used by external systems (e.g., clearinghouses) to identify the payer.']
  is_excluded_from_reporting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payer is excluded from regulatory reporting aggregates.']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Indicates if this payer is designated as a preferred network for contracts.']
  last_claim_submission_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent claim submitted to this payer.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name as registered with regulatory bodies.']
  name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the payer organization.']
  network_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the payer\'s network or group affiliation.']
  npi_number varchar [note: 'NPI assigned to the payer when applicable (e.g., health plan).']
  payer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payer entity.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the payer.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the payer\'s address.']
  reimbursement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard percentage rate applied to claim amounts for this payer.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name for the payer.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the payer\'s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payer record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the payer record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payer. Referenced by payer_id.'
}

Table promotion.promo_campaign {
  promo_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotional campaign. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Primary merchandise category targeted by this campaign for assortment focus and performance measurement.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for Promotion Budget vs Actual report; campaigns are budgeted to cost centers to track spend versus allocated budget.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for Promotion Expense GL Mapping report; each campaign must post its costs to a specific GL account for monthly financial close.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Campaign‑pricing zone assignment report used to allocate promotional budgets per pricing zone.']
  primary_promo_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who provided final approval for the campaign to proceed to execution.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required: Campaigns must be linked to a compliance program to ensure advertising and health claim regulations are met.']
  promo_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_calendar. Business justification: Link campaigns to their planning calendar; child (promo_campaign) gets FK to parent (promo_calendar). No existing reverse link, resolves silo for promo_calendar.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: REQUIRED: Campaign Store‑Cluster Targeting report needs campaign.store_cluster_id to map each promo campaign to the store clusters it targets.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) vendor or supplier sponsoring or co-funding the campaign.']
  target_customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Customer segment the campaign is designed to reach, used for personalized offer eligibility and targeting.']
  vendor_category_captain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_category_captain. Business justification: Campaign approval authority resides with category captain; linking enables audit of captain approvals.']
  ad_circular_week varchar [note: 'ISO week identifier for the weekly ad circular publication associated with this campaign (format: YYYY-Www).']
  approval_workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the campaign in the multi-stage approval process across merchandising, finance, and legal functions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign received final approval and was authorized for execution.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for the campaign in USD, covering markdowns, vendor funding, and execution costs.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the campaign, used in marketing materials, ad circulars, and system integrations.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the promotional campaign for internal reference and reporting.']
  campaign_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business goal the campaign is designed to achieve, driving offer design and performance measurement.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotional campaign in the approval and execution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|completed|cancelled|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional campaign by execution format and channel strategy.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the campaign was cancelled or suspended before completion, if applicable.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign was cancelled or suspended.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign ends and offers are no longer available for redemption.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign becomes active and offers are available for customer redemption.']
  expected_incremental_sales decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected incremental revenue in USD attributed to the campaign, above baseline sales without promotion.']
  expected_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Forecasted number of offer redemptions expected during the campaign period, used for budget planning and inventory allocation.']
  is_digital_coupon_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign includes digital coupons that customers can clip and redeem via mobile app or online account.']
  is_loyalty_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether campaign offers are available exclusively to loyalty program members.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was most recently updated.']
  legal_disclaimer varchar [note: 'Legal terms, conditions, and disclaimers required for campaign advertising and offer redemption compliance.']
  planning_start_date date [note: 'Date when campaign planning and design activities commenced.']
  promo_campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the campaign strategy, target audience, key offers, and expected business outcomes.']
  promotion_mechanics varchar [note: 'Description of the offer structure and redemption rules, such as Buy One Get One (BOGO), Temporary Price Reduction (TPR), or multi-buy thresholds.']
  target_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales and fulfillment channel where the campaign will be executed and offers redeemed.']
  target_gmroi decimal(10,4) [note: 'Target Gross Margin Return on Investment ratio for the campaign, measuring profitability of promotional spend.']
  vendor_funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount in USD contributed by the vendor partner toward campaign costs, including slotting fees and markdown support.']

  Note: 'Master record for a promotional marketing campaign. Captures campaign identity, type (weekly ad circular, digital, in-store event, seasonal, vendor-sponsored), planning horizon, budget allocation, sponsoring vendor/CPG partner, campaign objectives (trial, frequency, basket size, loyalty acquisition), target channel (in-store, digital, omnichannel), approval workflow status, and lifecycle state (draft, approved, active, completed, cancelled). Top-level organizing entity for all promotional activity. Source of truth for campaign planning, budget management, and post-promotion performance measurement.'
}

Table promotion.promo_offer {
  promo_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotional offer. Primary key for the promo_offer product.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: REQUIRED: Offer Category Targeting analysis uses promo_offer.category_id to associate each offer with the category it discounts.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_grade. Business justification: Defines the specific fuel grade (e.g., diesel) a promo offer applies to, supporting grade‑specific discount pricing and compliance reporting.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Required: Each offer must reference the specific compliance obligation (e.g., age‑restriction, labeling) it must satisfy.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Offer zone‑specific pricing ensures correct discount application across regional price zones.']
  primary_promo_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this promotional offer record.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign that contains this offer.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) vendor or supplier funding or co-funding this offer. Null for grocer-funded offers.']
  target_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer segment this offer is targeted to. Null for mass offers or individual personalization.']
  trade_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.trade_agreement. Business justification: Offer funding and rebate terms are governed by trade agreements; linking enables agreement compliance reporting.']
  activation_requirement varchar [note: 'Action required by the customer to activate the offer. Auto-apply (no action), clip-required (digital coupon clipping), loyalty-required (loyalty program membership), or code-entry (manual coupon code input).']
  clip_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer must explicitly clip or activate this digital coupon before redemption. True if clipping required, False if auto-applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotional offer record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used to deliver this offer to targeted customers.']
  digital_coupon_barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode or QR code value for digital coupon redemption at Point of Sale (POS). Null for non-digital-coupon offers.']
  digital_coupon_format varchar [note: 'Barcode format standard used for digital coupon encoding. Null for non-digital-coupon offers.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the discount value is calculated and applied.']
  discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount. Interpretation depends on discount_type (percentage points for percentage, dollar amount for fixed_amount).']
  eligible_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel where this offer can be redeemed. Supports omnichannel promotion strategy.']
  exclusion_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules defining products, categories, or conditions that are explicitly excluded from this offer.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for funding the promotional discount. Vendor-funded (trade promotion), grocer-funded (markdown), or co-funded (shared investment).']
  gmroi_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target Gross Margin Return on Investment ratio for this promotional offer. Used for post-promotion performance measurement and optimization.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points awarded to the customer upon redemption of this offer. Null if no points are earned.']
  loyalty_points_required varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points required to redeem this offer. Null if not a points-based loyalty reward.']
  manufacturer_coupon_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a manufacturer-issued coupon funded by the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) vendor. True for manufacturer coupons, False for retailer coupons.']
  maximum_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cap on the total dollar discount that can be applied per transaction. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum dollar amount of qualifying items required to activate the offer. Null if no minimum threshold applies.']
  minimum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity of qualifying items required to activate the offer. Null if no quantity threshold applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotional offer record was last modified.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the promotional offer. Used for tracking, redemption, and customer communication.']
  offer_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the promotional offer including terms, conditions, and value proposition.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the promotional offer displayed to customers.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional offer.']
  offer_subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification indicating the delivery and targeting mechanism of the offer.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional offer mechanics. BOGO (Buy One Get One), TPR (Temporary Price Reduction), percent-off, dollar-off, mix-and-match, or bundle.']
  personalization_model_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the machine learning model or rule set used to generate this personalized offer. Null for mass offers.']
  personalization_type varchar [note: 'Method used to target and personalize this offer. ML model (machine learning recommendation), rule-based (business rule segmentation), mass (broadcast to all), or segment-targeted (predefined customer segment).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple offers apply to the same item. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  redemption_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer or household can redeem this offer during its validity period. Null for unlimited redemptions.']
  redemption_limit_per_transaction varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this offer can be applied within a single transaction. Null for unlimited applications per transaction.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer can be combined with other offers in the same transaction. True if stackable, False if mutually exclusive.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Full legal terms and conditions governing the use and redemption of this promotional offer.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date when the offer becomes active and eligible for redemption.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date when the offer expires and is no longer eligible for redemption.']

  Note: 'Atomic unit of promotional value delivered to shoppers within a campaign. Captures offer mechanics (BOGO, TPR, percent-off, dollar-off, mix-and-match, bundle, loyalty reward, digital coupon, personalized deal), discount calculation rules, minimum purchase thresholds, maximum redemption limits, offer validity window, eligible channels (in-store, app, web, kiosk), stackability rules, funding source (vendor-funded vs. grocer-funded), activation requirements (auto-apply vs. clip-required), personalization targeting (ML model, rule-based, mass), delivery channel for targeted offers, digital coupon attributes (barcode/QR code, clip status, clip timestamp, coupon code, issuing channel, CPG manufacturer funding), personalization metadata (generation source, targeting rationale, customer/household linkage, deal acceptance/redemption outcome, behavioral signal inputs). Each campaign contains multiple offers targeting different SKUs, customer segments, or channels. Single Source of Truth for ALL promotional offer types including digital coupons, personalized deals, loyalty rewards, and mass offers — no other product in this domain models offer-level promotional value.'
}

Table promotion.promo_calendar {
  promo_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotional calendar entry. Primary key for the promotional calendar planning entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary product category that this promotional calendar entry focuses on (e.g., Fresh Produce, Dairy, Meat, Bakery). Nullable if promotion spans multiple categories.']
  planning_owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Planning Owner report requires linking each promo calendar to the associate responsible for its planning, enabling accountability and schedule tracking.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store cluster or zone that this promotional calendar entry applies to. Store clusters group stores by geography, demographics, or performance characteristics for targeted promotional planning. Nullable if promotion applies to all stores.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Calendar budgeting and supplier‑funded promotions need supplier attribution for financial reconciliation.']
  ad_circular_print_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry will be featured in the printed weekly ad circular flyer. True means print ad placement is planned; False means digital-only or in-store signage only.']
  ad_circular_week varchar [note: 'The week number within the fiscal year that this promotional calendar entry corresponds to, used for weekly ad circular planning and coordination. Aligns with retail fiscal calendar conventions.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar entry received executive or merchandising leadership approval to proceed. Nullable if still in draft or planning status.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or manager who approved this promotional calendar entry. Used for governance and audit trail purposes. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  blackout_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this calendar entry represents a blackout period during which no new promotions should be scheduled. True means this is a blackout period (e.g., inventory count week, system maintenance); False means normal promotional activity is allowed.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the promotional calendar in operational systems and reports (e.g., \'Q4-2024-HOL\', \'SP-FRESH-01\').']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name for the promotional calendar period (e.g., \'Q4 Holiday Campaign 2024\', \'Spring Fresh Produce Event\').']
  calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotional calendar entry. Draft indicates initial planning; planned means dates are set but not approved; approved indicates executive sign-off; active means promotion is currently running; completed means promotion has ended; cancelled indicates promotion was terminated before completion.']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional calendar entry by its business purpose and funding model. Weekly ad circular represents recurring weekly promotional cycles; seasonal event covers spring, summer, fall, winter campaigns; holiday promotion targets specific holidays; category event focuses on specific product categories; vendor funded indicates supplier-sponsored promotions; markdown clearance represents end-of-season or overstock price reductions.']
  conflict_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any promotional conflicts detected with other calendar entries (overlapping dates, same category, same store cluster) and how they were resolved. Used for planning coordination and audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promotional calendar entry was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and planning timeline analysis.']
  digital_coupon_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry includes digital coupon offers that customers can clip via mobile app or website. True means digital coupons are part of the promotion; False means no digital coupon component.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional period ends. This is the last day that promotional pricing and offers are valid. Nullable for open-ended promotions.']
  estimated_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated total budget allocated for this promotional calendar entry, including markdown costs, advertising spend, and vendor co-op funding. Expressed in the retailer\'s reporting currency (typically USD).']
  event_theme varchar [note: 'The marketing theme or creative concept for the promotional period (e.g., \'Back to School\', \'Summer BBQ\', \'Holiday Feast\', \'Fresh Start January\'). Used for merchandising and marketing alignment.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) to which this promotional calendar entry belongs, used for quarterly promotional planning and performance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this promotional calendar entry belongs, following the retailer\'s fiscal calendar (e.g., 2024, 2025).']
  loyalty_exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry is exclusive to loyalty program members. True means only loyalty cardholders can access the promotional pricing; False means promotion is available to all customers.']
  merchandising_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from merchandising team regarding assortment planning, display requirements, inventory positioning, or special handling instructions for this promotional calendar entry.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this promotional calendar entry. Used for accountability and audit trail purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promotional calendar entry was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  overlap_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry can overlap with other promotions in the same category or store cluster. True allows concurrent promotions; False enforces exclusivity to prevent promotional conflicts and margin erosion.']
  slotting_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry is tied to a contractual slotting fee commitment with a vendor. True means vendor has paid for guaranteed promotional placement during this period; False means no slotting commitment exists.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional period begins. This is the first day that promotional pricing, offers, or ad circular content becomes effective in stores and digital channels.']
  target_channel varchar [note: 'The primary sales channel where this promotional calendar entry will be executed. In-store represents physical retail locations; online represents eCommerce website; mobile app represents mobile application; omnichannel indicates promotion spans all channels.']
  target_gmroi decimal(8,2) [note: 'The target Gross Margin Return on Investment ratio for this promotional calendar entry. GMROI measures the profitability of inventory investment and is a key performance metric for promotional effectiveness in retail. Calculated as gross margin dollars divided by average inventory cost.']
  vendor_funded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry includes vendor-funded promotional support (slotting fees, co-op advertising, markdown allowances). True means vendor is contributing funding; False means retailer-funded only.']

  Note: 'Promotional planning calendar that organizes all campaigns and offers across time. Tracks weekly ad circular cycles, promotional periods, blackout dates, holiday event windows, and overlap constraints. Supports merchandise planning and assortment management by providing a structured view of promotional cadence across store zones, categories, and channels. Enables conflict detection between simultaneous promotions and coordination with vendor slotting commitments.'
}

Table promotion.ad_circular {
  ad_circular_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ad circular. Primary key.']
  designer_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Design accountability report tracks which associate designed each ad circular, linking circular to designer associate for performance metrics.']
  promo_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_calendar. Business justification: Associate each ad circular with its planning calendar; child (ad_circular) gets FK to parent (promo_calendar). Removes redundant week number column.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Ad circular funding source tracking for supplier‑funded circulars required for marketing spend reporting.']
  target_customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Enables linking each ad circular to the targeted customer segment for segment performance reporting and media spend allocation.']
  bogo_offers_count varchar [note: 'Number of Buy One Get One (BOGO) promotional offers included in this circular.']
  circular_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the circular (e.g., \'Spring Savings Event\', \'Weekly Deals March 15-21\').']
  circular_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the circular edition, typically a sequential number or code used for tracking and reference (e.g., \'WK2401\', \'C-2024-15\').']
  circular_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the circular from planning through execution to expiration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_production|published|active|expired|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  circular_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the circular based on frequency and purpose (weekly standard circular, seasonal promotion, holiday special, etc.).']
  cover_page_headline varchar [note: 'Primary headline or promotional message displayed on the cover page of the circular.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad circular record was first created in the system.']
  digital_coupon_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this circular includes digital coupon offers that customers can clip and redeem online or via mobile app.']
  digital_platform varchar [note: 'Digital platform or technology used to publish and distribute the online version of the circular (e.g., \'Salesforce Commerce Cloud\', \'Mobile App\', \'Email Campaign\').']
  digital_reach_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of unique digital viewers or impressions for the online version of the circular.']
  distribution_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or market area where this circular is distributed (e.g., \'Northeast Region\', \'Metro Atlanta\', \'Zone 5\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the circular expires and promotional offers are no longer valid.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the circular becomes active and promotional offers are valid for customer redemption.']
  endcap_feature_count varchar [note: 'Number of endcap (end-of-aisle display) features promoted in this circular.']
  estimated_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total estimated marketing and production budget allocated for this circular in USD, including print costs, digital advertising, and promotional funding.']
  estimated_gmroi_target decimal(10,4) [note: 'Target Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) ratio for this circular, representing expected gross margin dollars returned per dollar of inventory investment.']
  featured_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category or department featured prominently in this circular (e.g., \'Fresh Produce\', \'Meat & Seafood\', \'Private Label\').']
  format_type varchar [note: 'Distribution format of the circular indicating whether it is available in print, digital, or both channels.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language of the circular content using 3-letter ISO 639-2 language codes (ENG=English, SPA=Spanish, FRA=French, CHI=Chinese).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad circular record was last updated or modified.']
  loyalty_exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this circular contains offers exclusive to loyalty program members.']
  merchandiser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the merchandising manager or team responsible for selecting offers and planning the circular content.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this circular edition.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages in the printed or digital circular.']
  print_circulation_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of physical copies printed and distributed for this circular edition.']
  print_vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external printing vendor or service provider used for physical circular production.']
  production_deadline_date date [note: 'Final deadline for completing circular production and sending to print or digital publishing.']
  proof_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the final proof of the circular was approved by merchandising and marketing teams.']
  proof_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the circular proof during the review and approval workflow.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date when the circular was published and made available to customers (print distribution or digital release).']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary customer segment or demographic group targeted by this circular (e.g., \'Value Shoppers\', \'Organic Enthusiasts\', \'Family Households\').']
  theme varchar [note: 'Marketing theme or campaign message for the circular (e.g., \'Back to School\', \'Summer BBQ\', \'Holiday Feast\').']
  total_offers_count varchar [note: 'Total number of promotional offers and SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) featured in this circular.']
  tpr_offers_count varchar [note: 'Number of Temporary Price Reduction (TPR) offers included in this circular.']
