  vendor_funded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether promotional offers in this circular are partially or fully funded by vendor slotting fees or trade allowances.']

  Note: 'Weekly or periodic printed and digital advertising circular that communicates promotional offers to shoppers. Captures circular edition number, effective date range, distribution zone, page count, item placements with page/position coordinates, endcap and gondola feature assignments, digital vs. print format flags, circulation reach, production deadline, and proof approval status. Links to specific offers featured in each circular. Core artifact for weekly promotional execution in retail-grocery — the primary vehicle for communicating TPRs and BOGO offers to price-sensitive shoppers.'
}

Table promotion.offer_item {
  offer_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the offer item association record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created this offer item association. Supports audit trail and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) compliance for promotional pricing controls.']
  modified_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this offer item association. Supports audit trail and change tracking for promotional pricing.']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.planogram. Business justification: REQUIRED: Offer Placement Planning process links each offer_item to a specific planogram for shelf‑space allocation.']
  primary_offer_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sellable item (product, SKU) that is eligible for this promotional offer.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent promotional offer that this item is associated with.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store cluster or group where this promotional item is active. Enables localized promotions tailored to regional preferences and competitive dynamics.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (supplier, manufacturer) funding or co-funding this promotional item. Used for vendor-funded promotion tracking and chargeback processing.']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_item. Business justification: Offer items must reference the exact vendor SKU to manage funding, cost, and inventory; required for vendor item performance tracking.']
  ad_circular_featured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is featured in the weekly promotional flyer (ad circular). True if the item appears in print or digital advertising materials.']
  age_restriction_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether age verification is required at POS for this promotional item (alcohol, tobacco, restricted medications). Ensures compliance with state and federal age-restricted product regulations.']
  channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Specifies which sales channels this promotional item is valid for. Supports omnichannel promotion strategy with channel-specific offers for POS, eCommerce, mobile app, curbside pickup, and delivery.']
  cost_override_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Special cost basis for this item during the promotion period, typically negotiated with vendors for promotional pricing. Used for accurate GMROI and margin analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offer item association record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and promotion planning analysis.']
  digital_coupon_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is eligible for digital coupon stacking on top of the base promotional offer. Enables omnichannel personalized deal combinations.']
  discount_override_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Item-specific discount amount that overrides the offer-level discount for this particular SKU. Enables variable discounts within a single promotional campaign.']
  discount_override_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Item-specific discount percentage that overrides the offer-level discount rate for this SKU. Allows differentiated markdown rates within the same promotion.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this item is no longer eligible for the promotional offer. Used for promotion calendar management and automatic expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this item becomes eligible for the promotional offer. Supports phased rollout and time-limited flash promotions.']
  facing_count varchar [note: 'Number of product fronts on shelf required for this promotional item. Ensures adequate visibility and stock availability during the promotion period.']
  inclusion_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether this item is included in the promotion, explicitly excluded, required for eligibility, or optional within a multi-item offer.']
  item_identifier_type varchar [note: 'Type of item identifier used for promotion eligibility determination. SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for internal codes, UPC (Universal Product Code) for standard barcodes, GTIN (Global Trade Item Number) for GS1 standards, PLU (Price Look-Up Code) for produce, EAN for European Article Numbers, ISBN for books.']
  item_identifier_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual identifier value (SKU code, UPC barcode, GTIN number, PLU code) used to match this item to the promotion at POS and digital checkout.']
  item_role varchar [note: 'Mechanical role this item plays in the promotion structure. Buy item vs get item in BOGO (Buy One Get One) offers, trigger item vs reward item in mix-and-match promotions, qualifying item for threshold-based offers, or bundle component in multi-item packages.']
  loyalty_points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to loyalty reward points earned on this item during the promotion. For example, 2.0 means double points, 3.0 means triple points.']
  maximum_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity of this item eligible for the promotional discount within a single transaction. Used to cap BOGO offers and prevent abuse.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to purchase this promotional item. Typically 18 for tobacco, 21 for alcohol in most US states.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offer item association record was last modified. Tracks changes to promotion eligibility, discount overrides, and item roles.']
  offer_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this offer item association. Active means the item is currently eligible for the promotion, pending means scheduled for future activation, expired means the effective period has ended, suspended means temporarily disabled, cancelled means permanently removed from the offer.']
  planogram_placement_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the visual merchandise display plan location where this promotional item should be placed (endcap, gondola, front-of-store, checkout lane). Supports execution of promotional planograms.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Ranking used to determine which promotion applies when an item qualifies for multiple overlapping offers. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for required and maximum quantities. Each for discrete items, weight units for produce and bulk goods, volume units for liquids, case or pack for wholesale quantities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|pound|kilogram|ounce|liter|gallon|case|pack — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  required_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Minimum purchase quantity of this item required to qualify for the promotional offer. Supports fractional quantities for weight-based items (e.g., 2.5 lbs of produce).']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional item is eligible for purchase using SNAP benefits (EBT - Electronic Benefits Transfer). Required for compliance with USDA SNAP regulations.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this offer item association (Oracle Retail Price Management, SAP SD Sales and Distribution, proprietary promotion engine). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  substitution_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether item substitution is permitted if the promoted item is out of stock (OOS). Relevant for online order fulfillment and click-and-collect services.']
  vendor_funded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotional discount for this item is funded by the vendor (manufacturer) rather than the retailer. Used for slotting fee negotiations and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional item is eligible for purchase using WIC benefits. Required for compliance with USDA WIC program regulations.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking promotional offers to specific sellable items (SKUs, UPCs, GTINs, PLU codes) eligible for the promotion. Captures included and excluded items, required purchase quantities, qualifying item roles (buy item vs. get item in BOGO, trigger item vs. reward item in mix-and-match), item-level discount overrides, and planogram placement context. Enables precise eligibility determination at POS, self-checkout, and digital checkout. Supports complex multi-item promotions where different items play different mechanical roles.'
}

Table promotion.offer_eligibility_rule {
  offer_eligibility_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the offer eligibility rule. Primary key.']
  primary_offer_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that created the eligibility rule record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  primary_offer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer segment that this rule targets. Used for household segmentation and personalized deal targeting.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional offer that this eligibility rule governs.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store cluster or group where the offer is valid. Used to target specific store formats, market types, or performance tiers.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Eligibility rules reference loyalty tier thresholds; FK enables tier‑based rule enforcement and reporting.']
  channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Sales channel or fulfillment method where the offer is valid. Restricts eligibility to specific shopping channels such as in-store, online, mobile app, curbside pickup, or delivery.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing regulatory compliance considerations, legal restrictions, or program-specific requirements (e.g., SNAP, WIC, age verification) associated with the rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the eligibility rule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the eligibility rule expires and is no longer enforced. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the eligibility rule becomes active and begins to be enforced for offer qualification.']
  evaluation_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the eligibility rule is evaluated or refreshed. Determines whether qualification is checked in real-time at transaction, on a batch schedule, or on-demand.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale or explanation for why certain customers or segments are excluded from the offer. Provides transparency for compliance and customer service purposes.']
  geographic_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the geographic zone, region, or market where the offer is valid. Used to restrict offers to specific trading areas, ZIP codes, or store clusters.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the eligibility rule record was most recently updated.']
  maximum_age_years varchar [note: 'Maximum age in years for customer eligibility. Used for youth-targeted promotions or senior discount programs with upper age limits.']
  minimum_age_years varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required for a customer to qualify for the offer. Used for age-restricted products such as alcohol, tobacco, or pharmacy items.']
  minimum_loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'The minimum loyalty program tier or level required for a customer to qualify for the offer. Examples include Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, or program-specific tier names.']
  new_customer_definition_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since first transaction or account creation that defines a customer as \'new\' for eligibility purposes. Typically ranges from 30 to 90 days.']
  new_customer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer is restricted to new customers only. Used for customer acquisition campaigns and first-time shopper incentives.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple eligibility rules apply to the same offer. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  purchase_history_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative purchase amount required in the customer\'s transaction history to qualify for the offer. Used for spend-based eligibility rules.']
  purchase_history_threshold_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the lookback period for evaluating purchase history thresholds. Defines the time window over which customer spending or transaction counts are assessed.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the eligibility rule, including business context, intended audience, and any special conditions or exceptions.']
  rule_logic_expression varchar [note: 'Structured expression or formula defining the eligibility logic. May contain conditional operators, attribute references, and threshold values that are evaluated to determine customer qualification.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the eligibility rule, used for identification and reporting purposes.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility rule. Indicates whether the rule is actively enforced, temporarily suspended, in draft state, or expired.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of eligibility rule that determines the nature of the restriction or qualification. Defines whether the rule is based on loyalty membership, customer newness, geography, household segmentation, purchase history, government assistance program eligibility (SNAP/EBT, WIC), loyalty tier, age, or sales channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: loyalty_member_only|new_customer|geographic_zone|household_segment|purchase_history_threshold|snap_ebt_eligible|wic_eligible|minimum_loyalty_tier|age_restriction|channel_restriction — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the eligibility rule. Incremented with each modification to support rule history tracking and rollback capabilities.']
  snap_ebt_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer is available to customers using SNAP or EBT payment methods. Ensures compliance with federal nutrition assistance program regulations.']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer is available to customers using WIC benefits. Ensures compliance with WIC program restrictions and approved product lists.']

  Note: 'Business rules governing customer eligibility for promotional offers. Captures rule type (loyalty member only, new customer, geographic zone, household segment, purchase history threshold, SNAP/EBT eligible, WIC eligible), rule logic expressions, customer segment targeting criteria, minimum loyalty tier requirements, and exclusion conditions. Supports personalized deal targeting and compliance with program-specific restrictions such as WIC and SNAP regulations.'
}

Table promotion.digital_coupon {
  digital_coupon_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the digital coupon. Primary key for the digital coupon entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category to which this digital coupon applies. Used for category-level coupon offers rather than Stock Keeping Unit (SKU)-specific offers.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broader marketing or promotional campaign to which this digital coupon belongs. Links coupon to campaign planning and performance measurement.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Required: Digital coupons often need a regulatory filing record for advertising disclosures and audit trails.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer segment targeted by this personalized digital coupon offer. Null for mass-distribution coupons.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.shopper. Business justification: Required for tracking which shopper clips each digital coupon, used in loyalty analytics and regulatory reporting of coupon usage.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) manufacturer or vendor funding the coupon offer. Null if retailer-funded.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'GS1-compliant barcode number for Point of Sale (POS) scanning. Typically UPC (Universal Product Code) or EAN format for digital coupon redemption.']
  clip_status varchar [note: 'Current activation and usage status of the digital coupon: available for clipping, clipped by customer, redeemed at Point of Sale (POS), or expired without redemption.']
  clip_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer activated or clipped the digital coupon to their loyalty account. Null if coupon has not been clipped.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the digital coupon offer. Used for tracking and redemption validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the digital coupon record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and coupon lifecycle tracking.']
  current_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Current cumulative number of times this digital coupon has been redeemed across all customers. Used to enforce total redemption limits.']
  digital_coupon_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the digital coupon offer including terms, conditions, eligible products, and redemption instructions.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Type of discount mechanism applied by the coupon: percentage off, dollar amount off, Buy One Get One (BOGO), fixed price, or free item.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the digital coupon becomes valid and available for customer clipping and redemption.']
  eligible_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) identifiers eligible for this digital coupon offer. Null if coupon applies to entire category or store-wide.']
  eligible_store_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of store identifiers where this digital coupon can be redeemed. Null if coupon is valid at all locations or online only.']
  excluded_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) identifiers explicitly excluded from this digital coupon offer despite category or store-wide eligibility.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the digital coupon expires and can no longer be clipped or redeemed. After this date, the coupon is invalid.']
  face_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value or percentage discount amount displayed on the coupon. For percentage discounts, represents the percentage; for dollar discounts, represents the dollar amount.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'Uniform Resource Locator (URL) of the promotional image or graphic associated with the digital coupon for display in mobile app, website, and email.']
  issuing_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel through which the coupon was originally issued or made available to customers: mobile app, website, email campaign, SMS, loyalty portal, or social media.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the digital coupon record was last updated or modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  loyalty_points_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchases made using this digital coupon are still eligible for loyalty program points accrual. True if points can be earned, false if coupon disqualifies points.']
  manufacturer_funded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coupon discount is funded by a Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) manufacturer or vendor rather than by the retailer. True if manufacturer-funded, false if retailer-funded.']
  maximum_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount that can be discounted when redeeming this coupon. Applies primarily to percentage-based offers to cap total discount.']
  maximum_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer can redeem this digital coupon during the offer period. Null indicates unlimited redemptions per customer.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction subtotal required to qualify for coupon redemption. Null if no minimum purchase requirement exists.']
  personalization_source varchar [note: 'Method by which the coupon was personalized or targeted: mass distribution to all customers, targeted based on segment, behavioral analysis, demographic profile, or predictive Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) modeling.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Display priority ranking for the digital coupon when multiple offers are available. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for customer presentation.']
  qr_code varchar [note: 'Quick Response code data payload for mobile app or self-checkout scanning. Encodes coupon identifier and validation data.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this digital coupon can be combined or stacked with other coupons, promotions, or Temporary Price Reductions (TPR) in a single transaction. True if stackable, false if exclusive.']
  store_eligibility_type varchar [note: 'Defines which store locations or channels are eligible for coupon redemption: all stores, specific store list, store cluster, online orders only, or in-store purchases only.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Full legal terms and conditions governing the use, redemption, and restrictions of the digital coupon. Required for regulatory compliance and customer transparency.']
  title varchar [note: 'Customer-facing title or headline of the digital coupon offer displayed in mobile app, website, and loyalty platform.']
  total_redemption_limit varchar [note: 'Total number of redemptions allowed across all customers before the coupon offer is exhausted. Null indicates no aggregate limit.']

  Note: 'Digital coupon issued through the grocer\'s mobile app, website, or loyalty platform. Captures coupon code, barcode/QR code, face value, discount type, issuing channel (app clip, email, web), clip status, clip timestamp, expiration date, maximum redemption count, CPG manufacturer funding flag, and personalization source (targeted vs. mass). Distinct from paper coupons and in-store TPRs — digital coupons require explicit customer clip/activation before redemption.'
}

Table promotion.promotion_redemption {
  promotion_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion redemption transaction. Primary key for this entity.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cashier or associate who processed the transaction at POS. Null for self-checkout and digital channels.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: Needed for Redemption‑Member reporting linking each redemption to the member for ROI and engagement analysis.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Point of Sale (POS) transaction or order in which this promotion was redeemed.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the omnichannel order (delivery or pickup) if redemption occurred in digital checkout. Null for in-store POS transactions.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional offer that was redeemed. Links to the promotion master entity.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Shift‑level sales analysis requires linking each redemption to the shift during which it occurred, enabling labor productivity reporting.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who redeemed the promotion. Links to loyalty account or shopper profile.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: REQUIRED: Redemption by Store‑Cluster performance report needs promotion_redemption.store_cluster_id to aggregate redemptions at the cluster level.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical store location where the redemption occurred. Null for pure digital fulfillment.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier who funded or co-funded the promotion. Used for vendor funding reconciliation and slotting fee settlement.']
  ad_circular_week varchar [note: 'Week identifier for the weekly ad circular in which this promotion was featured. Format: YYYY-WW. Used for ad circular performance measurement.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the promotion was redeemed: POS (Point of Sale), self-checkout kiosk, online web storefront, mobile app, curbside pickup, or in-store pickup.']
  clearing_house_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the coupon redemption was submitted to the clearing house for vendor reimbursement. Null if not yet submitted.']
  clearing_house_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this manufacturer coupon redemption has been submitted to the coupon clearing house for reimbursement. True if submitted, False otherwise.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Unique coupon code or barcode scanned at redemption. Used for digital coupon clips, manufacturer coupons, and personalized deal codes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  digital_coupon_clip_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer clipped or activated the digital coupon in the mobile app or website. Null for non-digital offers.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of discount applied to the transaction as a result of this promotion redemption. Expressed in local currency.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied if the promotion was percentage-based (e.g., 20% off). Null for fixed-amount discounts.']
  ebt_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the redeemed item was eligible for EBT/SNAP/WIC payment. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  final_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final unit price after the promotional discount was applied. Used for net revenue and margin calculation.']
  item_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the item purchased that qualified for the promotion. Supports BOGO and multi-buy offer tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  loyalty_account_number varchar [note: 'Customer loyalty program account number used at time of redemption. Enables tracking of personalized deal usage and reward point accrual.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points earned by the customer as a result of this promotion redemption. Used for loyalty program accrual tracking.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points redeemed by the customer to activate or apply this promotion. Used for loyalty liability tracking.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional offer redeemed: BOGO (Buy One Get One), TPR (Temporary Price Reduction), digital coupon, paper coupon, personalized deal, loyalty reward, or vendor-funded promotion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bogo|tpr|digital_coupon|paper_coupon|personalized_deal|loyalty_reward|vendor_funded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  original_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original unit price of the item before the promotional discount was applied. Used for markdown and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the transaction: credit card, debit card, cash, EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer), SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women Infants and Children), gift card, mobile wallet, or check. Critical for SNAP/WIC eligibility tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|ebt|snap|wic|gift_card|mobile_wallet|check — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  personalized_deal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this was a personalized deal targeted to the specific customer based on purchase history or predictive analytics. True if personalized, False otherwise.']
  promotion_end_date date [note: 'End date of the promotional offer period. Used to validate redemption timing and identify expired offer redemption attempts.']
  promotion_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the promotional offer period. Used to validate redemption timing and measure promotion lifecycle performance.']
  redemption_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the redemption event. Used for daily aggregation and ad circular performance reporting.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the promotion was redeemed at POS, self-checkout, or digital checkout. Core business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  register_number varchar [note: 'POS register or terminal number where the redemption was processed. Used for audit trail and transaction reconciliation.']
  reimbursement_status varchar [note: 'Status of vendor reimbursement for this coupon redemption: pending (awaiting clearing house processing), approved (validated by vendor), rejected (not eligible for reimbursement), or paid (funds received).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason why the promotion redemption was rejected or partially applied. Examples: item not eligible, minimum purchase not met, limit exceeded, expired offer.']
  retailer_funded_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the discount amount funded by the retailer. Used for internal cost allocation and GMROI analysis.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the item on which the promotion was applied. Supports item-level redemption tracking.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code (barcode) of the item redeemed. Used for manufacturer coupon validation and vendor funding reconciliation.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the redemption validation: approved (fully applied), rejected (not eligible), partially applied (some conditions not met), pending review (manual verification needed), expired (offer past end date), or invalid coupon (code not recognized).']
  vendor_funded_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the discount amount funded by the vendor or manufacturer. Used for vendor chargeback and coupon clearing house settlement.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a promotional offer being redeemed at POS, self-checkout, or digital checkout. Captures redemption timestamp, transaction/order reference, store or fulfillment channel, offer redeemed (including digital coupon clips and personalized deal acceptances), discount amount applied, customer loyalty account, payment method used (including EBT/SNAP/WIC), item-level redemption detail, and redemption validation status (approved, rejected, partially applied). Core transactional entity for post-promotion performance measurement, vendor funding reconciliation, and coupon clearing house settlement.'
}

Table promotion.tpr_event {
  tpr_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the TPR event record. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category for reporting and analysis of TPR performance by category.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Required: Temporary price‑reduction events must be tied to compliance obligations governing price advertising.']
  primary_tpr_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this TPR event. Null if not yet approved or approval not required.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent promotional campaign that this TPR event is part of, if applicable. Null for standalone TPRs.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that is subject to this temporary price reduction.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific store where this TPR is applied. Null if TPR applies to a zone or all stores.']
  store_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store zone (geographic or cluster grouping) where this TPR applies. Used when TPR is applied at zone level rather than individual store.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor/supplier associated with this SKU, used for tracking vendor-funded TPRs and slotting fee negotiations.']
  tertiary_tpr_created_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this TPR event record in the system.']
  ad_circular_week varchar [note: 'The ad circular week identifier (format YYYY-Wnn) when this TPR is featured in the promotional flyer. Null if not featured in ad circular.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TPR requires management approval before activation, typically based on discount depth thresholds or margin impact.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this TPR event was approved for execution. Null if not yet approved or approval not required.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why this TPR event was cancelled before completion. Null if not cancelled.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this TPR event was cancelled. Null if not cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this TPR event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this TPR event record.']
  discount_depth_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount from original SRP, calculated as ((original_srp_amount - tpr_price_amount) / original_srp_amount) * 100. Key metric for TPR performance analysis.']
  display_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific in-store display location for this TPR (e.g., endcap number, gondola aisle, front-end display). Null if standard shelf location.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the temporary price reduction ends and pricing reverts to standard SRP. Inclusive end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the temporary price reduction becomes active at point of sale. TPR pricing is applied automatically at POS starting this date.']
  expected_revenue_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Forecasted total revenue expected from this TPR event, calculated as expected_unit_sales * tpr_price_amount.']
  expected_unit_sales varchar [note: 'Forecasted number of units expected to sell during the TPR period, used for inventory planning and performance measurement.']
  is_ad_circular_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TPR is featured in the weekly ad circular promotional flyer. True = featured in ad; False = in-store only TPR.']
  is_endcap_display boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TPR SKU is featured on an endcap (end-of-aisle display) for increased visibility. True = endcap placement; False = standard shelf location.']
  is_vendor_funded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor/supplier is providing funding or allowances to support this TPR. True = vendor-funded; False = retailer-funded markdown.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this TPR event record was last updated. Audit trail field for tracking changes.']
  markdown_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code for the temporary price reduction. Seasonal = seasonal promotion; Clearance = inventory clearance; Competitive = competitive response; Vendor_funded = vendor trade spend; Category_driver = traffic driver; New_item_intro = new product launch support; Overstock = excess inventory liquidation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seasonal|clearance|competitive|vendor_funded|category_driver|new_item_intro|overstock — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  maximum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units per transaction eligible for TPR pricing, used to limit exposure on deep discounts. Null if no limit.']
  minimum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units customer must purchase to receive the TPR price. Typically 1 for standard TPRs; may be higher for multi-buy offers. Null if no minimum.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, instructions, or context about this TPR event. Used for internal communication and documentation.']
  original_srp_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard suggested retail price of the SKU before the temporary price reduction is applied. Used as baseline for discount depth calculation.']
  pos_activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the TPR price was successfully activated in POS systems and began applying at checkout.']
  pos_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of TPR price transmission to POS systems. Pending = awaiting transmission; Transmitted = sent to POS; Active = confirmed active at POS; Failed = transmission error; Reverted = price reverted to SRP.']
  target_gmroi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) ratio for this TPR event, used to evaluate promotional profitability. Calculated as gross_margin / average_inventory_cost.']
  tpr_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the TPR event, used for external reference and reporting. Typically follows format TPR-XXXXXXXX.']
  tpr_price_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The reduced price at which the SKU is sold during the TPR period. This is the shelf price automatically applied at POS without customer action.']
  tpr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the TPR event. Planned = scheduled but not yet approved; Approved = ready for execution; Active = currently in effect at POS; Completed = ended normally; Cancelled = terminated before completion; Suspended = temporarily paused.']
  vendor_funding_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total dollar amount of vendor funding or allowances provided to support this TPR. Null if not vendor-funded.']

  Note: 'Temporary Price Reduction (TPR) event record capturing a time-bound price reduction applied to specific SKUs at store or zone level. Tracks TPR start and end dates, original SRP, TPR price, discount depth percentage, store zone applicability, category, vendor funding flag, and whether the TPR is featured in the ad circular or endcap display. Distinct from digital coupons and loyalty offers — TPRs are shelf-level price changes applied automatically at POS without customer action.'
}

Table promotion.vendor_funding {
  vendor_funding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor funding record. Primary key for this entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category this vendor funding applies to (e.g., beverages, snacks, dairy). Used for category management and slotting fee allocation.']
  primary_vendor_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the category manager or buyer who negotiated this vendor funding agreement.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required: Vendor funding agreements are subject to a compliance program for audit and regulatory reporting.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign this vendor funding supports. Links funding to specific marketing initiatives.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store cluster or group this funding applies to. Null if funding applies to all stores.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CPG manufacturer or DSD distributor providing the promotional funding.']
  tertiary_vendor_created_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this vendor funding record in the system.']
  accrued_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of vendor funding that has been earned or accrued to date based on promotional performance and agreement terms.']
  actual_performance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual performance achieved during the promotional period, measured in units corresponding to the performance metric type.']
  agreement_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor funding agreement expires and the promotional period ends. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  agreement_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor funding agreement becomes effective and the promotional period begins.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this vendor funding agreement was approved by authorized personnel.']
  committed_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount the vendor has committed to provide for this promotional funding agreement.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the signed vendor funding agreement contract document stored in the document management system.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code for internal allocation of vendor funding benefits, used for profitability analysis and departmental accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this vendor funding record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this funding agreement.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the vendor funding reconciliation is disputed, including details of the disagreement between retailer and vendor.']
  funding_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the vendor funding agreement, used for contract reference and vendor reconciliation.']
  funding_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which vendor funding is earned, expressed as dollars per unit of performance (e.g., $0.50 per case sold, 2% of sales dollars).']
  funding_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the funding rate: per unit sold, per case, per pallet, percentage of sales dollars, or flat fee.']
  funding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor funding agreement: draft (being negotiated), pending approval (awaiting internal sign-off), approved (contract signed), active (funding period in effect), completed (funding period ended and reconciled), cancelled (agreement terminated), or disputed (reconciliation disagreement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|completed|cancelled|disputed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  funding_type varchar [note: 'Category of vendor promotional funding: slotting fee for shelf space allocation, endcap placement for end-of-aisle displays, new item introduction support, ad circular co-op, TPR (Temporary Price Reduction) support, BOGO (Buy One Get One) support, display allowance, markdown allowance, volume rebate, or other trade promotion funding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: slotting_fee|endcap_placement|new_item_intro|ad_circular|tpr_support|bogo_support|display_allowance|markdown_allowance|volume_rebate|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code where vendor funding is recorded for financial reporting and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) offset.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this vendor funding agreement is currently active and in effect.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this vendor funding record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the vendor funding agreement, including special terms, conditions, or historical context.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which vendor funding will be remitted: check, ACH (Automated Clearing House), wire transfer, credit memo applied to future invoices, or deduction from current invoices.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Terms defining when and how the vendor will remit the promotional funding: net 30/60/90 days, upon promotion completion, quarterly settlement, annual settlement, or performance-based milestone payments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_30|net_60|net_90|upon_completion|quarterly|annual|performance_based — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  performance_metric_type varchar [note: 'Type of performance metric used to measure promotional success and determine funding accrual: sales volume (units sold), sales dollars (revenue), unit movement (velocity), display compliance (planogram adherence), distribution points (store penetration), or market share gain.']
  performance_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum performance level required to earn the vendor funding, measured in units corresponding to the performance metric type.']
  received_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of vendor funding that has been actually received or credited to the retailer\'s accounts payable.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor funding was reconciled and final accrual amounts were confirmed between retailer and vendor.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the funding reconciliation process: not started, in progress, completed (both parties agree), disputed (disagreement on amounts), or resolved (dispute settled).']
  sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU identifiers or UPC codes that are eligible for this vendor funding. May be null if funding applies to entire category or vendor portfolio.']

  Note: 'Vendor promotional funding record tracking all financial contributions from CPG manufacturers and DSD distributors, including slotting fees for shelf space allocation, endcap placement, new item introductions, and other trade promotion funding arrangements.'
}

Table promotion.funding_claim {
  funding_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor funding claim transaction. Primary key for the funding claim record.']
  primary_funding_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who submitted the funding claim to the vendor. Typically a category manager, buyer, or promotional analyst responsible for vendor funding recovery.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign for which vendor funding reimbursement is being claimed. Links claim to specific marketing initiative.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor (supplier or manufacturer) from whom promotional funding reimbursement is being claimed. Links to the vendor master data.']
  vendor_funding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional funding agreement or contract that authorizes this reimbursement claim. Defines terms, rates, and eligible promotional activities.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar difference between claimed funding amount and approved funding amount. Positive values indicate vendor reductions; negative values indicate vendor increases.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the funding claim received internal approval before submission to vendor. Part of the SOX-compliant approval workflow audit trail.']
  approved_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Vendor-approved reimbursement amount after claim review and validation. May differ from claimed amount due to disputes, adjustments, or partial approvals.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the funding claim was cancelled before or after submission. Reasons may include promotional campaign cancellation, incorrect claim data, or vendor agreement termination.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the funding claim was cancelled. Used for audit trail and to track claim lifecycle from creation through cancellation.']
  claim_period_end_date date [note: 'Ending date of the promotional activity period covered by this funding claim. Defines the end of the redemption window for which reimbursement is requested.']
  claim_period_start_date date [note: 'Beginning date of the promotional activity period covered by this funding claim. Defines the start of the redemption window for which reimbursement is requested.']
  claim_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the funding claim used in vendor communications and EDI transactions. Format: FC-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the funding claim in the vendor reimbursement workflow. Tracks progression from draft through payment or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|under_review|approved|rejected|partially_approved|paid|disputed|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  claimed_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of promotional discounts provided to customers during the claim period, for which vendor reimbursement is being requested. Gross claim amount before adjustments.']
  claimed_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total vendor funding reimbursement amount being claimed based on the funding agreement terms, claimed units, and discount amounts. Net amount requested from vendor.']
  claimed_units_sold decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of promotional units sold during the claim period for which vendor funding reimbursement is being requested. Supports per-unit funding calculations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the promotional campaign and associated vendor funding claim. Used for internal cost allocation and GMROI analysis by business unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this funding claim record was first created in the system, typically in draft status before submission.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this claim (claimed, approved, adjustment, paid). Typically USD for US operations.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Vendor-provided explanation for claim rejection, partial approval, or adjustment. Captures reasons such as ineligible products, incorrect calculations, missing documentation, or contract term violations.']
  dispute_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of dispute resolution process when vendor and retailer disagree on claim amount or eligibility. Tracks negotiation and escalation workflow.']
  edi_transaction_set_code varchar [note: 'EDI transaction set identifier for the electronic claim submission, typically EDI 810 Invoice or custom promotional claim transaction. Used for EDI audit trail and reconciliation.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the vendor funding reimbursement will be posted in the financial system. Typically a promotional allowance or vendor funding receivable account.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this funding claim record was most recently updated, including status changes, amount adjustments, or supporting documentation additions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, special instructions, or internal notes related to the funding claim processing, vendor communications, or dispute resolution.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the vendor remitted the approved funding amount. ACH and wire transfer are most common for large claims; credit memos may offset future invoices.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor funding reimbursement payment was received and posted to accounts receivable. Used for cash flow tracking and vendor payment performance analysis.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-provided payment reference number, check number, wire confirmation number, or remittance advice identifier used to reconcile the funding payment in accounts receivable.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the funding claim was officially submitted to the vendor for reimbursement processing. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  supporting_evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to supporting documentation for this claim, such as POS transaction reports, redemption summaries, or scan data extracts required by vendor for validation.']
  vendor_acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has acknowledged receipt of the funding claim submission via EDI 997 Functional Acknowledgment or other communication channel.']
  vendor_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vendor acknowledged receipt of the funding claim, typically via EDI transaction or vendor portal confirmation.']
  vendor_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vendor provided their approval, rejection, or partial approval decision on the funding claim.']

  Note: 'Transactional claim submitted to a vendor for reimbursement of promotional funding. Captures claim reference number, vendor identifier, funding agreement reference, claim period, claimed units sold, claimed discount amount, supporting redemption evidence, submission date, vendor acknowledgment status, and payment received date. Supports EDI-based vendor claim processing and accounts receivable reconciliation for promotional funding.'
}

Table promotion.personalized_deal {
  personalized_deal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the personalized deal record. Primary key.']
  eligible_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category eligible for this personalized deal. Links to the category entity. Null if deal applies to specific SKUs.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the household to which this personalized deal was targeted. Links to the household master entity for multi-member household-level targeting.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent promotional campaign under which this personalized deal was generated. Links to the promo_campaign entity.']
  promotion_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point-of-sale or online transaction in which this personalized deal was redeemed. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer segment used for targeting this personalized deal. Links to the customer_segment entity.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual shopper to whom this personalized deal was targeted. Links to the shopper master entity for individual-level targeting.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where this personalized deal was redeemed, if redeemed in-store. Links to the store entity. Null if redeemed online or not yet redeemed.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant or experiment group to which this personalized deal belongs. Used for testing deal effectiveness and optimization.']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer activated or clipped this personalized deal for use. Null if not yet activated.']
  actual_discount_applied decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual dollar amount of discount applied at redemption. May differ from discount_amount due to purchase amount or maximum discount cap. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  bonus_points varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty bonus points awarded upon redemption of this deal, if deal_type is bonus_points.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why this personalized deal was cancelled before redemption or expiration, if applicable. Null if deal was not cancelled.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this personalized deal was cancelled. Null if deal was not cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this personalized deal record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  deal_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to this personalized deal for redemption tracking and customer reference. Displayed in app, email, or kiosk.']
  deal_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the personalized deal, displayed to the customer (e.g., \'Your Weekly Savings on Fresh Produce\').']
  deal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the personalized deal. Tracks progression from generation through redemption or expiration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: generated|delivered|viewed|activated|redeemed|expired|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional offer mechanism for this personalized deal. Defines the discount structure or reward type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage_discount|fixed_amount_discount|bogo|buy_x_get_y|free_item|bonus_points|cashback — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this personalized deal was successfully delivered to the customer via the specified delivery channel. Null if not yet delivered.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which this personalized deal was delivered to the customer. Supports omnichannel personalization tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_app_push|email|sms|in_store_kiosk|website_banner|direct_mail|pos_receipt — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount discount offered by this deal, if deal_type is fixed_amount_discount. Expressed in local currency.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered by this deal, if deal_type is percentage_discount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off).']
  eligible_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU identifiers eligible for this personalized deal. Null if deal applies to categories or all items.']
  generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this personalized deal was generated by the system. Represents the business event time of deal creation.']
  generation_source varchar [note: 'System or process that generated this personalized deal. Identifies whether the deal was created by machine learning model, business rules, CRM campaign, or manual curation.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this personalized deal can be combined with other promotions or coupons in a single transaction. True if stackable, False if exclusive.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this personalized deal record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for record modification.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Loyalty program tier of the targeted customer or household at the time of deal generation. Used for tier-based personalization.']
  maximum_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount that can be discounted under this deal, if applicable. Used to cap percentage-based discounts.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase amount required to qualify for this personalized deal. Null if no minimum applies.']
  ml_model_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the machine learning model or algorithm version that generated this deal, if applicable. Used for model performance tracking and A/B testing.']
  offer_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the personalized deal offer, including terms, conditions, and customer-facing messaging.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this personalized deal relative to other deals for the same customer. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for display and redemption sequencing.']
  redeemed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this personalized deal was redeemed at point of sale or online checkout. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this personalized deal has been redeemed by the customer or household to date. Incremented at each redemption.']
  redemption_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this personalized deal can be redeemed by the targeted customer or household. Null if unlimited.']
  targeting_rationale varchar [note: 'Business reason or logic for why this deal was targeted to this customer or household. May include segment, purchase history pattern, or behavioral signal summary.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the validity window for this personalized deal. Customer can redeem the deal on or after this date.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'End date of the validity window for this personalized deal. Customer must redeem the deal on or before this date.']
  viewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer first viewed this personalized deal in the app, email, or kiosk. Null if not yet viewed.']

  Note: 'Personalized promotional deal generated for a specific customer or household based on purchase history, loyalty tier, segment, and behavioral signals. Captures deal generation source (ML model, rule-based, CRM campaign), offer details, targeting rationale, customer or household identifier, deal delivery channel (app push, email, in-store kiosk), deal validity window, and acceptance/redemption outcome. Supports Customer Loyalty and Promotions Management with 1:1 personalization at scale.'
}

Table promotion.promo_zone {
  promo_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotional zone. Primary key.']
  approved_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this promotional zone definition. Null if not yet approved.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this promotional zone record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary distribution center that services stores in this promotional zone. Used for coordinating promotional inventory allocation and DSD planning.']
  last_modified_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this promotional zone record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  parent_zone_promo_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent promotional zone if this zone is part of a hierarchical zone structure (e.g., sub-zones within a regional zone). Null for top-level zones.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Align promotional zones with pricing zones for consistent regional pricing strategy.']
  zone_manager_associate_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.associate']
  ad_circular_distribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether printed ad circulars are distributed to stores in this zone. True if ad circulars are distributed; false if zone is digital-only or does not receive circulars.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for this promotional zone definition. Draft zones are being configured; pending zones await merchandising approval; approved zones are active; rejected zones require revision.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotional zone was approved for use in promotion planning. Null if not yet approved.']
  average_household_income decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average household income in the geographic area covered by this promotional zone, in USD. Used for demographic-based promotion targeting and price sensitivity analysis.']
  competitive_market_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the competitive intensity and market maturity within this promotional zone. Used to tailor promotional strategies and pricing aggressiveness to local market conditions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotional zone record was first created in the system.']
  digital_coupon_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether digital coupons are enabled for stores in this promotional zone. True if digital coupons are available; false otherwise.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this promotional zone definition expires or expired. Null for open-ended zones.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this promotional zone definition became or will become active for promotion planning and pricing.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which this promotional zone definition was created or last revised. Used for year-over-year zone performance analysis.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market area that this promotional zone covers (e.g., \'Pacific Northwest\', \'Mid-Atlantic\', \'Gulf Coast\'). Used for regional promotion planning and competitive analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotional zone record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  loyalty_program_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether loyalty program promotions and personalized offers are enabled for stores in this zone. True if loyalty features are active; false otherwise.']
  markdown_strategy varchar [note: 'Markdown and clearance pricing strategy for this zone. Aggressive zones markdown faster to clear inventory; conservative zones preserve margin and markdown more slowly.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes for merchandising and pricing teams regarding this promotional zone, including historical performance insights, competitive intelligence, or special handling instructions.']
  population_density varchar [note: 'Population density classification of the geographic area covered by this zone. Urban zones have high density and different promotional needs than rural zones.']
  price_zone_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification for this zone, reflecting the overall price positioning strategy. Premium zones have higher SRP; value/discount zones have lower SRP to match local market conditions and competitive pressure.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'Primary language spoken in this promotional zone, using ISO 639-3 three-letter language codes (e.g., ENG for English, SPA for Spanish). Used for ad circular and digital coupon localization.']
  promo_zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the promotional zone, including its purpose, geographic boundaries, store membership criteria, and any special considerations for promotion planning.']
  snap_ebt_participation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transactions in this zone that use SNAP/EBT payment methods. Used to tailor promotional strategies to customer demographics and ensure compliance with SNAP-eligible product promotions.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Number of stores currently assigned to this promotional zone. Used for zone sizing and promotion budget allocation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this promotional zone (e.g., America/New_York, America/Los_Angeles). Used for coordinating promotion start/end times and ad circular release timing.']
  tpr_frequency_target varchar [note: 'Target frequency of TPR promotions for this zone. High-frequency zones run more frequent promotions; low-frequency zones focus on EDLP (Every Day Low Price) strategies.']
  vendor_funding_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is eligible for vendor-funded promotional programs and slotting fee negotiations. True if vendor funding is allowed; false otherwise.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the promotional zone, used in operational systems and reporting. Typically alphanumeric, 3-10 characters.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the promotional zone (e.g., \'Northeast Metro\', \'Southern Rural\', \'West Coast Urban\').']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional zone. Active zones are in use for promotion planning and execution; inactive zones are no longer used; pending zones are awaiting approval; suspended zones are temporarily disabled; archived zones are retained for historical reference only.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional zone by its primary business purpose: pricing zone (SRP/TPR management), ad zone (circular distribution), distribution zone (DC coverage), competitive market zone (market-based pricing), fulfillment zone (delivery/pickup), or regional zone (geographic grouping).']

  Note: 'Geographic or store-cluster zone definition used to scope promotional pricing and offer applicability. Captures zone name, zone type (pricing zone, ad zone, distribution zone), store membership, geographic region, competitive market classification, and zone manager. Enables zone-level TPR and SRP management, ensuring promotions are applied consistently across stores within the same competitive market cluster.'
}

Table promotion.promo_performance {
  promo_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion performance measurement record.']
  primary_promo_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who conducted the post-promotion performance measurement.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports Profit Center Promotion Impact analysis; performance metrics are rolled up to profit centers for P&L attribution.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional campaign that was executed and measured.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific promotional offer within the campaign that was measured.']
  promo_zone_id varchar [note: 'Geographic or store cluster zone where the promotion was executed and measured.']
  actual_units_sold decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total actual units sold during the promotion period including baseline and incremental.']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and commentary from the analyst conducting the post-promotion performance analysis.']
  baseline_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted baseline units that would have been sold without the promotion based on pre-promotion trends.']
  cannibalization_effect decimal(18,2) [note: 'Negative revenue impact on adjacent or substitute SKUs due to the promotion, measured as revenue decline in related products.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of goods sold for units sold during the promotion period.']
  fill_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of promotion demand that was fulfilled without stockouts, calculated as fulfilled_units divided by total_demand_units.']
  gmroi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Gross margin return on investment ratio measuring profitability of the promotion, calculated as gross_margin divided by inventory investment.']
  gross_margin decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross margin dollars calculated as net_revenue minus cost_of_goods_sold.']
  gross_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue generated during the promotion period before discounts.']
  halo_effect decimal(18,2) [note: 'Positive revenue impact on complementary categories or products driven by the promotion, measured as incremental revenue in related products.']
  incremental_units_sold decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total incremental units sold during the promotion period above baseline forecast.']
  inventory_investment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total inventory investment at cost for the promoted SKUs during the promotion period.']
  lift_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage lift in unit sales versus baseline, calculated as (incremental_units / baseline_units) * 100.']
  measured_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance measurement was completed and recorded.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the post-promotion measurement period.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the post-promotion measurement period.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the post-promotion performance measurement lifecycle.']
  net_discount_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net discount cost after vendor funding recovery, calculated as total_discount_cost minus vendor_funding_recovered.']
  net_margin_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net margin impact of the promotion after accounting for all costs including discounts and vendor funding.']
  net_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net revenue after applying all promotional discounts.']
  out_of_stock_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days the promoted SKU was out of stock during the promotion period.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative performance rating assigned to the promotion based on achievement versus targets.']
  planned_units_target decimal(18,3) [note: 'Original planned target for total units to be sold during the promotion.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of vendor funding reconciliation process for this promotion performance record.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Total number of promotion redemptions or coupon clips during the measurement period.']
  redemption_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of targeted customers who redeemed the offer, calculated as redemption_count divided by target audience size.']
  repeat_purchase_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers who made repeat purchases of the promoted SKU during the measurement period.']
  roi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Overall return on investment for the promotion, calculated as net_margin_impact divided by total promotion cost.']
  target_gmroi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Original planned target GMROI for the promotion.']
  total_discount_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of discounts provided to customers during the promotion period.']
  unique_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of unique customers who participated in the promotion.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance measurement was validated and approved.']
  variance_to_plan_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between actual and planned units, calculated as (variance_to_plan_units / planned_units_target) * 100.']
  variance_to_plan_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Variance between actual units sold and planned units target, calculated as actual_units_sold minus planned_units_target.']
  vendor_funding_recovered decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total vendor funding amount recovered or received to offset promotion costs.']

  Note: 'Post-promotion performance measurement record capturing actual results versus planned targets for a completed promotional campaign, offer, or TPR event. Tracks incremental units sold, baseline units (pre-promo), lift percentage, total discount cost, vendor funding recovered, net margin impact, GMROI, redemption count, redemption rate, cannibalization effect on adjacent SKUs, and halo effect on complementary categories. Supports post-promotion analysis, vendor funding reconciliation, and future campaign planning optimization. Granularity is one record per campaign-offer-zone combination measured.'
}

Table promotion.promotional_event {
  promotional_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for promotional_event']
  parent_promotional_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on promotional_event (parent_promotional_event_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store or location where the promotion is active.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary budget allocated for the promotion (gross amount before discounts).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel through which the promotion is delivered to customers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotional event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the promotion, its purpose, and any special notes.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of discounts offered by the promotion (adjustment component).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the rules that determine customer eligibility (e.g., spend threshold, product purchase).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the promotion expires or is terminated.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the promotion for tracking and reporting.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotion used in marketing materials and internal reporting.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the promotion (e.g., circular, digital coupon, buy‑one‑get‑one, temporary price reduction, loyalty reward, slotting fee incentive).']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region covered by the promotion.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is exclusive (true) or can be combined with other offers (false).']
  is_personalized boolean [note: 'True if the promotion is targeted to individual customers based on behavior or loyalty status.']
  marketing_budget_category varchar [note: 'Category of the budget line supporting the promotion.']
  net_savings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net monetary savings after applying discounts (budget minus discount).']
  party_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal or external party responsible for creating/owning the promotion (e.g., marketing team, vendor).']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the product(s) to which the promotion applies.']
  promotional_tactic varchar [note: 'Strategic tactic used in the promotion (e.g., price discount, BOGO, rebate, gift with purchase).']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the promotion has been redeemed by customers.']
  redemption_value_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all redemptions for the promotion.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the promotion becomes effective for customers.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment(s) eligible for the promotion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotional event record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for promotional_event. Referenced by promotional_event_id.'
}

Table vendor.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier entity. Primary key for the supplier master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns supplier‑related expenses to a designated cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  default_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AP system defaults payable postings to a supplier‑specific GL account for expense classification and audit trail.']
  procurement_manager_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Assign internal buyer to each supplier for PO approval workflow; essential for supplier performance tracking and contract compliance.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required for Supplier Compliance Program Enrollment report; suppliers are enrolled in multiple compliance programs (food safety, organic).']
