  ethanol_blend_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ethanol blended into the gasoline product. Standard unleaded typically contains up to 10% (E10); E85 contains 51-83% ethanol. Null for diesel and non-ethanol products.']
  flash_point_fahrenheit varchar [note: 'Lowest temperature at which vapors of the fuel will ignite when exposed to an ignition source. Critical safety parameter for storage and handling. Gasoline typically -45°F; diesel typically 125-180°F.']
  fleet_card_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this fuel grade is available for purchase using RFID-enabled fleet cards and commercial fuel cards. True if accepted for fleet transactions; False if restricted to retail consumer purchases only.']
  grade_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the fuel grade within the system (e.g., REG87, MID89, PREM93, DSL, E85, DEF). Used for operational lookup and integration with POS and pricing systems.']
  grade_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the fuel grade including key characteristics, intended use, performance benefits, and any special handling or regulatory notes. Used for customer communication, training materials, and internal documentation.']
  grade_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the fuel grade as displayed to customers and used in reporting (e.g., Regular Unleaded, Mid-Grade, Premium, Diesel, E85 Flex Fuel, Diesel Exhaust Fluid).']
  grade_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fuel grade. Active grades are available for sale; inactive grades are temporarily unavailable; discontinued grades are permanently retired; seasonal grades are available only during specific periods (e.g., winter blends).']
  grade_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the fuel product type. Gasoline includes unleaded grades; diesel includes standard and bio-diesel blends; alternative fuel includes E85, CNG, electric; additive includes DEF and other supplemental products.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit Global Trade Item Number used for supply chain and EDI transactions. Provides globally unique identification for fuel products across trading partners and distribution channels.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'Department of Transportation hazardous materials classification code for the fuel grade. Used for transportation, storage, and emergency response planning. Example: UN1203 for gasoline, UN1202 for diesel fuel.']
  loyalty_discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether purchases of this fuel grade qualify for loyalty program fuel discount redemptions. Typically True for standard gasoline and diesel grades; may be False for premium or specialty fuels depending on program rules.']
  octane_rating varchar [note: 'The octane number representing the fuel\'s resistance to knocking or pinging during combustion. Typical values: 87 (Regular), 89 (Mid-Grade), 91-93 (Premium). Null for non-gasoline products such as diesel or DEF.']
  reformulated_gasoline_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fuel grade is reformulated gasoline designed to reduce smog-forming and toxic air pollutants. RFG is required in certain metropolitan areas with severe ozone pollution. True if RFG; False otherwise.']
  renewable_identification_number varchar [note: '38-character unique identifier assigned to batches of renewable fuel under the EPA Renewable Fuel Standard. Used to track and trade renewable fuel credits for compliance with federal mandates. Null for non-renewable fuels.']
  sulfur_content_ppm varchar [note: 'Sulfur content measured in parts per million. EPA Ultra-Low Sulfur Diesel (ULSD) standard requires maximum 15 ppm. Gasoline typically contains less than 10 ppm under Tier 3 standards. Critical for emissions compliance.']
  supplier_product_code varchar [note: 'Product code or SKU used by the primary fuel supplier or refiner to identify this grade in purchase orders, invoices, and EDI transactions. Facilitates supply chain integration and vendor reconciliation.']
  tax_category_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the applicable federal and state excise tax category for the fuel grade. Different grades and blends may have different tax rates (e.g., gasoline vs diesel vs alternative fuels). Used for tax calculation and regulatory reporting.']
  upc_code varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code used for point-of-sale scanning and inventory tracking. Aligns with GS1 standards for retail fuel products sold in packaged form or tracked via loyalty systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel grade record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for change tracking and data synchronization. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  winter_blend_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fuel grade is formulated as a winter blend with higher Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP) to improve cold-weather starting and performance. True if winter blend; False if summer blend or year-round formulation.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of all fuel grades and product types offered across fuel centers, including grade name (Regular Unleaded, Mid-Grade, Premium, Diesel, E85, DEF), octane rating, ethanol blend percentage, product code, applicable regulatory classification, and whether the grade qualifies for loyalty fuel discount redemption.'
}

Table fuel.tank {
  tank_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the underground storage tank (UST). Primary key.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fuel center location where this tank is installed.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: COMPLIANCE: Tank ownership/manufacturer tracking is needed for EPA compliance, inspections, and service agreements.']
  capacity_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of the tank measured in gallons. Critical for inventory management and refill scheduling.']
  cathodic_protection_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent cathodic protection system test. Required every three years for tanks with cathodic protection per EPA regulations.']
  corrosion_protection_type varchar [note: 'Method of corrosion protection applied to the tank (e.g., cathodic protection, protective coating, inherently non-corrosive material). EPA requires corrosion protection for steel tanks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tank record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  current_volume_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current fuel volume in the tank measured in gallons. Updated by automatic tank gauging systems. Used for inventory management and reorder triggers.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the tank was permanently removed from service and decommissioned per EPA closure requirements. Null for active tanks.']
  decommission_method varchar [note: 'Method used to decommission the tank (e.g., removed from ground, filled in place with inert material). EPA requires proper closure procedures.']
  epa_ust_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official EPA registration number for this underground storage tank, required for federal compliance tracking and reporting.']
  fuel_grade varchar [note: 'Type of fuel grade stored in this tank (e.g., regular unleaded, premium, diesel). Determines pricing and customer selection at pump.']
  high_level_alarm_threshold_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fuel volume level that triggers a high-level alarm during delivery to prevent overfill. Typically set at 90-95% of capacity.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the tank was originally installed at the fuel center. Used to track tank age and plan replacement schedules per EPA guidelines.']
  last_cleaning_date date [note: 'Date when the tank was last cleaned and inspected internally. Periodic cleaning removes sediment and water accumulation that can cause fuel quality issues.']
  last_delivery_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent fuel delivery to this tank. Used for inventory reconciliation and delivery scheduling.']
  last_delivery_volume_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Volume of fuel delivered during the most recent delivery. Used for inventory reconciliation and variance analysis.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or internal inspection of the tank and associated equipment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tank record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_tightness_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent precision tightness test performed on the tank to verify structural integrity and absence of leaks. EPA requires periodic testing.']
  leak_detection_method varchar [note: 'EPA-approved method used to detect leaks from this tank (e.g., automatic tank gauging, vapor monitoring, interstitial monitoring). Required for EPA compliance.']
  lining_type varchar [note: 'Type of protective lining installed inside the tank to prevent corrosion and leakage (e.g., epoxy, polyurethane). Critical for EPA compliance and leak prevention.']
  low_level_alarm_threshold_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fuel volume level that triggers a low-level alarm. Set above reorder threshold to provide early warning of potential stockout.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the tank. Used for warranty tracking and technical support.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary construction material of the tank shell (e.g., steel, fiberglass, composite). Impacts corrosion risk and maintenance requirements.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or designation for this tank type. Used for parts ordering and technical specifications.']
  next_cleaning_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next tank cleaning. Typically performed every 3-5 years depending on fuel quality and contamination risk.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection. Critical for maintaining EPA compliance and avoiding penalties.']
  next_tightness_test_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required tightness test. Used for compliance tracking and maintenance scheduling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, maintenance history, or special handling instructions for this tank.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the tank. Active tanks are in use; inactive tanks are installed but not currently dispensing; out-of-service tanks require repair; decommissioned tanks have been permanently removed from service.']
  overfill_prevention_device varchar [note: 'Type of overfill prevention equipment installed on the tank (e.g., automatic shutoff, high-level alarm). EPA requires overfill prevention for all USTs.']
  reorder_threshold_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum fuel volume that triggers a reorder or delivery request. Set based on daily sales velocity and delivery lead time to prevent stockouts.']
  secondary_containment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tank has secondary containment (double-walled construction or external containment system). Reduces environmental risk and may lower insurance premiums.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer. Used for warranty claims and equipment tracking.']
  spill_prevention_device varchar [note: 'Type of spill prevention equipment installed at the fill pipe (e.g., catchment basin, spill bucket). EPA requires spill prevention for all USTs.']
  tank_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the tank, typically a sequential number assigned at the fuel center (e.g., Tank 1, Tank 2). Used for operational reference and signage.']
  temperature_monitoring_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tank is equipped with temperature monitoring sensors. Temperature data is used for volume compensation and leak detection.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty expires. Used for maintenance planning and cost forecasting.']
  water_detection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tank is equipped with water detection sensors at the bottom. Water accumulation can cause fuel quality issues and microbial growth.']

  Note: 'Master record for each underground storage tank (UST) at a fuel center, including tank number, capacity in gallons, fuel grade stored, tank material and lining type, installation date, EPA UST registration number, leak detection method, last tightness test date, and current operational status. Critical for EPA UST compliance and Cold Chain integrity.'
}

Table fuel.tank_level_reading {
  tank_level_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tank level reading record. Primary key for this event log entity.']
  inventory_reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inventory reconciliation period to which this reading belongs. Links to daily or shift-level reconciliation cycles.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the fuel center and tank are located.']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel storage tank from which this reading was captured. Links to the fuel tank master resource.']
  alarm_status varchar [note: 'Alarm or alert status reported by the ATG system at the time of reading. Indicates operational issues requiring attention. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal|low_level|high_level|water_detected|leak_suspected|sensor_fault|overfill_warning — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  atg_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ATG device or sensor that captured this reading. Used for equipment maintenance tracking and data quality auditing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reading record was first inserted into the data system. Distinct from reading_timestamp which is the business event time.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment flag indicating the reliability of this reading. Suspect or invalid readings are excluded from compliance reporting and inventory calculations.']
  delivery_in_progress_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a fuel delivery was in progress at the time of this reading. True if delivery active; False otherwise. Used to exclude readings during delivery from leak detection calculations.']
  fuel_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or type of fuel stored in the tank at the time of reading. Octane rating for gasoline or fuel type designation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular_87|midgrade_89|premium_91|premium_93|diesel|e85|e15 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fuel_height_inches decimal(6,3) [note: 'Height of fuel in the tank measured in inches from the tank bottom. Raw measurement used in conjunction with tank strapping tables to calculate volume.']
  fuel_temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the fuel in the tank measured in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for temperature correction calculations to derive net volume.']
  gross_volume_gallons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of fuel in the tank measured in gallons, not adjusted for temperature. Raw measurement from the ATG probe.']
  leak_detection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the automatic leak detection test performed by the ATG system. EPA requires continuous leak detection monitoring for underground storage tanks.']
  net_volume_gallons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Temperature-corrected volume of fuel in the tank measured in gallons. Adjusted to standard temperature (60°F) for accurate inventory accounting and reconciliation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by operators or technicians regarding this reading. May include context for alarms, manual interventions, or unusual conditions.']
  reading_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this reading within the day or shift for the specific tank. Used to order readings chronologically and detect missing observations.']
  reading_source varchar [note: 'Source or method by which the tank level reading was obtained. ATG automated readings are continuous; manual dipstick readings are performed during inspections or system failures.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tank level reading was captured by the ATG system or manual measurement. Primary business event timestamp for this observation.']
  ullage_gallons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Available capacity remaining in the tank measured in gallons. Calculated as tank capacity minus current gross volume. Used to determine reorder timing and delivery scheduling.']
  variance_gallons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated variance between expected inventory level and actual measured level in gallons. Used for inventory reconciliation and shrink analysis. Positive variance indicates gain; negative indicates loss.']
  water_level_inches decimal(6,3) [note: 'Height of water detected at the bottom of the tank measured in inches. Water accumulation indicates contamination risk and must be monitored for EPA compliance and product quality.']

  Note: 'Time-series transactional records of tank inventory level readings captured by automatic tank gauges (ATG), including timestamp, tank identifier, gross volume in gallons, net volume (temperature-corrected), water level, ullage (available capacity), and reading source (ATG automated vs. manual dip stick). Supports inventory reconciliation, reorder triggering, and EPA leak detection compliance.'
}

Table fuel.price {
  price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel price record. Primary key for the fuel_price product.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who approved this price change. Required for manual overrides and discretionary pricing decisions. Supports audit trail and accountability for pricing decisions.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel center location where this price applies. Links to the fuel center master data.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price changes affect GL entries for cost of goods sold and margin calculations.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'FK to fuel.fuel_grade']
  price_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_rule. Business justification: Fuel price records must reference the specific pricing rule used to generate the price for audit and optimization reporting.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'FK to pricing.price_zone']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign associated with this price record. Links to promotion master data for campaign tracking and ROI analysis. Null for non-promotional pricing.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: PRICING NEGOTIATION: Fuel price records must reference the supplying vendor for price negotiations, audit trails, and regulatory price filing.']
  change_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Net change in street price per gallon (current price minus prior price). Positive values indicate price increases, negative values indicate decreases.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the price change event. Competitive response to nearby stations, rack price index threshold breach, promotional campaign, manual manager override, scheduled adjustment, or cost recovery action.']
  change_trigger_source varchar [note: 'System or process that initiated the price change. Automated competitive pricing feed, rack index threshold alert, manual manager decision, centralized pricing system recommendation, or promotional calendar event.']
  competitor_price_reference decimal(10,4) [note: 'Reference price from competitive intelligence feed representing nearby competitor pricing. Used to justify competitive response price changes and maintain market positioning.']
  cost_per_gallon decimal(10,4) [note: 'Wholesale cost per gallon paid to the supplier or based on rack pricing. Used for margin calculation and profitability analysis. Business-confidential pricing data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price record was first created in the system. Distinct from effective timestamp; represents data capture time for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. USD for United States Dollar. Supports potential future international expansion.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fuel price becomes active and is displayed on signage and charged at the pump. Supports intraday price changes in response to competitive moves or rack price fluctuations.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fuel price is superseded by a new price record. Null for the current active price. Enables complete price history reconstruction and temporal queries.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price record was last updated. Tracks corrections, adjustments, or status changes after initial creation. Supports data quality audit and change tracking.']
  loyalty_discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fuel grade and price point is eligible for loyalty program fuel discount redemptions. True allows loyalty members to apply cents-per-gallon discounts earned through grocery purchases.']
  margin_per_gallon decimal(10,4) [note: 'Gross profit margin per gallon calculated as street price minus cost per gallon. Key metric for fuel center profitability and pricing strategy effectiveness.']
  max_loyalty_discount_cents decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum cents-per-gallon discount that can be applied through loyalty program redemption for this fuel grade. Caps the discount to protect margin floors and prevent negative pricing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments explaining the business context for this price change. Used by pricing managers to document competitive intelligence, market conditions, or special circumstances.']
  pos_sync_status varchar [note: 'Status of price synchronization to the fuel center POS system and pump controllers. Confirmed indicates successful deployment, failed indicates synchronization error requiring intervention.']
  pos_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price change was successfully synchronized to the POS system and pump controllers. Critical for reconciling price effective time with actual system deployment.']
  prior_street_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Previous street price per gallon before this price change event. Used for price change magnitude analysis and competitive response tracking.']
  promotional_price_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price is part of a temporary promotional campaign. True for limited-time offers, grand opening specials, or competitive market entry pricing.']
  rack_price_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Wholesale terminal rack price index at the time of this price record. Used as a benchmark for cost-based pricing strategies and margin protection rules.']
  regulatory_posting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change requires regulatory notification or public posting under state fuel pricing transparency laws. True triggers compliance workflow for jurisdictions with price-posting requirements.']
  regulatory_posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price change was reported to regulatory authorities or posted to public transparency portals. Required for compliance audit trail in regulated jurisdictions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or pricing platform that generated this price record. Examples include Oracle Retail Price Management, proprietary pricing engine, or manual entry system identifier.']
  street_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Customer-facing retail price per gallon displayed on the fuel center sign and charged at the pump. This is the base price before any loyalty discounts or promotions.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for price changes within the same fuel center and fuel grade. Increments with each price change event to support complete audit trail and change frequency analysis.']

  Note: 'Current and historical fuel price records per fuel grade per fuel center, serving as the single source of truth for all pricing and price change event history. Each record captures street price, cost per gallon, margin, effective date/time, expiration date/time, and price zone assignment. Price change audit trail includes prior price, new price, price change reason code (competitive response, rack movement, promotional, manual override), change trigger source (automated competitive feed, rack index threshold, manager decision), approving manager, POS synchronization confirmation status and timestamp. Supports pricing compliance audits, margin analysis, competitive response tracking, and regulatory price-posting requirements.'
}

Table fuel.price_change_event {
  price_change_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel price change event. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or authorized personnel who approved this price change. Required for audit trail and compliance with pricing authorization policies.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel center location where the price change occurred.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel grade (regular, mid-grade, premium, diesel) affected by this price change.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign associated with this price change, if the change was triggered by a marketing promotion or loyalty fuel discount program.']
  change_trigger varchar [note: 'The business reason or trigger that initiated this price change event. Competitive response indicates reaction to competitor pricing, rack price movement indicates wholesale cost changes, promotional indicates marketing campaign, manual override indicates manager discretion, scheduled adjustment indicates planned periodic changes, cost recovery indicates margin protection.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the competitor whose pricing was referenced for this price change, if applicable.']
  competitor_reference_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'The competitor fuel price that was used as a reference or benchmark for this price change decision, if applicable. Used for competitive positioning analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. Typically USD for US-based grocery retail fuel operations.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the new fuel price became effective at the pump. This is the principal business event timestamp for the price change.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or sequence number for the price change event, used for audit and reference purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change event record was last modified or updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  loyalty_discount_applied boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this price change includes or is related to a loyalty program fuel discount redemption structure.']
  margin_per_gallon decimal(10,4) [note: 'The calculated gross margin per gallon (new_price minus rack_price minus taxes and fees). Critical metric for profitability analysis and pricing strategy.']
  new_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'The new fuel price per gallon that became effective with this price change event.']
  pos_sync_status varchar [note: 'Confirmation status indicating whether the price change has been successfully synchronized to the fuel center POS system and pump controllers. Critical for ensuring price accuracy at transaction time.']
  pos_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the price change was successfully synchronized to the POS system. Used to measure synchronization lag and ensure pricing integrity.']
  price_change_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'The absolute difference between the new price and prior price (new_price minus prior_price). Positive values indicate price increase, negative values indicate price decrease.']
  price_change_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the approving manager or pricing analyst explaining the rationale, context, or special circumstances for this price change.']
  price_change_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage change in price calculated as ((new_price - prior_price) / prior_price) * 100. Used for margin and competitive analysis.']
  price_sign_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the fuel center price sign was updated to display the new price.']
  price_sign_updated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the physical or digital price sign at the fuel center has been updated to reflect the new price. Required for customer transparency and regulatory compliance.']
  prior_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'The fuel price per gallon that was in effect immediately before this price change event.']
  rack_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'The wholesale terminal or rack price for this fuel grade at the time of the price change. Used for margin analysis and cost-based pricing decisions.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this price change was reviewed for compliance with state and federal fuel pricing regulations, including price gouging laws and EPA requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every fuel price change event at a fuel center, capturing the prior price, new price, fuel grade, effective timestamp, change trigger (competitive response, rack price movement, promotional, manual override), approving manager, and POS synchronization confirmation status. Provides a full audit trail for pricing compliance and margin analysis.'
}

Table fuel.fuel_transaction {
  fuel_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each fuel dispensing transaction event. Primary key for the fuel transaction record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocates transaction revenue to the cost center for expense tracking and budgeting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each fuel sale must be posted to a GL account for revenue recognition.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'FK to fuel.fuel_grade']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.household. Business justification: Needed for household‑level fuel rewards program: aggregate fuel spend per household to determine eligibility, tier status, and regulatory reporting of SNAP/EBT usage.']
  method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: REQUIRED: Link fuel sale to stored payment method for refunds, tokenization, and loyalty reward calculations.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: REQUIRED: Integrated POS transaction linking fuel sale to grocery order for combined loyalty points, sales reporting, and financial reconciliation.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reconcile each fuel sale with its POS payment record for daily financial reporting and settlement.']
  primary_fuel_associate_id varchar [note: 'Driver identifier captured during fleet card transaction. Used for driver-level fuel consumption reporting and expense allocation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Links each fuel sale to a profit center for profitability analysis.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Required for tracking which promo offer discounts a fuel transaction used, enabling redemption reporting and financial reconciliation.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.shopper. Business justification: Required for loyalty and CLTV reporting: each fuel purchase must be linked to the shopper who made it to calculate rewards, churn risk, and compliance with fuel discount regulations.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the fuel center is located. Links to the store master data.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Payment authorization code received from payment processor. Used for payment reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  carwash_bundled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a carwash service was bundled with this fuel purchase. Used for cross-sell analytics and bundled promotion tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this transaction record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction. Typically USD for US operations.']
  epa_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction requires inclusion in EPA emissions compliance reporting. Used for regulatory reporting and environmental compliance tracking.']
  federal_excise_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Federal excise tax amount collected on this fuel transaction. Required for IRS Form 720 quarterly federal excise tax reporting.']
  fleet_card_number varchar [note: 'Fleet card number used for commercial vehicle fuel purchases. Supports RFID fleet card transaction processing and fleet account reconciliation.']
  fuel_discount_cents_per_gallon decimal(6,3) [note: 'Discount amount in cents per gallon applied through loyalty program redemption. Used for loyalty-linked fuel discount reconciliation and program cost tracking.']
  gallons_dispensed decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of fuel dispensed in gallons. Primary measure for volume sales reporting, inventory depletion, and tank level management.']
  gross_sale_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total sale amount before any discounts or loyalty redemptions are applied. Calculated as gallons dispensed multiplied by unit price.']
  local_excise_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Local or municipal excise tax amount collected on this fuel transaction where applicable.']
  loyalty_card_number varchar [note: 'Customer loyalty card number used for this transaction. Links to customer loyalty profile for discount redemption and points accrual tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this transaction record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_amount_charged decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount charged to the customer after all discounts are applied. This is the revenue recognized for the transaction.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the transaction. Distinguishes between credit card, debit card, fleet card, Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT), cash, and mobile payment methods.']
  pos_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this fuel transaction to the corresponding POS system transaction record for payment reconciliation and receipt generation.']
  prepay_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this was a prepay transaction where payment was authorized before fuel dispensing. Used for fraud prevention and payment processing workflow.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Promotional campaign code applied to this transaction. Used for tracking effectiveness of fuel pricing promotions and temporary price reductions.']
  pump_number varchar [note: 'Physical pump identifier at the fuel center where fuel was dispensed. Used for equipment tracking and maintenance scheduling.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Unique receipt number printed for the customer. Used for customer service inquiries and transaction lookup.']
  state_excise_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'State excise tax amount collected on this fuel transaction. Required for state-level fuel tax reporting and remittance.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in the transaction. Includes federal, state, and local fuel excise taxes for regulatory reporting and remittance.']
  terminal_code varchar [note: 'Payment terminal identifier where the transaction was processed. Used for terminal reconciliation and equipment tracking.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of discount applied to this transaction. Calculated as fuel discount cents per gallon multiplied by gallons dispensed.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the fuel transaction in its lifecycle. Tracks whether transaction was successfully completed, voided, refunded, or failed.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel dispensing transaction occurred at the pump. Primary business event timestamp for volume sales reporting and reconciliation.']
  unit_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Price per gallon at the time of transaction. Reflects current fuel grade pricing including any temporary price reductions or promotional pricing.']
  vehicle_identification_number varchar [note: 'Vehicle identification number for fleet card transactions. Standard 17-character VIN used for fleet vehicle tracking and reporting.']
  vehicle_odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Odometer reading captured at time of fleet card transaction. Used for fleet management reporting and mileage tracking.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for every fuel dispensing event at a pump, capturing transaction date and time, pump number, fuel grade, gallons dispensed, unit price, gross sale amount, payment method (credit/debit/fleet card/EBT/cash), loyalty card number, fuel discount applied (cents-per-gallon), net amount charged, and POS transaction reference. Primary source for volume sales reporting and loyalty redemption reconciliation.'
}

Table fuel.fleet_card_account {
  fleet_card_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fleet card account. Primary key.']
  account_manager_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Account manager associate oversees fleet card accounts; required for account audit and billing responsibility reports.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_center. Business justification: Fleet card accounts are managed per fuel center; linking provides clear ownership and reporting.']
  account_close_date date [note: 'Date when the fleet card account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique account number assigned to the fleet operator for billing and identification purposes.']
  account_open_date date [note: 'Date when the fleet card account was first opened and activated for use.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fleet card account indicating whether it is operational, suspended, or closed.']
  active_cards_count varchar [note: 'Current count of active fleet cards under this account that are authorized for transactions.']
  authorized_fuel_grades varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fuel grades (regular, mid-grade, premium, diesel) that cards under this account are authorized to purchase. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|mid_grade|premium|diesel|e85|biodiesel|cng — promote to reference product]']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fleet card account contract automatically renews upon expiration.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address for invoice delivery and account correspondence.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (suite, unit, building number).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1-31) when the billing cycle closes and invoices are generated for this fleet account.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the billing address.']
  card_program_type varchar [note: 'Type of fleet card program under which this account operates (proprietary store-branded, WEX, Voyager, Mastercard Fleet, Visa Fleet, Fleet One).']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current fleet card contract or agreement expires and requires renewal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fleet card account record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit limit extended to the fleet account for fuel purchases and related transactions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this account (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  daily_transaction_limit_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of fuel transactions allowed per card per day under this account.']
  daily_volume_limit_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum total fuel volume (in gallons) that can be purchased per card per day under this account.']
  discount_rate_per_gallon decimal(5,3) [note: 'Per-gallon discount rate (in currency units) applied to fuel purchases for this fleet account.']
  discount_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier or discount level assigned to the fleet account based on volume commitments or negotiated contracts.']
  fleet_operator_name varchar [note: 'Legal or business name of the commercial fleet operator or company holding this account.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fleet card account record was last updated or modified.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent fuel transaction processed under this fleet account.']
  loyalty_program_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fleet account is linked to the retailer\'s loyalty program for additional fuel discount redemptions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, special instructions, or account-specific comments related to this fleet account.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due (e.g., Net 30, Net 15).']
  per_fill_volume_cap_gallons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum fuel volume (in gallons) allowed for a single transaction per card under this account.']
  pin_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIN is required for all card transactions under this account for enhanced security.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for account communications, billing notifications, and support.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person for this fleet account (fleet manager or account administrator).']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the fleet account contact person.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fleet cards under this account use RFID technology for contactless pump authorization.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax ID for the fleet operator, used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  total_cards_issued varchar [note: 'Total number of individual fleet cards issued under this account across all vehicles and drivers.']
  weekly_spending_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total spending allowed per card per week under this account.']

  Note: 'Master record for RFID fleet card accounts and all associated individual fleet cards used by commercial fleet customers at fuel centers. Account level: account number, fleet operator name, card program type (proprietary/WEX/Voyager/Mastercard Fleet), credit limit, billing cycle, discount tier, and account status. Card level: card number, vehicle ID or driver ID, card issue and expiration dates, PIN status, authorized fuel grades, per-fill volume cap, daily/weekly transaction limits, and card status (active/suspended/lost/expired). Serves as the SSOT for fleet card program management including account setup, card issuance lifecycle, RFID-based pump authorization, per-vehicle and per-driver fueling controls, fleet billing reconciliation, and card suspension/replacement workflows.'
}

Table fuel.fleet_card {
  fleet_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fleet card record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver or employee associated with this fleet card. Used for driver-specific cards to track fuel purchases per individual. Nullable for vehicle-specific cards.']
  fleet_card_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fleet card account that this individual card belongs to. Links to the fleet_card_account product.']
  authorized_fuel_grades varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fuel grades that this fleet card is authorized to purchase (e.g., regular, mid-grade, premium, diesel). Used to enforce fuel type restrictions at the pump.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date when the fleet card was permanently cancelled and removed from service. Nullable if the card is still active or suspended.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the fleet card was cancelled (e.g., vehicle sold, driver terminated, account closed). Nullable if card is not cancelled.']
  card_format varchar [note: 'The physical format and technology of the fleet card: magnetic stripe, chip (EMV), RFID, contactless (NFC), or hybrid (multiple technologies).']
  card_number varchar [note: 'The unique RFID fleet card number embossed or encoded on the physical card. Used for transaction authorization at fuel pumps.']
  card_replacement_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why this fleet card was issued as a replacement for a previous card. Tracks the history of card reissuance events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lost|stolen|damaged|expired|compromised|upgrade|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  card_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fleet card indicating whether it is active and authorized for use, or inactive due to suspension, loss, theft, expiration, or cancellation.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fleet card indicating its intended use: vehicle-specific, driver-specific, universal (any vehicle/driver), temporary, emergency, or contractor card.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this fleet card record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  daily_spend_limit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount that can be spent per day using this fleet card. Used for budget control and fraud prevention. Nullable if no limit is set.']
  daily_transaction_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of fuel transactions allowed per day using this fleet card. Used to detect and prevent fraudulent activity. Nullable if no limit is set.']
  day_of_week_restrictions varchar [note: 'Days of the week when this fleet card is authorized for use (e.g., Monday-Friday for weekday-only vehicles). Formatted as comma-separated day names or codes. Nullable if no day restrictions apply.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the fleet card expires and is no longer valid for transactions. Cards must be renewed or replaced before this date.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the fleet card was issued and activated for use.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this fleet card record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  loyalty_discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions using this fleet card are eligible for loyalty-linked fuel discount redemptions. True if eligible, false if excluded from loyalty programs.']
  merchant_category_restrictions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of merchant category codes (MCC) where this fleet card is authorized or restricted. Used to limit card usage to fuel purchases only or allow additional categories like maintenance.']
  odometer_prompt_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver must enter the vehicle odometer reading at the pump when using this fleet card. True if odometer entry is mandatory, false otherwise.']
  per_fill_volume_cap_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum number of gallons that can be purchased in a single transaction using this fleet card. Used to prevent fraud and enforce fueling limits. Nullable if no cap is set.']
  pin_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIN is required for transactions using this fleet card. True if PIN authentication is enabled, false if card can be used without PIN.']
  pin_last_changed_date date [note: 'The date when the PIN for this fleet card was last changed or reset. Used for security auditing and PIN rotation policy enforcement.']
  replaced_card_number varchar [note: 'The card number of the previous fleet card that this card replaces. Used to maintain card replacement history and link transactions across card generations. Nullable if this is the original card.']
  rfid_technology_type varchar [note: 'The type of RFID technology used in the fleet card: passive (no battery, powered by reader), active (battery-powered), or semi-passive (battery-assisted passive).']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date when the fleet card was suspended. Nullable if the card has never been suspended or is currently active.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the fleet card was suspended (e.g., suspected fraud, policy violation, vehicle out of service). Nullable if card is not suspended.']
  time_of_day_restrictions varchar [note: 'Time windows when this fleet card is authorized for use (e.g., 06:00-22:00 for day-shift drivers). Formatted as comma-separated time ranges. Nullable if no time restrictions apply.']
  vehicle_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle associated with this fleet card. Used for vehicle-specific cards to track fuel consumption per vehicle. Nullable for driver-specific or universal cards.']

  Note: 'Individual RFID fleet card record linked to a fleet card account, capturing card number, vehicle ID or driver ID associated with the card, card issue date, expiration date, PIN status, authorized fuel grades, per-fill volume cap, and card status (active/suspended/lost/expired). Enables per-vehicle or per-driver fueling controls and transaction attribution.'
}

Table fuel.loyalty_fuel_redemption {
  loyalty_fuel_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty fuel redemption transaction. Primary key.']
  fuel_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Point of Sale (POS) fuel transaction at the pump where the discount was applied.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program member who redeemed the fuel discount.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: REQUIRED: Tie fuel loyalty redemption to the overall order to reflect total loyalty activity in order‑level reports and customer satisfaction metrics.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external partner loyalty program (e.g., airline miles, credit card rewards) if the discount originated from a partner reward. Null if internal loyalty only.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign or offer that enabled this fuel discount. Null if not part of a specific promotion.']
  pump_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific fuel pump where the redemption was processed.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the fuel center is located and the redemption occurred.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the loyalty discount was activated or redeemed (at-pump, mobile app, kiosk, cashier-assisted).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount. Typically USD for US-based grocery retail operations.']
  discount_expiration_date date [note: 'Date by which the fuel discount offer must be redeemed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Null if no expiration.']
  discount_rate_cents_per_gallon decimal(5,2) [note: 'The discount rate applied to the fuel purchase, expressed in cents per gallon (e.g., 10 cents off per gallon).']
  fleet_card_number_masked varchar [note: 'Masked fleet card number used for the transaction (e.g., last 4 digits only). Applicable for fleet card transactions tracked via Radio Frequency Identification (RFID).']
  fuel_grade varchar [note: 'The grade or type of fuel purchased (e.g., regular, midgrade, premium, diesel) to which the discount was applied.']
  gallons_discounted decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total number of gallons of fuel to which the loyalty discount was applied during this transaction.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Loyalty program tier or membership level of the customer at the time of redemption (e.g., silver, gold, platinum). May influence discount eligibility or rate.']
  max_discount_limit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount of discount allowed per transaction based on loyalty program rules or promotional terms. Used to cap total discount.']
  max_gallons_limit decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum number of gallons eligible for discount per transaction based on loyalty program rules or promotional terms.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the fuel transaction (credit card, debit card, fleet card, mobile payment, cash). Distinct from redemption source.']
  points_balance_after varchar [note: 'Loyalty member\'s points balance immediately after this fuel discount redemption.']
  points_balance_before varchar [note: 'Loyalty member\'s points balance immediately before this fuel discount redemption.']
  points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed to obtain this fuel discount. Null if discount source is not loyalty points.']
  pos_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) terminal or pump controller that processed the redemption transaction.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the fuel redemption transaction was reconciled with the loyalty program system. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the redemption record in the loyalty program reconciliation process (reconciled with loyalty system, pending, discrepancy identified, adjusted).']
  redemption_source varchar [note: 'Source or origin of the fuel discount being redeemed (e.g., loyalty points accumulated, promotional offer, partner reward program, credit card linked discount).']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the fuel discount redemption transaction in its lifecycle (completed, pending, reversed, failed, expired).']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the loyalty fuel discount was redeemed at the pump. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for reversing or voiding the fuel discount redemption, if applicable. Null if redemption was not reversed.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of the fuel discount redeemed in this transaction (calculated as discount rate × gallons discounted).']
  transaction_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Receipt or confirmation number printed for the customer at the pump, linking the fuel purchase to the loyalty redemption.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']

  Note: 'Transactional record of loyalty-linked fuel discount redemptions at the pump, capturing redemption date and time, loyalty member identifier, fuel transaction reference, discount rate (cents-per-gallon), total gallons discounted, total discount dollar value, redemption source (loyalty points, promotional offer, partner reward), and post-redemption points balance impact. Links fuel domain to the loyalty domain for program reconciliation.'
}

Table fuel.delivery {
  delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel delivery record. Primary key for the fuel delivery lifecycle from order placement through receipt confirmation.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier delivering the fuel. May be the same as distributor or a third-party logistics provider.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel center receiving the delivery. Links to the fuel center location where fuel is being replenished.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns fuel delivery expense to the cost center responsible for inventory replenishment.']
  driver_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Needed for driver performance, safety incident, and EPA reporting; delivery driver is an associate employee.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Delivery costs are posted to a GL account for cost of goods sold.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Fuel procurement uses purchase orders; linking deliveries to orders enables spend reporting and order‑to‑receipt reconciliation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel distributor or supplier fulfilling the delivery. Links to the vendor providing the fuel.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier_site. Business justification: TRACEABILITY: Delivery manifests record the exact supplier site of origin for tax, traceability, and inventory reconciliation.']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underground storage tank receiving the fuel delivery. Links to the specific tank within the fuel center.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the fuel delivery occurred. Used for reconciliation and variance analysis against scheduled date.']
  actual_delivery_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the fuel delivery was completed. Marks the end of the physical fuel transfer and receipt confirmation.']
  actual_delivery_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the fuel delivery began. Marks the start of the physical fuel transfer process.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of lading number for the fuel shipment. Legal document issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of the fuel delivery in USD. Includes fuel product cost and may include transportation charges.']
  cost_per_gallon decimal(8,4) [note: 'Unit cost per gallon of fuel delivered in USD. Used for inventory valuation and margin analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fuel delivery record was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the delivery lifecycle tracking.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fuel delivery. Tracks the delivery from scheduling through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_transit|arrived|in_progress|completed|cancelled|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distributor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the fuel distributor or supplier company. Business name of the vendor fulfilling the delivery.']
  driver_signature_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver signature was captured electronically or on paper at delivery completion. Used for proof of delivery validation.']
  epa_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EPA emissions and environmental compliance was verified for the delivery. Tracks adherence to underground storage tank regulations.']
  estimated_gallons_ordered decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated volume of fuel ordered in gallons. Represents the planned quantity at the time of order placement.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Separate freight or transportation charge for the delivery in USD. May be itemized separately from fuel product cost.']
  fuel_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or type of fuel being delivered. Indicates octane rating for gasoline or fuel type classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular_87|midgrade_89|premium_91|premium_93|diesel|e85|e15 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gross_gallons_delivered decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross volume of fuel delivered in gallons before temperature correction. Raw measurement from the delivery truck meter.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice number for the fuel delivery. Links the delivery to accounts payable for payment processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fuel delivery record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any delivery attribute.']
  net_gallons_delivered decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net volume of fuel delivered in gallons after temperature correction to 60°F. Temperature-adjusted volume used for inventory and billing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the delivery. Captures exceptions, issues, or special circumstances during delivery execution.']
  order_confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'Confirmation reference number from the fuel order placement system. Links the delivery to the original replenishment order.']
  post_delivery_tank_level_gallons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Fuel tank level in gallons after the delivery was completed. Final measurement from Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system used for reconciliation and shrink analysis.']
  pre_delivery_tank_level_gallons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Fuel tank level in gallons before the delivery began. Baseline measurement from Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system used for reconciliation.']
  quality_test_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivered fuel passed quality testing at receipt. Used for quality assurance and compliance tracking.']
  reorder_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Method that triggered the fuel replenishment order. Indicates whether order was automated via Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system, Computer-Assisted Ordering (CAO), or manually placed.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for the fuel delivery. Used for supply planning and fuel availability management.']
  scheduled_delivery_end_time timestamp [note: 'End of the scheduled delivery time window. Defines the latest expected time for delivery completion.']
  scheduled_delivery_start_time timestamp [note: 'Beginning of the scheduled delivery time window. Precise timestamp for when the delivery is expected to begin.']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the fuel at the time of delivery in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for temperature correction calculations to determine net gallons.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'External delivery ticket number issued by the carrier or distributor. Serves as the business identifier for the delivery transaction.']
  truck_number varchar [note: 'Identification number of the delivery truck or tanker. Vehicle identifier for tracking and compliance purposes.']
  variance_gallons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Difference between estimated gallons ordered and net gallons delivered. Used for supply planning accuracy analysis and shrink detection.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between estimated and actual delivery quantity. Calculated as (variance_gallons / estimated_gallons_ordered) * 100.']

  Note: 'Complete lifecycle record of fuel replenishment from planning through delivery receipt for each fuel center. Schedule phase: scheduled delivery date/time window, fuel grade, estimated gallons ordered, distributor/carrier assignment, reorder trigger (ATG-automated CAO vs manual), order status, and confirmation reference. Delivery execution phase: actual delivery date/time, carrier/distributor name, bill of lading number, gross gallons delivered, net gallons (temperature-corrected), delivery ticket number, pre-delivery and post-delivery tank levels, driver signature confirmation, and variance from scheduled quantity. Serves as the SSOT for the entire fuel replenishment lifecycle from order placement through receipt confirmation, supporting supply planning, fuel availability management, inventory reconciliation, and shrink analysis.'
}

Table fuel.delivery_schedule {
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel delivery schedule record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for delivering the fuel shipment.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel center location receiving the delivery.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent store location associated with the fuel center.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel distributor or vendor assigned to fulfill this delivery.']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific underground storage tank at the fuel center that will receive this delivery.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the fuel delivery was completed, if applicable.']
  actual_gallons_delivered decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual volume of fuel in gallons that was delivered, recorded upon completion.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'The bill of lading number issued by the carrier for this fuel shipment, serving as a receipt and contract.']
  cancelled_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code if the delivery schedule was cancelled (e.g., supplier issue, weather, tank maintenance).']
  confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'External confirmation number or reference code provided by the distributor or carrier for this scheduled delivery.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the on-site contact person at the fuel center for coordinating the delivery.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the on-site contact person for delivery coordination.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user or automated process that created this delivery schedule record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the delivery cost (e.g., USD).']
  current_tank_level_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'The current fuel inventory level in gallons in the target tank at the time the delivery was scheduled.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions or notes for the carrier regarding site access, safety protocols, or delivery procedures.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the driver who delivered the fuel shipment.']
  epa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery meets EPA environmental and emissions compliance requirements (True/False).']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'The estimated total cost in USD for this fuel delivery, including product and transportation charges.']
  estimated_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'The estimated volume of fuel in gallons ordered for this delivery.']
  fuel_grade varchar [note: 'The grade or type of fuel scheduled for delivery (e.g., Regular, Premium, Diesel).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user or automated process that last modified this delivery schedule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery schedule record was last updated or modified.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the fuel order associated with this delivery schedule.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery schedule (e.g., Scheduled, Confirmed, In Transit, Delivered, Cancelled, Delayed).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency or priority level assigned to this delivery to prevent out-of-stock (OOS) conditions.']
  quality_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification or quality assurance reference number confirming the fuel meets EPA and industry standards.']
  reorder_threshold_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'The inventory level in gallons at which a reorder is automatically triggered to prevent stockouts.']
  reorder_trigger_type varchar [note: 'The method that triggered the reorder: ATG (Automatic Tank Gauge) automated, CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) automated, manual, or emergency.']
  rescheduled_from_date date [note: 'The original scheduled delivery date if this delivery was rescheduled from a prior date.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'The planned date on which the fuel delivery is scheduled to occur.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The end of the scheduled delivery time window, including date and time.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The beginning of the scheduled delivery time window, including date and time.']
  temperature_at_delivery decimal(5,2) [note: 'The fuel temperature in degrees Fahrenheit recorded at the time of delivery, used for volume correction calculations.']
  unit_price_per_gallon decimal(10,4) [note: 'The contracted or estimated price per gallon in USD for the fuel being delivered.']
  variance_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'The difference in gallons between the estimated and actual delivered quantities (actual minus estimated).']
  vehicle_number varchar [note: 'Identifier or license plate number of the delivery vehicle (tanker truck) used for this shipment.']

  Note: 'Master schedule of planned fuel deliveries for each fuel center, capturing scheduled delivery date and time window, fuel grade, estimated gallons ordered, distributor/carrier assignment, reorder trigger (ATG-automated CAO vs. manual), order status, and confirmation reference. Supports supply planning and ensures fuel availability to prevent out-of-stock (OOS) conditions at the pump.'
}

Table fuel.inventory_reconciliation {
  inventory_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel inventory reconciliation record. Primary key for this entity.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved or closed the reconciliation record. Links to workforce associate master data for audit trail.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fiscal period to which this reconciliation belongs. Links to fiscal calendar for financial reporting and period-over-period analysis.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the fuel center and tank are located.']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel storage tank being reconciled. Links to the fuel tank master data.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reconciliation was approved or closed. Part of audit trail for compliance and internal controls.']
  atg_physical_volume_gallons decimal(15,3) [note: 'Physical inventory volume in gallons measured by the Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system at the end of the reconciliation period. Represents actual measured inventory in the tank.']
  atg_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ATG system or device that provided the physical volume reading. Used for equipment tracking and calibration audit trails.']
  average_tank_temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average fuel temperature in the tank during the reconciliation period measured in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for temperature compensation calculations and thermal expansion adjustments.']
  closing_book_volume_gallons decimal(15,3) [note: 'Calculated book inventory volume in gallons at the end of the reconciliation period. Formula: opening_book_volume + total_deliveries - total_dispensed. Represents theoretical ending inventory.']
  cost_of_variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial impact of the inventory variance calculated by multiplying variance volume by the weighted average cost per gallon for the period. Used for shrink cost reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) impact analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost of variance amount. Typically USD for US operations.']
  delivery_count varchar [note: 'Number of fuel delivery transactions received during the reconciliation period. Used to validate delivery volume totals and identify delivery frequency patterns.']
  fuel_grade_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the fuel grade or product type stored in the tank (e.g., Regular 87, Premium 93, Diesel). Standardized fuel product classification.']
  investigation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting investigation findings, root cause analysis, corrective actions taken, and resolution details for variances requiring investigation.']
  investigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the variance requires formal investigation by operations or compliance teams. True triggers investigation workflow and root cause analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reconciliation record was last updated. Part of audit trail for change tracking and compliance.']
  leak_detection_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this reconciliation meets EPA Underground Storage Tank (UST) leak detection compliance requirements. True indicates compliance, False indicates potential leak or non-compliance requiring immediate action.']
  opening_book_volume_gallons decimal(15,3) [note: 'Book inventory volume in gallons at the start of the reconciliation period. Represents the theoretical starting inventory based on prior period closing balance.']
  reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the inventory reconciliation. Manual indicates human-performed reconciliation, automated indicates system-generated reconciliation, hybrid indicates combination of both.']
  reconciliation_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the inventory reconciliation period. Defines the close of the measurement window for this reconciliation cycle.']
  reconciliation_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the inventory reconciliation period. Defines the beginning of the measurement window for this reconciliation cycle.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inventory reconciliation record. Tracks approval workflow and investigation state.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the physical inventory reconciliation was performed and recorded. Primary business event timestamp for this reconciliation.']
  shrink_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory variance into business-defined shrink categories based on variance magnitude and direction. Used for shrink management reporting and escalation workflows.']
  temperature_compensation_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether temperature compensation was applied to volume measurements to adjust for thermal expansion/contraction of fuel. True indicates volumes are temperature-corrected to standard conditions.']
  total_deliveries_volume_gallons decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of fuel delivered to the tank during the reconciliation period in gallons. Sum of all delivery receipts for this tank and fuel grade.']
  total_dispensed_volume_gallons decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of fuel dispensed from the tank during the reconciliation period in gallons. Sum of all Point of Sale (POS) transactions for this tank and fuel grade.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of Point of Sale (POS) fuel dispensing transactions during the reconciliation period. Used to validate dispensed volume totals and assess transaction activity.']
  variance_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Inventory variance expressed as a percentage of closing book volume. Formula: (variance_volume / closing_book_volume) * 100. Used to assess materiality of variance and trigger investigation thresholds.']
  variance_threshold_exceeded_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the variance exceeds the acceptable threshold defined by business policy or EPA leak detection requirements. True indicates variance requires investigation.']
  variance_volume_gallons decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory variance in gallons between book and physical inventory. Formula: atg_physical_volume - closing_book_volume. Positive values indicate overage (gain), negative values indicate shrink (loss).']
  water_level_inches decimal(6,2) [note: 'Water level detected at the bottom of the tank measured in inches. Water accumulation can indicate tank integrity issues or contamination. Part of EPA compliance monitoring.']

  Note: 'Periodic fuel inventory reconciliation record comparing book inventory (opening stock + deliveries - sales) against physical ATG readings to compute variance (shrink/overage) per tank per fuel grade, capturing reconciliation period, opening volume, total deliveries, total dispensed volume, closing book volume, ATG physical volume, variance in gallons, variance percentage, and reconciliation status. Supports EPA leak detection compliance and shrink management.'
}

Table fuel.pump_maintenance {
  pump_maintenance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pump maintenance record. Primary key for the pump maintenance transaction.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician or associate who performed the maintenance work. May be internal staff or vendor technician.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel center location where the pump is installed.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links pump maintenance expenses to the cost center that owns the pump.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maintenance costs are recorded in a GL account for expense tracking.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Maintenance work orders are driven by specific compliance obligations (e.g., calibration, leak detection) tracked in compliance_obligation.']
  pump_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel dispenser pump that received maintenance. Links to the pump master resource.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor or service provider who performed the maintenance, if applicable. Null for internal maintenance.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance work was completed and the pump was ready for return to service.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance work actually began. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued by the calibration authority or weights and measures inspector, if calibration was performed.']
