  business_area varchar [note: 'The SAP business area classification for segment reporting, such as Retail, Pharmacy, Fuel, or eCommerce. Enables intercompany analysis by line of business.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the intercompany transaction record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for SOX compliance and financial controls.']
  document_date date [note: 'The business date of the underlying transaction or invoice that triggered the intercompany posting. May differ from posting_date due to processing lag.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the intercompany transaction amount is due for settlement between entities. Calculated based on posting_date and payment_terms.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this intercompany transaction requires elimination during financial consolidation to prevent double-counting of revenue, expenses, or assets across legal entities.']
  elimination_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the elimination process for this intercompany transaction: pending (awaiting consolidation run), eliminated (successfully removed in consolidated financials), not_required (no elimination needed), manual_review (requires finance team intervention).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction_amount to functional or group currency. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate table at posting_date.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the transaction was posted. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, used for period-end close and consolidation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the intercompany transaction was posted, following Grocery\'s fiscal calendar. Used for year-end consolidation and elimination entries.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount converted to the functional currency of the sending company code for local statutory reporting. Conversion uses the exchange rate at posting_date.']
  group_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount converted to the group reporting currency (typically USD) for consolidated financial statements. Critical for intercompany elimination at the corporate level.']
  intercompany_transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the intercompany transaction providing business context, such as \'DC to Store 1234 inventory allocation for Q1\' or \'Corporate IT shared service charge for January\'. Supports audit trail and user understanding.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID or system process that last modified the intercompany transaction record. Tracks changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the intercompany transaction record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under SOX.']
  netting_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount after offsetting reciprocal intercompany balances between sending and receiving entities. Used for intercompany settlement and cash pooling arrangements.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms for settlement of the intercompany transaction, such as Net 30, Net 60, or Due on Receipt. Governs intercompany cash flow and working capital management.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the intercompany transaction was posted to the general ledger in both sending and receiving entities. Critical for period-end close and financial consolidation timing.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code of the legal entity receiving the intercompany transaction (e.g., distribution center, shared service center, subsidiary). Four-character alphanumeric identifier per SAP FI configuration.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the intercompany transaction was successfully reconciled and matched between sending and receiving entities. Null if reconciliation is pending or unmatched.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the intercompany reconciliation process indicating whether the transaction has been confirmed and matched between sending and receiving entities: matched (both sides agree), unmatched (discrepancy exists), partially_matched (amount variance), disputed (under investigation), resolved (discrepancy corrected).']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The source document number that triggered the intercompany transaction, such as an invoice number, purchase order, transfer order, or allocation run ID. Provides audit trail back to originating transaction.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this intercompany transaction is a reversal of a previously posted transaction. Used for error correction and period-end adjustments.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The transaction_number of the original intercompany transaction that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag is True, providing audit trail for corrections.']
  sending_company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code of the legal entity originating the intercompany transaction (e.g., store, distribution center, corporate entity). Four-character alphanumeric identifier per SAP FI configuration.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the intercompany transaction was settled through cash transfer, netting, or other payment mechanism. Null if settlement is pending.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'The method used to settle the intercompany transaction: cash_transfer (actual funds moved), netting (offset against reciprocal balances), offset (applied against other intercompany transactions), accrual_only (no cash settlement, accounting entry only).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount calculated on the intercompany transaction based on applicable tax_code and jurisdiction rules. May be zero for tax-exempt intercompany transfers.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to the intercompany transaction for VAT, GST, or sales tax purposes. Some intercompany transactions may be tax-exempt depending on jurisdiction and transaction type.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of the intercompany transaction in the transaction currency, before any adjustments or eliminations. Represents the amount posted to both sending and receiving entities.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany transaction was originally denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN for North American operations).']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number for intercompany postings, typically following format IC-XXXXXXXXXX for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the cross-entity posting: cost_allocation (store-to-DC allocations), shared_service_charge (corporate overhead), intercompany_loan (financing arrangements), management_fee (corporate services), inventory_transfer (goods movement), royalty (brand/IP licensing).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID or system process that created the intercompany transaction record. Critical for SOX audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Intercompany financial transaction record capturing cross-entity postings between Grocery legal entities — store-to-DC cost allocations, shared service charges, intercompany loans, and management fee billings. Records sending and receiving company codes, transaction type, elimination flag, netting amount, and reconciliation status. Critical for financial consolidation and intercompany elimination under GAAP.'
}

Table finance.cost_allocation {
  cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Reference to the original cost_allocation_id that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Enables tracing of reversal chains for audit purposes.']
  space_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost allocation record. Primary key for the cost allocation transaction.']
  cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost element (expense category) being allocated. Cost elements represent the nature of the expense (utilities, rent, depreciation, IT services, HR services) and map to general ledger accounts.']
  primary_sender_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center from which costs are being allocated. Sender cost centers typically represent shared service functions (IT, HR, facilities, corporate overhead, distribution center operations) whose costs are distributed to benefiting units.']
  receiver_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the profit center receiving the allocated costs. Used when costs are allocated directly to profit centers for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Nullable if allocation targets cost centers only.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount allocated from the sender cost center to the receiver cost center or profit center. Represents the share of shared costs assigned to the receiver based on the allocation method and driver quantities. Positive values indicate cost transfers; negative values may represent reversals or corrections.']
  allocation_cycle_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the periodic cost allocation cycle run in SAP CO (Controlling). Examples include monthly overhead allocation, quarterly shared services distribution, or annual corporate cost assessment cycles.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs from sender to receiver objects. Percentage allocates based on proportional shares, quantity uses driver volumes (e.g., square footage, headcount), fixed uses predetermined amounts, assessment distributes full sender balance, distribution splits based on statistical key figures, direct assigns costs one-to-one, step_down cascades costs sequentially, reciprocal handles mutual service relationships. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage|quantity|fixed|assessment|distribution|direct|step_down|reciprocal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the allocation. May include explanations for manual adjustments, special allocation rules, or business justifications for allocation method changes.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sender cost center total costs allocated to this receiver. Used when allocation_method is percentage. Sum of all allocation_percentage values for a given sender and cycle should equal 100.00. Nullable if allocation method is not percentage-based.']
  allocation_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Rate per unit of driver quantity used to calculate the allocated amount. Calculated as total sender costs divided by total driver quantity across all receivers. allocated_amount = driver_quantity × allocation_rate. Nullable if allocation method is fixed or percentage.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the cost allocation record. Posted indicates the allocation has been successfully posted to CO and FI documents. Reversed indicates the allocation was reversed due to error or period-end adjustment. Pending indicates the cycle has run but posting is not yet complete. Error indicates a posting failure. Cancelled indicates the allocation was voided before posting.']
  co_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP CO document number generated when the allocation was posted. Links this allocation record to the controlling document for audit trail and drill-down analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity for which the allocation was posted. Used for multi-entity consolidation and legal entity reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'SAP controlling area representing the organizational unit for cost accounting. Controlling areas may span multiple company codes for consolidated management reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking for SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount. Typically USD for US-based grocery operations, but may vary for international subsidiaries.']
  cycle_run_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation cycle was executed in SAP CO. May differ from posting_date if the cycle was run in advance and posted later.']
  driver_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the allocation driver (statistical key figure) used to calculate the allocated amount. Examples: square footage, headcount, transaction volume, sales revenue, labor hours. Used when allocation_method is quantity-based. Nullable if allocation method is fixed or percentage.']
  driver_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the driver quantity. Examples: square_feet, headcount, transactions, dollars, hours. Provides context for interpreting driver_quantity values.']
  fi_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI document number generated when the allocation was posted to the general ledger. Links this allocation record to the financial accounting document for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the allocation was posted. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, may include special periods 13-16 for year-end adjustments.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year in which the cost allocation was executed. Aligns with the company\'s financial reporting calendar.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person or system process that last modified the allocation record. Supports SOX compliance and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking for SOX compliance and financial reporting accuracy.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the cost allocation transaction was posted to the general ledger and controlling documents. Determines the fiscal period assignment and financial statement impact.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this allocation record is a reversal of a previous allocation. True if this is a reversal transaction, False if this is an original allocation posting.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person or system process that created the allocation record. Supports SOX compliance and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'SAP CO periodic cost allocation and assessment cycle record distributing shared costs (utilities, corporate overhead, DC operating costs) from sender cost centers to receiver cost centers or profit centers. Captures allocation cycle name, fiscal period, sender/receiver objects, allocation method (percentage, quantity, fixed), allocated amount, and cycle run date. Enables accurate store-level and department-level P&L.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key.']
  concentration_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the master concentration account if this is a zero-balance account. Null for non-ZBA accounts.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Grocery legal entity that owns this bank account. Links to legal entity master for consolidation and intercompany reconciliation.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Must match legal entity name for corporate accounts.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Full bank account number. Stored in encrypted form for PCI compliance.']
  account_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose of the account: operating (AP/AR), payroll (employee payments), tax (remittances), investment (short-term cash), debt_service (loan payments), escrow (restricted funds).']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the bank account. Active: normal operations. Inactive: temporarily not in use. Closed: permanently closed. Frozen: blocked by bank or legal order. Restricted: limited transaction types allowed.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account by operational purpose: checking (operating accounts), savings (reserve accounts), money_market (short-term investment), zero_balance (subsidiary accounts swept daily), concentration (master cash pooling), payroll (dedicated payroll disbursement), lockbox (AR collection). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: checking|savings|money_market|zero_balance|concentration|payroll|lockbox — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  available_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current balance minus outstanding checks and holds. Represents funds available for immediate disbursement.']
  bank_key varchar [note: 'Country-specific bank identifier. In US: ABA routing number (9 digits). In EU: Bank Identifier Code (BIC/SWIFT). Maps to SAP bank key (BNKA-BANKL).']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the financial institution holding the account.']
  branch_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the bank branch.']
  branch_city varchar [note: 'City where the bank branch is located.']
  branch_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the bank branch location.']
  branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank branch where the account is domiciled.']
  branch_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the bank branch.']
  branch_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the bank branch location.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the bank account was closed. Null for active accounts.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP FI company code to which this bank account is assigned. Used for GL posting and financial statement consolidation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD, MXN).']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recent closing balance from the last processed bank statement. Used for daily cash position reporting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account number in the chart of accounts to which bank transactions are posted. Typically a cash or cash-equivalent account.']
  house_bank_code varchar [note: 'SAP-assigned identifier for the house bank configuration. Links to SAP FI bank master (T012).']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number for SEPA and cross-border payments in Europe and other IBAN-participating countries.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate earned on positive balances or charged on overdrafts, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.50%).']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent completed bank reconciliation. Used for SOX compliance monitoring.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bank statement received and processed for this account.']
  lockbox_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this account is a lockbox account used for automated AR collection processing. False otherwise.']
  masked_account_number varchar [note: 'Masked account number showing only last 4 digits for display purposes (e.g., XXXXXX1234).']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date the bank account was opened with the financial institution.']
  overdraft_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum negative balance allowed by the bank under overdraft protection agreement. Zero if no overdraft facility exists.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit ABA routing transit number for US domestic wire and ACH transactions.']
  signatory_authority_1 varchar [note: 'Name of the primary authorized signatory for the account. Required for SOX treasury controls and audit trail.']
  signatory_authority_2 varchar [note: 'Name of the secondary authorized signatory. Used for dual-control requirements on high-value transactions.']
  signatory_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount that can be authorized by a single signatory. Amounts above this require dual approval.']
  statement_format varchar [note: 'Electronic format in which bank statements are received: MT940 (SWIFT standard), BAI2 (US cash management), CAMT053 (ISO 20022 XML), PDF, CSV.']
  statement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often bank statements are issued: daily (for high-volume accounts), weekly, monthly.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'Bank Identifier Code (BIC) for international wire transfers. 8 or 11 character ISO 9362 code.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was last modified.']
  zero_balance_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this is a zero-balance account that is automatically swept to/from a concentration account daily. False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Bank account master and electronic bank statement record for all Grocery operating, payroll, concentration, and zero-balance accounts across legal entities. Captures account master data (bank name, masked account number, routing number, account type, currency, SAP house bank ID, signatory authorities) and full statement lifecycle (MT940/BAI2 imports, statement date/sequence, opening/closing balances, individual transaction lines — deposits/withdrawals/fees/lockbox items, bank transaction codes, value date, clearing status against open AR/AP items). Supports cash management, AP payment run bank selection, AR lockbox processing, daily cash position reporting, bank reconciliation, and SOX treasury controls.'
}

Table finance.sox_control {
  sox_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SOX internal control record. Primary key for the sox_control data product.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: SOX controls are defined per GL account; adding gl_account_id links control to the account it governs.']
  primary_sox_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate responsible for the design, execution, and monitoring of this control. Typically a manager or director in Finance, Operations, or IT.']
  sox_performer_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who actually executes the control activity. May differ from the control owner (e.g., staff accountant performs, manager owns).']
  assertion_category varchar [note: 'The financial statement assertion this control addresses per audit standards: existence (transactions occurred), completeness (all transactions recorded), accuracy (amounts correct), valuation (proper measurement), rights and obligations (entity owns/owes), presentation and disclosure (proper classification and disclosure).']
  auditor_reliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external auditors plan to rely on this control for their financial statement audit, which requires more extensive testing and documentation.']
  business_process varchar [note: 'The core business process or cycle this control supports (e.g., \'Revenue Recognition\', \'Inventory Valuation\', \'Accounts Payable\', \'Payroll Processing\', \'Financial Close\', \'Fixed Assets\').']
  company_level_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a company-level (entity-level) control that operates across the entire organization (e.g., code of conduct, whistleblower hotline, board oversight) versus a process-level control.']
  compensating_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this control serves as a compensating control to mitigate risk when a primary control is deficient or not operating effectively.']
  control_activity_detail varchar [note: 'Step-by-step procedures or specific actions performed as part of the control execution (e.g., \'Compare system-generated inventory report to physical count sheets; investigate variances exceeding $5,000 or 2%; document resolution in variance log\').']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing how the control is performed, what evidence is reviewed, what thresholds or criteria are applied, and what actions are taken when exceptions are identified.']
  control_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is performed or executed (e.g., daily reconciliations, monthly close reviews, quarterly management certifications, event-driven approvals). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annually|event_driven|continuous — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  control_nature varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the control is performed manually by personnel, fully automated by system, or a hybrid where manual steps depend on IT-generated reports or system configurations.']
  control_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique control identifier used in audit documentation and control testing workpapers. Typically follows a pattern like FIN-001, INV-025, REV-100.']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'The specific financial reporting assertion or business objective this control is designed to achieve (e.g., \'Ensure completeness and accuracy of accounts payable balances\', \'Prevent unauthorized inventory adjustments\').']
  control_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the control owner for reporting and audit trail purposes.']
  control_performer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the associate who performs the control for audit documentation and segregation of duties analysis.']
  control_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the control activity (e.g., \'Monthly Bank Reconciliation Review\', \'Inventory Valuation Approval\', \'Revenue Recognition Cut-off Verification\').']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of control based on its timing and purpose: preventive (stops errors before they occur), detective (identifies errors after occurrence), or corrective (remediates identified errors).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this control record was first created in the SOX control repository system.']
  deficiency_classification varchar [note: 'Severity classification of any identified control deficiency: none (control operating effectively), control deficiency (minor issue not rising to significant deficiency), significant deficiency (important enough to merit attention by those charged with governance), material weakness (reasonable possibility of material misstatement not prevented or detected).']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the control deficiency identified during testing, including root cause analysis, impact assessment, and specific instances where the control failed to operate as designed.']
  deficiency_identified_date date [note: 'Date when the control deficiency was first identified through testing, self-assessment, or audit procedures.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this control became effective and operational. Used for tracking control lifecycle and determining when testing should begin.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this control was retired, superseded, or no longer applicable. Null for active controls.']
  evidence_of_performance varchar [note: 'Description of the documentation or artifacts that demonstrate the control was performed (e.g., \'Signed reconciliation workpaper with preparer and reviewer signatures and dates\', \'System-generated approval workflow log\', \'Email confirmation from manager\').']
  financial_statement_line_item varchar [note: 'The specific financial statement account or line item this control impacts (e.g., \'Revenue\', \'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)\', \'Inventory\', \'Accounts Receivable\', \'Accrued Liabilities\').']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which this control record and testing results apply. SOX compliance is assessed annually.']
  it_general_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an IT general control (ITGC) covering system access, change management, backup/recovery, or operations versus an application control embedded in business processes.']
  key_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a key control that external auditors rely upon for their financial statement audit opinion. Key controls are subject to more rigorous testing and documentation requirements.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this control record. Critical for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this control record was most recently updated, including changes to control design, ownership, testing results, or deficiency status.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date when the control was most recently tested by internal audit, external auditors, or control owners as part of ongoing monitoring or annual SOX compliance testing.']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next control test based on control frequency and testing plan. Used for test scheduling and compliance monitoring.']
  remediation_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when remediation activities were completed and the control was redesigned or enhanced to address the identified deficiency.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed action plan to address and remediate the identified control deficiency, including specific steps, responsible parties, resources required, and interim compensating controls.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation efforts: not started (plan approved but work not begun), in progress (remediation underway), completed (remediation actions finished), validated (control retested and operating effectively), closed (deficiency fully resolved and documented).']
  remediation_target_date date [note: 'Target completion date for remediation activities. Material weaknesses typically require remediation within the fiscal year to avoid adverse audit opinion.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the inherent risk level associated with the business process or account balance this control mitigates. Critical and high-risk controls receive more frequent testing.']
  sec_disclosure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deficiencies in this control must be disclosed in SEC filings (Form 10-K, Form 10-Q) per SOX Section 302 and 404 requirements. Typically true for material weaknesses and significant deficiencies.']
  sox_control_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the control: active (in operation and being tested), inactive (temporarily suspended), under review (being evaluated for design effectiveness), retired (no longer in use).']
  test_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of control instances or transactions selected for testing during the most recent test cycle. Sample size is determined based on control frequency and risk rating.']
  testing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of control testing for the fiscal period: not tested (testing not yet started), in progress (testing underway), passed (no deficiencies identified), failed (deficiency identified), not applicable (control not in scope for current period).']

  Note: 'SOX internal control master record documenting financial reporting controls across Grocery\'s key business processes — revenue recognition, inventory valuation, AP/AR, payroll, and financial close. Captures control ID, control objective, control type (preventive/detective), frequency, control owner, testing status, deficiency classification (significant deficiency, material weakness), and remediation plan. Supports external auditor reliance and SEC disclosure requirements.'
}

Table finance.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_run']
  reversed_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_run (reversed_payment_run_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or counterparty receiving the payments.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual completion time when the payment run finished processing.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual start time when the payment run began processing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run was approved for execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who approved the payment run.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events for the payment run (e.g., status changes).']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit responsible for the payment run.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code associated with the payment run for internal accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or notes about the payment run.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system reference identifier for the payment run (e.g., SAP document number).']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment run was generated automatically by the system.']
  number_of_transactions varchar [note: 'Count of individual payment transactions included in this run.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment run was initiated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to execute payments in this run.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the payment run as assigned by the finance system.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Category of payments processed in this run.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for the payment run execution.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment run.']
  total_fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees (e.g., processing fees) for the payment run.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount of all payments before deductions.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after taxes and fees.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the payments in the run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment run record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the payment run record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

Table finance.cost_element {
  cost_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cost_element']
  parent_cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cost_element (parent_cost_element_id)']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs from this element to cost objects.']
  category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of cost elements for reporting.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to identify the cost element in financial systems.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center primarily associated with this cost element.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost element record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the default rate.']
  default_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Default monetary rate (per unit) associated with the cost element.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cost element purpose and usage.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the cost element becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the cost element is no longer effective; null if indefinite.']
  gl_account_number varchar [note: 'GL account number linked to this cost element for posting.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost element is deprecated and should not be used for new transactions.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost element.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost element.']
  tax_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates if taxes are applicable to this cost element.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default tax rate percentage applied to this cost element when tax_applicable is true.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category indicating the nature of the cost element.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost element record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cost_element. Referenced by cost_element_id.'
}

Table fulfillment.wave {
  wave_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wave planning record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse supervisor responsible for overseeing this wave execution. Used for accountability and escalation.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to ship orders from this wave. Used for carrier coordination and last-mile delivery dispatch.']
  created_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created this wave record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility (DC, MFC, or store backroom) where this wave is executed.']
  modified_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this wave record. Used for audit trail and change management.']
  promo_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_calendar. Business justification: Wave scheduling uses the promotional calendar to align wave start/end dates with campaign calendars.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotion‑driven wave planning report requires linking each wave to the active promo_campaign to allocate inventory.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery route assigned to this wave. Used for route optimization and last-mile delivery planning.']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.sla_policy. Business justification: Associating a wave with its SLA policy provides clear policy enforcement per wave and introduces needed inbound relationship for sla_policy.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when wave picking was completed. Used for cycle time analysis and SLA compliance measurement.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when wave picking began. Used for performance analysis and variance tracking against planned start time.']
  cancelled_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of order lines cancelled during wave execution due to inventory issues, quality problems, or customer cancellations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wave record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and wave planning analysis.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Primary material handling equipment type used for this wave: pallet jack, forklift, order picker, reach truck, cart, or automated system (AS/RS, AMR).']
  facility_zone varchar [note: 'Physical zone or area within the facility where this wave is executed (e.g., ambient zone, refrigerated zone, freezer zone, backroom section).']
  fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of order lines successfully picked and fulfilled in this wave. Calculated as (lines picked / total lines) × 100. Key metric for inventory availability and fulfillment performance.']
  is_cold_chain_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this wave maintained required temperature controls throughout execution per HACCP standards. True if compliant, False if temperature excursions occurred.']
  is_rush_wave boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is an expedited rush wave requiring immediate processing outside of normal wave planning cycles. True for rush waves, False for standard waves.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wave record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments about this wave execution, including special handling instructions, issues encountered, or process deviations.']
  pick_method varchar [note: 'Picking methodology used for this wave: discrete (single order at a time), batch (multiple orders picked together), zone (pickers assigned to zones), wave (coordinated multi-zone), or cluster (cart-based multi-order).']
  picker_count varchar [note: 'Number of warehouse pickers assigned to execute this wave. Used for labor productivity analysis and staffing optimization.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when wave picking is planned to be completed. Used for SLA tracking and downstream process coordination.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when wave picking is planned to begin. Used for labor scheduling and capacity planning.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this wave for resource allocation and sequencing. Urgent waves are processed first, followed by high, normal, and low priority waves.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the wave was released to the warehouse floor for execution. Marks the transition from planned to active status.']
  short_pick_count varchar [note: 'Number of order lines that could not be fully picked due to insufficient inventory (out-of-stock or OOS). Used for inventory accuracy and replenishment analysis.']
  sla_cutoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Service level agreement deadline by which this wave must be completed to meet customer delivery commitments. Used for on-time performance tracking.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for this wave: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (cold chain 0-4°C), frozen (below 0°C), or multi-temp (mixed temperature requirements). Critical for cold chain integrity tracking.']
  total_lines varchar [note: 'Total number of order lines (SKU-level picks) across all orders in this wave. Used for pick task generation and labor allocation.']
  total_orders varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct orders included in this wave. Used for batch size optimization and order fulfillment tracking.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total number of individual units (eaches) to be picked in this wave across all orders and SKUs. Used for workload estimation and productivity tracking.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume in cubic meters of all items to be picked in this wave. Used for container and vehicle capacity planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight in kilograms of all items to be picked in this wave. Used for equipment planning (forklifts, pallet jacks) and carrier capacity allocation.']
  wave_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing wave identifier used for operational tracking and communication. Externally-known unique code for this wave.']
  wave_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wave: planned (created but not released), released (active for picking), in-progress (picking underway), completed (all tasks finished), cancelled (aborted), or on-hold (temporarily suspended).']
  wave_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the wave based on fulfillment purpose: replenishment (store restocking), ecommerce (online order fulfillment), DSD (Direct Store Delivery), store transfer, bulk pick, or cross-dock operations.']
  wms_wave_identifier varchar [note: 'Native wave identifier from the source WMS system (Manhattan Associates, SAP WM, or other). Used for system integration and cross-reference.']

  Note: 'Wave planning record representing a batch of pick tasks grouped for coordinated execution within a DC, MFC, or store backroom. Captures wave number, planned start/end times, wave type (replenishment, e-commerce, DSD), facility zone, total units, total orders, wave status (planned, released, in-progress, completed, cancelled), priority tier, and WMS wave identifier. Waves are the primary unit of work orchestration in fulfillment — all picking activity flows through wave release.'
}

Table fulfillment.wms_task {
  wms_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wms_task']
  assigned_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse associate assigned to execute this task. Used for labor tracking and productivity analysis.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center, micro-fulfillment center, or store backroom where this task is executed.']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.planogram. Business justification: PICK TASK PLANNING: WMS pick tasks use the store\'s planogram to locate items; linking enables task generation and audit of planogram adherence.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Traceability reports link each pick task to the underlying product item for allergen, temperature, and regulatory compliance.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or replenishment order that originated this task. Links warehouse execution back to order fulfillment.']
  product_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Needed for Pick Task Detail Report; ties each WMS pick task to the exact order line it fulfills.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Pick task execution requires the exact SKU to locate inventory; the WMS picking process uses SKU for routing and allocation.']
  slot_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.slot_location. Business justification: Linking each WMS task to its exact slot location enables precise location tracking and removes redundant location attributes from wms_task.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Pick task must reference the exact stock position to locate items for picking, required for the \'Pick Execution\' process and inventory accuracy reports.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wave planning batch that grouped this task with other tasks for optimized execution. Wave planning consolidates orders by shipping window, carrier, or zone.']
  actual_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity actually picked, put away, or counted by the warehouse associate. Variance from target quantity triggers exception handling.']
  cold_chain_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task involves temperature-controlled inventory requiring cold chain integrity monitoring. True for frozen, refrigerated, or temperature-sensitive pharmaceutical products.']
  cycle_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from task start to completion. Key performance indicator for labor productivity and process efficiency analysis.']
  damage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether damaged goods were encountered during task execution. True when product damage was identified, triggering shrink recording and disposition workflows.']
  equipment_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the material handling equipment used for task execution, such as forklift, pallet jack, or RF scanner device.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for task exception or variance. Examples include location full, damaged goods, wrong item, out of stock, quantity mismatch, or label discrepancy.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the exception or variance encountered during task execution. Provides context for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product should not be sold or consumed. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) putaway and picking logic to minimize shrink from spoilage.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer batch or lot identifier for traceability and recall management. Critical for perishable goods and regulatory compliance.']
  pick_method varchar [note: 'Strategy used for pick task execution. Single pick processes one order at a time; batch pick consolidates multiple orders; cluster pick uses carts for multiple orders simultaneously; zone pick assigns associates to specific warehouse zones; wave pick groups tasks by time window.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for task execution sequencing, with lower numbers indicating higher priority. Used by WMS to optimize task assignment and ensure time-sensitive orders are processed first.']
  putaway_strategy varchar [note: 'Logic used to determine target location for putaway tasks. Directed assigns specific slots; random uses any available location; FIFO (First In First Out) prioritizes oldest inventory; LIFO (Last In First Out) prioritizes newest; FEFO (First Expired First Out) prioritizes by expiration date; fixed location assigns dedicated slots per SKU.']
  sla_target_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Target timestamp by which the task must be completed to meet service level commitments. Used for on-time fulfillment tracking and carrier cutoff management.']
  source_location varchar [note: 'Origin location for putaway and replenishment tasks. For putaway, this is the receiving dock door or staging area. For replenishment, this is the reserve storage location.']
  substituted_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the substitute product picked when the original item was out of stock. Used for order fulfillment accuracy tracking and customer communication.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a product substitution was made during picking due to out-of-stock conditions. True when a substitute item was picked instead of the originally requested SKU.']
  target_location varchar [note: 'Destination location for putaway and replenishment tasks. For putaway, this is the assigned storage slot. For replenishment, this is the forward pick location.']
  target_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Expected quantity to be picked, put away, or counted as specified by the WMS. Measured in the item\'s unit of measure.']
  task_assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task was assigned to a warehouse associate. Used to measure queue time and assignment efficiency.']
  task_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task was successfully completed by the associate. Used for productivity measurement and SLA compliance tracking.']
  task_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the WMS task was generated by the system. Marks the beginning of the task lifecycle for cycle time measurement.']
  task_method varchar [note: 'Technology or interface used by the associate to receive and complete the task. Voice uses voice-directed picking; RF scanner uses handheld terminals; paper uses printed pick lists; mobile app uses smartphone applications; automated uses robotics or conveyor systems.']
  task_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the associate began actively working on the task. Marks the transition from assigned to in-progress status.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the WMS task. Pending tasks await assignment; assigned tasks have been allocated to an associate; in-progress tasks are actively being worked; completed tasks are finished; cancelled tasks were aborted; exception tasks encountered issues; suspended tasks are temporarily paused. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|assigned|in_progress|completed|cancelled|exception|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warehouse task indicating the primary operation to be performed. Pick tasks retrieve inventory for orders; putaway tasks store received goods; replenishment tasks move inventory to forward pick locations; cycle count tasks verify inventory accuracy; cross-dock tasks transfer goods without storage; returns processing tasks handle customer returns.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the task location and product. Ambient for room temperature; refrigerated for 32-40°F; frozen for below 0°F; pharmacy controlled for prescription medications requiring specific temperature ranges.']
  travel_distance_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled by the associate in feet during task execution. Used for warehouse layout optimization and labor standards development.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the quantity is expressed. Each represents individual items; case represents manufacturer cases; pallet represents full pallet quantities; inner pack represents intermediate packaging; display unit represents shelf-ready packaging.']
  wms_task_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the WMS task, used for tracking and reference across systems and by warehouse associates.']

  Note: 'Unified warehouse management system task representing any directed work assignment within a DC, MFC, or store backroom — including picking, putaway, replenishment, and cycle count tasks. For pick tasks: tracks task type (single, batch, cluster, zone), assigned associate, pick location (aisle, bay, level, bin), target quantity, actual quantity picked, pick method (voice, RF, paper), start and completion timestamps, exception reason (OOS, damaged, substituted), and WMS task ID. For putaway tasks: tracks task type (directed, random, cross-dock), source location (dock door, staging area), target slot location, SKU/UPC, quantity, lot number, expiration date, putaway method (FIFO, LIFO, FEFO), assigned associate, and exception code (location full, damaged goods, wrong item). All task types share common attributes: task status, priority, wave reference, facility zone, and completion metrics.'
}

Table fulfillment.pack_task {
  pack_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pack task record. Primary key.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center, micro-fulfillment center, or store backroom where packing occurred. Used for location-based performance analysis.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order being packed. Links to the originating order for traceability and fulfillment tracking.']
  primary_packer_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse associate who performed the packing task. Used for labor tracking and productivity analysis.']
  product_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Supports Packing Accuracy Audit by linking each pack task to the specific order line being packed.']
  tote_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tote or container used during picking that is now being packed. Tracks the physical container through the fulfillment workflow.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment wave that generated this pack task. Links to the wave planning and batch orchestration process.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the shipping carrier assigned to transport this packed order. Links to carrier master data for routing and delivery management.']
  cold_chain_flag varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the packed items. Critical for maintaining food safety and quality during fulfillment and delivery.']
  container_dimensions varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions of the packed container in format length x width x height (cm). Used for carrier selection and vehicle load optimization.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of container or packaging used for packing the order items. Determines handling requirements and shipping method compatibility.']
  container_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the packed container in kilograms including items and packaging. Used for carrier selection, freight calculation, and load planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pack task record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  gift_message_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a gift message was included in this packed order. Requires special handling during packing process.']
  label_print_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipping label was printed. Used for tracking label generation and shipment preparation timing.']
  label_printed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipping label was successfully printed for this pack task. Required for shipment readiness verification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pack task record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  pack_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds taken to complete the pack task. Key performance indicator for labor efficiency and process optimization.']
  pack_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing task was completed. Used to calculate pack duration and throughput metrics.']
  pack_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or issue encountered during packing (e.g., damaged item, missing item, wrong item). Used for exception management and root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: damaged_item|missing_item|wrong_item|quantity_mismatch|container_shortage|label_error|system_error — promote to reference product]']
  pack_exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the pack exception or special handling instructions. Provides context for exception resolution and quality improvement.']
  pack_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing task was started by the associate. Used for labor productivity analysis and SLA tracking.']
  pack_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical pack station or workstation where the packing activity occurred. Used for capacity planning and performance tracking.']
  pack_task_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the pack task. Used for operational tracking and communication.']
  pack_task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pack task. Tracks progression through the packing workflow from assignment to completion or exception handling.']
  packed_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual items packed into the container for this task. Used for quality control verification and productivity metrics.']
  packed_unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of units (quantity) packed across all items. Distinguishes between line count and total quantity for bulk items.']
  qc_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality control inspection for this pack task. Indicates whether the packed order passed quality verification before shipment.']
  qc_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality control check was performed. Used for compliance reporting and process timing analysis.']
  service_level_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the shipping service level (e.g., same-day, next-day, standard). Determines delivery speed and cost.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pack task was completed within the SLA target time. Key metric for operational performance and customer satisfaction.']
  sla_target_pack_time timestamp [note: 'Target completion timestamp for this pack task based on order SLA and delivery commitments. Used for on-time performance tracking.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any special handling requirements for this pack task (e.g., fragile, hazmat, gift wrap, alcohol verification). Ensures proper handling and compliance.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any items in this pack task were substituted due to out-of-stock conditions. Impacts customer satisfaction and requires communication.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the packed shipment. Enables end-to-end shipment visibility and customer communication.']

  Note: 'Pack task record capturing the packing of picked items into containers or bags for a specific order or tote. Tracks pack station ID, packer associate, container type (bag, box, tote, cooler bag), cold chain flag (ambient, refrigerated, frozen), packed item count, pack start and end timestamps, label printed flag, pack exception codes, and QC check status. Linked to the originating wave and order.'
}

Table fulfillment.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver assigned to deliver this shipment for last-mile delivery or internal fleet operations.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to fulfillment.carrier']
  destination_location_fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility or customer address to which the shipment is being delivered.']
  food_safety_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_violation. Business justification: Food safety incident reporting requires each shipment that triggers a violation to be linked to the violation record for traceability, recall, and regulatory reporting.']
  fulfillment_delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the optimized delivery route that includes this shipment, used for last-mile delivery planning and driver dispatch.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating order that this shipment fulfills. Links shipment to customer order or replenishment order.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Shipment cost allocation and reporting need the promo_campaign that generated the shipment for financial reconciliation.']
  shipment_fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility (DC, MFC, or store) from which the shipment originates.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Needed for Store Receiving and Inventory Reconciliation report, associating each inbound shipment with the destination store for receiving, inventory updates, and compliance reporting.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle (truck, van, or delivery vehicle) used to transport this shipment.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse wave planning batch that this shipment was assigned to for picking and packing optimization.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment was successfully delivered to the destination and accepted by the recipient.']
  actual_ship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment departed the origin facility and was handed to the carrier.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Legal document number issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment. Required for freight shipments.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment contains temperature-sensitive perishable goods requiring refrigerated or frozen transportation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment record was first created in the system, marking the start of the fulfillment lifecycle.']
  delivery_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of delivery attempts made by the carrier before successful delivery or final failure. Used for last-mile delivery performance analysis.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions provided by the customer or receiving location for delivery (e.g., gate code, leave at door, call upon arrival).']
  delivery_outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the delivery attempt. Delivered indicates successful handoff, failed outcomes require follow-up, returned indicates shipment sent back to origin. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: delivered|failed_no_access|failed_refused|failed_damaged|returned_to_sender|left_at_door|left_with_neighbor — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination type. Customer address for last-mile delivery, store for replenishment, DC for inter-facility transfer.']
  estimated_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Carrier-provided estimated time of arrival at the destination, updated in real-time during transit.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total transportation cost charged by the carrier for this shipment, used for freight audit and cost allocation.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and carrier certification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment record was last updated, used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  max_temperature_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for cold chain shipments. Violations trigger quality alerts and potential product rejection.']
  min_temperature_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for cold chain shipments. Violations trigger quality alerts and potential product rejection.']
  origin_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the origin facility type. DC is large regional distribution center, MFC is micro-fulfillment center for rapid local delivery, store is retail location fulfilling online orders.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual packages, cartons, or pallets included in this shipment.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Freight bill number assigned by the carrier for less-than-truckload (LTL) shipments, used for tracking and billing.']
  proof_of_delivery_photo_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a photo of the delivered shipment was captured at the delivery location as proof of delivery.']
  proof_of_delivery_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer signature was captured at delivery as proof of receipt.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned delivery date committed to the customer or receiving location, used for SLA tracking.']
  scheduled_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date when the shipment is scheduled to leave the origin facility, based on wave planning and carrier pickup schedule.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the shipment, used for tracking and customer communication.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment in the fulfillment workflow. Tracks progression from creation through final delivery or exception resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|wave_assigned|picked|packed|staged|loaded|in_transit|out_for_delivery|delivered|failed|returned|cancelled — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment based on its purpose and destination type. Last-mile delivery serves end customers, store replenishment moves inventory from DC to stores, inter-DC transfer moves goods between distribution centers, DSD is direct vendor delivery to stores.']
  stop_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Order of this delivery stop within the assigned route. Used for route optimization and driver navigation.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature control zone for the shipment. Ambient is room temperature, refrigerated is 32-40°F, frozen is 0-32°F, deep frozen is below 0°F.']
  total_volume_cubic_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment in cubic feet, used for truck loading optimization and dimensional weight calculations.']
  total_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total physical weight of the shipment in pounds, including all items and packaging materials. Used for carrier rating and capacity planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking identifier used for shipment visibility and customer tracking queries.']

  Note: 'Outbound shipment record representing the complete lifecycle of a physical dispatch of goods from a DC, MFC, or store to a customer or store location. Header: shipment type (last-mile, store replenishment, inter-DC transfer), carrier SCAC, tracking number, BOL, ship/delivery dates, weight, volume, cold chain requirements, temperature range, and status. Line detail: each SKU in the shipment with GTIN/UPC, quantities (ordered vs shipped), lot number, expiration date, temperature zone, FIFO flag, unit of measure, and exception codes (short-shipped, substituted, damaged). Last-mile dispatch: driver ID, vehicle ID, delivery route reference, ETA, actual delivery timestamp, delivery attempt count, outcome (delivered, failed, returned), proof-of-delivery (signature, photo, PIN), customer signature captured flag, and LMD carrier name (Instacart, DoorDash, internal fleet). Consolidates the full shipment lifecycle from creation through line-level fulfillment and last-mile delivery completion.'
}

Table fulfillment.pickup_staging {
  pickup_staging_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pickup staging record. Primary key for the pickup staging entity.']
  member_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member_offer. Business justification: Supports In‑Store Pickup Offer Redemption tracking, a business rule that records which member offer was applied at the pickup stage.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order being staged for pickup. Links to the omnichannel order that is being fulfilled via BOPIS (Buy Online Pickup In Store) or curbside pickup.']
  pickup_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store associate who staged the order for pickup. Used for performance tracking and accountability.']
  primary_pickup_handoff_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store associate who delivered the order to the customer. Used for performance tracking and customer service quality measurement.']
  rx_patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.rx_patient. Business justification: Supports in‑store prescription pickup audit: linking the staged order to the patient record for verification, HIPAA compliance, and refill eligibility checks.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the pickup order. Links to the shopper profile for loyalty and personalization.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store location where the pickup is staged. Identifies the physical retail location fulfilling the pickup order.']
  age_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer age was verified for restricted items (alcohol, tobacco, pharmacy). Required for regulatory compliance.']
  alcohol_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains alcohol and requires age verification at handoff. Ensures compliance with state and federal alcohol sales regulations.']
  check_in_method varchar [note: 'Channel or method used by the customer to notify the store of their arrival for pickup. Tracks customer preference and digital adoption.']
  cold_chain_tote_count varchar [note: 'Number of temperature-controlled totes containing perishable items (frozen, refrigerated, fresh produce). Critical for food safety and cold chain integrity compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pickup staging record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  customer_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer arrived at the store or checked in for pickup. Used to calculate customer wait time and handoff speed.']
  customer_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer signature or digital acknowledgment was captured at handoff. Used for proof of delivery and dispute resolution.']
  customer_wait_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Calculated time in minutes between customer arrival and order handoff. Key performance indicator for curbside and BOPIS service quality and NPS impact.']
  handoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was physically handed off to the customer. Marks completion of the pickup fulfillment process and is a key NPS (Net Promoter Score) driver.']
  loyalty_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is an active loyalty program member. Used for personalized service and loyalty point accrual.']
  order_ready_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was fully staged and marked ready for customer pickup. Critical SLA (Service Level Agreement) metric for BOPIS fulfillment speed.']
  parking_spot_number varchar [note: 'Designated curbside parking spot number where the customer is waiting. Used for efficient order delivery to the correct vehicle.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the pickup order. Tracks payment type for financial reconciliation and tender mix analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ebt|snap|wic|gift_card|store_credit|cash — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prescription_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order includes pharmacy prescription items. Requires special handling and HIPAA compliance for patient privacy.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the staging SLA target was achieved. Key metric for operational performance and customer experience.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for order staging completion from the time the order is assigned. Defines the service level commitment for pickup readiness.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for staging or handoff (e.g., fragile items, customer accessibility needs, contactless delivery). Ensures proper care and customer accommodation.']
  staging_bay_number varchar [note: 'Physical staging bay or holding area identifier where the order is placed for customer pickup. Used for curbside parking spot or in-store pickup zone designation.']
  staging_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Calculated time in minutes from staging start to order ready. Measures staging efficiency and associate productivity.']
  staging_exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the staging exception or issue. Provides context for operational review and customer service follow-up.']
  staging_exception_type varchar [note: 'Type of exception or issue encountered during the staging or handoff process. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  staging_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the staging associate began preparing the order for pickup. Marks the beginning of the staging workflow.']
  staging_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pickup staging process. Tracks progression from initial staging through customer handoff.']
  substitution_count varchar [note: 'Number of items in the order that were substituted due to out-of-stock (OOS) conditions. Impacts customer satisfaction and requires customer approval.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cold chain temperature requirements were maintained throughout staging. Critical for food safety and HACCP compliance.']
  tote_count varchar [note: 'Total number of totes or containers used to stage the order. Indicates order size and complexity for staging logistics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pickup staging record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes and status updates.']
  vehicle_description varchar [note: 'Description of the customer vehicle for curbside pickup identification (make, model, color). Helps associates quickly locate the customer in the parking lot.']

  Note: 'Curbside and click-and-collect pickup staging record tracking the readiness and handoff of a customer order at a store pickup location. Captures order ready timestamp, staging bay number, tote count, cold chain tote count, customer arrival timestamp, staging associate, customer check-in method (app, text, kiosk), handoff timestamp, handoff associate, and staging exception (missing item, wrong order, late staging). Pickup staging is the final fulfillment touchpoint for BOPIS/curbside orders — staging time and handoff speed directly impact customer NPS.'
}

Table fulfillment.fulfillment_order {
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fulfillment order. This is the primary key for the fulfillment domain representation of an order released for physical execution.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to deliver this fulfillment order for home delivery or ship-to-store scenarios. Maps to the carrier master data in the transportation management system.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific facility (distribution center, micro-fulfillment center, or store) responsible for executing this fulfillment order. Maps to the facility master data.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial order in the order domain that originated this fulfillment order. Links the fulfillment execution back to the customer-facing order.']
  prescription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.prescription. Business justification: Required for Prescription Order Fulfillment process: each fulfillment order must reference the originating prescription for compliance and patient tracking.']
  primary_fulfillment_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate who performed the picking operations for this fulfillment order. Used for productivity tracking and quality accountability.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Order‑level promotion attribution is required for margin analysis and rebate calculations.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Required for Store Pickup Fulfillment Order Assignment report, linking each fulfillment order to the store that fulfills the in‑store pickup, enabling inventory allocation and SLA tracking.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pick wave to which this fulfillment order has been assigned. Wave planning groups orders for efficient batch picking and resource optimization in the warehouse.']
  alcohol_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains alcoholic beverages requiring age verification at delivery and compliance with state alcohol shipping regulations. True if alcohol is present, false otherwise.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why this fulfillment order was cancelled. Customer request for customer-initiated cancellation, inventory unavailable for stockout after release, address issue for undeliverable address, payment failure for payment authorization problems, fraud detected for suspected fraudulent orders, system error for technical failures.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fulfillment order was cancelled. Applicable when the order is cancelled after release to fulfillment but before completion.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'The transportation service level selected for this fulfillment order delivery. Same-day for ultra-fast delivery, next-day for overnight service, two-day for expedited service, standard for 3-5 day ground, economy for 5-7 day low-cost delivery.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains temperature-sensitive perishable items requiring cold chain integrity throughout pick, pack, and delivery. True if cold chain handling is required, false otherwise.']
