  market_segment varchar [note: 'Geographic market segment classification for the zone, used to align pricing with local market characteristics.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the pricing analyst or system that last modified this price zone record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price zone record was last modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this price zone. Ensures periodic reassessment of zone boundaries and strategies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or context about this price zone. Used by pricing analysts for documentation.']
  pharmacy_pricing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price zone applies to pharmacy prescription pricing. True if zone governs pharmacy prices; false if zone is retail-only.']
  price_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base prices to derive zone-specific retail prices. For example, 1.0500 means 5% uplift over base.']
  price_change_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether price changes in this zone require managerial approval before execution. True if approval workflow is enforced; false if automated.']
  price_optimization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether algorithmic price optimization is active for this zone. True if AI/ML pricing algorithms are used; false if manual pricing rules apply.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'The overarching pricing strategy applied to this zone: everyday_low (EDLP), high_low (frequent promotions), premium (higher margin), discount (low margin high volume), market_match (competitive parity), or penetration (aggressive entry pricing).']
  private_label_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing approach for private-label (store-owned brand) products in this zone: standard (parity with national brands), premium (higher margin), value (aggressive discount), or mixed (varies by category).']
  promotional_frequency varchar [note: 'Typical cadence of promotional pricing events in this zone, aligned with ad circular schedules and competitive response.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the broader geographic region or division that this price zone belongs to, for hierarchical rollup and reporting.']
  snap_ebt_volume_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction volume in this zone paid via SNAP/EBT benefits. Influences pricing sensitivity and product mix decisions.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Number of stores currently assigned to this price zone. Updated as stores are added or removed.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the price zone, used in pricing systems and reports. Typically alphanumeric and unique across the enterprise.']
  zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price zone, including the rationale for grouping stores (e.g., competitive environment, demographics, cost structure).']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the price zone, such as \'Metro East\', \'Rural Southwest\', or \'High Competition Urban\'.']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price zone. Active zones are in use for pricing; inactive zones are temporarily disabled; pending zones are under review; archived zones are historical.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price zone strategy: competitive (based on competitor density), geographic (regional boundaries), demographic (customer income/profile), cost_based (distribution cost), hybrid (multiple factors), or promotional (special event-driven).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the pricing analyst or system that created this price zone record.']

  Note: 'Defines geographic or competitive pricing zones used to differentiate retail prices across store clusters. Each zone groups stores with similar competitive environments, cost structures, or market demographics. Supports zone-based pricing strategies where the same SKU carries different shelf prices in different zones. Referenced by retail_price, price_change, and competitive_price records as the geographic pricing boundary.'
}

Table pricing.price_change {
  price_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price change event. Primary key.']
  approver_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or pricing authority who approved the price change.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Price Change Impact Dashboard aggregates changes by category to assess competitive response and margin effect.']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Price changes often depend on the underlying cost basis; linking to cost_price captures the cost record driving the price change and provides a direct relationship.']
  price_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the batch or group of price changes processed together, used for bulk price updates.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the geographic or competitive price zone where this price change applies.']
  primary_price_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the buyer or category manager who initiated the price change request.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this price change to a promotional campaign if the change is promotion-driven. Nullable for non-promotional changes.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU for which the price is being changed.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific store location if this price change applies to a single store rather than a zone. Nullable for zone-level changes.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier associated with this price change, particularly for vendor-funded price reductions or cost-driven increases.']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the price change was activated and became effective in Point of Sale (POS) systems.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute difference between new price and old price (new_price minus old_price). Positive for increases, negative for decreases.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the price change request.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the price change was approved by the pricing authority.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the price change event, often used in approval workflows and audit trails.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Categorical code indicating the business reason for the price change: cost increase, competitive response, seasonal adjustment, vendor-funded promotion, markdown, or clearance.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price change: regular price adjustment, promotional pricing, temporary price reduction (TPR), permanent price change, or markdown.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel to which this price change applies: in-store, online eCommerce, mobile app, curbside pickup, or home delivery.']
  competitive_price_reference decimal(18,4) [note: 'The competitor price that influenced this change, captured from competitive intelligence systems.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor whose pricing influenced this change, used for competitive response tracking.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting compliance considerations, regulatory review outcomes, or audit trail details for FTC pricing compliance.']
  compliance_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change required Federal Trade Commission (FTC) pricing compliance review due to regulatory sensitivity (True) or was routine (False).']
  cost_change_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The change in product cost from the vendor that triggered this retail price change. Used for margin analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when the new price becomes active and is applied at Point of Sale (POS).']
  elasticity_coefficient decimal(10,6) [note: 'The price elasticity of demand coefficient used in the pricing decision, representing the expected percentage change in quantity demanded for a 1% price change.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when this price change expires and reverts or transitions to another price. Nullable for permanent changes.']
  forecasted_revenue_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'The projected change in revenue resulting from this price change, calculated from volume and price elasticity models.']
  forecasted_volume_impact decimal(18,4) [note: 'The projected change in sales volume (units) resulting from this price change, based on demand forecasting models.']
  margin_impact_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The projected impact on Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) resulting from this price change, expressed as a percentage.']
  new_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The new retail price effective after this change event.']
  old_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The previous retail price before this change event.']
  percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage change in price calculated as ((new_price - old_price) / old_price) * 100.']
  price_optimization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change was generated by an automated price optimization algorithm (True) or manually initiated (False).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system that originated this price change record (e.g., Oracle Retail Price Management, SAP Retail SD).']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the price change request was submitted into the pricing system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every approved retail price change event for a SKU in a given price zone. Captures old price, new price, effective date, expiration date, change reason code (cost increase, competitive response, seasonal, vendor-funded), initiating buyer, approval status, and batch reference. Supports FTC pricing compliance audit trails, comp-sales impact analysis, and price change velocity reporting.'
}

Table pricing.markdown {
  markdown_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the markdown event. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Markdown Planning process uses category to set markdown thresholds and evaluate sell‑through per category.']
  markdown_approved_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'The pricing team member or manager who approved this markdown. Null if not yet approved or if auto-approved by system rules.']
  markdown_associate_id varchar [note: 'The category manager who proposed or authorized this markdown event.']
  price_rule_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the automated markdown rule that triggered this event. Null if manually created.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'The pricing zone to which this markdown applies. Null if markdown is store-specific or enterprise-wide.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'The SKU subject to this markdown event.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'The store where this markdown is applied. Null if markdown applies to a zone or all stores.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Business process: Supplier‑funded markdowns (allowances) are tracked per markdown record; linking to supplier captures funding source for margin and compliance reporting.']
  actual_margin_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual gross margin impact realized from this markdown event, calculated after completion.']
  actual_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual revenue impact realized from this markdown event, calculated after completion by comparing markdown revenue to baseline revenue projection.']
  actual_units_sold varchar [note: 'The actual number of units sold during the markdown period. Populated after markdown completion for performance analysis.']
  ad_circular_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this markdown is featured in the weekly ad circular or promotional flyer (True) or is an in-store-only markdown (False).']
  amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The absolute dollar amount by which the price is reduced. Null if markdown is expressed as a percentage.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this markdown was approved by the pricing team.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason this markdown was cancelled before activation (e.g., \'inventory sold out\', \'pricing error\', \'vendor credit received\', \'competitive intelligence changed\'). Null if not cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this markdown record was first created in the system.']
  days_on_shelf_threshold varchar [note: 'The number of days an item has been on the shelf that triggered this markdown (e.g., 30 days for slow movers). Null if not applicable to this markdown type.']
  days_to_expiration_threshold varchar [note: 'The number of days remaining until product expiration that triggered this markdown for perishables. Null if not applicable.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the markdown expires and pricing reverts to the original or a new price. Null for permanent markdowns.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the markdown becomes active and the reduced price takes effect.']
  estimated_margin_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated gross margin impact (in dollars) from this markdown, calculated at planning time using COGS and markdown price.']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated revenue loss or gain from this markdown event, calculated at planning time. Negative values indicate revenue reduction; positive values indicate incremental revenue from volume lift.']
  ftc_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any FTC pricing compliance considerations for this markdown, such as comparative pricing claims, former price disclosures, or advertising restrictions.']
  inventory_units_threshold varchar [note: 'The inventory unit count threshold that triggered this markdown due to overstock. Null if not applicable.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this markdown was triggered automatically by a system rule (True) or manually created by a category manager (False).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this markdown record was last updated.']
  markdown_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the markdown event, used in reporting and communication with category managers.']
  markdown_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the markdown event: planned (proposed by category manager), approved (authorized by pricing team), active (currently in effect), completed (end date reached), cancelled (revoked before activation).']
  markdown_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the markdown: permanent (ongoing price reduction), temporary (time-bound markdown), clearance (end-of-life inventory liquidation), seasonal (seasonal rotation), or perishable (shrink reduction for fresh items nearing expiration).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the category manager or pricing analyst providing additional context, rationale, or instructions for this markdown event.']
  original_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The regular retail price (SRP) before the markdown is applied, in local currency.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the original price (e.g., 25.00 for 25% off). Null if markdown is expressed as an absolute amount.']
  planned_units_to_clear varchar [note: 'The target number of inventory units the category manager intends to sell through with this markdown. Used for clearance and overstock scenarios.']
  pos_signage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether in-store signage or shelf tags must be updated to reflect the markdown price (True) or if the price change is system-only (False).']
  price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The final retail price after the markdown is applied, in local currency.']
  reason varchar [note: 'The business reason triggering the markdown: slow_mover (low sell-through rate), overstock (excess inventory), damage (product quality issue), expiration (approaching best-by date), seasonal_rotation (end of season), discontinued (product phase-out).']
  sell_through_rate_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The sell-through rate percentage threshold that triggered this markdown (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Null if not applicable.']
  triggering_condition varchar [note: 'The business rule or threshold that triggered this markdown event (e.g., \'days on shelf > 30\', \'sell-through rate < 20%\', \'inventory units > 500\', \'days to expiration < 7\'). Free-text field capturing the logic used by the category manager or automated system.']

  Note: 'Records planned and executed markdown events for clearance, seasonal rotation, or perishable shrink reduction. Captures markdown type (permanent, temporary, clearance), markdown percentage or absolute amount, start and end dates, triggering condition (e.g., days-on-shelf threshold, sell-through rate), and store or zone scope. Distinct from TPR promotional pricing — markdowns are inventory-driven price reductions managed by category managers.'
}

Table pricing.tpr {
  tpr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the temporary price reduction record.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Temporary Price Reduction analysis reports performance by category, requiring a FK from tpr to category.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the geographic pricing zone for the TPR. Null if store‑specific.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stock keeping unit to which the TPR applies.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where the TPR is scoped. Null if the TPR is zone‑wide.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor providing funding for the TPR, if applicable.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Absolute monetary discount applied (regular_price - tpr_price).']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount relative to the regular price.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the temporary price reduction expires.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or justification of the TPR.']
  price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Shelf price of the SKU during the TPR period.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the TPR price entry: system‑generated, manually entered, or supplied by vendor.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price change; for this entity it will be \'tpr\'.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the business reason for the temporary price reduction (e.g., competitor match, inventory clearance).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the TPR record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the TPR record.']
  regular_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard shelf price of the SKU before any temporary reduction.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the temporary price reduction becomes effective.']
  tpr_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the TPR event, often used in reporting and audit trails.']
  tpr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the TPR record.']
  vendor_funded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount is subsidized by the vendor (True) or absorbed by the retailer (False).']

  Note: 'Temporary Price Reduction (TPR) record linking a SKU to a short-term shelf price reduction that is NOT tied to a formal promotion campaign. Captures the TPR price, regular price, discount amount, start and end dates, store or zone scope, and vendor funding flag (indicating whether a CPG vendor is subsidizing the reduction via a trade deal). Distinct from markdown (clearance) and from promotion-driven pricing.'
}

Table pricing.competitive_price {
  competitive_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each competitive price observation record.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Competitive price observations are zone‑specific; linking to price_zone provides a canonical zone reference and removes redundant store_zone and region columns.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific competitor store where the price was observed.']
  competitive_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the observation record.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competing retailer or chain providing the price observation.']
  confidence_score double [note: 'System‑generated confidence level (0‑1) indicating reliability of the observation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the observed price.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price observation data.']
  observation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the price (e.g., in‑store scan, manual entry, API feed).']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the competitor price was observed or recorded.']
  observed_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Price quoted by the competitor for the SKU at the observation time.']
  our_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current shelf price of the same SKU at Grocery stores.']
  plu varchar [note: 'Code used primarily for fresh produce items to identify pricing.']
  price_change_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the observed price differs from the previous observation for the same SKU and competitor.']
  price_gap decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference between competitor price and Grocery\'s price (competitor - ours).']
  price_gap_percentage double [note: 'Relative price gap expressed as a percentage of Grocery\'s price.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the observed price (e.g., regular shelf, promotional, clearance).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competitive price record was first loaded into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this competitive price record.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal SKU of the product for which the competitive price is captured.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Standardized 12‑digit barcode identifier for the product.']

  Note: 'Stores competitive intelligence price observations for key SKUs and PLUs captured from competitor stores, third-party price intelligence feeds (e.g., Numerator, Datasembly), or manual audits. Captures competitor store identifier, competitor chain name, observed price, observed date, data source, and price gap versus Grocery\'s current shelf price. Feeds competitive pricing response workflows and price optimization algorithms.'
}

Table pricing.price_rule {
  price_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing rule.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the rule.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Pricing rules are often scoped to specific categories; linking enables rule‑to‑category enforcement and audit.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Geographic or store zone identifier where the rule applies.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Pricing rules are scoped to profit centers to enable profit‑center profitability analysis and budgeting.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Price rule activation tied to promotion campaigns for automated price adjustments during campaign periods.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Price rule may be triggered by specific promo offers, enabling dynamic pricing based on offer conditions.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Business process: Supplier‑specific pricing rules (margin floors, discounts) are defined in price_rule for price optimization. Retail‑grocery pricing teams need to link each rule to its supplier.']
  applicable_category_code varchar [note: 'Category (e.g., grocery, produce) to which the rule applies.']
  applicable_department_code varchar [note: 'Department identifier (e.g., dairy, pharmacy) for rule scope.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule was approved.']
  competitive_index_cap decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed price relative to a competitive index.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective.']
  gmroi_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum GMROI value required for rule applicability.']
  is_system_rule boolean [note: 'True if the rule is system‑defined; false if user‑defined.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule.']
  margin_floor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum gross margin percentage required by the rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments or rationale for the rule.']
  price_ceiling decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable price for items governed by this rule.']
  price_ending_convention varchar [note: 'Standard price ending pattern (e.g., .99, .95).']
  price_floor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable price for items governed by this rule.']
  price_rule_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the pricing rule.']
  price_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating rule precedence; lower numbers have higher priority.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule indicating its purpose and calculation method.']

  Note: 'Business-managed pricing rules and guardrails that govern automated and manual price-setting decisions. Captures rule type (margin floor, competitive index cap, EDLP vs. Hi-Lo strategy flag, price-ending convention), applicable category or department, minimum and maximum price bounds, GMROI threshold, and rule priority. Used by price optimization algorithms and CAO systems to constrain price recommendations within acceptable business parameters.'
}

Table pricing.cost_price {
  cost_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each cost price record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Cost of Goods Sold reporting: each SKU cost record must be charged to a cost center for internal accounting.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product (SKU) to which this cost applies.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor supplying the product.']
  allowance_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cost reductions such as trade discounts, promotional allowances, or rebates.']
  base_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Core purchase price before any allowances, freight, or other adjustments.']
  buyer_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the purchasing buyer has reviewed and accepted the cost change.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Comments related to FTC pricing compliance or other regulatory considerations.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to allocate cost when multiple cost layers exist.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of the cost (e.g., raw material, packaging, duty).']
  cost_change_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the cost change request.']
  cost_change_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the cost change takes effect; mirrors effective_start_date for reporting.']
  cost_change_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the cost was adjusted (e.g., commodity index, tariff, packaging change).']
  cost_change_source varchar [note: 'Source that initiated the cost change: vendor‑initiated, internal procurement, or system‑generated.']
  cost_reference varchar [note: 'Business‑visible reference number for the cost price record.']
  cost_status varchar [note: 'Operational status indicating if the cost is active, pending approval, or archived.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost (e.g., standard, actual, replacement, forward‑buy).']
  cost_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for audit and trend analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the cost record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the currency of the cost values.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the cost is no longer applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First date the cost is valid for pricing and margin calculations.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert foreign‑currency cost components to the base reporting currency.']
  freight_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Transportation cost incurred to move the product from vendor to distribution center.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account number to which the cost is posted for financial reporting.']
  is_freight_included boolean [note: 'True if freight cost is already rolled into the total cost.']
  is_tax_included boolean [note: 'True if applicable taxes are included in the total cost.']
  landed_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total cost after adding freight, duties, taxes, and other landed‑cost items.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the cost record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the cost record.']
  margin_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost change is expected to affect gross margin reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations about the cost record.']
  original_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cost value prior to the most recent change, retained for audit trail.']
  price_optimization_flag boolean [note: 'True if this cost record should be considered by automated price‑optimization algorithms.']
  retail_price_action_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost change triggers an automatic retail price update.']
  source_document_date date [note: 'Date of the vendor invoice or contract that provided the cost data.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the vendor invoice or contract supporting the cost.']
  total_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Aggregated cost (base + allowances + freight + landed) that serves as the cost basis for GMROI and margin analysis.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the cost record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of the vendor invoice cost (COGS basis) for each SKU, including base cost, allowances, deal costs, freight, and landed cost components. Captures effective date, expiration date, vendor agreement reference, cost type (standard, actual, replacement, forward-buy), and currency code supporting multi-currency operations. Includes vendor-initiated cost change tracking with old cost, new cost, change effective date, vendor justification (commodity index, freight surcharge, tariff, packaging), buyer acknowledgment status, and resulting retail price action flag. Serves as the cost basis for margin calculation, GMROI analysis, price change justification, and gross profit reporting. Temporal versioning maintains full cost history for audit and trend analysis.'
}

Table pricing.optimization_run {
  optimization_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each price optimization execution.']
  automation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automation rule that scheduled the run.']
  constraint_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price rule set applied to constrain the optimization.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Optimization runs are evaluated against a fiscal period for budgeting, performance measurement, and variance analysis.']
  primary_optimization_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who reviewed the run\'s recommendations.']
  acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of recommended price changes that were accepted by reviewers.']
  accepted_recommendations_count varchar [note: 'Number of recommendations that were accepted by reviewers.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the run was formally approved for execution.']
  confidence_interval_high decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the confidence interval for the model\'s impact estimates.']
  confidence_interval_low decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the confidence interval for the model\'s impact estimates.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall confidence level (0‑100) assigned by the model to the run\'s recommendations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the optimization run record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency used for monetary estimates.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system(s) that supplied input data for the run.']
  estimated_margin_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected incremental gross margin (in currency) from the run\'s recommendations.']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected incremental revenue (in currency) resulting from the run\'s recommendations.']
  estimated_units_change decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected change in units sold as a result of the price recommendations.']
  estimated_velocity_change decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected change in sales velocity (units per day) due to the recommendations.']
  ftc_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes related to Federal Trade Commission pricing compliance for the run.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run was triggered automatically by a schedule.']
  model_execution_time_ms varchar [note: 'Time taken by the pricing model to compute recommendations, in milliseconds.']
  model_input_data_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the input data snapshot used for the run.']
  model_output_data_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the output data generated by the model.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the pricing algorithm used in the run.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the run.']
  overridden_recommendations_count varchar [note: 'Number of recommendations that were manually overridden.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when a recommendation is manually overridden.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary pricing strategy applied in the optimization.']
  recommendation_status varchar [note: 'Aggregated status of the run\'s SKU‑level recommendations.']
  rejected_recommendations_count varchar [note: 'Number of recommendations that were rejected by reviewers.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the reviewer during evaluation.']
  run_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the optimization run for tracking.']
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total execution time of the run measured in seconds.']
  run_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the optimization algorithm finished execution.']
  run_scope varchar [note: 'Business scope of the run – which segment of the assortment the optimization covered.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the optimization algorithm began execution.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the optimization run.']
  sku_evaluated_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct SKUs that were evaluated in this run.']
  total_sku_recommendations varchar [note: 'Total number of price recommendations generated by the run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the run record.']

  Note: 'Operational record of each price optimization algorithm execution, its configuration, and its resulting SKU-level price recommendations. Captures run date, model version, category or department scope, constraint set applied (referencing price_rule), number of SKUs evaluated, acceptance rate, estimated revenue and margin impact, and run status. Contains line-item recommendations per SKU including recommended price, current price, expected margin impact, expected unit velocity change, confidence score, recommendation status (pending, accepted, rejected, overridden), reviewer ID, and override reason. Supports governance, auditability, and performance tracking of algorithmic pricing decisions. Bridges algorithmic output with buyer review and approval workflows through the recommendation lifecycle within each run.'
}

Table pricing.pricing_recommendation {
  pricing_recommendation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each price recommendation generated by the optimization engine.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Internal user or system that reviewed and acted on the recommendation.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Price recommendation engine outputs suggestions per category for merchandising planning.']
  price_optimization_run_id varchar [note: 'Batch identifier linking the recommendation to a specific execution of the price optimization engine.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing zone (e.g., regional, market, or store cluster) that governs the recommendation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the product (SKU) to which the recommendation applies.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail location for which the price recommendation is generated.']
  algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing optimization model or rule set that generated the recommendation.']
  confidence_score double [note: 'Statistical confidence (0‑1) that the recommendation will achieve the projected outcomes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the recommendation record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  current_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Existing price of the SKU in the store/zone before the recommendation is applied.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date the recommended price is valid; after this date a new recommendation is required.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date the recommended price may be applied.']
  expected_margin_impact decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated change in gross margin percentage if the recommended price is adopted.']
  expected_unit_velocity_change decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage increase or decrease in units sold per period resulting from the recommendation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the recommendation record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or constraints associated with the recommendation.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why a recommendation was manually changed or rejected.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business driver for the recommendation (e.g., markdown, competitive match, seasonal demand, margin recovery).']
  recommendation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the recommendation in the review workflow.']
  recommendation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time the price optimization engine produced the recommendation.']
  recommended_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Proposed selling price for the SKU in the given store/zone, expressed in US dollars.']

  Note: 'Individual SKU-level price recommendation generated by the price optimization engine during a price_optimization_run. Captures recommended price, current price, expected margin impact, expected unit velocity change, confidence score, recommendation status (pending, accepted, rejected, overridden), reviewer ID, and override reason. Bridges algorithmic output with buyer review and approval workflows.'
}

Table pricing.channel_price {
  channel_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the channel price record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager who approved the price change.']
  price_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing rule or algorithm that generated the price change, if applicable.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the product SKU to which this channel price applies.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change was approved.']
  channel_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'The price charged to the customer for the SKU in the specified channel, expressed in the applicable currency.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The sales channel for which the price override is defined (e.g., in‑store, e‑commerce, curbside pickup, delivery, third‑party marketplace).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel price record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the channel price (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the channel price expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the channel price becomes active.']
  is_price_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price is an override to the default shelf price (true) or inherits the base price (false).']
  price_change_justification varchar [note: 'Free‑form text explaining the justification for the price change.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the price adjustment (e.g., delivery surcharge, marketplace fee pass‑through).']
  price_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price value was last modified (distinct from record update timestamp).']
  price_change_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the price was changed: manual entry, automated rule, or algorithmic optimization.']
  price_comment varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to the channel price.']
  price_differential decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between the channel price and the base shelf price (positive for surcharge, negative for discount).']
  price_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the channel price record.']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel price record.']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the channel price record to support change tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the channel price record.']

  Note: 'Defines channel-specific price overrides for SKUs sold through digital commerce, delivery, or pickup channels where pricing may differ from in-store shelf price. Captures channel type (in-store, eCommerce, curbside pickup, third-party delivery marketplace), channel price, currency code for multi-currency support, price differential versus shelf price, effective dates, and business justification (delivery surcharge, marketplace fee pass-through, competitive digital pricing). Supports omnichannel pricing consistency governance and regulatory compliance for price representation across channels.'
}

Table pricing.promo_price_link {
  promo_price_link_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each promotional price link record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the promotional price link.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Promotional pricing reports track promo effectiveness by category, needing a direct FK.']
  order_channel_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales channel (e.g., in‑store, online, mobile) for which the promotion applies (optional).']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the geographic or market zone for zone‑based pricing (optional).']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion that this price link is associated with.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU (product) to which the promotional price applies.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where the promotional price is effective (optional, null if not store‑specific).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the promotional price link was approved.']
  automation_rule_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing automation rule that created this promotional price link.']
  competitor_price_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier for the competitor price used in competitive pricing analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the promotional price link record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the promotional price (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount subtracted from the regular price when discount_type is \'dollar\'.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction applied to the regular price when discount_type is \'percent\'.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism of the discount (percentage off, dollar amount off, buy‑one‑get‑one, multi‑buy, or buy‑X‑get‑Y).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional price expires.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional price becomes active.']
  external_promo_price_link_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems or partners for this promotional price link.']
  ftc_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes documenting compliance with Federal Trade Commission pricing regulations.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotional price was generated automatically by pricing rules.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the promotional price link record.']
  minimum_advertised_price_flag boolean [note: 'True if the promotion must respect a manufacturer‑set minimum advertised price.']
  multi_buy_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity threshold for multi‑buy promotions (e.g., buy 3 for price of 2).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, comments, or audit notes related to the promotional price link.']
  price_change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the promotional price was created or changed.']
  price_floor decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable price for the SKU under any promotion to protect margin.']
  price_lock_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when a locked promotional price becomes editable again.']
  price_lock_flag boolean [note: 'True if the promotional price is locked from further changes until expiration.']
  price_optimization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotional price was generated by a price‑optimization algorithm.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Tier or segment classification for the promotional price (e.g., premium, economy).']
  promo_price_link_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or special conditions of the promotional price link.']
  promo_price_link_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional price link.']
  promotional_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final price of the SKU after applying the promotion, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotional price is eligible for Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program transactions.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that originated the promotional price link (e.g., ORACLE_PRICING, SAP_SD).']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be stacked with other promotions such as TPRs.']
  supersede_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional price overrides any existing price (true) or co‑exists (false).']
  tpr_flag boolean [note: 'True if the promotion is a temporary price reduction (TPR) rather than a standard promotion.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the promotional price is expressed (e.g., each, lb, oz).']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotional price is eligible for Women, Infants, and Children program transactions.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a promotional offer (from the promotion domain) to its corresponding price effect on a SKU within the pricing domain. Captures the promotional price, discount type (BOGO, percent-off, dollar-off, multi-buy), price floor, effective dates, and whether the promotional price supersedes or stacks with a TPR. Ensures the pricing domain maintains a complete view of all active price-affecting events without duplicating promotion master data.'
}

Table pricing.price_batch {
  price_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price batch record.']
  approver_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the price batch.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing zone to which the batch applies.']
  primary_price_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system user who created and submitted the price batch.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Business process: Price change batches are often driven by supplier agreements; linking batch to supplier enables audit, compliance, and attribution of price changes to contracts.']
  actual_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date‑time when the batch was executed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the batch.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the batch was approved or rejected.']
  batch_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code or number assigned to the batch for tracking and reference.']
  batch_owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the batch (e.g., Merchandising, Pricing Operations).']
  batch_source_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the originating system or process that generated the batch.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Overall processing state of the batch.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the nature of the batch such as regular update, ad circular load, emergency price change, or scheduled price update.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts in the batch.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the price changes in the batch become active in stores and channels.']
  ftc_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes related to Federal Trade Commission pricing compliance for the batch.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'True if the batch was created to address an emergency price change.']
  is_rollback_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch can be rolled back after execution.']
  is_test_batch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch is a test run and not intended for live stores.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the batch.']
  price_batch_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and scope of the price batch.']
  price_change_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual price change records contained in the batch.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the batch for processing order.']
  rollback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when a rollback of the batch was performed, if applicable.']
  scheduled_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date‑time when the batch will be executed in the pricing engine.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the batch (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Merchandising).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the batch was submitted for processing.']
  total_price_change_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary impact of all price changes in the batch.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKUs included in the batch.']

  Note: 'Operational batch record grouping multiple price changes submitted together for a scheduled price update cycle (e.g., weekly ad price load, quarterly SRP review). Captures batch submission date, effective date, submitting buyer or category manager, approval status, total SKU count, batch source system, and batch type (regular, ad circular, emergency). Supports price change workflow management and rollback capabilities.'
}

Table pricing.compliance_audit {
  compliance_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for compliance_audit']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: FTC price compliance audits must be reported per legal entity for regulatory filing and audit trails.']
  primary_compliance_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or third‑party who performed the audit.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where the audit was conducted.']
  advertised_price_match_flag boolean [note: 'True if advertised shelf price matches the regulated price.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the audit was formally approved.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the audit focus.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the audit (date component of audit_timestamp).']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the audit was performed manually or by an automated system.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the audit for external reference and tracking.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the audit.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit (e.g., store, region, chain).']
  audit_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that generated the audit (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Retail).']
  audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the compliance audit was performed.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of pricing compliance audit.']
  audit_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the audit record to support re‑audits or updates.']
  check_type varchar [note: 'Category of compliance check performed.']
  compliance_audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compliance audit.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional observations or comments from the auditor.']
  compliance_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit requires further compliance review.']
  corrective_action_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which corrective action must be completed.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action performed to remediate the identified violation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for fine_amount (e.g., USD).']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documentation or evidence files.']
  fine_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed for the violation, if any.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the violation is deemed critical to compliance risk.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or local jurisdiction code (e.g., CA, NY) where the audit applies.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority whose pricing rules are being audited.']
  related_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Reference code of the specific regulation or statute being audited.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments from the auditor or reviewer.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was completed and the issue resolved.']
  scanner_accuracy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether barcode scanner pricing matched system price (true=accurate).']
  sku_count_audited varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKUs examined during the audit.']
  unit_pricing_label_flag boolean [note: 'True if unit‑price label complies with legal requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pricing or labeling violations identified.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of each violation discovered.']
  weights_measures_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'True if weight/measure labeling passes inspection.']

  Note: 'Operational compliance record tracking pricing regulation adherence across all applicable jurisdictions for advertised prices, scanner accuracy, and price representation. Captures compliance check type (scanner accuracy audit, advertised price verification, unit pricing label check, weights-and-measures inspection), check date, store location, SKU count audited, violation count, violation description, corrective action taken, resolution date, and regulatory body reference. Supports federal, state, and local regulatory reporting including FTC pricing rules, state consumer protection statutes, and weights-and-measures audit responses. Jurisdiction-neutral design accommodates multi-state and international regulatory requirements.'
}

Table pricing.price_override {
  price_override_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each price override event.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price overrides affect revenue postings; linking to a GL account enables proper accounting of override adjustments.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS transaction to which this price override belongs.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing zone (e.g., geographic or channel zone) applicable to the transaction.']
  primary_price_authorizing_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate (cashier, manager, or self‑checkout system) who authorized the price override.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the stock keeping unit (SKU) whose price was overridden.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the override occurred.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the override was approved by a manager or system.']
  associate_role varchar [note: 'Role of the associate who performed the override.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price override record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the price values.']
  ftc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the override complies with FTC scanner‑accuracy regulations (true = compliant).']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'True if the override was entered manually by an associate rather than automatically by a system rule.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price override record.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the override line within the transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments captured at the time of override.']
  original_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Registered shelf price of the SKU before the override, expressed in the store\'s currency.']
  override_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price entered at the point of sale after the override, expressed in the store\'s currency.']
  override_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the price override was entered at the POS.']
  override_type varchar [note: 'Category describing why the price was overridden.']
  price_difference decimal(12,4) [note: 'Calculated difference between override_price and original_price (override_price - original_price).']
  price_override_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the override record.']
  quantity_override varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU affected by the price override.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the specific reason for the override (e.g., R001 for damaged goods).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the override reason.']
  shrink_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the override is linked to a shrink investigation (e.g., damaged or missing inventory).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the override (e.g., NCR POS, Oracle Retail).']

  Note: 'Records point-of-sale price overrides where a cashier, store manager, or self-checkout system applied a price different from the registered shelf price. Captures override type (manager override, price match, damaged goods, customer dispute, rain check), original price, override price, reason code, authorizing associate, store location, and POS transaction reference. Critical for FTC scanner accuracy compliance, shrink analysis, and cashier accountability.'
}

Table pricing.constraint_set {
  constraint_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for constraint_set']
  parent_constraint_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on constraint_set (parent_constraint_set_id)']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Scope level to which the constraint set applies (e.g., specific store, geographic region, sales channel, or SKU).']
  constraint_set_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used in pricing rules and external integrations.']
  constraint_set_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the constraint set.']
  constraint_set_type varchar [note: 'Category of constraint, indicating the pricing logic it governs.']
  constraint_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold or factor applied by the constraint (e.g., minimum price, discount percent).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the constraint set record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the constraint set\'s business purpose and rules.']
  effective_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the constraint set expires or is deactivated.']
  effective_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the constraint set becomes active for pricing calculations.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this constraint set is the default for its type when no specific set is assigned.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or audit information entered by data stewards.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating precedence when multiple constraint sets apply; lower numbers have higher priority.']
  rule_expression varchar [note: 'Serialized expression or script defining the logical rule of the constraint (e.g., SQL‑like or DSL).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the constraint set.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the constraint set record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for constraint_set. Referenced by constraint_set_id.'
}

Table pricing.automation_rule {
  automation_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for automation_rule']
  superseded_automation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on automation_rule (superseded_automation_rule_id)']
  action_expression varchar [note: 'Expression that defines the pricing change or action applied when the condition is true.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression evaluated to determine if the rule should fire.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule purpose, logic and scope.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the rule expires; null indicates an open‑ended rule.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the rule becomes active.']
  execution_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the rule has been executed.']
  ftc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule adheres to FTC pricing compliance requirements.']
  is_system_rule boolean [note: 'True if the rule is maintained by the pricing engine; false if defined by business users.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule was last evaluated and applied.']
  last_execution_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent rule execution.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing automation rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or operational notes about the rule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules are eligible; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of the rule (e.g., pricing, promotion, fuel).']
  rule_owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the rule\'s lifecycle.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of rule defining the pricing action it performs.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the rule definition.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'Event or condition that initiates rule evaluation.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the rule record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for automation_rule. Referenced by automation_rule_id.'
}

Table pricing.price_book {

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price book becomes effective for pricing calculations.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price book is the default for its scope.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the price book.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplemental free‑text notes or comments about the price book.']
  price_book_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or team responsible for maintaining the price book.']

  store_type varchar [note: 'Type of store or location where the price book is applicable.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_book. Referenced by price_book_id.'
}

Table assortment.category {
  category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchandise category. Primary key.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Category KPI Assignment: each category is assigned a primary KPI for performance tracking, used in dashboards and reporting.']
  parent_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent category in the hierarchy. Null for top-level departments.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead vendor designated as category captain for this category. Category captains collaborate on assortment planning, space allocation, and promotional strategy.']
  average_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Average shelf life in days for products in this category. Used for inventory planning and shrink management.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the category used across systems and reporting. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by merchandising team.']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the category scope, including product types, brands, and assortment guidelines.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the merchandise category (e.g., Yogurt, Fresh Produce, Bakery).']
  category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the category. Active categories are available for assortment planning and SKU assignment.']
  category_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the category node type within the merchandise hierarchy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this category record was first created in the system.']
  dsd_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category is eligible for direct store delivery from vendors, bypassing the distribution center.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this category was discontinued or became inactive. Null for currently active categories.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this category became active and available for assortment planning.']
  endcap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this category are eligible for end-of-aisle endcap promotional displays.']
  gmroi_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target GMROI ratio for this category. Measures gross margin dollars returned for every dollar invested in inventory. Used for category performance evaluation.']
  haccp_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category requires HACCP food safety protocols for handling and storage.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the category tree (1=Department, 2=Class, 3=Subclass, 4=Category, 5=Sub-Category).']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full path from root to this category (e.g., Grocery > Dairy > Yogurt). Used for navigation and rollup reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this category record was last updated.']
  localized_assortment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category supports localized assortment variations by store cluster based on demographics and regional preferences.']
  markdown_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing markdown strategy for clearance and slow-moving items in this category.']
  minimum_facings varchar [note: 'Minimum number of product facings (front-facing units on shelf) required for this category to maintain visual merchandising standards.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category requires USDA organic certification for included products.']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category contains perishable items requiring cold chain management and FIFO inventory practices.']
  planogram_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category requires formal planogram execution for visual merchandising and space allocation.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category includes store-owned private label brand products.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Primary replenishment method for this category: CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering), manual ordering, vendor-managed inventory, or cross-dock.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category has seasonal assortment variations (e.g., holiday baking, summer grilling, back-to-school).']
  shrink_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected inventory shrink rate percentage for this category due to theft, spoilage, or damage. Used for financial planning and loss prevention.']
  snap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this category are eligible for purchase using SNAP/EBT benefits.']
  space_allocation_square_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard space allocation norm for this category in square feet. Used for planogram planning and store layout design.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature control zone for products in this category (ambient, refrigerated, frozen, or multi-temperature).']
  wic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this category are eligible for purchase using WIC benefits.']

  Note: 'Master hierarchy of merchandise categories used for assortment planning and category management. Represents the full category tree from department down to sub-category level (e.g., Grocery > Dairy > Yogurt). Owned by the assortment domain as the SSOT for category structure. Includes category captain designation, GMROI targets, space allocation norms, and seasonal planning flags. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System item hierarchy.'
}

Table assortment.planogram {
  planogram_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the planogram. Primary key for the planogram data product.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise category this planogram represents. Links to the product category hierarchy for assortment planning and category management.']
  planogram_approved_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or category manager who approved this planogram for deployment. Supports governance and approval workflow tracking.']
  planogram_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or space planner who created this planogram. Supports accountability and workflow tracking.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store cluster or format group this planogram is designed for. Enables localized assortments by store type, size, or demographic profile.']
  subcategory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise subcategory within the broader category. Provides finer-grained classification for space allocation and assortment optimization.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planogram was approved. Captures approval event in the planogram lifecycle for audit and compliance tracking.']
  compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether strict compliance to this planogram is mandatory. True for corporate-mandated sets, false for suggested guidelines. Drives store execution accountability.']
  compliance_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance percentage from the planogram specification before a store is considered non-compliant. Used for compliance scoring and audit thresholds.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planogram record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and lifecycle tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this planogram is scheduled to be replaced or retired. Nullable for open-ended planograms. Supports lifecycle management and reset planning.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this planogram becomes active and should be implemented in stores. Supports seasonal resets and promotional timing.']
  estimated_weekly_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected weekly sales revenue in dollars for all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) in this planogram. Supports space-to-sales ratio analysis and fixture ROI evaluation.']
  estimated_weekly_sales_units varchar [note: 'Projected weekly sales volume in units for all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) in this planogram. Used for space productivity forecasting and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.']
  fixture_depth_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total depth of the fixture in inches. Constrains product depth and facing orientation for shelf placement.']
  fixture_height_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total height of the fixture in inches. Determines vertical space available for product placement and shelf allocation.']
  fixture_type varchar [note: 'Type of physical store fixture or display unit this planogram is designed for. Gondola for standard aisle shelving units, cooler for refrigerated cases, freezer for frozen food cases, pallet for floor stacks, display rack for specialty merchandisers, pegboard for hanging items, shelf for general shelving. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gondola|endcap|cooler|freezer|pallet|display_rack|pegboard|shelf — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fixture_width_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total width of the fixture in inches. Critical dimension for space planning and ensuring planogram fits physical store layout.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL (Uniform Resource Locator) or file path to the visual rendering or photograph of the planogram. Provides reference image for store teams during reset execution.']
  layout_orientation varchar [note: 'Primary orientation of product placement within the planogram. Horizontal for side-by-side shelf arrangements, vertical for top-to-bottom brand blocking, mixed for combination layouts.']
  merchandising_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary merchandising strategy applied in this planogram. Brand blocking groups products by manufacturer, price point arranges by cost tiers, size progression orders by package size, color blocking groups by visual appeal, cross merchandising combines complementary categories.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planogram record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and version control.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions for store execution teams. May include setup guidance, display requirements, or merchandising tips for implementing the planogram.']
  planogram_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the planogram, typically describing the category, fixture, or section it represents (e.g., \'Cereal Aisle Endcap Q1 2024\').']
  planogram_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the planogram, typically used in space planning systems and store communications. Externally-known unique code for this visual merchandise display plan.']
  planogram_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planogram. Draft indicates work-in-progress, approved indicates ready for deployment, active indicates currently in use in stores, inactive indicates temporarily suspended, archived indicates historical record.']
  planogram_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planogram by merchandising purpose. Standard for regular shelf sets, promotional for temporary displays, seasonal for holiday or seasonal merchandise, endcap for end-of-aisle displays, power aisle for high-traffic promotional zones, checkout for point-of-sale impulse areas.']
  private_label_facing_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total facings allocated to private label or store-owned brand products. Key metric for private label penetration and margin optimization strategy.']
  space_planning_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier or code from the source space planning system (e.g., Blue Yonder, JDA, Oracle Retail) where this planogram was designed. Supports system integration and data lineage.']
  total_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Total product capacity of the fixture in units, summing all SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) positions and depths. Represents maximum inventory that can be displayed on the fixture at full stock.']
  total_facing_count varchar [note: 'Total number of product facings (product fronts visible to shoppers) across all shelves in this planogram. Key metric for space productivity and SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) representation.']
  total_shelf_count varchar [note: 'Total number of shelves or levels within the fixture. Used for vertical space allocation and capacity planning.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) included in this planogram. Measures assortment breadth and variety for the fixture.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the planogram, incremented with each revision. Supports change tracking and rollback capabilities for space planning iterations.']

  Note: 'Visual merchandise display plan defining the exact placement, facings, and shelf positions of SKUs within a store fixture or gondola section.
