  restocking_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee deducted from the refund amount charged to the customer for restocking returned merchandise. Null if no restocking fee applied. Used for certain product categories per return policy.']
  return_to_original_tender_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund was issued back to the original payment method used in the source transaction. True if refunded to original tender, False if refunded via alternative method such as store credit.']
  store_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of store credit issued to the customer as part of this refund. Null if no store credit issued. Used when customer opts for store credit instead of original tender refund or when original payment method is unavailable.']
  store_credit_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether store credit was issued as part of this refund transaction instead of or in addition to monetary refund. True if store credit issued, False otherwise.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the refund amount representing sales tax being returned to the customer. Subset of refund_amount.']
  wic_reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this refund involves reversal of a Women Infants and Children (WIC) program payment. True for WIC refunds which require special handling per USDA regulations, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of payment refunds issued to shoppers across all channels including in-store POS returns, eCommerce order cancellations, and pharmacy prescription reversals. Captures refund_type (full, partial, exchange), refund_status (pending, approved, processed, failed), original_transaction_id reference, refund_amount, refund_tender_type, refund_reason_code, refund_reason_description, initiated_by_channel, initiated_by_associate_id, approved_by_associate_id, refund_timestamp, return_to_original_tender_flag, store_credit_issued_flag, and store_credit_amount. Sourced from NCR POS returns workflow and Salesforce Commerce Cloud order management.'
}

Table payment.gift_card {
  gift_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gift card record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who processed the gift card issuance or activation. Used for audit trail and fraud investigation.']
  primary_gift_shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who purchased the gift card. Nullable for anonymous purchases or third-party bulk orders.']
  replacement_for_gift_card_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original gift card that this card replaces, in cases of lost, stolen, or damaged cards. Nullable for original issuances.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the gift card was issued or purchased. Nullable for online or bulk purchases.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current activation lifecycle status of the gift card: pending (issued but not yet activated), activated (ready for use), suspended (temporarily blocked), or closed (permanently deactivated).']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the gift card was activated and became available for redemption. Nullable for cards that have not yet been activated.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode or UPC (Universal Product Code) printed on physical gift cards for POS scanning. Nullable for digital cards.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number for physical gift cards, used for inventory tracking and recall management. Nullable for digital cards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the gift card record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the gift card monetary value (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'The current available balance on the gift card after all redemptions, reloads, and adjustments. Updated in real-time with each transaction.']
  escheatment_date date [note: 'The date on which the gift card balance was escheated to state authorities due to dormancy. Nullable for non-escheated cards.']
  escheatment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the gift card balance with respect to state escheatment (unclaimed property) laws. Not_applicable for active cards, pending for dormant cards approaching escheatment, escheated for balances transferred to state authorities.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the gift card expires and can no longer be used for purchases. Nullable for non-expiring cards. Subject to state and federal gift card regulations.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift card has been flagged for suspected fraudulent activity. True if fraud is suspected, False otherwise.']
  fraud_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason the gift card was flagged for fraud (e.g., stolen card, unauthorized use, suspicious transaction pattern). Nullable if no fraud flag is set.']
  gift_card_type varchar [note: 'Type of gift card instrument: physical (plastic card issued in-store or mailed), digital (mobile wallet or app-based), or e-gift (email delivery).']
  initial_load_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original monetary value loaded onto the gift card at the time of issuance or first activation. Immutable after creation.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift card is currently active and available for use. False if the card has been deactivated, suspended, or closed.']
  is_expired boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift card has passed its expiration date and is no longer valid for redemption. True if expired, False if still valid.']
  is_reloadable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift card supports reloading of additional funds after initial issuance. True for reloadable cards, False for single-load cards.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date the gift card was issued or created in the system. Distinct from activation date.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the gift card record was last updated. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  last_transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent transaction (redemption, reload, or balance inquiry) on this gift card. Used for dormancy tracking and fraud detection.']
  number_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized gift card number for PCI DSS compliance. The actual card number is stored securely in the payment gateway; this token is used for transaction processing and balance inquiries.']
  personalized_message varchar [note: 'Custom message from the purchaser to the recipient, displayed on e-gift cards or included with physical card delivery. Limited to 250 characters.']
  pin_hash varchar [note: 'One-way cryptographic hash of the gift card PIN for secure validation. The actual PIN is never stored in plaintext.']
  pin_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIN is required for gift card transactions. True for cards requiring PIN entry at POS or online checkout, False for swipe-only cards.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the gift card program or campaign (e.g., Grocery Gift Card, Holiday Gift Card, Employee Appreciation Card). Used for marketing segmentation and program performance tracking.']
  purchase_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the gift card was originally purchased: POS (in-store point of sale), ecommerce (online storefront), mobile_app (mobile application), customer_service (phone or chat), bulk_order (corporate or wholesale), or third_party (external retailer or marketplace).']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the gift card recipient for e-gift delivery and balance notifications. Required for digital and e-gift card types.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the gift card recipient for SMS delivery and balance inquiry notifications. Optional field for digital card delivery.']

  Note: 'Master record for Grocery-issued gift cards and store credit instruments. Captures gift_card_number_token, gift_card_type (physical, digital, e-gift), initial_load_amount, current_balance, currency_code, issue_date, expiration_date, is_expired flag, is_active flag, is_reloadable flag, purchase_channel, activation_status, activation_timestamp, last_transaction_timestamp, and program_name (e.g., Grocery Gift Card, Holiday Gift Card). Supports gift card issuance, activation, balance inquiry, and redemption workflows across POS and eCommerce channels.'
}

Table payment.gift_card_transaction {
  gift_card_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gift card transaction record. Primary key for the gift card transaction ledger.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who processed the gift card transaction at POS. Used for audit trail and training purposes. Null for self-service channels.']
  gift_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the gift card involved in this transaction. Links to the gift card master record for balance tracking and lifecycle management.']
  original_transaction_gift_card_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original gift card transaction being reversed or voided. Null for original transactions, populated for reversals to maintain audit trail.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent payment transaction when this gift card activity is part of a larger purchase or load transaction. Null for standalone balance inquiries.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order when the gift card was used as payment tender for a purchase. Null for activations, loads, and balance inquiries.']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch in which this gift card transaction was reconciled. Used for financial close and liability reporting.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who initiated the gift card transaction. Links to customer master for loyalty integration and purchase history.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store location where the gift card transaction occurred. Links to store master for location-based reporting and liability allocation.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the gift card processing system confirming the transaction was approved. Used for dispute resolution and reconciliation.']
  balance_after decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gift card balance immediately after this transaction was applied. Represents the new available balance for future redemptions.']
  balance_before decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gift card balance immediately before this transaction was applied. Used for audit trail and balance reconciliation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the gift card transaction was initiated. Distinguishes in-store POS, online, mobile app, customer service, and self-service kiosk transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gift card transaction record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount. Typically USD for domestic operations.']
  decline_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the gift card transaction was declined. Examples include insufficient balance, expired card, inactive card, or system error. Null for approved transactions.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used for the gift card transaction. Used for fraud detection and multi-device tracking. Captured for digital channels.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the gift card balance expires per regulatory and business policy. Null for non-expiring gift cards or transactions before expiration policy was applied.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Service fee charged for the gift card transaction. Examples include activation fees, dormancy fees, or reload fees. Zero for most redemption transactions.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift card transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent by automated or manual review. True for flagged transactions requiring investigation.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned to the gift card transaction by the fraud detection system. Higher scores indicate higher risk. Used for transaction review and blocking decisions.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device from which the gift card transaction was initiated. Used for fraud detection and security analysis. Captured for ecommerce and mobile app channels.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gift card transaction record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Updated when status changes or corrections are applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the gift card transaction. Used by customer service and operations teams to document exceptions, issues, or special handling.']
  pos_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal or device where the gift card transaction was processed. Used for terminal-level reconciliation and fraud detection.']
  processor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party payment processor or gateway that handled the gift card transaction. Used for reconciliation and processor performance analysis.']
  processor_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment processor. Used for cross-system reconciliation and dispute resolution with the processor.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal of a previous gift card transaction. True for voids and refunds, false for original transactions.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the gift card transaction was settled and posted to the general ledger. May differ from transaction date for end-of-day batch processing.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax amount associated with the gift card transaction. Typically zero for gift card loads and activations, may be non-zero for redemptions depending on jurisdiction.']
  transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the gift card transaction. Positive for loads and activations, negative for redemptions and expirations, zero for balance inquiries.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique transaction number for this gift card activity. Used for customer service inquiries and reconciliation.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the gift card transaction in the authorization and settlement workflow.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the gift card transaction was initiated at the point of interaction. Business event timestamp for activity sequencing and reconciliation.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of gift card transaction activity. Activation creates new card, load adds funds, redemption deducts funds, void reverses prior transaction, balance_inquiry checks current balance, expiration removes unused balance.']

  Note: 'Transactional ledger of all gift card activity including activations, loads, redemptions, balance inquiries, and voids. Captures gift_card_id reference, transaction_type (activation, load, redemption, void, balance_inquiry, expiration), transaction_amount, balance_before, balance_after, pos_terminal_id, store_id, channel, transaction_timestamp, and associated_payment_transaction_id reference. Provides complete gift card activity history for balance reconciliation and liability reporting.'
}

Table payment.fraud_case {
  fraud_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fraud case record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loss prevention or fraud analyst assigned to investigate this case.']
  chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated chargeback record if a chargeback was filed. Null if no chargeback filed.']
  method_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment method (card, account) involved in the fraud case.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Obligation tracking: each fraud case fulfills a specific compliance obligation to investigate and remediate fraud, supporting obligation‑based reporting.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction that triggered the fraud alert. May be null if case originated from pattern analysis.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required for Fraud Case Compliance Program Reporting: links each fraud case to the compliance program governing anti‑fraud policies, enabling program‑level metrics.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or shopper account associated with the suspected fraud activity.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the fraudulent transaction occurred. Null for ecommerce or mobile transactions.']
  account_blocked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account or payment method was blocked as a result of this fraud case.']
  alert_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the fraud alert based on risk scoring and detection confidence.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Type of real-time fraud detection alert that triggered the case investigation.']
  case_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud case was closed or resolved. Null if case is still open.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique case number assigned to the fraud investigation for external reference and tracking.']
  case_opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud case was first opened and investigation initiated.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fraud case investigation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the fraudulent transaction was attempted or completed.']
  chargeback_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a chargeback was filed with the card issuer or payment processor related to this fraud case.']
  confirmed_fraud_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of confirmed fraudulent transactions after investigation completion. Null if investigation is ongoing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this fraud case record was first created in the database.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fraud amounts.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'System or channel that originated the fraud detection alert or case.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used for the transaction, generated by device fingerprinting technology.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Final determination or outcome classification of the fraud investigation.']
  disposition_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed investigator notes documenting findings, evidence reviewed, and rationale for the case disposition.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score (0-100) assigned by the fraud detection system at the time of alert generation.']
  fraud_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fraud incident based on the method or pattern detected.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the transaction location based on IP geolocation or device GPS.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the transaction location based on IP geolocation or device GPS.']
  investigation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the case based on financial impact, risk, and resource availability.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Internet Protocol address from which the suspicious transaction originated. Applicable to digital channels.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this fraud case record was last updated.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency to which the case was referred. Null if not referred.']
  law_enforcement_case_number varchar [note: 'External case number assigned by law enforcement agency. Null if not referred or not yet assigned.']
  law_enforcement_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case was referred to law enforcement authorities for criminal investigation.']
  loss_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net financial loss to the business after recovery efforts, calculated as confirmed fraud amount minus recovery amount.']
  pci_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fraud case constitutes a PCI DSS security incident requiring formal reporting to payment brands and acquirers.']
  recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount recovered from the fraudulent transaction through chargebacks, insurance claims, or other recovery mechanisms.']
  related_case_count varchar [note: 'Number of other fraud cases linked to this case through common patterns, accounts, or devices.']
  suspected_fraud_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of suspected fraudulent transactions associated with this case at the time of case opening.']

  Note: 'Operational record of confirmed or suspected payment fraud cases including real-time detection alerts that triggered investigation. Captures case lifecycle (case_number, fraud_type, case_status, opened/closed dates), financial impact (suspected and confirmed amounts), detection context (alert_type, alert_severity, triggering_transaction, detection_source), investigation details (investigator_id, disposition_notes), and law enforcement referral. Supports PCI DSS fraud management, real-time fraud intervention at POS, and loss prevention operations.'
}

Table payment.reconciliation {
  reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment reconciliation record. Primary key.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Associates each reconciliation batch with its fiscal period, enabling period‑based variance analysis and SOX control reporting.']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment gateway or processor for which this reconciliation applies. Links to payment gateway configuration.']
  reconciliation_approved_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisor or manager who approved this reconciliation and authorized exception resolution actions.']
  reconciliation_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cash office employee or finance user who performed or reviewed this reconciliation.']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'External batch identifier from the payment gateway or bank settlement report used to match transactions for this reconciliation.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location for which this reconciliation applies. Links to store master data.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation was formally approved by a supervisor or manager, authorizing financial close progression.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation process was completed and all exceptions were resolved or escalated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this reconciliation record.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual exception records identified during this reconciliation requiring detailed investigation and resolution.']
  external_report_filename varchar [note: 'Filename or reference identifier of the external bank settlement report or gateway confirmation file used as the reconciliation source.']
  external_report_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the external settlement report or gateway confirmation was received from the bank or payment processor.']
  external_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total payment amount reported by the external payment gateway, bank, or processor settlement report for this reconciliation scope.']
  external_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of payment transactions reported by the external payment gateway, bank settlement report, or processor for this reconciliation scope.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier (YYYYMM or YYYYPP format) to which this reconciliation belongs for financial reporting and close processes.']
  gl_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI General Ledger (GL) document number generated when reconciliation variances or adjustments are posted to the financial ledger.']
  gl_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the General Ledger (GL) journal entry posting in SAP FI for this reconciliation, tracking financial close integration.']
  internal_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total payment amount recorded in Grocery\'s internal systems (POS, OMS) for this reconciliation scope, representing the merchant\'s view of transactions.']
  internal_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of payment transactions recorded in Grocery\'s internal Point of Sale (POS) and Order Management System (OMS) for this reconciliation scope.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reconciliation record was last updated, tracking audit trail for changes to reconciliation status or resolution.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Overall reconciliation outcome status indicating whether internal and external totals match within acceptable tolerance levels.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting reconciliation findings, variance explanations, and resolution actions taken by cash office or finance team.']
  pci_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation record was selected for Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance audit review.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Business date for which this reconciliation was performed, representing the transaction date being reconciled.']
  reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform this reconciliation, indicating level of automation versus manual cash office review.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reconciliation process, tracking progress from initiation through exception resolution to closure.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Category of reconciliation being performed, indicating the payment processing scope and settlement method.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) internal control testing for financial reporting accuracy.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation process was initiated, either automatically by batch job or manually by cash office user.']
  tolerance_threshold_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable variance amount for this reconciliation type before escalation is required, supporting materiality-based exception management.']
  tolerance_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable variance percentage for this reconciliation type before escalation is required.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated difference between internal_total_amount and external_total_amount, representing the reconciliation discrepancy requiring investigation.']
  variance_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Calculated difference between internal_transaction_count and external_transaction_count, indicating missing or extra transactions.']

  Note: 'Daily payment reconciliation record comparing Grocery\'s internal payment transaction totals against bank settlement reports and gateway confirmations, including individual exception records for discrepancies requiring resolution. Captures reconciliation header (reconciliation_date, store_id, reconciliation_type, internal_total_amount vs external_total_amount, variance_amount, match_status) and exception details (exception_type such as missing_in_bank/missing_in_pos/amount_mismatch, exception_amount, transaction_reference, root_cause_code, resolution_status, resolved_by, resolution_notes, resolution_timestamp). Supports daily cash office reconciliation, systematic exception management, financial variance resolution, and SAP FI financial close processes.'
}

Table payment.payment_plan {
  payment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment plan record. Primary key.']
  approved_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Financing plans must be approved by a store associate; the approving employee is recorded for regulatory audit and risk management.']
  auto_pay_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment method used for automatic installment payments.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or wholesale account associated with this payment plan.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Store location where the payment plan was originated, if applicable.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the payment plan application approval process.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that approved the payment plan.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment plan was approved.']
  auto_pay_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this plan.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for payment plan cancellation, if applicable.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment plan was cancelled.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment plan was fully paid and completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this plan.']
  days_past_due varchar [note: 'Number of days the payment plan is overdue, calculated from the most recent missed payment date.']
  default_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment plan was marked as defaulted due to non-payment.']
  delinquency_status varchar [note: 'Classification of payment plan delinquency based on days past due. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current|30_days|60_days|90_days|120_plus_days|default — promote to reference product]']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total fees charged for the payment plan (origination, service, administrative).']
  final_payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the final installment payment.']
  first_payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the first installment payment.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after a missed payment before late fees are applied or the plan is marked delinquent.']
  interest_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total interest charged over the life of the payment plan.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate (APR) applied to the payment plan, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0599 for 5.99%).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment plan record was last updated.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard late fee charged per missed or overdue installment payment.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan is scheduled to be fully paid and closed.']
  next_payment_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount due for the next scheduled installment payment.']
  next_payment_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next installment payment is due.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the payment plan for internal reference.']
  number_of_installments varchar [note: 'Total number of scheduled installment payments for this plan.']
  originating_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment plan was initiated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pos|ecommerce|mobile_app|pharmacy|customer_service|wholesale_portal — promote to reference product]']
  origination_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan was established and became effective.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the payment plan.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which installment payments are scheduled.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the payment plan, used for customer communication and account management.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|completed|defaulted|cancelled|suspended|pending — promote to reference product]']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment plan arrangement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: layaway|pharmacy_payment_assistance|b2b_net_terms|installment_purchase|deferred_payment|other — promote to reference product]']
  principal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base amount financed before interest and fees.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the payment assistance or financing program (e.g., Pharmacy Patient Assistance, Wholesale Net-30 Terms).']
  program_sponsor varchar [note: 'Organization or entity sponsoring the payment plan program (e.g., pharmaceutical manufacturer, third-party lender).']
  total_paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount paid to date across all installments.']
  total_plan_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount financed under the payment plan, including principal and any fees.']

  Note: 'Master record for installment payment plans and deferred payment arrangements offered by Grocery, with embedded installment schedule tracking. Captures plan_type (layaway, pharmacy payment assistance, B2B net terms), plan_status (active, completed, defaulted, cancelled), total_plan_amount, number_of_installments, payment_frequency (weekly, biweekly, monthly), interest_rate, origination_date, originating_channel, and customer_reference. Includes installment-level tracking: scheduled_date, scheduled_amount, actual_payment_date, actual_amount_paid, installment_status (scheduled, paid, overdue, waived), late_fee_applied, and days_past_due. Supports pharmacy patient payment assistance programs, B2B wholesale account net terms, and collections management.'
}

Table payment.promotion_application {
  promotion_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the promotion_application association']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the payment transaction']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promo offer']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Code of the digital coupon used for this promo offer in the transaction']
  discount_amount decimal [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied by this promo offer to the transaction']

  Note: 'This association product represents the application of a promotional offer to a payment transaction. It captures the discount amount and coupon code that are specific to each transaction‑offer pair.. Existence Justification: In Grocery, promotional offers are applied to payment transactions at the point of sale or online. A single payment transaction can have multiple promo offers applied, and each promo offer can be applied to many payment transactions across stores and channels. The application of an offer is tracked with specific data such as the discount amount and coupon code, which belong to the transaction‑offer relationship itself.'
}

Table analytics.kpi_definition {
  kpi_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each KPI definition record.']
  owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: KPIs are owned by a specific associate (e.g., senior analyst) for accountability; linking enables KPI ownership reporting.']
  parent_kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on kpi_definition (parent_kpi_definition_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: KPIs are defined to monitor performance of compliance programs; linking KPI to its governing program enables program‑level scorecards.']
  calculation_methodology varchar [note: 'Plain‑text description of the formula or logic used to compute the KPI.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KPI definition record was first created.']
  data_quality_rule varchar [note: 'Rule or constraint ensuring data quality for the KPI (e.g., non‑null sales amount).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary source system or data domain feeding the KPI (e.g., POS, ERP, WMS).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the KPI definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the KPI definition is retired or superseded (null if indefinite).']
  kpi_definition_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business domain the KPI belongs to.']
  kpi_definition_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or short code for the KPI (e.g., COMP_SALES_QTR).']
  kpi_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the KPI purpose and calculation context.']
  kpi_definition_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the KPI as used in reports and dashboards.']
  kpi_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the KPI definition.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the KPI definition was last reviewed for relevance or accuracy.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or responsible team for the KPI.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the KPI is calculated and reported.']
  strategic_objective varchar [note: 'Enterprise strategic goal that the KPI supports (e.g., Increase Same‑Store Sales).']
  target_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Acceptable lower/upper threshold range for the KPI; stored as a single numeric bound when only one side is defined.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target or goal value for the KPI (e.g., 5.0 for 5%).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the KPI value (e.g., percent, dollars, count).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the KPI definition.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the KPI definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) defined and governed by the enterprise analytics team at Grocery. Captures KPI name, business owner, calculation methodology, unit of measure, target thresholds, reporting frequency, and alignment to strategic objectives. Serves as the authoritative reference for all KPI metadata used across dashboards, scorecards, and automated reporting. Covers comp sales, same-store sales (SSS), basket size, units per transaction (UPT), shrink rate, fill rate, and category performance KPIs.'
}

Table analytics.metric_definition {
  metric_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the metric definition. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity classified as OTHER, does not fit standard role categories.']
  derived_from_metric_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on metric_definition (derived_from_metric_definition_id)']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Financial KPI metrics are derived from specific GL accounts; linking provides traceability from metric to underlying account.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Metric definitions belong to a KPI; many metrics per KPI, so metric_definition gets kpi_definition_id FK.']
  metric_owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Metric definitions are authored and maintained by a data‑analytics associate; the link supports metric stewardship and audit.']
  price_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_rule. Business justification: REQUIRED: KPI metrics track compliance and impact of pricing rules, so each metric definition references the rule it measures.']
  tender_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.tender_type. Business justification: Defines metric tracking usage volume per tender type for strategic payment channel optimization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the metric definition record was first created.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'Data type of the metric\'s result value.']
  metric_audience varchar [note: 'Intended consumer audience for the metric.']
  metric_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category the metric belongs to.']
  metric_confidentiality varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the metric definition.']
  metric_data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) representing the assessed data quality of the metric\'s source data.']
  metric_definition_source varchar [note: 'System or process that originated the metric definition (e.g., SAP Retail, Blue Yonder).']
  metric_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the metric measures and its business purpose.']
  metric_formula varchar [note: 'Expression or calculation logic that derives the metric from source data fields.']
  metric_grain varchar [note: 'Granularity at which the metric is calculated (e.g., per store per day).']
  metric_is_aggregated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the metric is an aggregated value (true) or a raw atomic measure (false).']
  metric_is_time_variant boolean [note: 'True if the metric value can change over time; false if static.']
  metric_last_validated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent validation of the metric\'s correctness.']
  metric_lineage varchar [note: 'Narrative of source tables, joins, and transformations used to derive the metric.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the atomic business metric.']
  metric_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or team responsible for the metric.']
  metric_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the metric definition.']
  metric_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of business tags or keywords associated with the metric.']
  metric_units varchar [note: 'Units of measure for the metric value (e.g., dollars, count, percent).']
  metric_valid_from date [note: 'Effective start date when the metric definition becomes valid.']
  metric_valid_to date [note: 'Effective end date after which the metric definition is retired (nullable).']
  metric_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the metric definition, allowing change tracking.']
  source_domain varchar [note: 'Data domain that provides the source data for the metric (e.g., sales, inventory).']
  source_product varchar [note: 'Operational system or product that supplies the raw data used to compute the metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the metric definition.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of atomic business metrics that underpin KPI calculations at Grocery. Distinct from KPIs in that metrics are raw or lightly derived measures (e.g., total_sales_dollars, transaction_count, avg_basket_value, units_sold, gross_margin_pct) rather than composite performance indicators. Captures metric name, formula expression, grain (store/day, category/week), source domain, source product, and data type. Enables governed metric reuse across dashboards and ad-hoc analysis without recalculation inconsistency.'
}

Table analytics.dashboard {
  dashboard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each dashboard record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Brand performance dashboards require a direct FK to the brand entity to surface brand‑level KPIs and metrics.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_center. Business justification: Fuel Center Performance Dashboard needs to associate the dashboard with the fuel center it monitors for KPI tracking.']
  cloned_from_dashboard_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dashboard (cloned_from_dashboard_id)']
  dashboard_associate_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the primary owner (business analyst or data steward) responsible for the dashboard.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Dashboard displays a KPI; add KPI reference to dashboard.']
  owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.associate']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.associate']
  pricing_recommendation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.recommendation. Business justification: REQUIRED: Pricing recommendation dashboards need to surface the underlying recommendation record for drill‑down and audit.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Store managers access dashboards tailored to their store; the FK ties each dashboard instance to the relevant store location.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Access control level governing who can view the dashboard.']
  audience varchar [note: 'Primary consumer group(s) for the dashboard.']
  average_load_time_seconds double [note: 'Average time in seconds for the dashboard to load for end users.']
  compliance_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulatory or policy tags applicable to the dashboard (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dashboard record was first created in the catalog.']
  dashboard_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or short code used to reference the dashboard in documentation and governance.']
  dashboard_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the dashboard\'s purpose, scope, and key metrics.']
  dashboard_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the dashboard as displayed to users.']
  dashboard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dashboard.']
  dashboard_type varchar [note: 'Category of the dashboard indicating its primary purpose or audience.']
  data_governance_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the dashboard\'s data according to corporate data policy.']
  data_source_summary varchar [note: 'Brief description of the primary data sources feeding the dashboard.']
  domain varchar [note: 'High‑level business domain the dashboard serves (e.g., finance, store operations, supply chain).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the dashboard is scheduled to be retired or archived.']
  is_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dashboard has passed governance certification.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dashboard data was last refreshed.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Technology platform used to author and publish the dashboard.']
  publication_status varchar [note: 'Current publication state of the dashboard within the analytics catalog.']
  refresh_cadence varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the dashboard data is refreshed.']
  related_reports varchar [note: 'Identifiers of other dashboards or reports that are logically related.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the dashboard metadata is retained before archival.']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed time for the dashboard to become available after a refresh request.']
  tags varchar [note: 'User‑defined tags for search and categorization (e.g., \'holiday_sales\', \'inventory_turnover\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent metadata change to the dashboard record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier (e.g., 1.0.3) for change management.']
  view_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the dashboard has been viewed by users.']

  Note: 'Master record for every business intelligence dashboard published and governed within the Grocery analytics platform (Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI). Captures dashboard name, owning business domain, target audience (store ops, category management, finance, executive), refresh cadence, platform, publication status, and certified/draft flag. Serves as the authoritative catalog of all active and retired dashboards enabling governance, access control, and impact analysis when upstream data products change.'
}

Table analytics.report_definition {
  report_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each report definition record.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Report definitions are built around KPIs; add KPI reference.']
  parent_report_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on report_definition (parent_report_definition_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Compliance programs mandate periodic reports; report_definition records the report that satisfies a specific program\'s reporting requirement.']
  report_owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Reports have a designated owner associate (e.g., BI analyst); linking enables ownership tracking and compliance.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Data classification governing who may view the report.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or internal compliance rules that the report satisfies (e.g., SOX, FDA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the report definition record was first created in the system.']
  data_freshness_sla_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days allowed between source data refresh and report generation.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the quality of source data at the time of report generation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system(s) that provide the underlying data (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Merchandising).']
  delivery_format varchar [note: 'File or channel format used to deliver the generated report.']
  is_ad_hoc boolean [note: 'True if the report is primarily generated on demand rather than on a fixed schedule.']
  is_scheduled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the report runs on a recurring schedule (true) or is on‑demand only (false).']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the report was most recently generated.']
  next_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date‑time for the next scheduled execution of the report.']
  owner_domain varchar [note: 'Analytics domain that owns and maintains the report definition (e.g., sales, inventory, finance).']
  recipient_list varchar [note: 'Semicolon‑separated list of email addresses or user IDs that receive the report.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the report is required for external regulatory filing.']
  report_audience varchar [note: 'Intended audience for the report: internal stakeholders or external partners.']
  report_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business area the report belongs to.']
  report_definition_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or short code used to reference the report in catalogs and schedules.']
  report_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the report purpose, scope, and key metrics.']
  report_definition_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the report as shown to business users.']
  report_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the report definition.']
  report_definition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the report purpose: operational, financial, regulatory, or ad‑hoc.']
  report_distribution_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to distribute the report (email, portal, FTP).']
  report_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the report content is generated (e.g., EN, ES).']
  report_last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User name of the person who last edited the report definition.']
  report_last_modified_by_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the user who last modified the report definition.']
  report_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the business user or team responsible for the report.']
  report_owner_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the report owner for notifications.']
  report_owner_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the report owner.']
  report_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level for report generation and delivery.']
  report_tags varchar [note: 'Free‑form tags or keywords associated with the report for searchability.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the generated report files are retained before archival or deletion.']
  schedule_cron varchar [note: 'Cron expression that defines the recurring execution schedule for the report.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the report definition.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the report definition to track changes over time.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all scheduled and on-demand business reports produced by the Grocery analytics platform. Captures report name, report type (operational, financial, regulatory, ad-hoc), owning domain, delivery format (PDF, Excel, CSV, email, SFTP), schedule expression (cron), recipient list, and data freshness SLA. Distinct from dashboards in that reports are point-in-time document artifacts delivered to stakeholders rather than interactive visualizations. Covers weekly ad performance reports, comp sales reports, category scorecards, and shrink reports.'
}

Table analytics.comp_sales_period {
  comp_sales_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each comparable sales period definition.']
  prior_year_comp_sales_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on comp_sales_period (prior_year_comp_sales_period_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the period definition record was first created.']
  current_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the current analysis period.']
  current_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the current analysis period.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'When this period definition becomes active for analytics.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'When this period definition is retired; null if still active.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual description of the rules a store must meet to be included (e.g., open ≥52 weeks, no major remodel).']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a store is excluded; limited to common categories.']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store satisfies the eligibility criteria for this period.']
  is_excluded boolean [note: 'True when the store is excluded from comp‑sales calculations for this period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or audit notes about the period definition.']
  period_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the period pair, e.g., "FY2023 Q1".']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period granularity used for comp‑sales analysis.']
  prior_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the prior‑year comparable period.']
  prior_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the prior‑year comparable period.']
  relocation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the store changed location or footprint during the period.']
  remodel_flag boolean [note: 'True if the store underwent a remodel during the period.']
  store_open_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks the store was open in the fiscal year, used for eligibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the period definition.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the period definition for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the comparable sales (comp sales) period pairs used in same-store sales (SSS) analysis at Grocery. Captures current period start/end dates, prior-year comparable period start/end dates, period type (week, 4-week, quarter, fiscal year), comp eligibility criteria (store must be open 52+ weeks), and exclusion flags for remodeled or relocated stores. This is the authoritative reference that governs which periods are used in all comp sales KPI calculations across the enterprise, ensuring consistent year-over-year comparisons.'
}

Table analytics.comp_sales_result {
  comp_sales_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each comparable sales result record.']
  banner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the banner (brand family) grouping the store.']
  comp_sales_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.comp_sales_period. Business justification: Comp sales results are calculated for a defined comp period; link result to period.']
  price_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_change. Business justification: REQUIRED: Comparative sales reports link results to the specific price change that triggered the sales variance.']
  prior_period_comp_sales_result_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on comp_sales_result (prior_period_comp_sales_result_id)']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier for reporting roll‑up.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail store to which the result applies.']
  basket_size_current decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average transaction amount (basket size) for the current period.']
  basket_size_prior decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average transaction amount for the prior comparable period.']
  basket_size_variance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Difference in average basket size between current and prior periods.']
  comp_sales_dollar_variance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference in net sales between current and prior comparable periods (current – prior).']
  comp_sales_percent_change decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage change in net sales between current and prior periods.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in this record.']
  discount_current decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount and promotional reductions applied in the current period.']
  discount_prior decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount and promotional reductions applied in the prior-year comparable period.']
  eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is eligible for comp‑sales reporting in this period.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a store was excluded from comp‑sales calculation, if eligibility_flag is false.']
  gross_sales_current decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross sales amount for the current period before discounts and adjustments.']
  gross_sales_prior decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross sales amount for the comparable prior-year period.']
  net_sales_current decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net sales for the current period after discounts; primary KPI for comp analysis.']
  net_sales_prior decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net sales for the prior-year comparable period after discounts.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the comparable sales record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the comparable sales record.']
  result_key varchar [note: 'Composite business key (e.g., store_id + period) uniquely identifying the result within business processes.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the comparable sales record.']
  result_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the end of the reporting period for which the result is calculated.']
  transaction_count_current varchar [note: 'Number of sales transactions recorded in the current period.']
  transaction_count_prior varchar [note: 'Number of sales transactions recorded in the prior comparable period.']
  transaction_count_variance varchar [note: 'Difference in transaction count between current and prior periods.']
  units_sold_current varchar [note: 'Total number of individual items sold in the current period.']
  units_sold_prior varchar [note: 'Total number of individual items sold in the prior comparable period.']
  units_variance varchar [note: 'Difference in units sold between current and prior periods.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing computed comparable (comp) sales and same-store sales (SSS) results at the store-period grain for Grocery. Stores current period net sales, prior-year comparable period net sales, comp sales dollar variance, comp sales percent change, transaction count comp, basket size comp, and units comp. Includes store eligibility flag, exclusion reason if applicable, and banner/region rollup keys. This is the authoritative Silver Layer fact table for all comp sales reporting consumed by store operations, finance, and executive dashboards.'
}

Table analytics.basket_summary {
  basket_summary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the basket summary record.']
  prior_period_basket_summary_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on basket_summary (prior_period_basket_summary_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where basket data was captured.']
  store_sales_fact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.store_sales_fact. Business justification: Basket summary aggregates store sales; link to the underlying store_sales_fact.']
  average_basket_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per basket.']
  average_discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average discount percentage applied per basket.']
  average_gross_margin decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average gross margin for baskets.']
  average_items_per_basket decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of items per basket.']
  average_transaction_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Average time taken to complete a basket transaction.']
  basket_count varchar [note: 'Number of baskets captured in the period.']
  basket_date date [note: 'Date of the basket activity (store‑day grain).']
  basket_key varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the basket summary (e.g., concatenation of store, date, and sequence).']
  basket_size_tier varchar [note: 'Overall basket size classification based on value.']
  basket_summary_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the basket summary record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  fill_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of basket items that were fully fulfilled from inventory.']
  fiscal_week varchar [note: 'Fiscal week number for the basket period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for the basket period.']
  large_basket_count varchar [note: 'Count of baskets classified as large (e.g., >$50).']
  loyalty_basket_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the basket belongs to a loyalty program member.']
  medium_basket_count varchar [note: 'Count of baskets classified as medium (e.g., $20‑$50).']
  multi_category_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of baskets containing items from multiple categories.']
  net_sales_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net sales after discounts for the period.']
  out_of_stock_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of basket items that were out of stock at time of purchase.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment method used for the basket.']
  private_label_penetration decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of basket value contributed by private‑label products.']
  promotional_basket_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the basket includes any promotional items.']
  record_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the basket summary record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the basket summary record.']
  shrink_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of basket value lost due to shrinkage.']
  small_basket_count varchar [note: 'Count of baskets classified as small (e.g., <$20).']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Format type of the store.']
  store_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region code of the store (e.g., NE, MW, SE).']
  total_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount value applied across all baskets.']
  total_sales_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross sales amount for all baskets in the period.']
  transaction_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the basket was completed.']

  Note: 'Transactional analytical record capturing basket-level purchase behavior metrics at the store-day or store-week grain for Grocery. Stores average basket size (ABS) in dollars, average items per basket (UPT — units per transaction), basket count by size tier (small/medium/large), multi-category basket rate, private-label penetration per basket, and loyalty vs. non-loyalty basket split. Derived from order and order_line domain data and materialized in the Silver Layer for basket analysis dashboards, category management, and promotional effectiveness measurement.'
}

Table analytics.category_performance {
  category_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each category performance record.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category (e.g., dairy, produce) associated with the performance metrics.']
  prior_period_category_performance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on category_performance (prior_period_category_performance_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store for which the performance metrics are recorded.']
  average_inventory_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average dollar value of inventory on hand for the category during the period.']
  average_retail_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average selling price per unit for the category, weighted by units sold.']
  category_penetration_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total store transactions that purchased at least one SKU from the category.']
  category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the category (e.g., active, inactive, discontinued).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the category performance record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Composite score (0‑1) reflecting the quality of the source data for this record.']
  gross_margin_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of gross margin (sales minus cost of goods sold) for the category.']
  gross_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Gross margin expressed as a percentage of net sales.']
  inventory_turn decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of cost of goods sold to average inventory cost for the category.']
  markdown_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of price reductions applied to the category.']
  markdown_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate markdown expressed as a percentage of original price.']
  net_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total net sales dollars for the category after discounts and returns.']
  out_of_stock_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the category was out of stock during the period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period (e.g., week end).']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period (e.g., week start).']
  price_change_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net dollar change in average price for the category over the period.']
  price_change_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any price adjustments occurred for the category during the period.']
  price_change_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change in average price for the category over the period.']
  promotional_sales_lift_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Incremental sales dollars attributed to promotional activities for the category.']
  promotional_sales_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Promotional sales lift expressed as a percentage of baseline sales.']
  shrink_dollars decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar value of inventory loss due to theft, spoilage, or damage for the category.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the source data for this record.']
  stock_on_hand varchar [note: 'Quantity of units physically present in the store for the category at period end.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of POS transactions that included at least one item from the category.']
  units_sold varchar [note: 'Total number of units sold for the category during the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the category performance record.']

  Note: 'Transactional analytical record capturing category-level sales and margin performance at the category-store-period grain for Grocery. Stores net sales, gross margin dollars and percent, units sold, transactions with category purchase, category penetration rate, average retail price, promotional sales lift, shrink dollars, and inventory turn. Aligned to the assortment.category hierarchy. Serves as the authoritative Silver Layer fact for category management reviews, category captain reporting, and assortment optimization decisions.'
}

Table analytics.dq_rule {
  dq_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the data quality rule.']
  metric_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.metric_definition. Business justification: Data quality rules are often scoped to a metric; add metric reference.']
  parent_dq_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dq_rule (parent_dq_rule_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created.']
  dq_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose and logic of the rule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the rule is retired or no longer enforced.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is executed automatically as part of data pipelines.']
  last_pass_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed pass percentage from the most recent rule execution.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the rule.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rule definition.']
  pass_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable pass rate for the rule expressed as a percentage.']
  remediation_owner varchar [note: 'Team or individual responsible for addressing rule violations.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of the rule (e.g., data_quality, governance, compliance).']
  rule_expression varchar [note: 'SQL or Great Expectations expression that defines the rule logic.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of rule such as completeness, uniqueness, validity, referential integrity, timeliness, or consistency.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact level of a rule failure used for prioritization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System or tool where the rule is authored or executed.']
  target_column varchar [note: 'Column within the target entity that the rule validates.']
  target_domain varchar [note: 'Business domain (e.g., product, customer, inventory) to which the rule applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product|customer|store|inventory|order|pharmacy|promotion|vendor|finance|loyalty|fuel|digital_commerce|workforce — promote to reference product]']
  target_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the entity/table that the rule validates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule record.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all data quality rules defined and enforced across the Grocery Lakehouse Silver Layer. Captures rule name, rule type (completeness, uniqueness, validity, referential integrity, timeliness, consistency), target domain, target product, target column, rule expression (SQL or Great Expectations syntax), severity level (critical/warning/info), expected pass threshold percentage, and remediation owner. Serves as the authoritative governance registry for all DQ rules enabling automated DQ execution, lineage tracking, and SLA enforcement.'
}

Table analytics.dq_result {
  dq_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the data quality result record.']
  dq_rule_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the data quality rule that was executed.']
  previous_dq_result_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dq_result (previous_dq_result_id)']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Data‑quality rule results need to reference the exact product item they evaluate for traceability and remediation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations added by the data quality analyst.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this data quality result record was first inserted.']
  data_asset varchar [note: 'Logical name of the data asset (product) that the rule validates.']
  data_asset_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the data asset at the time of validation.']
  dq_result_status varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail status of the rule execution against the defined threshold.']
  evaluated_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records examined by the rule during this run.']
  execution_environment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compute environment or cluster where the rule was executed.']
  failed_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that violated the rule conditions.']
  pass_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of records that passed the rule (passed / evaluated * 100).']
  passed_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that satisfied the rule conditions.']
  remediation_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the ticket created to address the data quality issue.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule type (e.g., completeness, uniqueness, range).']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the data quality rule.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data quality rule execution was performed.']
  sample_failed_keys varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of primary‑key values for a sample of records that failed the rule.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact severity assigned to the rule failure.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system or pipeline that generated the data quality result (e.g., Databricks, Spark job).']
  target_column varchar [note: 'Column within the target product that the rule evaluated.']
  target_domain varchar [note: 'Business domain (e.g., product, customer, inventory) to which the rule was applied.']
  target_product varchar [note: 'Name of the data product (table or view) that was validated.']
  threshold_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold that the rule\'s pass rate must meet or exceed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this data quality result record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the outcome of each data quality rule execution run against a target data product in the Grocery Lakehouse. Captures run timestamp, rule ID, target domain, target product, target column, records evaluated count, records passed count, records failed count, pass rate percentage, pass/fail status against threshold, sample failed record keys, and remediation ticket reference. Enables DQ trend monitoring, SLA breach alerting, and root-cause investigation for data quality incidents across the Silver Layer.'
}

Table analytics.dq_issue {
  dq_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the data quality issue record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity classified as OTHER because it does not fit standard master or transaction roles.']
  dq_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DQ rule that generated the issue.']
  product_item_attribute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_attribute. Business justification: Quality issues are often attribute‑specific; linking allows issue tracking directly to the offending product attribute.']
  related_dq_issue_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dq_issue (related_dq_issue_id)']
  affected_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of data records impacted by the issue.']
  assigned_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/team responsible for remediation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Any extra free‑text comments from stakeholders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the issue record was first created.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating sensitivity of the issue data.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'How the issue was discovered.']
  domain varchar [note: 'Primary business domain affected by the issue (e.g., customer, product, inventory).']
  dq_issue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the data quality issue.']
  impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0‑100) representing overall business impact.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the issue is deemed critical for business operations.']
  is_regulatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issue affects regulatory reporting or compliance.']
  issue_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of the issue type.']
  issue_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature, scope, and impact of the data quality problem.']
  issue_type varchar [note: 'Category of data quality problem (e.g., completeness, accuracy).']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the issue by the owner.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority (e.g., 1‑5) used for internal triage.']
  product varchar [note: 'Specific data product or dataset where the issue was detected. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: assortment|pricing|replenishment|sales|loyalty|pharmacy|supply_chain|store|finance — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority impacted (e.g., FDA, HIPAA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|USDA|DEA|FTC|OSHA|EPA|PCI_DSS|HIPAA|SOX — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action to correct the data quality problem.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing how the issue was resolved or why it was waived.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the underlying cause of the issue.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating the business impact of the issue.']
  sla_due_date date [note: 'Service‑level‑agreement date by which the issue must be addressed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Manhattan). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP|Oracle|Manhattan|BlueYonder|NCR|Salesforce|McKesson|JDA|Workday — promote to reference product]']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the issue should be resolved.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the data quality issue.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the issue record.']

  Note: 'Operational record tracking active and resolved data quality issues identified through DQ rule execution or manual discovery at Grocery. Captures issue title, severity, affected domain and product, root cause category (source system, ETL, business rule change, schema drift), assigned remediation owner, target resolution date, current status (open/in-progress/resolved/waived), and resolution notes. Distinct from dq_result in that it represents the managed lifecycle of a DQ problem, not just a single rule execution outcome. Enables DQ incident management and SLA tracking.'
}

Table analytics.analytical_dataset {
