  analytical_dataset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the analytical dataset record.']
  derived_from_analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on analytical_dataset (derived_from_analytical_dataset_id)']
  access_tier varchar [note: 'Governance classification that determines who may consume the dataset.']
  analytical_dataset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and contents of the dataset.']
  analytical_dataset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the dataset as used by analysts and data scientists.']
  analytical_dataset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dataset.']
  catalog_path varchar [note: 'Full Databricks Lakehouse catalog location where the dataset resides.']
  certified_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dataset has passed data quality and governance certification.']
  column_count varchar [note: 'Number of columns (fields) in the dataset schema.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dataset metadata record was first created in the catalog.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Governance classification applied to the dataset\'s content.']
  data_freshness_sla varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed latency between source data change and dataset availability (e.g., 24h, 48h).']
  dataset_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the dataset for cataloging and lookup.']
  domain varchar [note: 'Broad business domain the dataset belongs to (e.g., customer, product, store, supply_chain, inventory, order, pharmacy, promotions, workforce, finance, vendor, fuel, digital_commerce, loyalty). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer|product|store|supply_chain|inventory|order|pharmacy|promotions|workforce|finance|vendor|fuel|digital_commerce|loyalty — promote to reference product]']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the dataset became active for consumption.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the dataset is scheduled to be retired or become inactive (nullable).']
  grain_description varchar [note: 'Description of the dataset\'s granularity (e.g., store‑day, SKU‑week, customer‑month).']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dataset was most recently refreshed.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the business owner or team responsible for the dataset.']
  refresh_schedule varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the dataset is refreshed.']
  row_count varchar [note: 'Number of rows contained in the most recent refresh of the dataset.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system(s) that feed the dataset (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Retail, Manhattan WMS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the dataset metadata.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of curated analytical datasets and Silver Layer data marts published by the Grocery analytics team for consumption by BI tools, data scientists, and self-service analysts. Captures dataset name, owning domain, grain description (e.g., store-day, SKU-week, customer-month), row count, column count, refresh schedule, data freshness SLA, access tier (public/internal/restricted), Databricks catalog path, and certified status. Serves as the enterprise data catalog entry point for all analytics-ready datasets, enabling discoverability and governed self-service.'
}

Table analytics.store_sales_fact {
  store_sales_fact_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each store-day sales fact record.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_center. Business justification: Daily Store Sales Dashboard requires linking each sales fact to the specific fuel center to report per‑center fuel revenue.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Sales facts need to be tied to the fiscal period entity for period‑based financial consolidation and SOX reporting.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Daily Sales Reconciliation Report requires tracing each store sales fact row to its originating order header for audit and variance analysis.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Needed for Transaction Detail Drill‑Down report that joins sales fact to underlying payment transaction for finance reconciliation.']
  pricing_price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: REQUIRED: Zone‑level sales reporting needs each sales fact linked to its price zone for accurate margin and pricing analysis.']
  prior_day_store_sales_fact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on store_sales_fact (prior_day_store_sales_fact_id)']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Daily product‑level sales reporting per store used in performance dashboards; linking enables granular sales analysis.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Store sales are allocated to profit centers for profit‑and‑loss reporting; linking enables profit‑center level sales performance dashboards.']
  promo_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_zone. Business justification: Required for zone‑level sales reporting used in regional performance dashboards.']
  retail_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.retail_price. Business justification: REQUIRED: Transaction‑level price audit links each sales fact to the exact retail price used, enabling compliance and margin verification.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail store for which the sales are recorded.']
  average_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Gross margin expressed as a percentage of net sales.']
  average_price_per_unit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average selling price per item unit (net_sales_amount / total_units_sold).']
  average_ticket_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average sales amount per transaction (net_sales_amount divided by transaction_count).']
  average_units_per_transaction decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean number of items purchased per transaction (total_units_sold / transaction_count).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales fact row was first inserted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values; default USD for Grocery.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'Name of the weekday for sales_date. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — promote to reference product]']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of discounts, coupons, and price reductions applied.']
  fuel_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Revenue from fuel transactions at the store (if applicable) for the day.']
  fuel_sales_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fuel sales amount expressed as a percentage of total net sales.']
  gross_margin_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Revenue remaining after cost of goods sold; calculated as net_sales_amount minus COGS (provided by downstream process).']
  gross_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Gross margin expressed as a percentage of net sales.']
  gross_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sales dollars before discounts, returns, and promotions for the store on the sales_date.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'True if sales_date is a recognized public or store‑specific holiday.']
  is_promotional_day boolean [note: 'True if any store‑wide promotion was active on sales_date.']
  is_return_day boolean [note: 'True if the store processed a high volume of returns on sales_date.']
  loyalty_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net sales generated from loyalty program members, including any loyalty discounts.']
  loyalty_sales_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Loyalty sales amount expressed as a percentage of net sales.']
  month_of_year varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) for the sales_date.']
  net_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net sales after discounts and promotions (gross_sales_amount minus discount_amount).']
  net_sales_per_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Net sales amount divided by store_sqft, a key productivity metric.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any manual adjustments, comments, or anomalies related to the day\'s sales.']
  price_index decimal(5,3) [note: 'Inflation/deflation factor applied to sales for price normalization.']
  promo_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sales dollars attributable to promotional pricing, BOGO, TPR, and ad circular offers.']
  promo_sales_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Promotional sales amount expressed as a percentage of net sales.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the sales were generated: in‑store, online, curbside pickup, or home delivery.']
  sales_date date [note: 'Calendar date representing the day of sales aggregation (store local time).']
  sales_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the daily sales record was materialized in the silver layer.']
  shrink_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar value of inventory loss due to theft, spoilage, or damage for the day.']
  shrink_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Shrink amount expressed as a percentage of net sales.']
  store_sales_fact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the daily sales record: provisional (pre‑validation), final (validated), or adjusted (post‑close correction).']
  store_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total selling area of the store in square feet.']
  store_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the store format based on size and service offering.']
  total_units_sold varchar [note: 'Aggregate count of all items (units) sold across all transactions.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual POS transactions recorded for the store on the sales_date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sales fact row.']
  week_of_year varchar [note: 'ISO week number (1‑53) for the sales_date.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year for the sales_date.']

  Note: 'Core Silver Layer sales fact record at the store-day grain for Grocery, serving as the primary analytical foundation for store operations reporting. Captures total net sales dollars, transaction count, total units sold, average transaction value, gross margin dollars, shrink dollars, promotional sales dollars, loyalty sales dollars, and fuel sales dollars (where applicable). Aggregated from order domain transactional data and materialized as a governed Silver Layer fact. Distinct from comp_sales_result (which focuses on year-over-year comp) — this is the base daily sales ledger for all store performance reporting.'
}

Table analytics.promo_effectiveness {
  promo_effectiveness_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each promotion effectiveness record. [_canonical_skip_reason: analytical fact does not fit predefined role categories]']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Promo effectiveness results are stored in analytical datasets.']
  baseline_promo_effectiveness_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on promo_effectiveness (baseline_promo_effectiveness_id)']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promoted product (SKU).']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign being evaluated.']
  promo_price_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promo_price_link. Business justification: REQUIRED: Measuring promo ROI requires tying effectiveness metrics to the specific promo price link that was applied.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where the promotion was executed.']
  actual_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sales dollars generated by the promoted product during the promotion period.']
  actual_units_sold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Units sold of the promoted product during the promotion period.']
  baseline_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected sales dollars without the promotion (baseline).']
  baseline_units_sold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected units that would have been sold without the promotion (baseline).']
  cannibalization_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of sales lift that displaced sales of other SKUs (negative effect).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the record was first created in the analytics layer.']
  halo_effect_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental sales dollars of non-promoted items in adjacent categories.']
  halo_effect_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental units sold of non-promoted items in adjacent categories due to the promotion.']
  incremental_gross_margin decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional gross margin earned due to the promotion after accounting for variable costs.']
  is_targeted boolean [note: 'True if the promotion was limited to a defined customer segment.']
  is_test_promotion boolean [note: 'True if the promotion was a pilot or test; otherwise false.']
  lift_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental sales dollars above baseline due to the promotion.']
  lift_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental units sold above baseline due to the promotion.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the measurement period for the promotion.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First date of the measurement period for the promotion.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the promotion.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotion mechanism.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Total count of promotion redemptions (e.g., coupons used, BOGO applied).']
  redemption_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of eligible transactions that redeemed the promotion.']
  roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'ROI of the promotion expressed as a percentage (net benefit divided by cost).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the promotion was delivered to the customer.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Code representing the customer segment the promotion was aimed at.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the record.']
  vendor_funding_recovered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of vendor contributions (co-op) that were recouped.']

  Note: 'Transactional analytical record measuring the effectiveness of promotional campaigns and offers at the offer-store-period grain for Grocery. Captures promoted item sales lift (units and dollars vs. baseline), redemption count, redemption rate, incremental gross margin, cannibalization rate, halo effect on adjacent categories, vendor funding recovered, and ROI percentage. Derived from promotion and order domain data. Serves as the authoritative Silver Layer fact for post-promotion analysis (PPA) consumed by category managers, buyers, and the marketing analytics team.'
}

Table analytics.loyalty_analytics_fact {
  loyalty_analytics_fact_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each loyalty analytics fact record.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Loyalty analytics facts are part of an analytical dataset.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the loyalty program member (customer).']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Loyalty Points Earned Report calculates points earned per order, requiring a direct link from loyalty fact rows to the order header.']
  prior_period_loyalty_analytics_fact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on loyalty_analytics_fact (prior_period_loyalty_analytics_fact_id)']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program to which the member belongs.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store (or digital channel) where the activity was recorded.']
  active_member_count varchar [note: 'Number of members actively participating in the loyalty program during the period.']
  avg_points_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mean points balance held by members at period end.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the loyalty activity data.']
  is_lapsed_member boolean [note: 'True if the member became inactive or lapsed during the period.']
  is_new_member boolean [note: 'True if the member enrolled during the reporting period.']
  lapsed_members varchar [note: 'Number of members whose loyalty status became inactive or lapsed during the period.']
  loyalty_sales_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net sales revenue generated from transactions where the loyalty member received points or discounts.']
  loyalty_sales_penetration decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of total sales attributable to loyalty members (loyalty_sales_amount / total_sales_amount).']
  new_enrollments varchar [note: 'Count of new members who enrolled in the loyalty program in the period.']
  offer_acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of personalized offers accepted by members (offers_accepted / offers_sent).']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period (e.g., month, week).']
  points_accrued varchar [note: 'Total loyalty points earned by members in the period.']
  points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total loyalty points redeemed by members in the period.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the loyalty program for the period.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fact record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fact record.']
  redemption_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of accrued points that were redeemed (points_redeemed / points_accrued).']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business‑defined segment classification of members based on value or behavior.']
  tier_bronze_count varchar [note: 'Number of members in the Bronze loyalty tier during the period.']
  tier_gold_count varchar [note: 'Number of members in the Gold loyalty tier during the period.']
  tier_silver_count varchar [note: 'Number of members in the Silver loyalty tier during the period.']
  total_sales_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Overall net sales revenue for the period across all customers.']

  Note: 'Transactional Silver Layer fact capturing loyalty program performance metrics at the member-period or program-period grain for Grocery. Stores active member count, new enrollments, lapsed members, points accrued, points redeemed, redemption rate, average points balance, tier distribution, loyalty sales penetration (loyalty vs. non-loyalty spend), and personalized offer acceptance rate. Derived from loyalty domain data. Serves as the authoritative analytical record for loyalty program health reporting consumed by the loyalty and marketing analytics teams.'
}

Table analytics.inventory_analytics_fact {
  inventory_analytics_fact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the inventory analytics fact record.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Each inventory analytics fact belongs to a curated analytical dataset.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category hierarchy to which the SKU belongs.']
  prior_day_inventory_analytics_fact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inventory_analytics_fact (prior_day_inventory_analytics_fact_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the stock keeping unit (SKU) being measured.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Enables Stock Position Detail Report that analyzes on‑hand value and shrink per stock position across stores.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Supports Location‑Level Inventory Dashboard tracking on‑hand units per storage location for capacity planning.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail store for which the inventory snapshot is recorded.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor or supplier that provides the SKU.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.wave. Business justification: Inventory snapshot per wave supports inventory turn and stock‑out analysis tied to wave execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inventory analytics record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the source inventory data.']
  days_of_supply decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of days the current on‑hand inventory will satisfy forecasted demand.']
  fifo_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of inventory moves that adhered to First‑In‑First‑Out rotation policy.']
  fill_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of ordered units that were fully supplied from the distribution center.']
  gmroi decimal(5,2) [note: 'Financial efficiency metric calculated as gross margin divided by average inventory cost.']
  in_stock_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the SKU was available for sale during the reporting period.']
  inventory_turns decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of times inventory was sold and replaced over the period.']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is classified as perishable (true) or non‑perishable (false).']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is a store‑owned private label product; otherwise false.']
  last_replenishment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent replenishment receipt for the SKU at the store.']
  last_replenishment_quantity varchar [note: 'Units received in the most recent replenishment event.']
  last_stockout_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU most recently went out of stock at the store.']
  last_stockout_duration_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of days the SKU remained out of stock during the most recent stock‑out event.']
  on_hand_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of on‑hand units based on standard cost or average cost.']
  on_hand_units varchar [note: 'Quantity of units physically available on the sales floor or backroom at snapshot time.']
  on_order_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of units on order based on purchase price.']
  on_order_units varchar [note: 'Quantity of units that have been ordered from the distribution center or vendor but not yet received.']
  out_of_stock_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the SKU was unavailable for sale during the reporting period.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the analytics record.']
  shrink_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of shrinkage calculated using standard cost.']
  shrink_units varchar [note: 'Units lost due to theft, damage, spoilage, or administrative error.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'Date of the inventory snapshot representing the grain of the fact (SKU‑store‑day).']
  snapshot_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the inventory snapshot; typically daily for operational reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inventory analytics record.']

  Note: 'Transactional Silver Layer fact capturing inventory health and availability metrics at the SKU-store-day grain for Grocery. Stores on-hand units, on-order units, days of supply (DOS), in-stock rate, out-of-stock (OOS) rate, shrink units and dollars, FIFO rotation compliance rate, and fill rate from replenishment. Derived from inventory domain data. Serves as the authoritative analytical record for inventory performance reporting consumed by store operations, supply chain, and category management teams.'
}

  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor as defined in the master vendor dimension.']
  average_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mean monetary value of purchase orders for the vendor.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of chargebacks assessed against the vendor.']
  chargeback_count varchar [note: 'Number of chargeback events recorded for the vendor during the period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fact record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP Retail SD, Oracle Retail Replenishment).']
  fill_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered line items that were fully supplied in a single shipment.']
  inbound_shipment_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume (e.g., pallets or cubic meters) of goods received from the vendor.']
  lead_time_actual_days decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average number of days from purchase order issuance to receipt of goods.']
  lead_time_sla_days decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractual target lead time defined in the vendor service level agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the vendor performance for the period.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries that arrived at the store on or before the promised delivery window.']
  order_accuracy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of orders delivered without discrepancies between ordered and received quantities.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period for the vendor metrics.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period for the vendor metrics.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the record was materialized into the silver layer.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fact record.']
  total_orders varchar [note: 'Count of purchase orders placed with the vendor in the period.']
  total_units_received varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of individual units (SKUs) received from the vendor.']
  total_units_shipped varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of units shipped by the vendor to distribution centers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the fact record.']
  vendor_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score reflecting overall vendor adherence to contractual and operational metrics.']

  Note: 'Transactional Silver Layer fact capturing vendor supply chain performance metrics at the vendor-period grain for Grocery. Stores on-time delivery rate, fill rate, order accuracy rate, chargeback count and dollars, lead time actuals vs. SLA, inbound shipment volume, and vendor compliance score. Derived from supply and vendor domain data. Serves as the authoritative analytical record for vendor performance scorecards consumed by the procurement, supply chain, and category management teams.'
}

Table analytics.shopper_behavior_fact {
  shopper_behavior_fact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each shopper behavior record. _canonical_skip_reason: Fact table does not map to a standard entity role, treated as analytical fact.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Shopper behavior facts are part of an analytical dataset.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Customer Lifetime Value model needs to associate shopper behavior metrics with completed orders to calculate revenue per shopper.']
  prior_period_shopper_behavior_fact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shopper_behavior_fact (prior_period_shopper_behavior_fact_id)']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the shopper (customer) to which the behavior metrics belong.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store (or fulfillment location) where the shopper activity was recorded.']
  avg_spend_per_visit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary amount per visit (total_spend divided by visit_count).']
  basket_size_avg_items decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of distinct line items per transaction for the shopper.']
  basket_size_avg_units decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average total quantity of units purchased per transaction (including multiple units of the same SKU).']
  basket_value_avg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary value of a single transaction (total_spend divided by visit_count).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the shopper made purchases during the period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shopper behavior record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  distinct_category_count varchar [note: 'Number of unique product categories purchased by the shopper in the period.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Total loyalty points accrued by the shopper during the period.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total loyalty points redeemed (used for discounts or rewards) by the shopper in the period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the analysis period for which the shopper metrics are calculated.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the analysis period for which the shopper metrics are calculated.']
  private_label_penetration_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of total spend attributable to store-owned private‑label brands (percentage).']
  promotional_spend_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of total spend that occurred under promotional pricing or BOGO offers (percentage).']
  rfm_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite Recency‑Frequency‑Monetary score calculated for the shopper in the period.']
  rfm_tier varchar [note: 'Segment tier derived from the RFM score (platinum, gold, silver, bronze).']
  total_spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount (USD) spent by the shopper across all purchases in the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shopper behavior record.']
  visit_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct store or channel visits made by the shopper in the period.']

  Note: 'Transactional Silver Layer fact capturing individual shopper purchase behavior metrics at the shopper-period grain for Grocery. Stores visit frequency, average spend per visit, total spend in period, category breadth (number of distinct categories purchased), private-label penetration, channel mix (in-store vs. online vs. pickup), promotional sensitivity score, and recency-frequency-monetary (RFM) tier. Derived from order and loyalty domain data. Serves as the authoritative analytical record for customer analytics, personalization, and segmentation consumed by marketing and loyalty teams.'
}

  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the performance record.']
  store_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the store location.']

  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax collected on sales for the period.']
  units_sold varchar [note: 'Total quantity of items sold across all SKUs for the channel and period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional Silver Layer fact capturing omnichannel sales and fulfillment performance metrics at the channel-store-period grain for Grocery. Covers in-store POS, online web, mobile app, curbside pickup, and last-mile delivery channels. Stores net sales by channel, order count, average order value, fulfillment rate, on-time fulfillment rate, cancellation rate, and digital penetration percentage. Derived from order and commerce domain data. Serves as the authoritative analytical record for omnichannel performance reporting consumed by the digital commerce and store operations teams.'
}

Table analytics.labor_analytics_fact {
  labor_analytics_fact_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each labor analytics fact record.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Labor analytics facts belong to an analytical dataset.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Labor cost reporting requires mapping each labor record to the cost center for budgeting and variance analysis; finance cost_center defines the budget structure.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the store department (e.g., produce, pharmacy) for which labor metrics are recorded.']
  prior_period_labor_analytics_fact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on labor_analytics_fact (prior_period_labor_analytics_fact_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the store to which the labor metrics apply.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.wave. Business justification: Labor cost analysis per wave requires linking labor metrics to the specific wave where work occurred.']
  actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of labor hours actually worked during the period.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the labor analytics event was recorded (typically period_end_timestamp).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the type of event; fixed value \'labor_analytics\' for this fact.']
  labor_cost_percent_of_sales decimal(5,2) [note: 'Labor cost expressed as a percentage of total sales for the period.']
  labor_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost in U.S. dollars incurred during the period, including regular and overtime wages.']
  open_shift_count varchar [note: 'Number of shifts that remained open (unfilled) at the end of the period.']
  overtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of labor hours worked beyond scheduled hours that are classified as overtime.']
  period_date date [note: 'Calendar date representing the labor reporting period (typically the day for daily grain).']
  period_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the end of the labor measurement window.']
  period_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the start of the labor measurement window.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fact record was initially created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fact record.']
  sales_per_labor_hour_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross sales generated per labor hour worked, expressed in U.S. dollars.']
  schedule_adherence_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of scheduled labor hours that were actually worked.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of labor hours that were scheduled for the period.']
  transactions_per_labor_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of POS transactions processed per labor hour.']

  Note: 'Transactional Silver Layer fact capturing labor productivity and scheduling efficiency metrics at the store-department-period grain for Grocery. Stores scheduled hours, actual hours worked, overtime hours, labor cost dollars, sales per labor hour (SPLH), transactions per labor hour, labor cost as percent of sales, schedule adherence rate, and open shift count. Derived from workforce domain data. Serves as the authoritative analytical record for labor productivity reporting consumed by store operations, HR analytics, and finance teams.'
}

Table analytics.shrink_analytics_fact {
  shrink_analytics_fact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each shrink analytics record.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Shrink analytics are stored in analytical datasets.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store department (e.g., produce, dairy) associated with the shrink.']
  prior_period_shrink_analytics_fact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shrink_analytics_fact (prior_period_shrink_analytics_fact_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store where the shrink was recorded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shrink record was first created in the analytics layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary amounts.']
  cycle_count_variance_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar variance between recorded inventory and cycle count.']
  cycle_count_variance_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units variance identified during cycle count reconciliation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the underlying shrink data.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period for which the shrink metrics apply.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the reporting period for which the shrink metrics apply.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shrink values are estimated (true) or based on actual count (false).']
  markdown_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar value of shrink attributable to markdowns.']
  markdown_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units lost due to markdown-driven shrink.']
  period_date date [note: 'The calendar date representing the reporting period for the shrink metrics.']
  perishable_shrink_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar value of shrink due to spoilage or perishable loss.']
  perishable_shrink_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units of perishable items lost to spoilage.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shrink analytics record.']
  shrink_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of shrink for the period.']
  shrink_percent_of_sales decimal(5,2) [note: 'Shrink expressed as a percentage of total sales dollars for the period.']
  shrink_reason_code varchar [note: 'Internal code describing the specific reason for the shrink event.']
  shrink_type varchar [note: 'Category of shrink loss (e.g., theft, administrative, vendor, spoilage, markdown, cycle count variance).']
  shrink_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total number of units lost to shrink for the period.']
  total_sales_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross sales dollars for the store‑department‑period grain.']
  total_sales_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total number of units sold for the store‑department‑period grain.']
  unknown_shrink_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar value of shrink with no identified cause (administrative).']
  unknown_shrink_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units lost with unknown cause.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shrink record.']
  vendor_shrink_dollars decimal(12,2) [note: 'Shrink dollars attributed to vendor-related loss (e.g., short shipments).']
  vendor_shrink_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units lost due to vendor-related issues.']

  Note: 'Transactional Silver Layer fact capturing shrink and loss prevention metrics at the store-department-period grain for Grocery. Stores total shrink dollars and units, shrink as percent of sales, breakdown by shrink type (known theft, unknown/administrative, vendor, spoilage/perishable), cycle count variance, and markdown-driven shrink. Derived from inventory domain shrink_record and cycle_count data. Serves as the authoritative analytical record for shrink management reporting consumed by store operations, loss prevention, and finance teams.'
}

  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the access request was submitted.']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the requester.']
  resource_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific dashboard, report, or dataset.']
  resource_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the BI asset being requested.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Category of the BI asset being requested (dashboard, report, or analytical dataset).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the access request record.']

  Note: 'Operational record managing the lifecycle of access requests to analytics dashboards, reports, and analytical datasets within the Grocery BI platform. Captures requester identity, requested resource (dashboard/report/dataset), access level (view/edit/admin), business justification, approver, approval status, approval date, access grant date, and expiry date. Enables governed self-service access management for the analytics platform ensuring data classification compliance (restricted/confidential/internal/public) is enforced before access is provisioned.'
}

Table analytics.analytical_lineage {
  analytical_lineage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each analytical lineage entry. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity represents business‑level data lineage metadata, not fitting standard role categories.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the target analytical dataset produced by this lineage.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the KPI or metric that this lineage supports.']
  upstream_analytical_lineage_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on analytical_lineage (upstream_analytical_lineage_id)']
  analytical_lineage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lineage record.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent change to the lineage definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lineage record was created.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall data quality score for the lineage path (0‑100).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this lineage definition is effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which this lineage definition remains valid (null for open‑ended).']
  last_validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lineage record was last validated against source systems.']
  lineage_confidentiality varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the lineage record.']
  lineage_depth_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the lineage chain (0 = source, higher numbers indicate downstream steps).']
  lineage_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes about the lineage entry.']
  lineage_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or team responsible for the lineage definition.']
  source_domain varchar [note: 'Domain of the source data feeding the analytical lineage entry.']
  source_object_name varchar [note: 'Specific source object or table name within the source product.']
  source_product varchar [note: 'Name of the source data product (e.g., sales_transactions, inventory_snapshot).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system of record (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Retail).']
  target_system varchar [note: 'Target analytical system where the dataset resides (e.g., Databricks Lakehouse).']
  transformation_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the transformation logic.']
  transformation_type varchar [note: 'Category of transformation applied to the source data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lineage record.']

  Note: 'Master record documenting the business-level data lineage for each analytical dataset, KPI, and metric at Grocery. Captures source domain, source product, transformation type (aggregation, join, filter, enrichment), target analytical dataset or KPI, lineage depth level, and last validated timestamp. Distinct from IT-level ETL pipeline logs — this is a business-facing lineage catalog that enables data consumers to understand where a metric comes from, which source products feed a dashboard, and the impact of upstream data changes on downstream analytics.'
}

Table analytics.forecast_baseline {
  forecast_baseline_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the forecast baseline record.']
  metric_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.metric_definition. Business justification: Forecast baselines are defined per metric; add metric reference.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Store‑level sales forecast baseline needed for weekly forecasting reports; each baseline is tied to a specific store location.']
  superseded_forecast_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast_baseline (superseded_forecast_baseline_id)']
  baseline_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the baseline in external systems.']
  baseline_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the forecast baseline.']
  baseline_type varchar [note: 'Category of metric the baseline represents.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Primary forecasted metric value for the defined grain and horizon.']
  confidence_lower decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the baseline value.']
  confidence_upper decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the baseline value.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the baseline record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary baseline values.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the baseline becomes valid for planning.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the baseline expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  forecast_baseline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the baseline record.']
  forecast_frequency varchar [note: 'Regularity at which the baseline is refreshed or recalculated.']
  grain varchar [note: 'Granularity of the forecast (e.g., SKU‑store‑week).']
  horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks forward the baseline forecasts cover.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the baseline value has been manually adjusted.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the statistical or ML method used to generate the baseline.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the baseline.']
  seasonality_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to capture seasonal effects in the baseline.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system or tool that generated the baseline (e.g., Blue Yonder).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the baseline record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the baseline for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the statistical baseline forecasts used as the foundation for business planning and promotional lift measurement at Grocery. Captures baseline type (sales, units, traffic, margin), grain (SKU-store-week, category-store-week), forecast horizon, baseline methodology (moving average, regression, ML model reference), baseline value, confidence interval, and seasonality index applied. Serves as the authoritative reference baseline against which actual performance and promotional lift are measured in comp sales, promo effectiveness, and category performance analytics.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the scorecard becomes effective for business use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the scorecard is retired or superseded.']

  overall_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Color‑coded rating summarizing the scorecard\'s performance against targets.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing aggregated performance.']
  publication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the scorecard was published to the audience.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the period covered by the scorecard.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the period covered by the scorecard.']
  scorecard_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the scorecard in reports and communications.']

  scorecard_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the scorecard (e.g., "Q2 Store Operations Scorecard").']
  scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the scorecard.']
  scorecard_type varchar [note: 'Category of the scorecard indicating the business domain it evaluates.']
  target_audience_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level for which the scorecard is intended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scorecard record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for revisions of the scorecard.']

  Note: 'Master record for business performance scorecards published to store, district, region, and banner leadership at Grocery. Captures scorecard name, scorecard type (store ops, category, vendor, loyalty, digital), reporting period, target audience level (store/district/region/banner), constituent KPI list, overall performance rating (green/yellow/red), and publication timestamp. Distinct from dashboard (interactive BI tool) — a scorecard is a structured periodic performance summary document with explicit ratings against targets, used in operational reviews and leadership meetings.'
}

  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year for which the target is defined.']
  is_automated_target boolean [note: 'True if the target value is generated automatically by a forecasting system.']
  kpi_target_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the KPI target purpose and scope.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall record status indicating if the target is in use, retired, or pending.']
  minimum_acceptable_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lowest acceptable performance level before corrective action is required.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplemental comments or remarks about the target.']
  organization_level varchar [note: 'Level of the organization to which the target applies.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the KPI target period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the KPI target period.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that supplied the target value (e.g., Blue Yonder forecasting, SAP, manual entry).']
  stretch_target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggressive stretch target value for the KPI, used for high‑performance incentives.']
  target_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the KPI target was officially approved.']
  target_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the KPI target (e.g., TM_SALES_Q1).']
  target_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the target (e.g., top‑down corporate goal, bottom‑up store forecast).']
  target_owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the KPI target.']
  target_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for formal review of the KPI target performance.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KPI target record.']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Category of the KPI target indicating its business domain.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Primary performance target value for the KPI.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the target values (e.g., %, USD, units).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the KPI target record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the KPI target record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the approved performance targets set for each KPI at a specific organizational level (store, district, region, banner, enterprise) and fiscal period at Grocery. Stores KPI definition reference, target value, stretch target value, minimum acceptable threshold, target-setting methodology (top-down, bottom-up, benchmark-based), approving business owner, and target status (draft/approved/locked). Enables variance analysis between actual KPI results and approved targets across all scorecards and dashboards. Distinct from kpi_definition which defines the KPI itself.'
}

  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compliance program record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the program definition (e.g., v1.2).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each formal compliance program managed by Grocery, such as the FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) program, OSHA Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP), ADA Transition Plan, CCPA Privacy Program, and EPA UST Compliance Program. Captures program name, sponsoring regulatory framework, program owner, scope, program status, and the set of regulatory requirements it addresses. Provides the organizational structure for grouping related compliance activities.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compliance obligation record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Compliance Cost Allocation process assigns each obligation to a cost center responsible for funding and tracking compliance expenses.']
  obligation_associate_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for overseeing compliance with this obligation.']

  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: Certain obligations (e.g., controlled‑substance handling) are assigned to specific pharmacy locations.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Regulatory obligations (e.g., labeling, allergen disclosure) are assigned per SKU; the Compliance Obligation report requires linking each obligation to the specific product_item.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Compliance obligations belong to a compliance program; replace the existing string field with a proper FK to compliance_program.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Regulatory obligations are often supplier‑specific; tracking obligations per supplier is required for compliance dashboards.']
  attached_files_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting files are attached to the record.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance obligation record was first created in the system.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compliance obligation record.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit responsible for fulfilling the obligation.']
  compliance_metric decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric metric representing compliance performance (e.g., percentage of requirements met).']
  compliance_status_detail varchar [note: 'Additional detail describing the current compliance condition (e.g., "awaiting audit", "partial compliance").']
  department varchar [note: 'Department within the store or corporate structure that must satisfy the obligation.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the obligation must be satisfied.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the obligation becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the obligation expires or is no longer required (nullable).']
  evidence_documentation varchar [note: 'Reference or path to the document(s) that prove compliance.']
  evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting evidence must be provided for compliance.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the obligation applies.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation was last reviewed for relevance or changes.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status field last changed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the obligation.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code that uniquely identifies the regulatory or policy obligation.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the compliance obligation.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the obligation.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the obligation indicating its origin or focus area.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the obligation.']
  recurrence_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency with which the obligation repeats.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority that issued the requirement (e.g., FDA, OSHA).']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned corrective action if the obligation is not met.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with non‑compliance.']

  Note: 'Operational assignment record linking a specific regulatory requirement to a Grocery business unit, store location, department, or operational entity that must fulfill it. Captures obligation owner, due date, recurrence schedule (annual, quarterly, monthly), obligation status (pending, in-progress, fulfilled, overdue), and the compliance program it belongs to. Serves as the actionable work item driving compliance execution across all locations.'
}

Table compliance.audit_plan {
  audit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit plan record.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Audit plans are owned by a compliance program; adding program_id provides program ownership context.']

  audit_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria and standards against which audit findings are measured.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the audit is scheduled to occur.']
  audit_objectives varchar [note: 'Specific objectives the audit aims to achieve.']
  audit_outcome_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of audit results and conclusions.']
  audit_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit plan.']
  audit_resources varchar [note: 'Resources (personnel, tools) allocated for the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit as internal, external, or regulatory.']
  compliance_programs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance programs evaluated in the audit.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for implementing corrective actions from audit findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit plan record was created.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored audit plan document.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Total count of findings identified during the audit.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the audit plan was last reviewed.']
