  methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology or approach used for the audit (e.g., risk-based, checklist).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next review of the audit plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the audit plan.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Department that owns and maintains the audit plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for the audit plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the audit plan.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the audit plan.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for the audit.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the audit.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the audit plan.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory agency overseeing the audit (e.g., FDA, OSHA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|OSHA|USDA|DEA|FTC|EPA|HIPAA|SOX — promote to reference product]']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level assigned to the audit plan.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score derived from risk assessment.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit covering specific compliance areas (e.g., food safety, OSHA, privacy).']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated tags for categorization and search.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit plan record.']

  Note: 'Master record for scheduled compliance audit plans covering internal audits, third-party audits, and regulatory agency inspections across all Grocery operational areas. Captures audit plan name, audit type (internal, external, regulatory), scope (food safety, OSHA, ADA, privacy, financial controls), planned start and end dates, assigned lead auditor, audit methodology, and the compliance programs being assessed. Drives the annual audit calendar and resource allocation.'
}

Table compliance.audit_engagement {
  audit_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each audit engagement.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead auditor who oversaw the engagement.']
  audit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit plan under which this engagement is executed.']
  followup_audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_engagement (followup_audit_engagement_id)']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Audit engagement costs are captured via internal orders to track expenses against audit projects.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Audit engagements belong to a compliance program; direct program link simplifies reporting.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, pharmacy, or fuel center audited.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the audit work was finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the audit work actually began on site.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the audit was performed.']
  audit_methodology varchar [note: 'Primary technique applied during the audit (e.g., checklist, interview).']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Additional observations, comments, or contextual information recorded by the auditor.']
  audit_objective varchar [note: 'Statement of the audit\'s intended goals or focus areas.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Business area or asset category that the audit covers.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the audit engagement in its workflow.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit (e.g., internal, external, regulatory, vendor).']
  compliance_issue_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct compliance violations recorded during the audit.']
  corrective_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of remediation actions mandated as a result of the audit.']
  corrective_action_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which all corrective actions must be resolved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp indicating when the audit engagement record was first inserted.']
  engagement_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the audit engagement for tracking and reference.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Concise narrative of key observations and issues identified.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag set to true if any finding is classified as critical.']
  is_follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating that a subsequent audit must be scheduled.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Summarized rating indicating audit result.']
  rating_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., 0‑100) derived from audit findings.']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the detailed audit report stored in the document repository.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from the audit outcomes.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Date the audit was scheduled to be completed.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Date the audit was scheduled to begin.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the audit engagement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each individual audit engagement executed against an audit plan, representing a specific audit instance at a store, DC, pharmacy, or fuel center. Captures engagement number, linked audit plan, location audited, actual start and end dates, lead auditor, audit status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled), overall audit rating (satisfactory, needs improvement, unsatisfactory), and final report reference. The primary operational record for audit execution tracking.'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each audit finding.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Audit findings reference analytical datasets used as evidence; storing analytical_dataset_id ties each finding to the supporting dataset.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or auditor assigned to remediate the finding.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit engagement to which the finding belongs.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Audit traceability: audit findings often reference specific payment transactions that exhibited non‑compliance, supporting root‑cause analysis.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: Required for Pharmacy Compliance Audit Findings report linking each finding to the specific pharmacy location for remediation tracking.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: REQUIRED: Each audit finding is recorded under a specific compliance program (e.g., Food Safety Program) for program‑level reporting and corrective action tracking.']
  related_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_finding (related_audit_finding_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Audit findings need to reference the responsible supplier for root‑cause analysis and supplier scorecard calculations.']
  audit_finding_category varchar [note: 'Secondary categorization used for reporting (e.g., Process, Documentation, Training).']
  audit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the finding (open, in progress, closed, or deferred).']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'High‑level category of compliance (e.g., Food Safety, Occupational Safety, Privacy).']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Prescribed steps to remediate the finding and prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the finding was first entered into the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department (e.g., Produce, Pharmacy, Supply Chain) affected by the finding.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing the corrective action.']
  evidence_documentation varchar [note: 'Path, URL, or identifier of files that provide evidence for the finding.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Full description of the issue, including what was observed and why it matters.']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Short, human‑readable title that captures the essence of the finding.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Category of the finding: non‑conformance, observation, or opportunity for improvement.']
  regulatory_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific regulation, standard, or policy that the finding relates to (e.g., FDA Food Safety, OSHA Workplace Safety).']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the finding was marked as resolved or closed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating (e.g., 0.00‑10.00) reflecting the impact and likelihood.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Explanation of the underlying cause(s) that led to the finding.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned to the finding (critical, major, or minor).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the finding record.']

  Note: 'Individual finding or observation identified during an audit engagement. Captures finding ID, linked audit engagement, finding type (non-conformance, observation, opportunity for improvement), severity (critical, major, minor), regulatory requirement violated, affected location and department, finding description, root cause analysis, and recommended corrective action. Drives the corrective action and remediation workflow post-audit.'
}

Table compliance.corrective_action {
  corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the corrective action record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or team responsible for executing the corrective action.']
  drug_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug. Business justification: Corrective actions for drug‑related compliance issues (labeling, recall) must reference the exact drug involved.']
  followup_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on corrective_action (followup_corrective_action_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Corrective actions are driven by a compliance program; linking provides program ownership.']
  related_finding_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit finding or issue that triggered this corrective action.']
  related_violation_food_safety_violation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory violation (e.g., FDA, OSHA) linked to this corrective action.']
  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective or preventive action to be taken.']
  action_title varchar [note: 'Short, human‑readable title that identifies the corrective action.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CAPA as corrective, preventive, or both.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was actually completed.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit trail entries, comments, and observations.']
  closure_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was formally approved as closed.']
  closure_approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager who approved the closure of the corrective action.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) applicable to the corrective action.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corrective action.']
  cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual monetary cost associated with implementing the corrective action.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the corrective action record was created.']
  is_external boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an external vendor, contractor, or regulator is involved in the corrective action.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the corrective action record.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of any preventive measures added to avoid recurrence of the issue.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority for addressing the corrective action.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the issue based on impact and likelihood.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Narrative analysis identifying the underlying cause of the finding or violation.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective action should be completed.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action was effective (e.g., audit, inspection, test).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the verification activity.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record tracking the remediation of audit findings, regulatory violations, food safety incidents, and OSHA citations. Captures CAPA ID, linked finding or violation, assigned owner, corrective action description, preventive action description, target completion date, actual completion date, verification method, verification status, and closure approval. Manages the full CAPA lifecycle from identification through verified closure.'
}

Table compliance.food_safety_plan {
  food_safety_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the food safety plan record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the facility (store, DC, or production site) the plan applies to.']
  primary_food_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the plan.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Food safety plans are part of a compliance program.']
  superseded_food_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on food_safety_plan (superseded_food_safety_plan_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the food safety plan was formally approved.']
  audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the latest audit.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination against regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic version of the food safety plan document.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the plan expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  food_safety_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  hazard_analysis_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of identified hazards and risk assessments.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the plan contains confidential information.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit of the plan.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of the plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the food safety plan (e.g., FS-2023-001).']
  plan_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the operational scope covered by the plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the food safety plan type as required by FDA regulations.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  preventive_controls_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of preventive controls defined to mitigate identified hazards.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory agency governing the plan.']
  revision_history varchar [note: 'Chronological log of revisions made to the plan.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating associated with the plan\'s hazards.']
  training_completed_date date [note: 'Date when required training was completed.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff training is required for this plan.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plan record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for FSMA-mandated food safety plans (HACCP/HARPC) at each Grocery store, DC, and food production facility. Captures plan ID, facility, plan type (HACCP, HARPC, Preventive Controls), plan version, approval date, next review date, plan owner, and the set of hazard analyses and preventive controls defined. Serves as the authoritative food safety management document required by FDA 21 CFR Part 117.'
}

Table compliance.food_safety_inspection {
  food_safety_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the food safety inspection record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the inspector who performed the inspection.']
  followup_food_safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on food_safety_inspection (followup_food_safety_inspection_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Food safety inspections are conducted under a compliance program.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, pharmacy, bakery, deli, or distribution center where the inspection took place.']
  agency varchar [note: 'Agency or authority that conducted or oversaw the inspection.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Specific compliance domain addressed (e.g., hygiene, temperature control, labeling).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which required corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if any corrective actions were mandated.']
  critical_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical (high‑risk) violations identified.']
  follow_up_inspection_date date [note: 'Planned date for the follow‑up inspection, if required.']
  follow_up_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow‑up inspection is scheduled.']
  food_category varchar [note: 'Category of food items inspected (e.g., produce, dairy, bakery, pharmacy).']
  food_safety_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection record.']
  hygiene_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Numeric score assessing overall hygiene conditions.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'External or internal reference number assigned to the inspection.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection was performed.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection purpose: routine, follow‑up, or complaint‑driven.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the inspector conducting the inspection.']
  non_critical_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of non‑critical (low‑risk) violations identified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the inspector.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the overall result of the inspection, typically on a 0‑100 scale.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Outcome of the inspection: pass, conditional pass, fail, or closure order.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection record.']
  temperature_control_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature control requirements were met.']
  violation_details varchar [note: 'Detailed description of each violation identified during the inspection.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of food safety inspections conducted at Grocery store locations, delis, bakeries, pharmacies, and DCs by regulatory agencies (FDA, USDA, local health departments) or internal food safety auditors. Captures inspection ID, location, inspection date, inspector name and agency, inspection type (routine, follow-up, complaint-driven), overall score or rating, critical violations count, non-critical violations count, and inspection outcome (pass, conditional pass, fail, closure order). Distinct from the general store.health_inspection which is store-domain owned; this record is the compliance-domain authoritative record for food safety regulatory inspections.'
}

Table compliance.food_safety_violation {
  food_safety_violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the food safety violation record.']
  food_safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection or monitoring event linked to this violation.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU or product associated with the violation.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Food safety violations are tracked against a compliance program.']
  related_food_safety_violation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on food_safety_violation (related_food_safety_violation_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store where the violation occurred.']
  ccp_affected varchar [note: 'Critical control point or prerequisite program impacted by the violation.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Action taken on-site to remediate the violation.']
  corrective_action_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the violation record was first created in the system.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to detect the violation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the affected product batch.']
  food_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the food product involved in the violation.']
  food_safety_violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature of the violation.']
  food_safety_violation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the violation record.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the violation is deemed critical to public health.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for the product associated with the violation.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory clause or statute cited for the violation.']
  repeat_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is a repeat of a prior issue (true/false).']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who reported the violation.']
  reporter_role varchar [note: 'Job role of the person reporting the violation.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the violation was formally resolved.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes regarding how the violation was resolved.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score calculated based on severity, repeat flag, and product impact.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating assigned to the violation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the violation (e.g., SAP, Oracle, manual entry).']
  temperature_recorded decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading captured at the time of violation (if applicable).']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the recorded temperature (Celsius or Fahrenheit).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the violation record.']
  violation_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the specific food safety violation as defined by FDA or local health authority.']
  violation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the violation was detected or recorded.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the violation based on impact severity.']

  Note: 'Individual food safety violation or critical control point (CCP) deviation identified during a food safety inspection or internal monitoring event. Captures violation ID, linked inspection or monitoring event, violation code (per FDA/local health code), violation description, CCP or prerequisite program affected, corrective action taken on-site, repeat violation flag, and regulatory citation reference. Drives targeted remediation and trend analysis for food safety risk management.'
}

Table compliance.osha_citation {
  osha_citation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the OSHA citation record.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: OSHA citations are issued within the context of a compliance program.']
  related_osha_citation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on osha_citation (related_osha_citation_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Grocery store location where the violation occurred.']
  abatement_deadline date [note: 'Date by which Grocery must correct the cited violation.']
  affected_department varchar [note: 'Business department or functional area within the store impacted by the citation (e.g., Warehouse, Front End).']
  citation_date date [note: 'Date the citation was formally issued by OSHA.']
  citation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the violation and circumstances.']
  citation_number varchar [note: 'External citation number assigned by OSHA for tracking and reference.']
  citation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the citation.']
  citation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the citation based on severity and nature as defined by OSHA.']
  contest_deadline date [note: 'Last date to submit a formal contest or appeal.']
  contest_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any contest or appeal filed against the citation.']
  corrective_action_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the required corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required varchar [note: 'Specific actions required by OSHA to remediate the violation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the citation record was first entered into the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for penalty amounts (e.g., USD).']
  final_settlement_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount ultimately settled after negotiation or appeal.']
  issuing_osha_office varchar [note: 'Regional OSHA office that issued the citation.']
  proposed_penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount initially proposed by OSHA as a penalty.']
  standard_violated varchar [note: 'Specific OSHA or related regulatory standard (e.g., 29 CFR 1910.1200) that was violated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the citation record.']

  Note: 'OSHA citation record issued to Grocery following a workplace safety inspection or investigation. Captures citation ID, issuing OSHA area office, citation date, citation type (willful, serious, other-than-serious, repeat, failure-to-abate), standard violated (29 CFR reference), affected location and department, proposed penalty amount, abatement deadline, contest status, and final settlement amount. Distinct from workforce.safety_incident which captures the incident itself; this record captures the regulatory enforcement action.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the regulatory filing record.']
  amended_regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on regulatory_filing (amended_regulatory_filing_id)']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory filings are filed by specific legal entities, requiring a link to the entity’s financial record for compliance reporting.']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: Regulatory filings (e.g., DEA, state board) are submitted per pharmacy location, so the filing must reference that location.']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: FDA/USDA filings are filed for individual products; the Regulatory Filing register tracks the product_item associated with each filing.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Regulatory filings are associated with a compliance program; adding compliance_program_id provides program context.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Regulatory filings (e.g., FDA, USDA) are tied to the supplier that provided the product; needed for audit trails.']
  agency varchar [note: 'Government agency responsible for the filing (e.g., FDA, USDA, EPA, DEA).']
  attached_document_path varchar [note: 'File system or storage location of the submitted filing document.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the latest audit.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks from compliance reviewers.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance review for this filing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for the fee currency.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP, Oracle) that supplied the filing data.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the filing becomes legally effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the filing expires or must be renewed (nullable for open‑ended filings).']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee associated with the filing, if any.']
  filing_category varchar [note: 'High‑level compliance domain the filing belongs to.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the regulatory agency or internal tracking system.']
  filing_period varchar [note: 'Reporting period covered by the filing (e.g., FY2023 Q1).']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Category of the filing such as registration, certification, periodic report, license, or public notice.']
  is_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing is a renewal of a prior submission.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State, province, or country code where the filing applies.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit related to this filing.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next filing or renewal must be submitted.']
  regulatory_filing_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the filing.']
  regulatory_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the filing.']
  regulatory_section varchar [note: 'Specific legal section or code referenced by the filing (e.g., 21 CFR Part 11).']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'True if a future renewal is mandatory based on regulatory rules.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the filing was submitted to the agency.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'How the filing was delivered to the agency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the filing record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all mandatory regulatory filings and submissions made by Grocery to government agencies, including FDA facility registrations, USDA organic certifications, EPA Tier II chemical inventory reports, state alcohol beverage control license renewals, pharmacy DEA registrations, and CCPA privacy notices. Captures filing ID, regulatory agency, filing type, filing period, submission date, submission method, filing status (draft, submitted, accepted, rejected, pending renewal), and next due date.'
}

Table compliance.license_permit {
  license_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the license or permit record.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Licenses and permits are managed under a compliance program.']
  renewed_from_license_permit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on license_permit (renewed_from_license_permit_id)']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, legal entity, pharmacy, or fuel center that holds the license.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Many permits (e.g., alcohol, tobacco) are held by suppliers; linking permits to suppliers supports compliance reporting.']
  attached_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to scanned copy of the license or related documentation.']
  authority_code varchar [note: 'Standard code identifying the issuing authority (e.g., FDA, USDA).']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain the license belongs to (e.g., food safety, environmental). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food_safety|environmental|health|alcohol|tobacco|pharmacy|fuel — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which any required corrective actions must be completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the license record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the license becomes effective for business operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the license expires and must be renewed.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the renewal fee (e.g., USD).']
  fee_paid_date date [note: 'Date the renewal fee was paid.']
  fee_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the renewal fee.']
  holder_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity that holds the license (store, legal entity, pharmacy, fuel center).']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the license was originally issued.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Government or agency that issued the license or permit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|USDA|DEA|State_Board_of_Pharmacy|EPA|FTC|OSHA|Local_Municipality — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or local jurisdiction code (e.g., CA, TX) where the license is valid.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection for this license.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Official license or permit number assigned by the issuing authority.']
  license_permit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the license purpose and scope.']
  license_permit_status varchar [note: 'Overall operational status of the license.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Category of the license (e.g., food handler, alcohol beverage, tobacco, pharmacy, fuel station, business operating). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food_handler|alcohol_beer|alcohol_wine|alcohol_spirits|tobacco|pharmacy|fuel|business_operating — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the license.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date the license was last renewed or is scheduled for renewal.']
  renewal_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee required for license renewal.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current renewal state of the license.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the license based on compliance history.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational scope covered by the license.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the license record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all business licenses, operating permits, and regulatory certifications held by Grocery locations and entities, including food handler permits, alcohol beverage licenses (beer/wine/spirits), tobacco retail licenses, pharmacy licenses, fuel station operating permits, and business operating licenses. Captures license ID, license type, issuing authority, license number, holder (store or legal entity), issue date, expiration date, renewal status, and associated fees. Drives license renewal workflows and expiration alerts.'
}

Table compliance.privacy_request {
  privacy_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the privacy request record.']
  amended_privacy_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on privacy_request (amended_privacy_request_id)']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: Privacy requests often target loyalty member data; linking to membership enables precise data retrieval.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Privacy data retrieval: privacy requests may require extracting transaction details for a shopper, linking request to the exact transaction.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Privacy requests are governed by a compliance program.']
  rx_patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.rx_patient. Business justification: HIPAA privacy requests often target a specific patient’s prescription data; linking to rx_patient enables proper processing.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or household that submitted the privacy request.']
  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the privacy request was closed.']
  data_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of personal data the request pertains to.']
  data_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of data covered by the request (e.g., all data, specific items).']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the request fulfillment process.']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the request reached its current fulfillment status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes added by compliance staff during processing.']
  privacy_law varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the request (e.g., CCPA, GDPR).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the privacy request record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the privacy request record.']
  request_details varchar [note: 'Additional details or specifications supplied with the privacy request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the privacy request for tracking.']
  request_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the reason for the privacy request, as provided by the requestor.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of consumer privacy right being exercised (e.g., access, deletion, correction, portability, opt‑out of sale).']
  requestor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requestor (e.g., shopper, household, employee).']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which the request must be responded to.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the privacy request was received.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the privacy request was submitted.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the requestor\'s identity.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requestor\'s identity was verified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for consumer privacy rights requests received by Grocery under CCPA, state privacy laws, and internal privacy policies. Captures request ID, request type (access, deletion, opt-out of sale, correction, portability), requestor identity (shopper or household reference), submission channel (web form, phone, in-store), submission date, verification status, response due date (statutory deadline), fulfillment status, and closure date. Manages the full CCPA/privacy request lifecycle from intake through verified fulfillment.'
}

Table compliance.privacy_incident {
  privacy_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the privacy incident record.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Privacy incidents are tracked within a compliance program.']
  related_privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on privacy_incident (related_privacy_incident_id)']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.shopper. Business justification: Required for breach reporting: each privacy incident must identify the affected shopper per data protection regulations.']
  affected_data_categories varchar [note: 'Data classifications impacted by the incident (e.g., Personally Identifiable Information).']
  affected_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the party (customer, employee, vendor) whose data was impacted.']
  affected_systems varchar [note: 'List of internal systems or applications where the breach occurred.']
  containment_actions varchar [note: 'Actions taken to stop further data exposure (e.g., system isolation, password reset).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was initially created in the system.']
  discovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the incident was first detected.']
  estimated_affected_individuals varchar [note: 'Approximate count of distinct individuals whose data was compromised.']
  financial_impact_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary loss or cost associated with the breach.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the incident (e.g., INC-2023-0001).']
  incident_severity varchar [note: 'Risk rating based on impact and sensitivity of data exposed.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Category describing how the privacy breach occurred.']
  investigation_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or team responsible for the incident investigation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form field for additional comments, observations, or follow‑up actions.']
  notification_deadline date [note: 'Legal deadline by which affected parties must be notified.']
  privacy_incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the privacy incident.']
  regulatory_notifications varchar [note: 'Regulators or authorities notified about the incident (e.g., HIPAA, FTC).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the incident was fully resolved and closed.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Underlying reason for the incident (e.g., misconfiguration, insider threat).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the incident record.']

  Note: 'Operational record for data privacy incidents and potential data breaches at Grocery, including unauthorized access to shopper PII, pharmacy patient data exposure, payment card data incidents, and vendor data sharing violations. Captures incident ID, incident type, discovery date, affected data categories (PII, PHI, PCI), estimated number of affected individuals, affected systems, containment actions taken, regulatory notification obligations triggered (state breach notification laws, HIPAA), notification deadlines, and incident status. Distinct from payment.fraud_case which covers payment fraud.'
}

Table compliance.ada_assessment {
  ada_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each ADA accessibility assessment record.']
  followup_ada_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ada_assessment (followup_ada_assessment_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: ADA assessments are part of a compliance program.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the store location being assessed.']
  ada_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment record.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the ADA assessment was performed.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier or code assigned to the assessment (e.g., AA‑2023‑001).']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the assessment was conducted: self‑assessment, third‑party, or DOJ‑driven.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or firm that performed the assessment.']
  barriers_identified varchar [note: 'Narrative description of physical or digital accessibility barriers discovered during the assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected monetary cost to remediate all identified barriers, expressed in USD.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations captured by the assessor.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation activities should be completed.']
  remediation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to address identified barriers, based on impact and regulatory risk.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current progress status of the remediation work.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility assessment record for Grocery store locations, capturing the evaluation of physical accessibility compliance including parking, entrance ramps, aisle widths, restroom accessibility, checkout lane accessibility, and digital accessibility (website/app WCAG compliance). Captures assessment ID, location, assessment date, assessor, assessment type (self-assessment, third-party, DOJ-driven), barriers identified, remediation priority, estimated remediation cost, and remediation status.'
}

Table compliance.environmental_compliance {
  environmental_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each environmental compliance record.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference key for the scanned or digital compliance document.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Environmental compliance records are scoped to a compliance program.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical location (store, distribution center, fuel station, etc.) that the compliance record pertains to.']
  superseded_environmental_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on environmental_compliance (superseded_environmental_compliance_id)']
  agency_contact varchar [note: 'Phone number or email address for the agency liaison.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government or state agency overseeing the regulation (e.g., EPA, State Environmental Agency).']
  compliance_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the compliance cost amount.']
  compliance_cost_estimated boolean [note: 'True if the compliance cost is an estimate; false if actual.']
  compliance_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred to achieve compliance, expressed in US dollars.']
  compliance_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the reporting period for which compliance is measured.']
  compliance_period_start date [note: 'First day of the reporting period for which compliance is measured.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall status indicating whether the facility met the regulatory requirements for the period.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Progress state of the remediation work.']
  correspondence_date date [note: 'Date when the last formal communication with the agency occurred.']
  correspondence_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the agency communication was delivered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the compliance record was first entered into the system.']
  document_received_date date [note: 'Date the supporting compliance documentation was received by the organization.']
  energy_consumption_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total electricity or fuel energy used by the facility, expressed in megawatt‑hours.']
  epa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the facility is in full compliance with applicable EPA regulations.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the facility.']
  hazardous_waste_quantity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total amount of hazardous waste generated, expressed in short tons.']
  hazardous_waste_type varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous waste (e.g., universal waste, PCB, lead‑acid battery).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency performed the latest inspection.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or team that conducted the inspection.']
  last_inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection: pass, fail, or conditional.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the compliance record.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is currently active, inactive, or archived.']
  refrigerant_quantity_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of refrigerant stored or in use at the facility, measured in kilograms.']
  refrigerant_type varchar [note: 'Chemical name or code of the refrigerant (e.g., R‑410A, R‑22).']
  regulation_code varchar [note: 'Standard code or identifier for the specific regulation (e.g., EPA‑608, EPA‑UST).']
  regulation_type varchar [note: 'Broad category of the environmental regulation applicable to the record.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective steps required to address the violations.']
  remediation_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the corrective actions must be finished to avoid penalties.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record (e.g., SAP, custom EPA interface).']
  stormwater_permit_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the stormwater permit becomes invalid unless renewed.']
  stormwater_permit_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NPDES stormwater permit issued to the facility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the compliance record.']
  usta_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the facility meets all Underground Storage Tank (UST) regulatory requirements.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of separate regulatory violations recorded for the inspection.']
  violation_details varchar [note: 'Full narrative of each violation, including citation numbers and regulatory references.']
  violation_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level description of the violations identified.']

  Note: 'Environmental regulatory compliance record for Grocery operations covering EPA and state environmental obligations including refrigerant management (EPA Section 608), underground storage tank (UST) compliance, hazardous waste disposal (RCRA), stormwater permits (NPDES), and energy reporting. Captures record ID, facility, environmental regulation type, compliance period, compliance status, inspection date, violations identified, remediation actions, and regulatory agency correspondence. Complements fuel.epa_compliance_report which is fuel-center-specific; this record covers all non-fuel environmental compliance.'
}

Table compliance.training_completion {
  training_completion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each training completion record.']
  primary_training_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who completed the training.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Training completions are required by a compliance program.']

  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store or facility where training was completed.']
  training_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the required training program.']
  assessment_pass_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the assessment.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assessment used to evaluate the associate.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Log of changes made to the training completion record.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number issued upon successful completion.']
  completion_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the training completion record.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the associate completed the training.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Specific compliance domain addressed (e.g., Food Safety, Workplace Safety).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Resulting compliance status after training.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the training to the associate.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the training session in minutes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the training certification expires and must be renewed.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates if the training is mandatory for the associate\'s role.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language in which the training was delivered.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations about the training completion.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the associate passed the training.']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'Regulatory program satisfied by this training (e.g., FDA, OSHA, HIPAA).']
  role_required varchar [note: 'Job role for which the training is required.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the associate on the training assessment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the record (e.g., Kronos, Workday).']
  training_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the training (e.g., Safety, Data Privacy, Food Safety).']
  training_completion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training completion.']
  training_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the training program.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the training content.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual associate compliance training completions tracked by the compliance domain for regulatory reporting purposes. Captures completion ID, associate reference, training requirement, completion date, training delivery method, score achieved, pass/fail status, certificate number, expiration date, and the regulatory program it satisfies. Complements workforce.certification (which tracks professional licenses and certifications) by focusing specifically on mandatory regulatory compliance training completions needed for audit evidence.'
}

Table compliance.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each policy record.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Policies are scoped to a compliance program.']
  superseded_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on policy (superseded_policy_id)']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Describes where the policy applies (e.g., "All US stores", "Pharmacy department").']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the person or title who granted final approval for the policy.']
  attached_document_path varchar [note: 'Location of the electronic policy document in the document repository.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain linked to the policy (e.g., "FDA Food Safety", "OSHA Workplace Safety").']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification indicating how the policy content should be handled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy entry was initially loaded.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the policy starts to apply.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the policy is no longer in force (null for indefinite).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the policy must be followed; false if advisory.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the policy was last reviewed.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date scheduled for the upcoming policy review.']
  owning_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the policy (e.g., "Food Safety", "HR", "Legal").']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the policy within Grocery.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the intent, requirements, and key provisions of the policy.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the policy (e.g., "Food Safety Standard Operating Procedure").']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is in draft, active, inactive, revoked, or pending approval.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of the policy indicating its regulatory or operational domain.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific law, regulation, or standard (e.g., "21 CFR Part 117").']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the policy record must be retained for audit purposes.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval at which the policy must be reviewed and re‑approved.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer incremented each time the policy is revised.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system (e.g., "SAP Governance Module").']
  status_change_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the status reason was applied.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation why the policy is in its current status.']
  tags varchar [note: 'User‑defined tags that aid discovery and categorization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to any policy field.']
  version varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version label (e.g., "v2.3").']

  Note: 'Master record for all internal compliance policies, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and codes of conduct governing Grocery operations. Captures policy ID, policy name, policy type (food safety SOP, OSHA safety policy, privacy policy, code of conduct, alcohol sales policy, age verification policy), owning department, policy version, effective date, review cycle, approval authority, and applicability scope. Serves as the authoritative policy register driving compliance obligation fulfillment.'
}

Table compliance.policy_acknowledgment {
  policy_acknowledgment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each policy acknowledgment record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the associate (employee) who acknowledged the policy.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the compliance policy, SOP, or code of conduct being acknowledged.']
